Introduction
(Note: This document has tabs to separate all of the content. Try the Google Docs app if the tabs aren’t working on mobile. Faction tabs marked with * are actually sub-factions.)


Current Version: Beta 1.3.5 in progress


Join the Discord! https://discord.gg/NY2Uttmukv


Check out the List Builder by Vazsago! https://vazsago-art.github.io/trench-hammer/
Introduction
What is this?
This is a hack of the Trench Crusade rules for the 40k universe. It uses the core rules from Trench Crusade (https://www.trenchcrusade.com/rules), including Actions, Blood Markers, Injury rolls, Keywords, campaign rules, etc., but has its own factions, battlekit, and mercenaries (and a few new keywords, one new movement type, and psychic powers).


There are currently 25 playable factions, or over 100 if you include all variants. Some factions like Grey Knights or the god-specific Chaos Space Marines are technically variants instead of full factions, but they make heavy changes to their base factions.


Why are you doing this?
I had the idea and it would not leave my brain until I wrote it down. All of it.


Why not just play 40k?
I don’t personally like the 40k rules. Also I want a skirmish game. (If I want to play full army 40k, though, I can use Grimdark Future. Grimdark Future is great for big armies.)


How about Grimdark Future: Firefight?
Grimdark Future works very well, but Firefight is a little too simple and swingy of a skirmish game for me. Great as an intro game or something quick, but not what I really want long-term. A little more detail allows for that good good narrative to show through.


How about one of the many existing 40k skirmish games?
I like Kill Team and I do play it sometimes, but it’s designed with competition in mind and not narrative weirdness, and I want more variety of units and customization.


Necromunda has a great setting with good narrative focus, but its rules are fundamentally 40k, and also janky, complicated, and labyrinthine, and it doesn’t include the normal 40k factions.


Kill Team 1st edition, Heralds of Ruin, and Shadow War Armageddon all bring in 40k factions, but don’t have much in the way of recent models, and they still use 40k rules as a baseline. Which makes sense, as they are 40k skirmish games. What I want is perhaps unreasonable, but here we are. Come with me on this strange journey.


Or just play Trench Crusade?
Well, yeah. I do that too.


Does everything have to be 40k?
No, everything has to be Trench Crusade.
Who Am I?
I’m Chris “Goober” Ramsley (they/them). I make games. I currently work for Evil Genius Games creating the tabletop roleplaying game Everyday Heroes. Previously I’ve had some popular entries on the DM’s Guild for D&D, but I’ve been making stuff like this document forever. I’ve also been getting Trench Crusade going at my local store outside of Boston.


Fun fact: I have a degree in Interactive Media and Game Development.


This is purely a passion project, but if you want to contribute to my ability to keep making cool stuff, you could always buy me a Coffee: https://ko-fi.com/goober
Design Philosophy
Here are some ways in which I am designing this stuff.


Flyers. Most flying units in 40k also have Deep Strike, but Trench Crusade flyers typically don’t have any special setup. Some flying models get INFILTRATOR or DEEP STRIKE for other reasons.


Power Scaling Accuracy. When it comes to power level, I am leaning in the direction of 40k tabletop, as that allows things like Space Marines and Custodes to fight normal armies and have it be somewhat fair. But they should still feel more awesome than regular humans. Similarly, some of the light vehicles/walkers that are available have slightly reduced power to allow them to fit into a skirmish game better, and Necromunda gangs get to be a little more badass than usual.


Your Dudes. Customization is king. Lots of psychic power options add complexity, but more options is the goal here. Official 40k models should be mostly playable with their various weapon options available, plus lots more options.


Squad Leaders. Models like lieutenants, exarchs, or primes that are slightly cooler and normally lead full units don’t get their own special stat lines, but are instead intended to be regular models that have been promoted to Elite during a campaign.


Named Characters. Almost no named characters are included since Warbands are meant to be their own unique characters, but I try to include options to replicate the kinds of equipment and abilities they would have. That way you can include them if you like, use them as models for your own characters, or just use their traits as more options for customization.


Ongoing Effects. Trench Crusade mostly avoids having to remember any conditions or effects other than markers (blood, infection, blessing) and Down, but 40k has a lot of concepts that work best as temporary buffs, so I have included a number of these. You will likely want tokens or similar to track these conditions.

Changelog
Beta 1.3.5 (in progress)
General
* Pistoleer type abilities no longer grant ASSAULT. (Seraphim, Inceptor, Inquisitor, Vanus Infocyte, Starstorm, pirate Specialty, Grot Gunslinger, Hired Gun)
Battlekit
* Hand Flamers cost 25 instead of 20
* Weapons with Frag and Krak options no longer have Krak option
* Cyclone Missile Launcher and Missile Launcher have BLAST 2” instead of 3”
* Twin Heavy Stubber has 36” range, has HEAVY as intended
Campaign
* Champion skill does not apply to weapons that can be used in addition to normal attacks.
* Heavy Melee Training gives +1 INJURY DICE in melee if already STRONG
* Careful Exploration Skill still grant money
* Trader skill gives 2D6x10 credits
* Added Delete Exploration Skill
* Set Dice returns to being used after the roll
* Lookout skill replaced with Escapist
* ANATHEMA models cannot have Psychic Awakening
Mercenaries
* Grot Gunslinger Kustom Grot Blastas have ASSAULT

Beta 1.3.4: 5/03/2026
General
* Various typo and wording fixes
* Non-SCATTER weapons with BLAST cannot target a point.
* Added DANGEROUS Keyword to replace injuring self on a miss.
* Fixed a few more wordings that should use the VICIOUS Keyword.
* Effects that move Down models specify they can be moved despite being Down.
* Target Activation psychic power duration wording updated to end the next time the target’s Activation ends, removing the need for special wording for the caster itself’s current Activation, or new Fireteam wording.
* Fetch abilities do not work on LARGE models, except Macro-Grapplehawk
* Added ICON Keyword to simplify models that are better at mission-specific stuff.
Campaign
* Exploration options that provide one-time Campaign Shop access also give a discount on Glory cost.
* Some other Glory amounts and discounts in Exploration options updated.
Patrons
* Patron skills that grant Deny the Witch count as PSYKER 2 instead of 1.
* Patron skills that grant VICIOUS in melee don’t affect PISTOL weapons.
* Patron skills that treat allies as closer treat them as 6” close instead of 3”
* Administratum Prefectus Legal Action is a redeploy and not a move.
* Administratum Prefectus Threat Response has 8” range, cannot use a HEAVY weapon.
* Chaos Undivided Diabolit gives +1 DICE instead of +1 INJURY DICE.
* Chaos Warlord Flames of Spite effect replaced.
* Heretek Lord Architect Of Destruction increases BLAST by only 1”.
* Heretek Lord Bastion also grants BLOCK.

* Tzeentch Aetherstride wording updated to newer Diving Charge rules
* Tzeentch Incorporeal Form ignored also by STUN MARKERS or STUN.
* Pirate Lord The Boot prevents all Standing

Inquisition

Beta 1.3.3: 4/21/2026
Updated wording to Trench Crusade version 1.0.2.
* BURROW is now a Keyword instead of a movement type.
* Added FLYING Keyword to flying models.
* Replaced MULTISTRIKE with CLEAVE, now that it exists (they do the same thing).
* Updated 3d6 Injury weapons to DEADLY Keyword.
* Updated mine-setters to use MINED Keyword and gave them NEGATE MINED.
* Updated regenerating models to REGENERATE Keyword.
* Updated ammunition equipment to AMMUNITION Keyword.
* Updated Pistoleer style abilities to new wording from the skill.
* Updated Skill & Expertise Skill, but cannot choose Dash, and can choose battlekit actions.
* Resist and Bite Glorious Deed updated.
* Glorious Deeds that no model is involved in give XP to the Warband Leader (or any Elite with LEADER, technically).
* Patron skills that increase the limit for models or battlekit no longer continue applying if the model with the skill is removed from your Warband.
General
* Added SWEEPING Keyword to simplify weapons that hit every enemy in melee.
* Various wording and typo fixes and clarifications.
* Stunlock causes the model to fall/stay Down.
* Updated how placement and acquisition of markers for various campaign rules works (archeotech, ingredients, etc.). All are now recovered immediately rather than carried, and have more specific placement rules.
* Added Reinforce rule to make it more permissive.
* Two-handed weapons used in one hand via STRONG have MAIN HAND ONLY.
Battlekit
* Added Smoke Grenades.
* Stakethrower cost reduced cost to 10 from 15 in all factions that have it.
* Replaced old GRENADE Keyword with IGNORE COVER and IGNORE LONG RANGE.
* Krak Grenades cost 10 instead of 15.
* Melta Bombs have +1 INJURY DICE, but range is 6” instead of 8”.
* Psyk-Out Grenades moved to Shared Battlekit. Removed PSYCHIC Keyword.
* Las Cannons have 48” range and ARMOUR PIERCING 2 instead of +1 INJURY DICE. Updated to correct cost of 35 for Astartes and Heretic Astartes.
* Chain Glaive loses Shield Combo, but has VICIOUS 11
* Specified that a model can only equip one shield.
* Lightning Claw, Chain Fist, and Heavy Power Fist have HELD, and cost 12, 30, and 25 respectively.
* Storm Bolters no longer allow one-handing with STRONG by default.
* Terminator Armour allows STRONG to be used to one-hand a ranged weapon.
Psychic Powers
* Models with PSYKER Keyword cost reduced all around.
* Most PSYKER models can take powers from only one Discipline and no longer choose an additional Shared one.
* Many psychic powers updated in a variety of ways for better balance.
Shared Patrons
* Fixed missing Lord Inquisitor skill, moved some skill effects around.
* Ecclesiastic Inspiration skill from Ecclesiastic Cardinal grants 1 Miracle instead of 1D3.
Missions
* Toxic Crossing wording updated.
* Industrial Zone lasts five turns, and industrial accidents only happen is a machine is powered.
Mercenaries
* Fixed pistols missing PISTOL.
* Added Plague Zombie.
* Added Summoned Flesh Hound, Nurgling Swarm, Seeker, and Screamer.
* Added Accursed Cultist and Heretic Weapon Team.
* Hospitaller’s Medicus Ministorum, when you have Battle Hymns or Vows of Atonement, gets extra bonus within 6” (or 9” of a Dialogus) instead of 3”.
* Ministorum Priest’s The Emperor Protects updated to match Adeptus Ministorum Battle Hymn. Gains Standard Armour. Costs 4 Glory instead of 3.
* Ministorum Vindictor gets ASSAULT with its ranged attack and gains The Emperor’s Strength ability. Gains Standard Armour. Costs 4 Glory instead of 3.
* Questkeeper has +1 Ranged and +2 Melee, NEGATE FEAR, and costs 3 Glory instead of 4.
* Saint Celetine costs 2 Glory to deploy.
* Salvationist has +1 DICE with its Medicae Kit and Standard Armor.
* Flayed Ones cost 2 Glory instead of 3.
* Genestealers cost 2 Glory instead of 3.
* Ambull has CLEAVE 2 instead of two weapons.
* Venus Infocyte mercenary uses Ambidexterous ability text.
* Sister of Silence gets Psyk-Out Grenades.
* Blessed Blades cost 4 Glory instead of 5.
* Corsair Mercenaries have +2/+2 skill instead of +1/+1.
* Canoptek Reanimator’s claws get CLEAVE 2
* Canoptek Wraith coils have TWO-HANDED.
* Grot Gunslinger’s Ded Killy applies only to ranged attacks.
* Ordo Minorris Inquisitor has a Heavy Power Weapon instead of a Heavy Power Fist.
Adepta Sororitas
* Added Mortisanctus upgrade for battle sisters.
* Heavy Power Weapon no longer has LIMIT, but is Mortisanctus or Paragon Warsuit only.
* Hand Flamers fixed so they can be used by Battle Sisters.
* Repentia can take Purity Seals. (Why not?)
* Saint’s Pulpit costs 5 Glory instead of 4.
* Litanies of Faith applies only to Success Rolls.
* Armourium Cherub applies to a full Activation instead of one attack.
Adeptus Astartes
* Basic Astartes model renamed to Space Marine to avoid confusion with Keyword.
* Fenrisian Wolves have the ASTARTES Keyword.
* Dreadnought can use one-handed HEAVY weapons.
* Added Inceptor and Suppressor space marine upgrades.
* Can take 2 Plasma Pistols, and Inceptors can have them.
Adeptus Custodes
* Added Psyk-Out Grenades to Armoury.
* Interceptor Lance gets Shield Combo, but no base +1 INJURY DICE. Added to Armoury list for 15 credits.
Adeptus Mechanicus
* Redundant “except for Claw weapons” text removed.
* Firepoint Telemetry Cache simplified to use COVER Keyword.
* Clarified Sicarian Princeps gets ELITE and is the Warband leader.
* Sulphur Breath simplified to have ASSAULT keyword and clarified that it takes no hands.
* Skitarii Huscarls can take Power Armour.
Adeptus Ministorum
* The Emperor Protects Battle Hymn applies only to the first -1 Armour.
* Zealot’s Vindictor has ASSAULT and costs 45 instead of 40.
* Battle Cherubs can carry a Reliquarius.
* Miraculous always counts as having a Bludgeon.
* Dogmatas in the Ecclesiastic Militant Force get ORATOR and give BLESSING MARKERs instead of Miracles.
* Ecclesiastic Militant Force Fervor ability applies within 6” (or 9” of a Dialogus) instead of 3”.
Aeldari
* Wraithseer fixed to have 60mm base, -3 Armour, and +10 cost.
Astra Militarum
* Death Corp Sappers cost +15 instead of +20.
* Conscripts activate in Fireteams, and count as half a model for morale and model limit, but have -1 Ranged Skill. You cannot take more of them than other models.
* Moiraean Lance clarified to draw a line only when used to make a ranged attack.
* Traitor Guard can choose Heretek Lord or one of the four gods as their patron.
Chaos Cult
* Added Helot Cult Warband Variant.
* Cult Rabble can upgrade to Chaos Devotees and keep their special ability. They now have NO PROMOTION.
* Blade of Chaos gives a Bludgeon if it is temporary and the model has no melee weapon.
* Gun Morph gives a Las Pistol if it is temporary and the model has no ranged weapon.
* Warp Claws reworded to allow choosing a held weapon or a claw in battle.
* All Consuming Hatred does not stack.
* Added Stub Pistol to armoury.
* Added Psychic Familiar to armoury.
* Burning Censor has TWO-HANDED.
* Permanent Gifts of Chaos do not increase a model’s cost, and cost 5 for one-off games.
Drukhari
* Haemonculus Coven can take any number of Wracks.
* Wych Cult can take any number of Wyches.
* Tormentor Helm has IGNORE LONG RANGE.
Genestealer Cults
* Add Malstrain Variant.
* Broodlord has N/A Ranged Skill and costs 10 less, as its only ranged attacks uses its PSYKER Keyword.
* Broodcoven can recruit up to 5 Genestealers, but only up to 3 Acolytes.
* Final Day can take Von Ryan’s Leapers mercenaries.
Heretic Astartes
* Basic Heretic Astartes model renamed to Chaos Space Marine to avoid confusion with Keyword.
* Helbrute Hammer adds INJURY MODIFIER instead of INJURY DICE.
* Power Scourge uses SWEEPING Keyword and has AP 1.
* World Eaters can upgrade marines to Berzerkers, allowing them to ignore HEAVY on one melee weapon. They can no longer upgrade Havocs.
* Jakhals get +1 melee skill (total +0) and cost 5 more. Goremongers further gain SKIRMISHER.
* Blood Harpoon is Jakhal Only, LIMIT 1, and is a melee weapon with WHIP 18”.
* Added Twin Chain Blades to World Eaters armoury. Removed extra Chain Glaives.
* World Eaters can take two Chain Fists.
* Tallyband, Carnival of Excess, Changehost, and Daemonkin cannot recruit “Summoned” mercenaries.
Inquisition
* Has the full extra 11 Glory for one-off games instead of just 7.
Necromunda Gang
* Added Alliances to Campaign Shop and all of their associated mercenaries.
* Added Delegation Gang.
* Added Scavvies Gang.
* Added Slave Ogryn Gang.
* Added Scummers Gang.
* Added Ratskins Gang.
* Added Stim-Slug Cache equipment.
* Added Camo Cloak and Grav Chute to Armoury.
* Blessed By The Lord Of Skin & Sinew no longer has base -1 DICE.
* Corpsegrinder Cult have restricted access to Chaos mercenaries, but get Hive and Hive Beast mercenaries.
* Ironhead Squat Prospectors get IMPERVIOUS for -1 of their armour instead of extra armour, but cost 10 less. Exo-Drillers get IMPERVIOUS for -1 of their armour.
* Ironhead Squat Prospector Juves have +0 skills, and cost 10 more (net 0 with IMPERVIOUS).
* Ironhead Squat Prospector GANGERS count as VOTANN.
* Techmite Ferramite Plating upgrade also gives IMPERVIOUS to -1 of its armour.
* House Orlock has no limit on Boltguns.
* Digi Lasers replaced with updated Digital Lasers.
* Added Asbestos Mandilion to House Cawdor.
* House Escher Champions get +1 DICE instead of +1 INJURY DICE from their extra abilities.
* Sump Lord skill Tunnel Rat renamed to Tunnel Runner.
* Paired Blades cost 12 like other factions.
* Hared armoury Halberd costs 7 instead of 10.
* Added additional equipment to Ash Waste Nomads, but they cannot take normal chems.
* Blast Carbine and Blast Rifle cost 12 and 10 respectively, but replace -1 INJURY DICE and FIRE with CRITICAL.
* Added Ashwing Helamites to Ash Waste Nomads, but they can take only 1 Duneskuttler.
* Added Haunt upgrade for Palanite Enforcers Juves.
* Palanite Enforcers get Dum-Dum Ammunition and Power Weapons.
* House Goliath gets Dum-Dum Rounds.
Leagues of Votann
* All models besides Beserks get IMPERVIOUS for -1 of their armour and cost 5 more.
Necrons
* Reanimation Protocols has +1 DICE, but can succeed only once per Turn.
* Gauss Blaster is HEAVY and AUTOMATIC 2, but not ASSAULT, and costs 35. Twin version updated to match, costs 40, and is Tomb Blade Only.
* Gauss Cannon costs 40 instead of 30 and is LIMIT: 1.
* Tesla Carbine is ASSAULT, HEAVY, STUN, and VICIOUS 11 instead of FIRE, it can bounce once, and costs 25. Twin version updated to match, costs 30, and is Tomb Blade Only.
* Tesla Cannon increases range to 36”, costs 25 instead of 20.
* Heat Ray has LIMIT: 1.
* Necron Lord gets Relentless March, but has -2 Armour instead of -3.
* Royal Warden’s Engrammatic Logic requires no roll and has lots more options, it gets Relentless March, but it costs 100 instead of 85.
* Necron Warriors have -1 Armour instead of -2, but cost 45 instead of 70.
* Immortals get Heavy Gunner, but have -2 Armour instead of -3. They cost 5 less.
* Deathmarks and Lychguard cost 5 less to upgrade, but lose the Heavy Gunner ability.
* Lokhust Lord and Skorpekh Lord have +3/+3 skill each, but cost 15 more.
Officio Assassinorum
* Digital Laser campaign item replaced with Digital Laser Array, and Digital Laser added to armoury.
* Infocyte’s Ambidexterous updated to newer Pistoleer text.
Orks
* Adjusted the statistics and costs of most ranged weapons (and warbike which includes one), removing RISKY from most non-Mek specific guns and giving them -1 DICE instead, and largely reduced costs.
Pirate Crew
* Kin get IMPERVIOUS for -1 of their armour instead of extra armour, but cost less.
* Black Market Acquisitions counts credits for one-off games instead of a flat 3.
T’au Empire
* Ethereal’s Wisdom of the Guides invocation effect replaced. Sense of Stone and Zephyr’s Grace buffed.
* Demiurge Auxiliaries get IMPERVIOUS for -1 of their armour instead of extra armour, but cost less.
Tyranids
* Feed the Swarm ability for Assimilation Swarm simplified and works like a Medicae Kit.
* Zoanthrope has N/A Ranged Skill and costs 5 less.
* Neurotyrant has N/A Ranged Skill and costs 10 less.
Beta 1.3.2: 2/13/2026
Formatting
* Added list of available Mercenaries to each faction.
* Various wording fixes and clarifications.
Campaign
* Added Psychic Awakening skill to Wildcare chart, and removed War Luck skill.
Battlekit
* Psyker Staff renamed to Force Rod.
* Two-handed Force Staff weapon with a Smite ranged attack added.
* Force Weapons and similar reworked to be +1 INJURY DICE instead of ARMOUR PIECING 2 like Power Weapons, price increased slightly. LIMIT: 2 added to basic Force Weapons. LIMITS added to similar weapons.
Psychic Powers
* Smite power renamed to Bio Lightning so weapons can use Smite as the name of ranged attacks.
Adepta Sororitas
* Dialogus mercenary gets STUN in melee, weapon renamed.
* Added Virge of Admonition weapon.
* Trial of Purity also succeeds if your opponent fails morale.
Adeptus Astartes
* Dreadnaught cost increased to 170.
Adeptus Custodes
* Swapped TOUGH (and -1D Injury for elites) for a GOLEM-like ability, except Shield-Captain gets both. Shield-Captain has +20 cost.
* Custodian Wardens’ Living Fortress ability gives -1 of their armour IMPERVIOUS instead of regeneration, but costs 10 less.
* Strategic Mastery no longer requires a roll.
* Praesidium Shield costs 20 instead of 25.
Aeldari
* Increased skill bonuses for Elites, Guardians, and Aspect Warriors.
* Increased costs appropriately.
Aeldari and Harlequins
* Witchblade and Witch Staff conform to normal +1 INJURY DICE against PSYKER/DAEMON targets. Witchblade conforms to the new standard Force Weapon. Reduced cost for both.
Drukhari
* Various skill increases all around.
* Various new Pain abilities.
* Prices adjusted appropriately.
* Armour renamed and limited to more specific models, and added Wychsuits.
* Grotesque moved to Haemonculous Coven Only.
* Cronos and Talos moved to normal units.
* Heat Lance has FIRE (as it should have).
Genestealer Cults
* Religion institution also succeeds if your opponent fails morale.
Heretic Astartes
* Added Curseclaw, Depredator, and Warp Talons features to Night Lords, and they can choose to be Outlaw instead of Chaos.
* Helbrute cost increased to 165.
* Rubric Marines get NEGATE GAS, but range to get Dash bonus is 6” instead of 9”.
Necrons
* Heat Ray costs 40 instead of 60 due to not ignoring armour.
* Added Hyperphase Thresher weapon to Destroyer Cult.
* Only one model can reanimate normally per Turn, but the toll has +1 DICE.
* Plasmacyte Reanimator costs 10 instead of 15 and can alternatively be used to ignore the 1/Turn limit on reanimation.
* Nanoscarab Casket lets the model not roll of the trauma table instead of being a duplicate of Phylactery.
Orks
* Da Jump has a randomized effect, reduced range to 6” from 18”.
* Deff Dread can take 1 Assistant, including Grot Oiler.
* ‘Eavy Armour loses HEAVY, but causes non-STRONG models to roll D3 for charges. Costs 10 instead of 12.
* Da Biggest and da Best gives only 1 extra attack instead of 2.
* Stormboys must be equipped with a Jump Rokkit in order to be deployed.
T’au Empire
* Convict planet also succeeds if your opponent fails morale.
* Added Kroot Bayonet, and Bayonet Lug to some of their weapons.
* Javelins get ASSAULT, Hunting Javelins cost 15 instead of 10.
* Dart Bow can use Melee Skill.
Tyranids
* Blinding Venom costs 5 credits instead of 10 credits.
* Electroshock Grubs cost 7 credits instead of 10.
* Desiccator Larvae cost 10 credits instead of 7.
Votann 
* Volkanite Disintegrator exchanges ASSAULT for ARMOUR PIERCING 2, +5 cost.
* HEAVY Volkanite Disintegrator exchanges +1 INJURY DICE for IGNORE ARMOUR.
* Magna Rail Rifle cost increased to 45 credits from 35.
* Added Grav Pistols to armoury.
* Beserks get full STRONG.
Beta 1.3.1
General
* Reduced the cost of some melee-only models.
* Bladed Stance and similar abilities (except the Succubus, who has to pay to use it) can only be used by a model that has not acted yet, and costs its attack, but is a full Fight Action.
Psychic Powers
* Psychic Powers have been reformatted, PSYKER now has an X value assigned that determines the +DICE used for using powers, replacing abilities that allow additional powers before suffering penalties, and is used for attack powers instead of ranged/melee skill.
* Added PERILOUS Keyword that modifies the numbers needed to suffer Perils of the Warp.
* Use of multiple powers causes PERILOUS in addition to -DICE.
* Models with PSYKER 2 or 3 have increased cost.
* Deny the Witch slightly simplified.
Battlekit
* Hand flamers lose FLAMETHROWER in melee.
* Removed minimums from ARMOUR PIERCING.
* Lightning Claws cost 12 instead of 15.
* Added MAIN HAND ONLY Keyword. Some weapons were tweaked to accommodate.
* Heavy Shield grants cover instead of -1D Injury, as with Heavy Ballistic Shield in core game. Praesidium Shield loses -1D to be Injured, but adds IMPERVIOUS to -1 of armour.
* Thunder Hammer has +2 INJURY MODIFIER instead of +1 and AP 1.
* Renamed Power Fist to Heavy Power Fist, to be less confusing. Normal Power Fist is just a Power Weapon like any other.
Mercenaries
* Barhesi Raider is now an Outlaw mercenary, but Drukhari can still take it.
* Added Barghesi Thrall Outlaw, also available to Drukhari.
* Removed ELITE from Dialogus, to prevent it from becoming a must-take for Sororitas who benefit more from elites now.
Heretic Astartes
* Chaos Lord not STRONG by default, costs 10 less.
* Mark of Nurgle is ignored by Bloodbaths. Mark of Slaanesh gives +2” movement instead of +1”.
* God-specific Marks of Chaos cost 15 credits instead of 2 Glory.
* Death Guard’s Inevitable rule moved to Mark of Nurgle. Contagion ability is now extra, so they cost 5 more.
* Great Plague Blade gets power weapon armour piercing, costs +10.
* Limit 1 Thunder Hammer.
* Thousand Sons can recruit 2 Chaos Sorcerers instead of 3. (This was intended originally, but Exalted Sorcerer became its one model instead of an upgrade.)
* Bestial Prophet does not apply to self.
Sororitas and Ministorum
* Tweaks to Miracles rule and Sororitas elite abilities.
* Null Rod and Null Skull updated to add FEAR and Deny the Witch instead of an aura of no psychic powers.
* Repentia pay less for new Power Armour when promoted, but Battle Sisters’ armour becomes “reserved” while they are demoted.
Astartes
* Iron Halo costs 4 Glory instead of 2.
* Dreadnought equipment options clarified and simplified.
* Limit 1 Thunder Hammer.
* Great Frost Axe gets +2 INJURY DICE instead of +1, but loses AP1.
Custodes
* Custodes get STRONG, cost 5 more, but their main weapons get HEAVY and cost 5 less. Mostly this lets them use normal two-handed weapons in one hand.
* Custodes’ combined weapons modified to be closer to Power Weapons, prices re-evaluated.
* Custodes get Frag and Krak Grenades.
* Contemptor Dreadnoughts equipment options clarified and simplified.
Gene Stealer Cults
* Abominant hits all enemies in melee.
Necrons
* Destroyer Cult unique weapons tweaked and re-costed.
* Flayed Ones cost 70 instead of 75 or 80 for Court of the Flayer King and Destroyer Cult.
* Plasmacytes are now once per battle, but take no action or roll, and Accelerators give +2 DICE to the action. Accelerators cost 10 instead of 15.
* Resurrection Orb doesn’t require a roll.
Tyranids
* Shadow in the Warp causes increased DICE penalty to using multiple powers, since PERILOUS does not matter.
* Neuroloids’ location can be used as the Neurotyrant’s for psychic powers, but cost 45.
Pirate Crew
* Restricted Ork Campaign Shop items to ORK pirates.
* More specialization options.
Beta 1.3: 12/27/2025
* Wording fixes, typo fixes, keyword rearranging
* Reduced cost to models of melee/ranged skill across the board.
* Some minor balance tweaks and equipment updates to mercenaries.
* Reduced costs of some basic pistols across the board to match the cost of 6 for pistols in full TC rules release. Ion Pistols become Elite Only and LIMIT 2 and cost 25 instead of 15.
* Ion Rifle for T’au is now an infantry weapon. T’au Ion weapons get +1 INJURY DIE always, and BLAST 2” on overcharge, but now hurt the wielder on a missed overcharge like Imperial plasma weapons.
* Added Ion Beamer to Votann.
* Added Ecclesiastic Militant Force warband variant for Adeptus Ministorum.
* Arco-Flails ignore off-hand penalties and have ARMOUR PIERCING 1. Arco-Flagellants cost 1 more Glory, but earn 1 Glory on perma-death and give a BLESSING MARKER on OoA.
* Removed Expendable from Rat Swarm.
* Price of Las Rifle made 7 for more factions, 5 still for a few. Phosphor Blaster reduced cost to 10.
* Escort Supplies mission updated to have 3 transports.
* Parasite of Mortrex is ELITE now.
* Adeptus Ministorum melee hymn buffed and simplified. Ranged hymn affects accuracy instead of injury.
* Jump Pack loses INFILTRATOR, but cost is reduced to 20 from 25, or 15 for Pirates (due to replacing some Rigger benefits).
* Ravenguard now add DEEP STRIKE to their models with Jump Packs instead of replacing INFILTRATOR and ignoring the D3” movement.
* Raven Guard Corvus Marine costs 5 Glory instead of 6.
* Venatari Custodians cost +5 instead of +10.
* Effects that allow INFILTRATOR and DEEP STRIKE within 3” also remove D3” move for DEEP STRIKE.
* Swapped to using HELD Keyword for weapons that take up hands for both melee and ranged.
* Aeldari Guardians’ Ranger upgrade increased cost from 10 for 15, but now half can be Rangers.
* Farskitter Cloak is now a teleport instead of DEEP STRIKE.
* Ratling Tankstopper gets a Combat Helmet and Photo Goggles.
* Penal Legionnaire and Deathworld veteran Glory cost increased.
* Added Centurion Warsuit to Astartes.
* Updated Sororitas Miracles rule to use BLESSING MARKERS, and removed MIRACULOUS from some models.
* Tweaks to various Sororitas glory items.
* Vodmaster gets STRONG and costs 10 more.
* Big Shoota gets RELOAD.

* Bonded Exoskeleton gives IMPERVIOUS to -1 Armour instead of extra -1 Armour.
* Spore Mines and Mucaloid Spores ignore armour and have full effect outside of 1”.
* Tyranid Warriors Have +1 Melee Skill instead of +2.
* Raveners get TOUGH, but limited to 2.
* Added Subterranean Assault variant.
* Removed duplicate Norn Crown and increased cost of remaining one,
* Scorpion Claw is a one-handed weapon.
* Weapons Platform and Serpent’s Scale combined into Guardian Platform with two options, and they work differently.
* Made Mandiblasters equipment and gave Striking Scorpions Shadow Strike.
* Removed Windrider Host Warband Variant, but added Shining Spears to normal Aspect options.
* Added Phoenix Lord Patron.
* Lord Captain's Personal Flair clarified to be non-Patron skills, and no longer includes Exploration skill specifically (as they can get them from the Explorer skill chart).
* Fleetmaster grants Circle Back and Seek instead of any one Exploration Skill, as Expert Training can grant any of them due to the Explorer chart.
* Added Slanni to Pirate Crew Background options. Reduced cost for Duelist, Feral Worlder, and Voidborn.
* Photo Goggles ignore STEALTH now.
* Fellgor for Traitor Guard is now just Gor, and you always choose a type for it rather than upgrades. Reworked Slaangor.
* Added Chaos Pirates variant for Pirate Crew.
* Juves have -1/-1 skill and cost 15.
* Zoanthrope becomes a troop instead of a mercenary?
* Added Biophagus
* Space Marines and Custodes always turn Down into Minor Hits. Terminator Armour loses this property and costs 5 less, except for alternate version for Inquisitors.
* Wulfen Frost Claws cost 5 less, due to being Wulfen Only but not HEAVY, making them inefficient for the STRONG models.
* Custodian Guards max 2 of each upgrade, but you can take 5 instead of 4 total.
* Added 2 new Sororitas variants.
* Added Plague Vermin variant for Vermintide.

Beta 1.0
* First official version.
New Rules
New Rules
Base Sizes
Base size for a model is merely a recommendation. No mechanics reference base size, and the LARGE Keyword is used instead.
BLAST Targeting
If a weapon has the BLAST Keyword, but not the SCATTER Keyword, it cannot target a point, and must always target an enemy model, unless otherwise specified.
Mercenaries and Faction Rules
Faction rules do apply to mercenaries unless otherwise specified, though not if that rule specifies a Keyword a mercenary does not have.
Psychic Powers
Psychic powers are special abilities that PSYKER models have access to. They are purchased like Battlekit, and can be purchased after a model is recruited, but cannot be later sold. Each model’s statistics will tell you how many powers they can/must purchase, and from where.


In order to cast a psychic power, a model must take the Cast action, which always requires a Risky Success Roll. This roll is always considered to have the PSYCHIC Keyword, and the caster has +DICE equal to the X value of its PSYKER X Keyword.


A model can take the Cast Action multiple times during its Activation, but each time a model uses a psychic power during its Activation, it suffers a cumulative -1 DICE to all further psychic power Success Rolls, and its psychic powers have a cumulative PERILOUS ±1, made during that same Turn.


Note that some other Success Rolls, such as attacks with a Force Weapon, have the PSYCHIC Keyword, but do not count as psychic powers. They are not subject to any of the additional rules below, but PSYCHIC attacks can benefit from the PSYKER X Keyword (see that Keyword for more information).


There are two types of psychic power:


Attack. Psychic powers that provide attacks function just like weapons and have the same kind of statistics, but they use a model’s PSYCHIC X value to determine +DICE to Hit in place of Ranged Skill or Melee Skill. They do not take up any hands and special rules that specify “weapon” rather than “attack” do not apply to them. Melee psychic powers cannot normally be used as or alongside off-hand attacks. Using an attack power still counts as a normal use of Shoot or Fight in addition to Cast, and follows the same limits in addition to those for psychic powers.


Effect. An Effect psychic power applies a benefit, penalty, or other effect on a successful roll. It includes keywords, a range (Melee, X”, or No Range), what targets are allowed (Point, Self, One Ally, X Allies, One Enemy, X Enemies, No Targets, Each Model, Each Model Besides Self, etc.), and duration* (Immediate, End of Turn, Next Activation, Target Activation, End of Battle, or Special). On a successful roll, the listed effect takes place.


An Effect psychic power requires line of sight unless it has IGNORE LINE OF SIGHT.


*Duration definitions:
* Immediate: The effect takes place immediately and has no lasting effects.
* End of Turn: The effect lasts until the end of the current Turn.
* Next Activation: The effect lasts until the start of the caster’s next Activation.
* Target Activation: The effect lasts until next time the target’s Activation ends. If the power affects multiple targets, the effect ends on each individually.
* End of Battle: The effect lasts until the end of the battle.
* Special: The power describes its duration.
Deny the Witch
Whenever an enemy model uses a psychic power, before they roll, if one of your PSYKER models is within 12” of and can see that enemy, your model can attempt to cancel that psychic power. To do so, it makes a Success Roll, which has the PSYCHIC Keyword and +DICE equal to the X value of the model’s PSYKER X Keyword, then the opponent makes their normal Success Roll for the power.
* If your model’s result is equal to or higher than the opponent’s, the psychic power automatically fails. It can still cause Perils of the Warp as normal.
* If your model’s result is a failure, or a success but is lower than the opponent’s, the psychic power is used as normal.


Denying the Witch counts as a psychic power for the purpose of using multiple powers in one Turn, including suffering penalties if the model has already used powers this Turn, and is subject to Perils of the Warp. It is not otherwise considered a psychic power. A PSYKER can Deny the Witch only once during each Turn.
Perils of the Warp
Whenever a model uses a psychic power, if the result of at least one Success Roll made as part of that power is a 2 or a 12, then that model suffers Perils of the Warp, after any effects of the power, if any. When a model suffers Perils of the Warp, roll a D6 to determine what happens to it:
1. Dragged Into the Warp. The caster must make a Success Roll with -1 DICE for each psychic power it has used this Turn (including this one). On a failure, the caster is immediately taken Out of Action. On a success, the caster instead suffers D3 BLOOD MARKERS.
2. Mental Purge. The caster suffers 1 STUN MARKER and cannot use the psychic power that triggered the Perils of the Warp roll for the remainder of the battle.
3. Power Drain. The caster and each other PSYKER within 12” of it gain D3 STUN MARKERS each (roll once and use that result for all of them).
4. Psychic Backlash. The caster must roll on the Injury chart, with IGNORE ARMOUR.
5. Empyric Feedback. The caster gains 1 BLOOD MARKER.
6. Warp Surge. Until the end of the battle, the caster has +1 DICE to all PSYCHIC Success Rolls, but its psychic powers have PERILOUS ±2 and if it rolls this result again during this battle, it is treated as Dragged Into the Warp instead.


The PERILOUS ±X Keyword modifies the results that cause Perils of the Warp. For example, a power with PERILOUS ±2 causes Perils of the Warp on a roll of 2-4 and 10-12. PERILOUS always stacks with itself.
STUN MARKERS
STUN MARKERS are a new type of marker that function almost identically to BLOOD MARKERS, except you cannot spend them to add +DICE to Injury rolls or to cause a Bloodbath. You can spend them to apply -DICE to Success Rolls in the same manner as BLOOD or INFECTION MARKERS.


A model can have up to 6 STUN MARKERS in addition to any BLOOD or INFECTION MARKERS it may have.


Whenever an effect allows you to remove BLOOD MARKERS from one of your models, you can remove that many STUN MARKERS instead, or a mixture of the two.


Stunlock. In addition to the normal manner you can spend STUN MARKERS, when an enemy model activates, you can spend 6 of its STUN MARKERS to Stunlock that enemy, causing it to fall Down and ending its Activation immediately. If the model is Down, you can spend 3 STUN MARKERS instead, and the model remains Down.
Two-Handed Weapons with STRONG
When you use a two-handed melee weapon in one hand via the STRONG Keyword, treat it as if it had the MAIN HAND ONLY Keyword.
New Keywords
New Keywords
ARMOUR PIERCING X
As the base ARMOUR PIERCING Keyword, but weapons with this Keyword ignore -X worth of Armour.
BURROW
When moving, a model with BURROW can ignore Difficult and Impassable Terrain, as well as enemies, but it cannot end its movement inside of or surrounded by Impassable Terrain, and cannot end within close combat of an enemy unless it is Charging that enemy nor leave close combat without Retreating. Its movement also ignores Dangerous Terrain unless it ends that movement in contact with it.


A model with BURROW and FLYING can use only one at a time, choosing for each movement.
DANGEROUS (X)
After a model makes an attack with a DANGEROUS weapon, it must make a Success Roll. On a failure, the attacker must roll on the injury table. If any Keywords appear in parentheses after DANGEROUS, this injury roll has those Keywords.
DEEP STRIKE
Models with this Keyword are initially kept off the battlefield and can be deployed later. These models still count towards the number of models you have on the battlefield for purposes of who activates first, and for morale.


You can choose not to set up any model with this Keyword at the start of the battle. At the start of any turn after the first, so long as you have at least one other model on the battlefield, you can place any of those models anywhere on the table at least 8” away from the closest enemy, or anywhere within your deployment zone that is not in close combat with any enemy.


After each model is placed in this way, your opponent then adjusts it D3” from its current location, horizontally in the direction of their choice. Ignore terrain and other models when doing this, but it cannot end this placement in Impassable Terrain or surrounded by it, stopping at the closest legal location. If multiple levels of terrain are available at that horizontal location, such as a building with an interior and a roof, you choose which surface the model ends up on.


During its first Turn on the battlefield, a model placed using DEEP STRIKE cannot take any Actions added by the current mission's description or interact with any special mission objects or objectives, it cannot leave the battlefield of its own accord, and it does not count when determining control of any objectives or for the purposes of any scoring except by your opponent.


If you and your opponent each have models with this Keyword, alternate placing them, beginning with the player with the most total models. If a player chooses not to place one of these models, they cannot place any more during that Turn. i.e. they cannot see what their opponent places then change their mind.


If you choose not to place a model in this way that you kept off the battlefield for the entire battle, it does not count as having participated in the battle.
DEEP STRIKE (TUNNEL)
A model with this keyword uses the rules for DEEP STRIKE, but cannot be placed on any overhanging terrain or on top of any building with an interior (even if only imagined). Essentially, it must come up from the ground.
Faction Keywords
Factions Keywords mark a model as part of a specific faction. They don’t do anything on their own, but rules may reference them. The faction Keywords are:
* Adepta Sororitas: SORORITAS
* Adeptus Astartes: ASTARTES
* Adeptus Custodes: CUSTODES
* Adeptus Mechanicus: MECHANICUS
* Adeptus Ministorum: ECCLESIARCHY
* Astra Militarum: MILITARUM
* The Inquisition: INQUISITION
* Officio Assassinorum: ASSASSINORUM
* Rogue Trader: ROGUE TRADER
* Chaos Cult: CHAOS CULT
* Chaos Daemons: DAEMON
* Heretic Astartes: HERETIC ASTARTES
* The Vermintide: SKAVEN
* Aeldari: AELDARI
* Drukhair: DRUKHARI
* Genestealer Cults: GENESTEALER CULTS
* Harlequins: HARLEQUIN
* Leagues of Votann: VOTANN
* Necrons: NECRON
* Orks: ORK
* Slanni: SLANN
* T’au Empire: T’AU
* Tyranids: TYRANID
* Necromunda Gang: GANGER
* Pirate Crew: PIRATE
FUMBLE
(to remove after it is added to TC core rules)
If a weapon moves the enemy in the direction of the attacker's choice, the defender chooses in the case of a FUMBLE.
  

HELD
A weapon can have the HELD property, causing it to take up a hand, or two hands for a TWO-HANDED weapon, for both ranged and melee.


A model with STRONG can wield a TWO-HANDED HELD weapon in one hand for melee, allowing it to carry another melee weapon, but it still takes up both hands for ranged. CUMBERSOME prevents this.
ICON
A model with this Keyword has +1 DICE to all Success Rolls added by the current mission's description, it counts as two models for the purpose of counting how many models you have within a certain location, and it counts as being worth 50 more credits for the purposes of any VP you earn. Finally, while this model is on the battlefield and not Down, it counts as an additional model for the purpose of morale (but it is not counted as 2 for your starting number of models).
LARGE
Models with this Keyword are larger than other models, typically standing on 40mm bases. They are not affected by certain abilities or effects due to their size.


(This keyword replaces the rules for effects that do not work on models on bases larger than 32mm. If a model gains the LARGE Keyword, you can mount it on a 40mm or larger base to represent this, but you do not have to.)
LIMITED POTENTIAL
Models with this Keyword can learn only up to 3 skills from gaining XP if they are, or become, Elite.
MAIN HAND ONLY
This weapon cannot be used to make off-hand attacks.
NEGATE STUN
A model with this Keyword does not suffer a STUN MARKER due to the STUN Keyword. It can still suffer STUN MARKERS from other sources, such as the STUN MARKERS Keyword, Abilities, etc.
NONLETHAL
Injury rolls caused by this weapon treat Out of Action results as Down results instead.
NO PROMOTION
Models with this Keyword cannot be promoted to Elite during a campaign.
PERILOUS +/-X
A psychic power with this Keyword causes the caster to suffer Perils of the Warp on rolls of 2 to X higher than 2, and X lower than 12 to 12. PERILOUS always stacks with itself.


Example: A power with PERILOUS ±2 causes Perils of the Warp on a roll of 2-4 and 10-12.
PSYCHIC
PSYCHIC is a Keyword that can apply to Abilities and attacks, and all psychic powers have it by default.
PSYKER X
A model with PSYKER is capable of using psychic powers. The X value is the number of +DICE it adds when using psychic powers. It can use its X value in place of Ranged or Melee Skill when attacking with a PSYCHIC weapon.
STEALTH
Long Range attacks against a model with this Keyword suffer an additional -1 DICE to Hit. Attacks that ignore Long Range penalties also ignore this Keyword.
STUN
A weapon with STUN causes a STUN MARKER in addition to the normal result of its injury roll, even if no BLOOD MARKER was gained. If it also has STUN MARKERS, this is in addition to the normal STUN MARKERS.
STUN MARKERS
A weapon with STUN MARKERS causes STUN MARKERS instead of BLOOD MARKERS, including via other Keywords or Abilities.
SWARM
A model with this Keyword is made up of many tiny creatures. It cannot fall Down for any reason, but weapons with the BLAST Keyword have +1 INJURY DICE against it, as they strike all of the creatures at once.
SWEEPING
When a model armed with a SWEEPING weapon takes a Fight Action, you can make 1 Melee Attack against each enemy model with 1” of the attacking model. Resolve each Melee Attack one at a time in the order of your choice.
THROWN
This battlekit takes up no hands, but a model can be equipped with only one THROWN piece of battlekit at a time.
VICIOUS X
This weapon scores a Critical Hit on any roll of X or higher, if it otherwise would hit.
WHIP X”
A melee weapon with this keyword can be used to make a ranged attack with a range of X inches, with IGNORE LONG RANGE. Such attacks use the model’s Melee Skill. It still counts as a melee weapon for the purpose of hands used.
Campaign Content
Campaign Content
Mission Tables
Use these Mission Tables instead of those found in the Trench Crusade rulebook. Bolded missions are from the Trench Crusade rulebook.
Games 1-3
Roll D6 to determine which Scenario to play:
1. Claim No Man’s Land
2. The High Ground
3. Supply Raid
4. Meatgrinder
5. Toxic Crossing
6. The player with the fewest Campaign VP chooses one of the Scenarios listed above or to roll for a random mission. If tied, roll-off and the winner chooses.
Games 4-8
Roll D6 to determine which Scenario to play:
1. Hunt for Heroes
2. Escort Supplies
3. Industrial Zone
4. High Speed (or Claim No Man’s Land if appropriate terrain is not available)
5. Spec Ops
6. The player with the fewest Campaign VP chooses one of the Scenarios listed above or to roll for a random mission. If tied, roll-off and the winner chooses.
Games 9-11
Roll D6 to determine which Scenario to play:
1. From Below (call Hell Ticks “Xenos Beasts” instead)
2. Industrial Zone
3. Spec Ops
4. Fragile Bounty
5. The Ritual
6. The player with the fewest Campaign VP chooses one of the Scenarios listed above or to roll for a random mission. If tied, roll-off and the winner chooses.
Game 12
Roll D3 to determine which Scenario to play:
1. The Great War
2. The Inferno
3. The player with the fewest Campaign VP chooses one of the Scenarios listed above. If tied, roll-off and the winner chooses.
Skill Tables
These Elite skill tables replace those found in the Trench Crusade rulebook, and introduce the Explorer and Psychic skill tables. The Psychic skill table can be accessed only by a PSYKER model.


If a model already has an ability that causes a rolled skill to add nothing, treat it as if it had that skill already when rolling.
Melee & Strength Skills
Roll 2D6
Result
	Skill
	2
	Patron Skill. Pick one of the Skills offered by your Patron.
	3
	Stand Firm. This model treats the first Down result it suffers during each battle as a Minor Hit (does not affect Down results that replace Out of Action, such as via TOUGH Keyword).
	4
	Parry. Enemy models suffer -1 DICE to hit this model with Melee Attacks.
	5
	Close Quarters Combat. When this model is touching a piece of terrain, it gains +1 DICE to Hit and to Injure with Melee Attacks.
	6
	Relentless Charge. During Activations that this model has Charged, it gains +1 DICE to Hit with Melee Attacks.
	7
	Melee Proficiency. Add +1 to the Melee Skill of this model.
	8
	Heavy Melee Training. This model can ignore the HEAVY Keyword of any one melee weapon it carries. If it can already ignore the HEAVY Keyword of at least one piece of battlekit of any type, it gains the STRONG Keyword instead. If it already has STRONG, it instead has +1 INJURY DICE with all melee weapon attacks.
	9
	Hard as Nails. This model treats the first Down result it suffers in each battle as No Result.
	10
	Champion. Melee Weapons that do not have the CLEAVE Keyword which are used by a model with the skill gain the CLEAVE 2 Keyword. In addition, add -1 DICE to the Success Roll for the second Melee Attack made with each Melee Weapon that gains the CLEAVE 2 Keyword. Does not apply to weapons that can be used in addition to normal attacks.
	11
	Surgical Strike. Once during each of its Activation, this model can cause one of its melee weapon attacks to ignore Armour.
	12
	Patron Skill. Pick one of the Skills offered by your Patron.

	Patron Skill. Pick one of the Skills offered by your Patron.

	Hunter. This model has IGNORE COVER with all of its ranged attacks.
	4
	Far Shot. The range of all of this model’s ranged weapons that have at least a range of 12” are increased by 6”.

	Ranged Proficiency. Add +1 to the Ranged Skill of this model.
	8
	Heavy Ranged Training. This model can ignore the HEAVY Keyword of any one ranged weapon it carries. If it can already ignore the HEAVY Keyword of at least one piece of battlekit of any type, it gains the STRONG Keyword instead. If it is already STRONG, it counts as having this skill already.
	9
	Point Blank. While in melee, this model may make a Fight Action with a single Ranged Weapon it is equipped with instead of making any normal Fight Actions. This attack uses the model’s Ranged Skill. It can still use its Ranged Attack normally outside of Melee.
	10
	Hip Shop. Ranged weapons this model is equipped with have ASSAULT.
	11
	Head Shot. Ranged Attacks of this model have IGNORE ARMOUR on a Critical Hit.
	12
	Patron Skill. Pick one of the Skills offered by your Patron.

	Assassinate. This model gains +1 DICE to hit against models that have not been Activated this turn.

	Shadow Walker. This model gains the STEALTH Keyword.

	Athletic. This model has +1 DICE to all Climb, Jump, and Diving Charge Success Rolls, and injuries from falling damage against this model are rolled with -1 INJURY DICE.

	Sprinter. Add +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls this model takes.

	Nimble. This model does not suffer a penalty to its movement after it Stands.

Result
	Skill
	2
	Patron Skill. Pick one of the Skills offered by your Patron.
	3
	Chosen. This model begins each battle with 1 BLESSING MARKER.
	4
	Bad Company. This model does not count towards the normal limit of Elites you can have in your Warband.
	5
	But a Scratch. You may re-roll any Injuries on the Injury Chart for this model. The second result stands.
	6
	Serendipity. While this model is in your Warband, your Warband has the Lucky Exploration Skill.
	7
	Skill and Expertise. When you give a model this Skill, choose a Success Roll on that model’s Warband Entry, a Success Roll granted by a piece of battlekit, or 1 Success Roll associated with a Common Action apart from Cast, Dash, Fight, or Shoot, and write it on your Warband Roster. Add +1 DICE to the chosen type of Success Roll when taken by this model.
	8

	Glory Hound. At the end of each battle, if this model was fielded and was not taken Out of Action, your Warband gains an additional 1 Glory.
	10
	War Stories. At the end of the battle, each of your Elite models that does not have this skill gains +1 Experience Point.
	11
	Psychic Awakening. This model gains the PSYKER 1 Keyword (a PSYKER counts as having this skill already). Choose a Shared Discipline or a discipline that at least one model available to your faction can take powers from. This model can take up to three powers from that Discipline. It must be equipped with a PSYCHIC weapon to use these powers, and can always purchase a Force Rod for 3 credits.


This skill is altered in the following ways, depending on this model’s Keywords.
* ANATHEMA: You must reroll.
* ORK: Powers are chosen from the Ork Psychic Powers list.
* NECRON: You must reroll.
* TYRANID: Powers are chosen from the Tyranids Psychic Powers list. Does not need a PSYCHIC weapon to use powers.
	12
	Patron Skill. Pick one of the Skills offered by your Patron.

	Wild Talent. Randomly select a Shared Psychic Power (roll a D6 for Discipline and a D6 for power), rerolling powers this model already knows. This model learns that power without paying for it, which does not count towards its normal limits or towards your credit limit for a mission.
	9
	Burning Bright. When this model fails a PSYCHIC Success Roll, it can choose to reroll. Regardless of the result, it is subjected to Perils of the Warp, even if that action was not a psychic power or it would otherwise be unaffected by Perils of the Warp, after the results of the Success Roll.
	10
	Balanced. This model never suffers Perils of the Warp on a roll of 12. The PERILOUS Keyword only applies to numbers between 2 and 2 plus the PERILOUS X value.
	11
	Psychic Mastery. The second power this model uses during each of its Activations has +1 DICE.
	12
	Patron Skill. Pick one of the Skills offered by your Patron.
	________________


Reinforcing
When you Reinforce, you can acquire battlekit for existing models in addition to adding new models. You do not skip the Quartermaster Step.
Exploration Skills
Use these descriptions for Exploration Skills instead of those found in the Trench Crusade rulebook.
* Delete. After you roll, select any Exploration Die and remove it from your roll.
* Duplicate. After you roll, select any Exploration Die and add another die with an identical result, including any modifications, to your total.
* Careful. You may roll 1 fewer Exploration Die, to a minimum of 1. You earn D6x10 credits for each Exploration Die you do not roll in this way.
* Circle Back. Modify one Exploration Die result by -1.
* Extra Dice. You may roll 1 extra Exploration Die.
* Lucky. Roll an extra Exploration Die that is paired with one of your other dice. After you roll, choose one die in the pair to keep and one die in the pair to discard.
* Reroll. Reroll any single Exploration Die once.
* Seek. Modify one Exploration Die result by +1.
* Set Dice. After rolling, select any one Exploration Die and set it to a number you want instead of rolling it.
Exploration Tables
Use these Exploration Tables instead of those found in the Trench Crusade rulebook. Choose your table before rolling if there is a choice.


Battle #
	Exploration Dice
	Location Table
	1-3
	3D6
	Common
	4-8

	Common or Rare

	Rare or Legendary

	Location
	4
	Fallen Champion. You find the remains of a champion of your people.
* Loot. Now or during any quartermaster step, you can purchase a single piece of battlekit from your Campaign Shop that costs up to 9 Glory, paying for it, but its cost is reduced by 4 Glory.
* Memorialize (non-Tyranid Only). You earn 4 Glory.
* Consecrate (Adepta Sororita Only). Treat this exploration as if you finished an additional battle for the purposes of the Trial of Purity or Trial of Valor, having completed one of the requirements, even if you are not attempting that Trial.
* Retrieve (Adeptus Astartes Only). You earn 4 Glory, and add a Gene Seed to your reserves.
* Procession (Adeptus Ministorum Only). You earn 10 Flock Points.
* Lord Over (Vermintide Only). You earn 1 Glory and your Warband leader gains 4 Conniving Points.
	5
	Trained Beast. You find a strange creature that seems to already be tamed.
* Adopt (non-Tyranid Only). Add one of the following mercenaries to your Warband, ignoring normal limits. Roll a D6 to determine which.
1. Grapplehawk (Hive Beast)
2. Gyrinx Cat (Hive Beast)
3. Borewyrm Infestation (Wasteland Beast)
4. Giant Wasteland Rat (Wasteland Beast)
5. Millisaur (Wasteland Beast)
6. Sump Beast (Hive Beast)
* Sell (non-Tyranid Only). You earn 1 Glory.
* Slaughter (World Eaters Only). One of your Elite models gains 2D6 Claimed Skulls.
* Recruit (Exodites Only). Add either a Dragon Huntmate or a Dragonling Swarm, chosen at random, to your Warband, ignoring normal limits.
* Stomp (Orks Only). You earn 4 Stompin’ Points.
* Consume (Tyranids Only). You earn 2 Glory.
	6
	Chaos Shrine. You come upon an accursed shrine to the Ruinous Powers.
* Destroy (non-Chaos Only). You earn 2 Glory.
* Pray (Chaos Only). Up to two Elite models of your choice in your Warband each earn 2 additional XP.
* Dark Knowledge (Chaos Only). Your Warband gains the Lucky Exploration Skill.
* Foul Ceremony (Chaos Cult Only). Each of your eligible Elite models can roll for an additional Permanent Mutation.
* Dedicate (Chaos Daemons Only). You earn 1 Glory. You can add 1 Ascendancy to any god of your choice.
* Claim (Heretic Astartes Only). Add D3 to your Dark Gods Reputation.
* Deconsecrate (Adepta Sororitas, Adeptus Ministorum, Grey Knights, or Ordo Malleus Only). Up to two Elite models of your choice in your Warband each earn 2 additional XP.
	8
	Xenos Ruins. The ruins of an unknown xenos species lie ahead.
* Salvage (Xenos, Rogue Trader, Vermintide, or Pirate Crew Only). Now or during any quartermaster step, you can purchase a single piece of battlekit from your Campaign Shop that costs up to 5 Glory, paying for it, but its cost is reduced by 2 Glory.
* Study. Each of your Elite models earns 1 additional XP.
* Preach Against (Adeptus Ministorum Only, must be chosen if possible). You earn 5 Flock Points.
* Abhor (Adepta Sororitas Only, must be chosen if possible). Treat this exploration as if you finished an additional battle for the purposes of the Trial of Faith or Trial of Righteousness, having completed one of the requirements, even if you are not attempting that Trial yet.
* Destroy (Deathwatch or Ordo Xenos Only, must be chosen if possible). You earn 2 Glory.
* Claim (Drukhari Only). You can immediately purchase two random Territories for free.
* Acquire (Leagues of Votann Only). You earn 30 Energy Sources.
	9
	Supplier. You find a shady merchant or other source of rare equipment.
* Establish Contact. For the remainder of the campaign, you can purchase battlekit from your Campaign Shop that costs 3 Glory or less during the Quartermaster step, paying for it normally.
* Infect Supplies (Chaos Daemons Only). You earn 1 Glory. Add 4 to the Ascendancy of Nurgle.
* Recruit (Genestealer Cults Only). You earn 1 additional Infiltration Point.
* Convert (T’au Empire Only). You earn 1 additional Influence Point.
* Make a Deal (Necromunda Gang or Pirate Crew Only). The next Campaign Shop battlekit you purchase costs 1 less Glory.
	10
	Ammunition Cache. You find a supply of specialized ammunition.
* Take (access to compatible weapons* Only). Choose one Ammunition Equipment from the Shared Battlekit List. You gain D3 of that ammunition, which has no cost and ignores normal limits, though you can bring only 1 on each mission.
* Convert. Choose one Ammunition equipment from your Armoury or Battlekit List. You gain D3 of that ammunition, which has no cost and ignores normal limits, though you can bring only 1 on each mission. This equipment has CONSUMABLE even if it normally would not (i.e. Deathwatch special ammunition).
* Sell (or Consume). You earn an additional 100 credits.
*Autogun, Automatic Shotgun, Shotgun, Autopistol, Stub Pistol, Heavy Stubber, Bolt Carbine, Boltgun, Bolt Rifle, Bolt Sniper Rifle, Assault Bolter, Bolt Pistol, and/or Heavy Bolt Pistol in your Armoury/Battlekit List.
	11
	Relic Cache. You find a hidden cache of esoteric objects.
* Powerful Relic. Now or during any quartermaster step, you can purchase a single piece of battlekit from your Campaign Shop that costs up to 5 Glory, paying for it, but its cost is reduced by 1 Glory.
* Rare Relics. You earn 1 Glory.
* Arcane Secrets (Thousand Sons Only). You earn 2 additional Arcane Points.
* Raid (Drukhari Only). You earn 3 additional Raid Spoils.
	14
	Survivor. You find a survivor. You can add one of the following to your Warband, depending on your Faction, even if you could not normally purchase that model or its battlekit, or if it would exceed any limits:
* Imperial
   * Adepta Sororitas. A Novitiate with a Condemnor Boltgun and a Close Combat Weapon.
   * Adeptus Astartes. A Scout Marine with Standard Armour, a Bolt Pistol, and a Chain Blade.
   * Adeptus Custodes. An Anathema Psykana with an Executioner Greatblade.
   * Adeptus Mechanicus. A Skitarii with a Galvanic Rifle and a Close Combat Weapon.
   * Adeptus Ministorum. A Crusader with Standard Armour, a Shield, and a Blade.
   * Astra Militarum. A veteran Guardsman with a Las Pistol and a Regiment Flag.
   * The Inquisition. An Acolyte with a Stakethrower and a Shock Stave.
   * Officio Assassinorum. An Assassin Aspirant with Throwing Knives and a Poison Blade.
   * Rogue Trader. Randomly choose a survivor from among other Imperial Factions. (There are 8 total.) That model can be equipped from its normal Armoury or Battlekit List.
* Chaos
   * Chaos Cult. Cult Rabble with an Autogun and a Bludgeon.
   * Chaos Daemons. Varies depending on your Warband leader’s Keywords:
      * KHORNE: A Bloodletter with a Blade of Blood.
      * NURGLE: A Poxbringer with a Foul Balesword.
      * SLAANESH: A Daemonette with a Rapturous Standard.
      * TZEENTCH: A Blue Horror and a Blasted Standard (which it cannot be equipped with).
      * UNDIVIDED: A Chaos Furie with an Icon of Vengeance.
   * Heretic Astartes. A Chaos Cultist with an Autogun and a Bludgeon.
   * The Vermintide. A Clanrat with a Warpfuel Rifle and a Close Combat Weapon.
* Xenos
   * Aeldari. A Guardian with the Ranger upgrade, a Shuriken Catapult, and a Blade.
   * Drukhari. A Kabalite Warrior with a Shredder and a Blade.
   * Genestealer Cults. A Neophyte with the Miner upgrade and a Two-Handed Hammer.
   * Harlequins. A Mime with a Flip Belt, a Shuriken Pistol, Hallucinogen Grenades, and a Blade.
   * Leagues of Votann. A Hearthkyn with Standard Armour, a Boltgun, and a Plasma Blade.
   * Necrons. A Necron Warrior with a Plasmacyte Reanimator.
   * Orks. A Boy with a Kustom Shoota and a Choppa.
   * T’au Empire. A Fire Warrior with the Pathfinder upgrade and a Pulse Blaster.
   * Tyranids. A Termagant with two Chitinous Claws, a Strangleweb, and Adrenal Glands.
* Outlaw
   * Necromunda Gang. A Ganger (choose Bruiser or Shooter at random) with a Shotgun and a Close Combat Weapon.
   * Pirate Crew. A Pirate with a random Background and a random Specialty, an Autopistol, and a Close Combat Weapon.
	16
	City Ruins. You find the ruins of a destroyed city or district.
* Find Vantage. During your next Exploration, you have the Set Dice Exploration Skill twice.
* Scavenge. Now or during any quartermaster step, you can purchase a single piece of battlekit from your Campaign Shop that costs up to 4 Glory, paying for it, but its cost is reduced by 1 Glory.
* Recuperate. You can purchase models and battlekit until you have total credits worth up to your next mission’s credit limit, without paying for it. (Much like Reinforcing, but does not require you to discard unused battlekit or spare credits.)
* Scout (Astra Militarum Only). One model of your choice in your Warband earns the Recon Star Commendation.
* Pharmacy Raid (Emperor’s Children Only). You find D3 Common Ingredients and 1 Rare Ingredient.
* Acquire (Leagues of Votann Only). You earn 30 Galactic Intel.
	18
	Slag Field. The wreckage of ancient machines and the bones of giant creatures lie in disarray around you.
* Disassemble. You earn an additional 75 credits.
* Salvage (or Convert). Now or during any quartermaster step, you can purchase a single piece of battlekit from your Campaign Shop that costs up to 5 Glory, paying for it, but its cost is reduced by 1 Glory.
* Acquire (Leagues of Votann Only). You earn 30 Raw Minerals.
	20
	Battlefield. You come upon a battlefield full of fresh corpses.
* Loot (or Consume). You can add up to 100 credits worth of battlekit from your Armoury or Battlekit List to your reserves.
* Study. Your Warband gains your choice of one of the following Exploration Skills during your next exploration: Extra Dice or Careful. You can choose during that exploration.
* Acquire (Leagues of Votann Only). You earn 30 Biomatter.

Result

	5
	Archeotech Cache. You find a piece of Archeotech.
* Take. You gain a random Power Source, and roll a D2, or a D3 for an Adeptus Mechanicus Warband:
1. You gain a Weapon Part
2. You gain a Force Field Part
3. You gain a Techno-Arcana (Adeptus Mechanicus Only)


Use the Adeptus Mechanicus The Search For Archeotech special rules to reconstruct these pieces into a useful piece of battlekit, and any of your models can be equipped with the result as if it were part of your Armoury or Battlekit list. An Adeptus Mechanicus Warband can combine them with other pieces of Archeotech as they choose.
	6
	High Ranking Captive. You capture a high-ranking member of an enemy faction.
* Ransom. You earn 400 credits.
* Execute (Tyranids must choose this option). You earn 4 Glory.
* Imprison. Now or during any quartermaster step, you can purchase a single piece of battlekit from your Campaign Shop that costs up to 10 Glory, paying for it, but its cost is reduced by 4 Glory.
* Indenture. You gain the Set Dice Exploration Skill.
* Crump (Orks Only). You earn 8 Stompin’ Points.
	8
	Xenotech Cache. You find a cache of strange xenos technology.
* Take (Xenos, Rogue Trader, Vermintide, or Pirate Crew Only). Now or during any quartermaster step, you can purchase a single piece of battlekit from your Campaign Shop that costs up to 8 Glory, paying for it, but its cost is reduced by 3 Glory.
* Repurpose. Now or during any quartermaster step, you can take up to 3 pieces of battlekit from any Xenos Factions’ Armoury or Battlekit List (besides your own, if your Warband is Xenos), worth a combined total of up to 100 credits. You do not need to pay for this battlekit, and your models can equip it as if it were in your Armory or Battlekit List, but it counts as normal for credit limits, and normal limits apply.
* Meditate (Aeldari Only). Reduce your current Threat by D3.
* Acquire (Leagues of Votann Only). You earn 60 Energy Sources.
	9
	Black Market. You encounter a secret dealer, or similar being.
* Establish Contact. For the remainder of the campaign, you can purchase any battlekit from your Campaign Shop that costs 6 Glory or less during the Trade step, paying for it normally.
* Recruit (Genestealer Cults Only). The next time you choose an Institution, you earn 2 Infiltration Points immediately.
* Convert (T’au Empire Only). The next time you choose a Demographic, you earn 2 Infiltration Points immediately.
* Make a Deal (Necromunda Gang or Pirate Crew Only). The next Campaign Shop battlekit you purchase costs 2 less Glory.
	11
	Crash Site. You find a massive crashed ship.
* Salvage. Now or during any quartermaster step, you can purchase a single piece of battlekit from your Campaign Shop that costs up to 6 Glory, but its cost is reduced by 2 Glory.
* Report. You earn 2 Glory.
* Acquire (Leagues of Votann Only). You earn 60 Galactic Intel.
	13
	Augmetic Workshop. You find an ancient workshop where augmetic enhancements were once produced.
* Replace. You can remove one Trauma, though not the corresponding Battle Scar, from one Elite model in your Warband.
	14
	Drug Lab. You come upon a ruined lab, with some chemicals still frozen inside.
* Take. You find D3 random Combat Elixirs, as described in the Emperor’s Children Warband Variant. As normal, if you do not use them during the next battle, they are lost.
* Indulge. For the remainder of the campaign, your Warband rolls Morale tests with +1 DICE.
* Steal (Drukhari Only). You earn 2D3 additional Raid Spoils.
* Destroy (or Absorb; Adepta Sororitas, Adeptus Ministorum, and Tyranids must choose this option). You earn 2 Glory.
	17
	Mining Operation. You find the remains of a mining outpost, with a shipment of valuable ore left behind.
* Trade (or Consume). You earn 150 additional credits.
* Construct. Now or during any quartermaster step, you can purchase a single piece of battlekit from your Campaign Shop that costs up to 5 Glory, paying for it, but its cost is reduced by 1 Glory.
* Acquire (Leagues of Votann Only). You earn 60 Raw Minerals.
	19
	Promethium Deposit. You find a deposit of promethium gas, not quite exhausted.
* Rearm. You can add up to 150 credits worth of battlekit from your Armoury or Battlekit List to your reserves.
* Refuel. One VEHICLE model in your Warband gains +2 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls during your next battle.
* Huff (Chaos Daemons Only). You earn 1 Glory. Add 4 to the Ascendancy of Slaanesh.
* Acquire (Leagues of Votann Only). You earn 60 Biomatter.
	24
	Mercenary Company. You find a group of mercenaries that is friendly to your faction.
* Recruit. Now or during any quartermaster step, you can recruit one mercenary, reducing its cost by up to 2 Glory. If your Warband is not Aeldari, Drukhari, Harlequins, Necrons, Orks, Slann, or Tyranids, you can recruit a Hired Gun Hive Mercenary instead of a mercenary normally available to your Warband in this way.
* Duel (Heretic Astartes Only). You earn 1 Glory. Add D3 to your Personal Reputation.
* Fight (Orks Only). You earn 6 Stompin’ Points.
	28
	Warp Rift. You encounter a small, swirling rift of Warp energy.
* Mark Location. Your Warbands gains the Circle Back Exploration Skill.
* Summon (Chaos Only). Now or during any future quartermaster step, you can recruit one DAEMON mercenary, reducing its cost by 2 Glory.
* Syphon (requires PSYKER model). Choose one of your PSYKER models, then roll a D6 to determine what happens.
   * 1. The chosen model must roll on the Elite Trauma chart.
   * 2-5. The chosen model earns 2 additional XP.
   * 6. Randomly select a Shared Psychic Power (roll a D6 for Discipline and a D6 for power), rerolling powers the chosen model already knows. The chosen model learns that power without paying for it, which does not count towards its normal limits or towards your credit limit for a mission.
* Travel (Aeldari, Drukhari, Harlequins, T’au Empire, and Tyranids cannot choose this option). Roll a D6 to determine what happens. A Vermintide Warband can roll twice and choose one of the results.
   * 1. One of your Elite models, chosen at random, must roll on the Elite Trauma chart.
   * 2-5. Your Warband gains the Careful and Extra Dice Exploration Skills.
   * 6. Choose any other location you have not yet explored from the Common or Uncommon Exploration tables and explore that location. You still earn credits based on your original roll.
* Gaze (Aeldari Only). Reduce your current Threat by 2D3.
* Discover (Grey Knights Only). Give your Nemesis 3 True Name Points.

	Relic of the Ages. You discover an incredibly important object.

	Gene Bank. You discover a lost collection of powerful genetic modifications.
* Integrate. Choose one Elite model in your Warband. For the remainder of the campaign, at the start of each of that model’s Activations, remove 1 BLOOD MARKER or INFECTION MARKER from it. If a marker is removed in this way, the model gains 1 BLESSING MARKER.
* Recover (Adeptus Astartes Only). Add 3 Powerful Gene Seeds to your reserves. They function as Gene Seeds, but when used, the recruited model is also immediately promoted to Elite and earns 2 XP.
	10
	Secret Dealer. You find a powerful secret merchant or similar being.
* Establish Contact. For the remainder of the campaign, you can purchase any battlekit from your Campaign Shop, paying for it normally.
* Assassinate (Officio Assassinorum Only). You earn 2 Glory and one Elite model in your Warband of your choice earns 2 additional XP.
* Plot-Plan (Vermintide Only). You earn 2 Glory and one Elite model of your choice in your Warband besides your Warband Leader earns 10 Conniving Points.
* Recruit (Genestealer Cults Only). Choose an Institution you do not Control. You now Control that Institution.
* Convert (T’au Empire Only). Choose a Demographic you have not Converted. Convert that Demographic.
* Make a Deal (Necromunda Gang or Pirate Crew Only). The next Campaign Shop battlekit you purchase costs 3 less Glory.
	13
	Legendary Weapon. You find a legendary weapon of great value.
* Return. You earn 4 Glory.
* Wield. Now or during any quartermaster step, you can purchase any single weapon from your Campaign Shop, reducing its price by 4 Glory.
	15
	Lost Champion. You discover a champion of your people, lost until now. You can add one of the following to your Warband, depending on your Faction, even if you could not normally purchase that model or its battlekit, or if it would exceed any limits. If you have room for another Elite model in your Warband, you can immediately promote this model to Elite. If it normally has NO PROMOTION, it has LIMITED POTENTIAL instead.
* Imperial
   * Adepta Sororitas. A Battle Sister with the Impetuous Fervor Ability and the NEGATE FEAR Keyword (from having been a Novitiate and a Repentia), Power Armour, a Melta Gun, Frag Grenades, a Chain Blade, a Close Combat Weapon, and a Combat Helmet.
   * Adeptus Astartes. An Astartes with Power Armour, a Plasma Gun, Krak Grenades, a Chain Blade, a Close Combat Weapon, Sindexterous Cyberteknika, and a Combat Helmet.
   * Adeptus Custodes. A Custodian Guard with Auramite Armour, a Heavy Shield, a Guardian Spear, and a Combat Helmet.
   * Adeptus Mechanicus. An Electro-Priest with Standard Armour, Electrostatic Gauntlets, Vascular Cyberteknika, and a Combat Helmet.
   * Adeptus Ministorum. A Death Cult Assassin with Standard Armour, Incendiary Grenades, a Hand Flamer, a Blade, a Combat Helmet, Filter Plugs, and a Grapnel Launcher.
   * Astra Militarum. A Veteran Guardsman that is considered to have been upgraded from a Conscript, with the Drop Trooper Upgrade, Standard Armour, an Automatic Shotgun, Krak Grenades, two Close Combat Weapons, a Combat Helmet, and a Medicae Kit.
   * The Inquisition. A Jokaero Weaponsmith with Standard Armour, Digital Weapons, Frag Grenades, a Two-Handed Blade, and a Combat Helmet.
   * Officio Assassinorum. An Assassin Aspirant with Standard Armour, a Longlas, Krak Grenades, Paired Blades, A Combat Helmet, a Grapnel Launcher, and Photo Goggles.
   * Rogue Trader. Randomly choose a champion from among other Imperial Factions. (There are 8 total.) That model can be equipped from its normal Armoury.
* Chaos
   * Chaos Cult. A Chaos Devotee with an additional Permanent Mutation (roll twice and choose one), Standard Armour, a Heavy Stubber, Toxin Grenades, a Butcher’s Cleaver, a Close Combat Weapon, and a Covert Disguise.
   * Chaos Daemons. Varies depending on your Warband leader’s Keywords:
      * KHORNE: A Flesh Hound with a Collar of Khorne.
      * NURGLE: A Plaguebearer with a Plaguesword, Death's Heads, a Mischievous Nurgling, and a Sloppity Bilepipe.
      * SLAANESH: A Seeker with Lashes of Torment.
      * TZEENTCH: A Pink Horror with an Icon of Tzeentch.
      * UNDIVIDED: One of the above, chosen at random.
   * Heretic Astartes. A Heretic Astartes with Power Armour, a Heavy Stubber, Krak Grenades, a Chain Blade, a Close Combat Weapon, and a Combat Helmet.
   * The Vermintide. A Stormvermin with Power Armour, a Plasma Gun, Plasma Bombs, a Taser Goad, and a Combat Helmet.
* Xenos
   * Aeldari. An Aspect Warrior with the Striking Scorpion Aspect, Aspect Armour, Paired Chainsabres, Plasma Grenades, an Aeldari Helmet, and a Flip Belt.
   * Drukhari. An Incubus with an Incubus Warsuit, a Tormentor Helm, Demiklaives, Antitox, and Gruesome Talismens.
   * Genestealer Cults. An Acolyte with the Acolyte Miner Upgrade, Standard Armour, a Mining Laser, and a Heavy Rock Drill.
   * Harlequins. A Player with a Holo Suit, a Flip Belt, Hallucinogen Grenades, a Harlequin’s Kiss, and a Fusion Pistol.
   * Leagues of Votann. An Einhyr Hearthguard with Power Armour, a Volkanite Disintegrator, Frag Grenades, Twin Concussion Gauntlets, an Exoarmour Grenade Launcher, and a Teleport Crest.
   * Necrons. An Immortal with the Lychguard Upgrade, a Tesla Carbine, a Warscythe, and Necrodermal Plating.
   * Orks. A Nob with ’Eavy Armour, a Rokkit Launcha, Firey Stikkbombs, a Killsaw, and an Ammo Runt.
   * T’au Empire. A Kroot Carnivore with a Kroot Scattergun, Hunting Javelins, a Bladestave, a Kroothawk Flock, and a Medical Kit. You also gain a Missile Drone, which cannot become Elite or replace its NO PROMOTION Keyword.
   * Tyranids. A Tyranid Warrior with a Bonesword, a Lash Whip, a Deathspitter, and Dermic Symbiosis.
* Outlaw
   * Necromunda Gang. A Ganger Veteran from your Gang with Standard Armour, a Longlas, Krak Grenades, a Las Cutter, a Close Combat Weapon, a Combat Helmet, and a Grapnel Launcher.
   * Pirate Crew. A Pirate Veteran with a random Background and a random Specialty, a Plasma Gun, Incendiary Grenades, Paired Blades, Blade Venom, and a Combat Helmet.
	17
	Derelict Ship. You come upon an immobilized, but in-tact ship.

* Burn (non-Chaos Only). You earn 3 Glory.

* Blood Ritual (Chaos Cult Only). Each of your eligible models can roll for an additional Permanent Mutation, and each of your Elite models earns an additional 1 XP.
* Blood for the Blood God (Chaos Daemons Only). You earn 2 Glory. Add 4 to the Ascendancy of Khorne.
* Claim (Heretic Astartes Only). You earn 1 Glory. Add D3 to your Dark Gods Reputation.
* Bathe (World Eaters Only). One Elite model of your choice in your Warband gains 20 Claimed Skulls.
* Deconsecrate (Adepta Sororitas, Adeptus Ministorum, Grey Knights, or Ordo Malleus Only). Up to three Elite models of your choice in your Warband each earn 2 additional XP.
