Battlekit
The Commissar must be equipped with a Pistol weapon. It can otherwise be equipped with any weapons, armour, or equipment from the Astra Militarum Armoury.


Abilities
            * Summary Execution. Whenever you fail a Morale Test, you can select one friendly non-ELITE MILITARUM model within 6” of any of your Commissars that can see that model and that is not Down. If you do, that model is automatically taken Out of Action, and you can reroll the Morale Test with +1 DICE. You can repeat this process, adding the +DICE cumulatively, until you succeed or you run out of soldiers you can execute.

	0
	25mm
	

Battlekit
The Conscript must be equipped with a Lasgun and a Bayonet. It cannot be equipped with any other weapons, armour, or equipment.


Abilities
            * Conscripted. After a Conscript has participated in at least two missions, or if it scores a Glorious Deed or takes an enemy Elite model Out of Action, you can upgrade it to a Guardsman at the end of a battle for free. It uses its new cost when calculating how many credits worth of models you can bring to a mission.
            * Legion. When you activate a Conscript, activate another unactivated Conscript, treating them as a Fireteam, if one is available. The Conscript counts for only half of a model for the purpose of Field Strength and Morale. Round up after counting all Conscripts.

Warband Variant: Catachan Jungle Fighters  
            * Big Muscles. Your Primaris Psykers, Guardsmen, Veteran Guardsmen, and Heavy Weapons Squads have +1 Melee Skill and cost +5 credits each. You cannot recruit any Conscripts.
            * Trap Setting. Up to three of your Guardsmen can be upgraded to Trappers for +10 credits each. A Trapper has NEGATE MINED and gains the following ability:
            * Mine Layer. As an Action with a Success Roll with +2 DICE, the Sapper can mine a piece of terrain they alone are touching, no bigger than 8” x 8” (an 8” section of trench, a wall, a tree, a building etc). If successful, the terrain piece is now MINED.
            * Jungle Stealth. Instead of Drop Droopers, you can upgrade up to half of your Veteran guardsmen, rounded up, to Hunters for +10 credits each. A Hunter gains the INFILTRATOR and STEALTH Keywords.
            * Survivors. Up to 2 of your Veteran Guardsmen can be Sergeants, gaining the ELITE Keyword. Sergeants do not count towards the maximum number of Veteran Guardsmen you can recruit.
            * Commissar Distrust. You can recruit only up to 1 Commissar.
            * Human Focus. You cannot recruit any Ratlings, and only up to 1 Ogryn. Your patron must be a Lord General Militant.

Warband Variant: Death Korp of Krieg  
            * Born to Die. You have +1 DICE to all Morale tests you make.
            * Entrenched. You can recruit up to 4 Heavy Weapons Squads, and take up to 2 Mortars. You can purchase up to 2 Blasting Charges (CONSUMABLE) for 15 credits each
            * Sappers. You cannot upgrade any of your Veteran Guardsmen to Drop Troopers. Instead, up to two of your Veteran Guardsmen can be upgraded to Sappers for +15 credits each. A Sapper ignores the HEAVY Keyword of Blasting Charges and Heavy Armour, has NEGATE MINED, and gains the following ability:
            * Mine Layer. As an Action with a Success Roll with +2 DICE, the Sapper can mine a piece of terrain they alone are touching, no bigger than 8” x 8” (an 8” section of trench, a wall, a tree, a building etc). If successful, the terrain piece is now MINED.
            * Krieg Alone. You cannot recruit any Ratlings or Ogryn. Your patron must be a Lord General Militant.

Warband Variant: Imperial Navy  
            * Naval Authority. Your patron must be a Lord Admiral, described below.
            * Light Weaponry. You can take only up to 1 Heavy Weapons Squad, and you cannot take any Heavy Bolters, Heavy Flamers, Mortars, Multi-Meltas, or Twin Heavy Stubbers.
            * Naval Battlekit. You can purchase any number of Shields, up to 2 Blasting Charges (CONSUMABLE) for 15 credits each, and add the following battlekit to your Armoury:
            * C.A.T. Unit (5 credits, LIMIT: 1). Once during each battle, when making a ranged attack, the equipped model can gain IGNORE COVER, IGNORE LONG RANGE, and VICIOUS 11 with that attack.
            * Gheistskull Detonator (15 credits, LIMIT: 1, CONSUMABLE). 18”, BLAST 3”, IGNORE COVER, IGNORE LONG RANGE, SCATTER, SHRAPNEL, STUN, THROWN, this attack can be used only once during the battle.
            * Void Armour (18 credits, Armour). -1 to Injury rolls, IGNORE GAS
Patron: Lord Admiral
Or a similar Imperial Naval officer.
            * Blitz. This model has +1 DICE to Hit and +1 INJURY DICE with ranged attacks made against targets within 6”.
            * Deck Hand. This model ignores Difficult and Dangerous Terrain.
            * Grand Strategist. Select any skill you want from the Explorer Skills.
            * Honoured Duellist. This model has +1 DICE to Hit and +1 INJURY DICE with melee attacks against Elite enemies, but must attack an Elite target when making a melee attack if possible.
            * Lead By Example. When this model successfully issues an Order, it benefits from that Order as well until the end of the current Turn.
            * Precision Targeting. This model can ignore all -DICE to Hit with ranged attacks it makes.

Warband Variant: Ratling Regiment  
               * Chill Times. You cannot include any Commissars or Conscripts in your Warband.
               * Light Weaponry. You cannot take any Heavy Weapons Squads.
               * All Ratlings. Each of your MILITARUM models besides Ratling Marksmen, Ogryn, Ratling Trailblazers, Battlemutts, and Ratling Tankstoppers has +1 higher Ranged Skill and -1 lower Melee skill, the SKIRMISHER Keyword, and the following abilities:
               * Clamber. The model has +1 DICE to all Climb Success Rolls.
               * Small. The model cannot carry any HEAVY battlekit. If it somehow gains the STRONG Keyword, the two cancel out.
               * Ratling Elites. You can take up to 2 Ratling Trailblazers, described below, as ELITEs in your Warband.
               * Battlemutts. You can recruit up to 2 Battlemutts, described below, as Troops in your Warband.
               * Ogryn Allies. You can recruit up to 3 Ogryn.
               * Tankstopper. You can recruit a single Ratling Tankstopper Mercenary for 140 credits as a Troop in your Warband. You still cannot change its battlekit.
               * Expensive Taste. You must supply your troops with quality spices and other foodstuffs. You earn D3x5 fewer credits whenever you Explore.

Warband Variant: Traitor Guard  
               * Traitors. Your Warband counts as a Chaos Warband instead of an Imperium Warband, and you recruit mercenaries as such. Your patron must be one of the following Shared Patrons: Chaos Warlord, Chaos Undivided, Heretek Lord, Khorne, Nurgle, Slannesh, or Tzeentch.
               * Rule of Force. You cannot include any Commissars or Conscripts in your Warband. Instead, you can recruit up to 1 Enforcer, described below, as an Elite in your Warband.
               * Rogue Psykers. Your Primaris Psykers are known as Rogue Psykers, they have PSYKER 2 instead of PSYKER 1, and you can recruit up to 2 of them. Instead of the Psykana Discipline, Rogue Psykers can optionally take powers from the Hereticus Psychic Discipline from the Chaos Cult Faction. They gain the Power Unleashed Ability.
               * Power Unleashed. The Rogue Psyker has PERILOUS ±2 with all psychic powers.
               * Beastmen. You can recruit up to 3 Gor, described below, as Troops in your Warband.
               * Hard to Corrupt. You can recruit only up to 1 Ratling Marksman and 1 Ogryn.

If the friendly model was taken Out of Action, it also has +1 INJURY DICE with all attacks during that Activation.

               * Slaangor. The Slaangor has the SLAANESH Keyword and the first time during each Turn that it suffers any BLOOD MARKERS, if it is not Down, it can move up to 3”, and can move into or out of melee in this way, treating it as a Retreat or Charge as appropriate.
               * Tzaangor. The Tzaangor has +1 Ranged Skill and the SKIRMISHER and TZEENTCH Keywords.

*Grey Knights
Grey Knights
Grey Knights is an Adeptus Astartes Warband Variant. Don’t worry, they have extensive modifications that make them feel like their own Faction!
The Inquisition
The Inquisition
  

Inquisition Special Rules
Ordo. When creating an Inquisition Warband, you must choose one of the Ordos listed below. You can take the special models and battlekit from that Ordo, and follow any additional special rules it has, but you cannot take any models or battlekit from any of the other Ordos.


If a piece of battlekit appears in the Inquisition Armoury as well as your Chamber Militant’s Armoury, you must obey the highest LIMIT between those Armouries. Models can still only be equipped from their own Armoury unless otherwise stated. You can also purchase Campaign Shop battlekit from your Chamber Militant Faction, which can be used by models from that Faction as well as your INQUISITION models, following all normal limits.


Requisitions. During a campaign, your Warband begins with 11 Glory, but only 500 credits, and your Credit Threshold is always 200 credits lower than normal for any mission. In a one-off game, you have 200 fewer credits and 11 more Glory than normal.


Whenever you Reinforce during a campaign, you can also optionally perform all of the following:
               * Remove all unspent Glory that you have.
               * Count the amount of Glory spent on battlekit and Mercenaries in your Roster, Arsenal and Reserves. If the total is less than 11 plus twice the number of missions you have played, you can purchase battlekit that costs Glory and hire Mercenaries for free, up to a total cost equal to the difference.


Example: You have played 3 missions, and currently have 1 unspent Glory, a piece of battlekit worth 3 Glory, and three Mercenaries, worth 2, 3, and 4 Glory, for a total of 12 Glory. 11 plus twice the number of missions you have played is 17, so you can purchase/hire up to 5 more Glory worth of battlekit and Mercenaries for free, but must also give up your 1 unspent Glory before this.


Shadow Operations. During a campaign, at the start of each battle, roll a D6 to determine your Shadow Operation for that battle:
               1. Agent Provocateur. Secretly choose one enemy Elite model. At the end of the battle, reveal your choice. If the chosen model was taken Out of Action, you succeed at this Shadow Operation.
               2. Ancient Artefact. At the start of the battle, before deployment, your opponent places a 1” Artefact marker anywhere that is exactly 18” away from your deployment zone, but not within 6” of a battlefield edge or on Impassable Terrain or completely surrounded by it. As an Action with a Success Roll, any of your models in contact with the marker can attempt to recover that marker. On a success, remove the marker and you succeed at this Shadow Operation.
               3. Diabolical Ritual. At the start of the battle, your opponent places 3 1” Ritual markers anywhere on the battlefield outside of deployment zones, at least 6” away from any table edge and from each other marker, and not on Impassable Terrain or completely surrounded by it. As an Action that does not require any roll, any of your models in contact with a Ritual marker can attempt to uncover that marker. Roll to determine if the ritual was uncovered: uncovered on a 5+ on a D6 for the first marker, uncovered on a 4+ on a D6 for the second, automatically uncovered for the third. If the ritual was uncovered, you succeed at this Shadow Operation.
               4. Informant.  At the start of the battle, before deployment, your opponent places a 1” Informant marker anywhere on the battlefield outside of deployment zones, at least 6” away from any table edge, and not on Impassable Terrain or completely surrounded by it. At the end of the battle, if any of your models is in contact with the Informant marker, you succeed at this Shadow Operation.
               5. Sympathiser. At the end of the battle, if you have one or more models in your opponent’s deployment zone, you succeed at this Shadow Operation.
               6. Threat of Uprising. At the end of the battle, if you have more models in your own deployment zone than your opponent, you succeed at this Shadow Operation.


If you succeed at your Shadow Operation, one of your Elite models of your choice gains 1 additional XP.
Inquisition Patrons
Your patron must be the Lord Inquisitor Shared Patron.

Inquisition Campaign Shop
During a campaign, these pieces of battlekit are available only when your Warband is specifically allowed to purchase them.
               * Blackshroud (3 Glory, Inquisitor Only, Equipment). Injury rolls made against the wearer have -1 INJURY DICE.
               * Daemon Weapon (3 Glory, Inquisitor Only, Ordo: Malleus Only, Melee Weapon). Melee, +2 INJURY DICE, PSYCHIC
               * Ignis Judicium (4 Glory, Inquisitor Only, Ordo: Hereticus Only, Ranged Weapon). 12”, IGNORE ARMOUR, +1 INJURY DICE at short range (or in melee) or against a target with the DAEMON or PSYCHIC Keyword, RISKY, PISTOL
               * Malus Codicium (4 Glory, Inquisitor Only, Ordo: Malleus Only, Equipment). While the equipped model is within 6” of a friendly Daemonhost model, that model has +1 INJURY DICE with all attacks.
               * Relic Digital Lasers (2 Glory, Inquisitor Only, Ranged Weapon). 6”, PISTOL, -1 INJURY DICE, takes no hands, can be used in addition to any other attacks the equipped model makes. Cannot be combined with a Digital Laser.
               * Universal Anathema (6 Glory, Inquisitor Only, Ordo: Xenos Only, Equipment). When the equipped model hits an enemy that does not have the ARTIFICIAL or VEHICLE Keyword with a melee attack, that attack causes an additional BLOOD MARKER if it causes any BLOOD MARKERS at all.
               * Walking Throne (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Inquisitor Only, Ordo: Hereticus Only, Equipment). The equipped model is mounted on a throne atop a walking machine. The rider gains the FEAR, LARGE, and VEHICLE Keywords, is always treated as being 3” above its current location, and gains a movement speed of 8”/Infantry, but it cannot benefit from Cover or use INFILTRATOR or DEEP STRIKE.

Battlekit
The Inquisitor is equipped with a Rosarius, included in its cost. It can be equipped with any other weapons, armour, or equipment from the Inquisition Armoury.


Upgrades
When recruited, the Inquisitor can be given up to one of the following upgrades.
               * Duelist. At a cost of +10 credits, whenever an enemy Charges the Inquisitor, the Inquisitor can make a single melee attack with a non-HEAVY weapon against that enemy before it takes any further actions.
               * Frightening Reputation. At a cost of +5 credits, the Inquisitor gains the FEAR Keyword.
               * Great Strength. At a cost of +10 credits, the Inquisitor gains the STRONG Keyword.
               * Pistoleer. The Inquisitor has the following benefits while armed with Ranged Weapons with the PISTOL Keyword:
               * If it is equipped with 2 Weapons with the PISTOL Keyword, it can take a Shoot ACTION with one and then immediately take a Shoot ACTION with the other.
               * Add the IGNORE OFF-HAND WEAPON Keyword to any weapons that have the PISTOL Keyword.
               * Psyker. At a cost of +5 credits, the Inquisitor gains the PSYKER 1 Keyword. Select a Shared Psychic Discipline. The Inquisitor may purchase up to four powers from that Discipline, and must purchase at least one. It can only use Psychic Powers while wielding a PSYCHIC weapon.


Keywords
ELITE, INQUISITION, LEADER, NEGATE FEAR, TOUGH

Ordo: Xenos
               * Chamber Militant: Deathwatch.                * Xenopurge Discipline. When choosing Psychic Disciplines for your INQUISITION PSYKER models, you can choose the Xenopurge Discipline from the Deathwatch Warband Variant of the Adeptus Astartes Faction in place of another Discipline.
               * Xenos Artifacts. Add the following Xenos weapons to your Inquisition and Astartes Armouries, but you can purchase only a total of up to 4 of these weapons, and only up to 1 of each.
               * From the Aeldari Battlekit List: Avenger Shuriken Catapult, Death Spinner, Mist Staff, Plasma Grenades, Shuriken Cannon, Shuriken Catapult, Shuriken Pistol.
               * From the Drukhari Battlekit List: Agonizer, Splinter Cannon, Splinter Pistol, Splinter Rifle.
               * From the Leagues of Votann Battlekit List: Ion Blaster, Ion Pistol, Magna Rail Rifle, Volkanite Disintegrator.
               * From the Necrons Battlekit List: Gauss Blaster, Gauss Cannon, Gauss Flayer, Gauss Reaper, Tesla Cannon, Voidblade.
               * From the T’au Empire Battlekit List: Bladestave, EMP Grenades, Honor Blade, Photon Grenades, Pulse Blaster, Pulse Carbine, Pulse Rifle, Rail Rifle.
In addition, you can take up to 1 piece of equipment from the Aeldari, Drukhari, Leagues of Votann, or T’au Empire Campaign Shop as if it were your own, which can be equipped only by your Inquisitor, and follows all other limits.
Ordo: Minoris
               * Chamber Militant: Adeptus Arbites. You can recruit up to 1 Sergeant, up to 5 Patrolmen, up to 5 Rookies, up to 2 Hardcase Cyber Mastiffs, and up to 1 Sanctioner Automata from the Palanite Enforcers Gang of the Necromunda Gang Faction, representing Adeptus Arbites. They retain all of their Gang-specific special rules, and can be equipped from the Inquisition Armoury in addition to their normal options.

Officio Assassinorum
Officio Assassinorum
 r/Warhammer40k - Can you make an army out of the Officio Assassinorum? If so, what would be a good baseline set up for them be? 

Officio Assassinorum Special Rules
Execution Force. Your Warband does not have a leader. Instead, you must include at least one Elite model, and whenever an effect specifies your warband leader, you must choose any one of your Elite models for it to affect.


Assassination Targets. At the start of each battle, secretly choose one of your Elite models and one of your opponent’s Elite models. If your chosen Elite model takes their chosen Elite model Out of Action during that battle, your chosen model earns 1 additional XP. If it does not, your chosen model earns 1 fewer XP from that battle.
Officio Assassinorum Patrons
Your patron must be the Grand Master of Assassins.
Patron: Grand Master of Assassins
Or a similar high-ranking member of the Officio Assasinorum.
               * Ensnaring Traps. Whenever an enemy model successfully Charges this model, that enemy suffers 1 BLOOD MARKER.
               * Extremis Sanction: Melee. All melee attacks this model makes need 1 less BLOOD MARKER to cause a Bloodbath.
               * Extremis Sanction: Ranged. All ranged attacks this model makes need 1 less BLOOD MARKER to cause a Bloodbath.
               * Perfect Reflexes: Pounce. Once during each Turn, when an enemy ends its movement within 6” of this model, this model may immediately Charge that enemy.
               * Perfect Reflexes: Snapshot. Once during each Turn, when an enemy ends its movement within 6” of this model, this model may immediately make a single ranged attack against that enemy, if it is otherwise able to do so.
               * Will-Sapping Salvo. Whenever this model hits with a ranged attack, the target also suffers a STUN MARKER.

               * Phase Sword. Melee, IGNORE ARMOUR, CRITICAL
               * Sympatic Dataspike. Melee, CRITICAL, ARMOUR PIERCING 2, STUN
Officio Assassinorum Ranged Weapons
               * Digital Laser. 6”, PISTOL, -1 INJURY DICE, takes no hands, can be used in addition to any other attacks the equipped model makes, but can be used only once per battle.
               * Exitus Rifle. 48”, +1 DICE, Ignores -DICE to Injury Rolls, +1 INJURY DICE, CRITICAL, ARMOUR PIERCING 1, TWO-HANDED, Scope Capable
               * Exitus Pistol. 12” or Melee (always uses Ranged skill), Ignores -DICE to Injury Rolls, ASSAULT, CRITICAL, PISTOL, Scope Capable
               * Needlespine Blaster. 12”, PISTOL, when used to make a ranged attack, the wielder can make one attack with each profile below
               * Spine: GAS
               * Bolt: VICIOUS 11, CRITICAL
               * Neural Shredder. 12”, IGNORE ARMOUR, ASSAULT, STUN, TWO-HANDED
               * Toxin Ejector. 8”, AUTOMATIC 2, -1 INJURY DICE, FLAMETHROWER, IGNORE ARMOUR, GAS, TWO-HANDED
Officio Assassinorum Thrown Weapons
               * Poison Globes. 8”, IGNORE ARMOUR, ASSAULT, BLAST 3”, GAS, IGNORE COVER, IGNORE LONG RANGE, THROWN, FUMBLE
Officio Assassinorum Armour
               * Synskin Bodyglove. -1 INJURY MODIFIER, IMPERVIOUS
Officio Assassinorum Equipment
               * Sentinel Array. The equipped model can declare a Charge against an enemy it cannot see.
               * Spy Mask. This model ignores penalties for attacking at Long Range.
Officio Assassinorum Campaign Shop
During a campaign, these pieces of battlekit are available only when your Warband is specifically allowed to purchase them.
               * Death Card (1 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Equipment). Once during each battle, when this model takes an enemy Out of Action with a melee attack, place a Death Card marker at that enemy’s location. Enemy models within 3” of a Death Card marker have -1 DICE to all Success Rolls. Models that are unaffected by the FEAR Keyword ignore this effect.
               * Decoy Clones (8 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Equipment). When this model is hit by an attack, you can make a Success Roll to cause that attack to miss instead. Once this Success Roll succeeds twice, it cannot be used again during this battle.
               * Digital Laser Array (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Ranged Weapon). 6”, PISTOL, -1 INJURY DICE, takes no hands, can be used in addition to any other attacks the equipped model makes. Cannot be combined with a Digital Laser.
               * Esoteric Explosives (6 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Ranged Weapon). 8”, ASSAULT, BLAST 2”, FIRE, SHRAPNEL, IGNORE COVER, IGNORE LONG RANGE, THROWN, FUMBLE
               * Hyperstimms (2 Glory, LIMIT: 2, Equipment). Once during each battle, as an Action that requires no roll, this model can gain +3” to its movement speed and +1 DICE to Hit with all attacks until the end of its Activation.
               * Intraneural Biotech (4 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Equipment). When an enemy Charges this model, if this model has not Activated this Turn, it can immediately take the Fight Action against that enemy before it takes any further actions. This can be done only once per Turn, and this model counts as having already taken the Fight Action during its Activation this Turn.
               * Jet Pack (4 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Equipment). The model gains FLYING and +2” movement speed.
               * Micromelta Rounds (5 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Equipment). AMMUNITION (IGNORE ARMOUR). Bolt Sniper Rifle, Exitus Rifle, or Exitus Pistol only. Note that this ammunition does not have CONSUMABLE.
               * Psyber-Eagle (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Ranged Weapon). 12”, ASSAULT, this weapon can be used in addition to any other when taking the Shoot Action. (This bird takes up a ranged hand as normal.)
Officio Assassinorum Elites
Note that you are still limited to the normal 6 Elite models, even though there are 7 options presented here.

If it is then within 1” of another enemy, it can make an additional melee attack against that enemy.

Rogue Trader
Rogue Trader
  

Rogue Trader Special Rules
Best of the Best. You can have up to 1 additional Elite model in your Warband for every 500 credits in your Warband. In addition, you can recruit Elite models from among Imperial and Outlaw Factions, as well as from the Aeldari, Drukhari, and Leagues of Votann Factions, with a maximum of 1 model per Faction, or 3 for the Necromunda Gang Faction (but each must be from a different Gang). These models are equipped using their normal Armouries/Battlekit Lists, and you can purchase Campaign Shop battlekit for them from their Faction when allowed. If a piece of battlekit appears in multiple Armouries, you must obey the highest LIMIT among factions whose models you have recruited.


These models gain the ROGUE TRADER Keyword, and their abilities that specify one or more allies with a Faction Keyword apply to your ROGUE TRADER models as well. In addition, for each of them that has the LEADER Keyword, they cost 15 additional credits and lose that Keyword.


You can choose models from Warband Variants, following the rules for that Variant except for any rule that replaces one of the Faction’s normal special rules. The Ynnari Warband Variant of the Aeldari cannot be chosen from.


Your Warband also counts as an Imperial, Outlaw, Xenos (Aeldari), Xenos (Drukhari), and Xenos (Leagues of Votann) Warband for the purpose of recruiting Mercenaries.


Warrant of Trade. Do not keep track of how many credits you have. Instead, you can always afford anything that costs any amount of credits, but you can only have up to 20% more than the credit limit for any given mission worth of models and battlekit at any given time, and can still only bring the normal limit worth to a battle. Any excess must be left in reserves, but you can decide exactly what after learning the mission and your opponent’s Warband.


Example: Your next mission has a Threshold of 1100, so your credit limit is 1100 for that battle. You can purchase up to 1320 (1100 plus 20%, which is 220) credits worth of models and battlekit, but you can bring only 1100 worth of them to the battle.
Rogue Trader Patrons
Your patron must be your Warrant of Trade.
Patron: Warrant of Trade
               * Acquisitions (LIMIT: 1). While this model is in your Warband, your credit limit for missions is increased by 25.
               * Emperor’s Judgement (LIMIT: 1, Lord Captain, Voidmaster, or Navigator Scion Only). Once per battle, as an Action that does not require a roll, this model can choose a point on the battlefield within 24” that it can see. Each model within 3” of that point that does not have any terrain directly above it must roll on the Injury chart.
               * Expert Training. Choose any skill you want from any non-Patron Skill Table.
               * Fleetmaster. This model gains the Circle Back and Seek Exploration Skills.
               * Master of the Void (LIMIT: 2). At the start of each battle, before deployment, if this model is participating in the battle, you can give up to 2 non-Elite ROGUE TRADER models in your Warband the DEEP STRIKE Keyword until the end of the battle.
               * Personal Teleportarium Chamber (LIMIT: 3). This model gains DEEP STRIKE.

Chaos Factions
Chaos Factions
Chaos Cult
Chaos Cult
  

Chaos Cult Special Rules
Accursed Gifts. Your CHAOS CULT models can acquire Gifts of Chaos. A Gift of Chaos can either be permanent or temporary. Permanent Gifts of Chaos remain part of a model for as long as it is in your Warband, while temporary Gifts of Chaos last only until the end of the battle. Each Gift of Chaos can be gained only once. Reroll if a duplicate is rolled.


During a campaign, at the end of any battle, you can grant one of your non-Mercenary CHAOS CULT models a permanent Gift of Chaos. If that model is Elite, you can roll twice and choose either result. If any of your models performed a Glorious Deed or took an ELITE enemy Out of Action during that battle, you can repeat this process for any of those models (except one you already chose).


An ELITE CHAOS CULT model or a Chaos Spawn can have a maximum of 4 permanent Gifts of Chaos. Any other CHAOS CULT model can have a maximum of 2 permanent Gifts of Chaos. This includes any it began with.


During a one-off battle, roll for Gifts of Chaos before the game begins, and you can purchase permanent Gifts of Chaos for 5 credits each, up to the normal maximum, rolled for as above.


When a model gains a Gift of Chaos, roll a D66 and consult the following results:
               * 11-12. Spawnhood. Replace the model with a Chaos Spawn. It retains all of its Gifts of Chaos (including this one, and including costs), but replaces all other statistics. Any armour it was wearing is destroyed. If it was ELITE, it retains any skills it had, but cannot earn XP. Reroll if it is already a Chaos Spawn, or it is gaining a Gift of Chaos as it is recruited.
               * 13. Camouflage. The model gains the STEALTH Keyword.
               * 14. Strange Mobility. The model gains the SKIRMISHER Keyword.
               * 15. Beastial. The model gains the BEASTMEN Keyword and the following ability:
               * Frenzy. When the Bestial model is taken Out of Action, before removing it from the table, it can make a single melee attack against an enemy it is in close combat with. BLOOD MARKERS cannot be spent on this attack's Success Roll.
               * 16. Extra Arm. The model gains an extra arm, which can carry melee and ranged weapons as normal. It can make an additional attack with any weapon in that arm without off-hand penalties. If it cannot normally be equipped, this hand can be equipped with weapons, a shield, or any battlekit that takes a single hand from the Chaos Cult Armoury.
               * 21. Horns of the Beast. When the model successfully Charges one or more enemies, one of them gets a BLOOD MARKER.
               * 22. Barbs of Hatred. Whenever an enemy causes this model to suffer 1 or more BLOOD MARKERS in melee, that enemy also suffers 1 BLOOD MARKER.
               * 23. Warp Frenzy. The model can make one additional melee attack with a single weapon when it takes the Fight Action, at -1 DICE to Hit, or -2 DICE to Hit if it is a TWO-HANDED weapon (even if wielded in one hand).
               * 24. Twisted Wings. The model gains the FLYING Keyword.
               * 25. Strength of the Berzerker. The model gains the STRONG Keyword. If the model was already STRONG, it instead gains +1 INJURY DICE with all melee attacks.
               * 26. Arcane Occulum. The model gains +1 to its Ranged Skill.
               * 31. Cerebral Cogitator. The model can form a FIRETEAM with one other friendly model. It can change which model between battles.
               * 32. Bloated. The model gains the LARGE keyword and treats Down results as Minor Hits (does not affect Down results that replace Out of Action via TOUGH Keyword).
               * 33. Crystalline Body. The model has -1 INJURY MODIFIER, stacking with armour up to -3.
               * 34. Shield of Force. Ranged attacks against the model have -1 DICE to Hit.
               * 35. Mechanoid. The model gains the ARTIFICIAL and NEGATE GAS Keywords.
               * 36. Blade of Chaos. Choose one melee weapon the model is equipped with, or the next such weapon equipped by it if it has none. That weapon is fused into it and cannot be removed, becomes HELD, and has +1 DICE to Hit and +1 INJURY DICE. If this is a temporary mutation and the model does not have a melee weapon, it is considered to have a Bludgeon until the end of the battle.
               * 41. Witch-Eater. Injury rolls with the PSYCHIC or IGNORE ARMOUR Keyword have -1 INJURY DICE against the model. In addition, it can Deny the Witch as if it had PSYKER 1.
               * 42. Cosmic Fate. At the start of each battle, or when this Gift of Chaos is gained, the model gains 1 BLESSING MARKER.
               * 43. Venomous. The model’s melee attacks gain the GAS Keyword.
               * 44. Unholy Crusader. The model has +1 DICE on all Dash Success Rolls.
               * 45. Meteoric Charge. When the model Charges, roll 2D6 instead of 1D6 and then add the highest of the two dice to its charge move.
               * 46. Icy Aura. At the end of the model’s Activation, each enemy in close combat with it suffers 1 BLOOD MARKER.
               * 51. Mind of Chaos. The model gains the PSYKER 1 Keyword. Roll a D6: On a 1-3, randomly select one Shared Discipline. The model can purchase up to three powers from that Discipline. On a 4-6 it can purchase up to three powers from the Hereticus Discipline. If it already had the PSYKER Keyword, instead increase the X value of its PSYKER Keyword by 1.
               * 52. Gun Morph. Choose one non-Grenade ranged weapon the model is equipped with, or the next such weapon equipped by it if it has none. That weapon is fused into it and cannot be removed, becomes HELD, and has +1 DICE to Hit and +1 INJURY DICE. If this is a temporary mutation and the model does not have a ranged weapon, it is considered to have a Las Pistol until the end of the battle.
               * 53. All-Consuming Hatred. The model gains the FEAR keyword. If it already has FEAR, instead your opponent has -1 DICE to Morale tests while at least one model with this ability is not Down or Out of Action.
               * 54. Warp Claws. The model gains a Warp Claw weapon in each of its hands. It can still carry other melee weapons, but cannot attack with a Warp Claw during the same Fight Action that it attacks with a melee weapon held in that hand.
               * Warp Claw. Melee, IGNORE OFF-HAND, CRITICAL
               * 55. Lifetaker. When the model takes an enemy Out of Acton in melee, remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from it.
               * 56. Blademaster. The model gains +1 to its Melee Skill.
               * 61. Extra Legs. The model cannot be forced to move by any effect an enemy model causes.
               * 62. Temporal Distortion. The model has +2” movement speed.
               * 63. Masochism. Injury rolls against the model have -1 INJURY DICE.
               * 64. Daemon Skin. The model gains NEGATE FIRE.
               * 65. Fragment of Immortality. The model gains the TOUGH Keyword. If it already had TOUGH, then instead the model does not fall Down or suffer the normal BLOOD MARKER when it uses its TOUGH Keyword.
               * 66. Dark Apotheosis. If the model is not ELITE, you have not reached your limit for ELITE models, and it is eligible for promotion, it immediately becomes ELITE. Then, whether or not it became ELITE, roll for another Gift of Chaos at no additional cost.


If the model is already ELITE, replace the model with a Daemon Prince. It retains all of its Gifts of Chaos (including this one, and including costs) and skills, but replaces all other statistics. Any armour it was wearing is destroyed. You can have only one Daemon Prince. Should you ever have more than one, choose one at random and the other is destroyed. If either had this Gift of Chaos as a permanent Gift of Chaos, the survivor now has it as a Permanent Gift of Chaos, if it did not already (including cost).


Reroll this result if the model is already a Daemon Prince or it is gaining a Gift of Chaos as it is recruited.
Chaos Cult Patrons
Your patron can be any of the following Shared Patrons: Chaos Undivided, Khorne, Nurgle, Slaanesh, or Tzeentch.

Chaos Cult Special Melee Weapons
               * Daemon Weapon. Melee, +2 INJURY DICE, PSYCHIC, MAIN HAND ONLY
               * Fell Dagger. Melee, PSYCHIC, RISKY, remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from the wielder on a hit that causes any BLOOD MARKERS or that takes the target Out of Action.

               * Strike: Melee, FIRE, CUMBERSOME
               * Flame: 6”, FLAMETHROWER, +1 INJURY DICE, IGNORE ARMOUR, FIRE
Chaos Cult Equipment

Chaos Cult Campaign Shop
During a campaign, these pieces of battlekit are available only when your Warband is specifically allowed to purchase them.
               * Baleful Script (1 Glory, LIMIT: 2, Equipment). Once per battle, you can replace one of the equipped model’s Gifts of Chaos (Permanent or Temporary) with a new random Temporary Gift of Chaos until the end of the battle.
               * Elixir of Apotheosis (7 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Equipment). The model gains the Dark Apotheosis Permanent Mutation. This Gift of Chaos does not count towards the normal limits. This battlekit cannot be removed or sold.
               * Elixir of Change (1 or 2 Glory, LIMIT: 4, Max 1 per Model, Equipment). The model gains a Permanent Gift of Chaos. If you spent 2 Glory on this equipment, you can roll three times and choose any result. This Gift of Chaos does not count towards the normal limits. This battlekit cannot be removed or sold.
               * Elixir of Mutation (5 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Equipment). The model gains the Spawnhood Permanent Mutation. This Gift of Chaos does not count towards the normal limits. This battlekit cannot be removed or sold.
               * Greater Elixir of Change (4 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Equipment). The model gains the Permanent Gift of Chaos of your choice, besides Spawnhood or Dark Apotheosis. This Gift of Chaos does not count towards the normal limits. This battlekit cannot be removed or sold.
               * Hex’iron Blade (4 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Melee Weapon). Melee, +1 INJURY DICE, ARMOUR PIERCING 2, PSYCHIC, MAIN HAND ONLY
               * Sacrificial Blood (5 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Psyker Only, Equipment). The first psychic power the equipped model uses during each of its Activations does not count towards the Using Multiple Powers rules.
               * Vile Blessing (8 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Chaos Spawn Only, Equipment). Injury rolls against the equipped model have -2 INJURY DICE. A Chaos Spawn can be equipped with this equipment despite normal limits, but it cannot be removed or sold.
               * Unholy Talisman (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Equipment). The equipped model has an additional -1 armour, which has IMPERVIOUS.
               * Warding Sigil (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Equipment). The bearer treats any Down result from the Injury Roll Table as a Minor Hit instead. This does not apply to Down results that already replaced another result, such as with TOUGH.
Hereticus Psychic Discipline
               * Creeping Terror (3 credits, Effect). No Range, No Targets, End of Turn, your opponent has -1 DICE to their Morale Test at the end of this Turn, if any.
               * Curse of Misfortune (5 credits, Effect). 18”, One Enemy, Target Activation, the target has -1 DICE to all Success Rolls.
               * Summon Daemon (2 Glory, Effect). 6”, Point, End of Turn, roll a D6 to determine what Mercenary model you summon, and place that model at the chosen point. It counts as one of your models, and activates immediately. On a failure, your opponent places the model anywhere within 1” of the caster instead, and it counts as one of their models and still activates immediately. The summoned model does not count towards Morale, and disappears at the end of the Turn.
               * 1. Summoned Furie
               * 2. Summoned Bloodletter
               * 3. Summoned Daemonette
               * 4. Summoned Plaguebearer
               * 5. Summoned Blue Horror
               * 6. Chaos Spawn
               * Suppression (5 credits, Effect). -1 DICE, 12”, Up to Three Down Enemies, Target Activation, the target cannot stand up from Down.
               * Unnatural Gift (5 credits, Effect). 6”, Self or One Ally, Immediate, the target gains a temporary Gift of Chaos.
               * Warp Flux (10 credits, Attack). 12”, +1 INJURY DICE. If the target benefits from any armour, set one Injury die to 6 instead of rolling it.

Battlekit
A Cult Demagogue can be equipped with any weapons, armour, or equipment, or from the Chaos Cult Armoury.


Abilities
               * Bestow Gift. As an Action with no roll, the Cult Demagogue can mutate itself or one ally within 12” of it that it can see. That model gains a temporary Gift of Chaos.
               * Incite Slaughter. As an Action with a Risky Success Roll, the Cult Demagogue can embolden one other CHAOS CULT model it can see within 12”. On a success, that model can immediately make a single melee attack.
               * Incite Urgency. As an Action with a Risky Success Roll, the Cult Demagogue can incite one other CHAOS CULT model it can see within 12”. On a success, that model can immediately take the Standard Move or Retreat Action.
               * Mutations. When the Cult Demagogue is recruited, it gains a permanent Gift of Chaos. You can roll twice and choose either result.

0-1 Daemon Prince (Cost: 190 credits)

Battlekit
The Daemon Prince is always equipped with Daemonic Hellplate, a Hellforged Weapon, and an Infernal Cannon, included in its price and statistics above. You cannot change its battlekit in any way.
               * Hellforged Weapon. Melee, +1 INJURY DICE, HEAVY, TWO-HANDED.
               * Strike: ARMOUR PIERCING 2
               * Sweep: SWEEPING
               * Infernal Cannon. 24”, +1 INJURY DICE, BLAST 2”, HEAVY, SHRAPNEL, TWO-HANDED


Abilities
               * Augmented. If the Daemon Prince gains a Gift of Chaos that requires a weapon, you can choose one of its existing weapons, or equip it with an appropriate weapon. If it has or gains such a Gift of Chaos, it can use one such weapon as an off-hand weapon, even if it is a ranged weapon (in which case it uses it during Shoot Actions, but with off-hand penalties). If that weapon is 2-handed, it has an additional -1 DICE to Hit.
               * Daemonic Resistance. Injuries rolled against the Daemon Prince have -1 DICE.
               * Overlord. The Daemon Prince is your Warband leader. Any other model you have with the LEADER keyword loses that Keyword while the Daemon Prince is in your Warband.


Keywords
DAEMON, LARGE, LEADER, STRONG, TOUGH

               * Growing Devotion. After a Cult Rabble has participated in at least two missions, or if it scores a Glorious Deed or takes an enemy Elite model Out of Action, you can upgrade it to a Chaos Devotee for free at the end of a battle. It uses its new cost when calculating how many credits worth of models you can bring to a mission, immediately gains a permanent mutation from its new Uncontrolled Mutations ability (counting it as it is were just recruited) so long as it has fewer than 2 permanent mutations already, and it retains its For the Dark Gods ability.

Warband Variant: Helot Cult  
               * Gang Connections. Your Campaign Shop is replaced with the Campaign Shop of the Necromunda Gang faction. You can recruit Hive and Hive Beast Outlaw Mercenaries.
               * Cut Off. You cannot recruit any Daemon, Heretic Astartes, Dark Mechanicum, or Skaven Mercenaries.
Chaos Daemons
Chaos Daemons
  

Chaos Daemons Special Rules
Daemon Legions. You can recruit models from any of the four Legions listed below. You must include exactly one model with the LEADER Keyword as your Warband’s leader and cannot include any other models with the LEADER Keyword, except via the Daemon Prince’s Overlord ability.


Depending on your leader’s other Keywords, the following additional restrictions apply:
               * KHORNE: You cannot recruit any models from the Legion of Excess.
               * NURGLE: You cannot recruit any models from the Scintillating Legion.
               * SLAANESH: You cannot recruit any models from the Blood Legion.
               * TZEENTCH: You cannot recruit any models from the Plague Legion.
               * UNDIVIDED: No other restrictions.


Warp Entities. At the start of each battle, you can select up to half of your DAEMON models, rounded up. Those models gain the DEEP STRIKE Keyword during this battle.


The Great Game. During a campaign, keep track of the influence of each god on your Warband, called Ascendency. When you first create your Warband, the gods have the following  Ascendency:
               * Khorne: 4 Ascendency
               * Tzeentch: 3 Ascendency
               * Nurgle: 2 Ascendency
               * Slaanesh: 1 Ascendency


At the end of each battle, each god gains D3 Ascendency in the following order: Khorne, Tzeentch, Nurgle, Slaanesh. Then the god corresponding to the Keyword of your Warband leader gains 1 Ascendency, or D3 Ascendency if you won the battle. If your Warband leader has the UNDIVIDED Keyword, each of the gods gains 1 Ascendency, or D3 if you won, in the order above.


The gods can never have the same Ascendency. After gaining any Ascendency, if a god has the same Ascendency as another, the god that just gained Ascendency gains 1 more.


Benefits and Penalties
During each battle, your models with a god’s Keyword have the following benefits or penalties, depending on its Ascendency:
               * Highest Ascendency: Once per battle, you can reroll one Success Roll made by, or Injury roll made against, one of these models. If your Warband leader has this god’s Keyword, its LEADER Keyword adds an additional +1 DICE to Morale tests.
               * Second Highest Ascendency: Once per battle, you can grant one Success Roll made by one of these models +1 DICE, or give one Injury roll made against one of them -1 DICE.
               * Third Highest Ascendency: No effect.
               * Lowest Ascendency: -1 DICE to Hit any model with another god’s Keyword. If your Warband leader has this Keyword, its LEADER Keyword does not add any bonus to Morale tests.


Rewards
At the end of every 4th battle, you can assign the following rewards to your Elite models, with a maximum of 1 reward per model:
               * Highest Ascendency: One Elite model with the Keyword of the god with the highest Ascendency, or with the UNDIVIDED Keyword, earns an additional 2 XP, and you can remove 1 Battle Scar and its corresponding Trauma from that model.
               * Second Highest Ascendency: One Elite model with the Keyword of the god with the second highest Ascendency, or with the UNDIVIDED Keyword, earns an additional 2 XP.
               * Third Highest Ascendency: One Elite model with the Keyword of the god with the third highest Ascendency, or with the UNDIVIDED Keyword, earns an additional 1 XP.


Then reset each god’s Ascendency to a value based on its current position:
               * Highest Ascendency: 4
               * Second Highest Ascendency: 3
               * Third Highest Ascendency: 2
               * Lowest Ascendency: 1


Optional: Combining the Great Game
If multiple Chaos Daemons Warbands are participating in the same campaign, you can optionally track the Ascendency of the gods together. In this case, Ascendency values are shared between those players, and the gods only gain the base D3 Ascendency after each battle once after each Chaos Daemons Warband has completed that battle. All other increases are applied from each player in a random order. Rewards are applied only after each player has finished their battle and applied their Ascendency increases.
	Chaos Cult Patrons
Your patron must be one of the following Shared Patrons, depending on your Warband leader’s Keywords:
               * KHORNE: Khorne.
               * NURGLE: Nurgle.
               * SLAANESH: Slaanesh.
               * TZEENTCH: Tzeentch.
               * UNDIVIDED: Chaos Undivided

Chaos Daemons Elites
0-1 Daemon Prince (210 Credits, or 190 for Prince of Darkness)
Name
	Movement
	Ranged
	Melee
	Armour
	Base
	Daemon Prince
	8”/Infantry
	+2
	+2
	-3
	60mm
	

Battlekit
The Daemon Prince is always equipped with Daemonic Hellplate, a Hellforged Weapon, and an Infernal Cannon, included in its price and statistics above. It can be equipped with any equipment from the Chaos Daemons Armoury.
               * Hellforged Weapon. Melee, +1 INJURY DICE, HEAVY, TWO-HANDED.
               * Strike: ARMOUR PIERCING 2
               * Sweep: SWEEPING
               * Infernal Cannon. 24”, +1 INJURY DICE, BLAST 2”, HEAVY, SHRAPNEL, TWO-HANDED


When you recruit a Daemon Prince, you must choose one of the following Types:
               * Daemon Prince of Darkness. The Daemon Prince and all allied models within 6” of it have the STEALTH Keyword. It gains the UNDIVIDED Keyword. It can be equipped with equipment from any of the Legions’ Battlekit Lists. A Daemon Prince of Darkness costs -20 credits.
               * Daemon Prince of Khorne. The Daemon Prince’s melee attacks have an additional +1 INJURY MODIFIER, and it gains the KHORNE Keyword. It can be equipped with equipment from the Blood Legion Battlekit List.
               * Daemon Prince of Nurgle. The Daemon Prince treats Down results as Minor Hits (does not affect Down results that replace Out of Action via TOUGH Keyword), and it gains the NURGLE Keyword. It can be equipped with equipment from the Plague Legion Battlekit List.
               * Daemon Prince of Slaanesh. The Daemon Prince gains +1” movement speed, +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls, and the SLAANESH Keyword.  It can be equipped with equipment from the Legion of Excess Battlekit List.
               * Daemon Prince of Tzeentch. The Daemon Prince’s ranged attacks have an additional +1 INJURY MODIFIER, and it gains the TZEENTCH Keyword.  It can be equipped with equipment from the Scintillating Legion Battlekit List.


Abilities
               * Daemonic Resistance. Injuries rolled against the Daemon Prince have -1 INJURY DICE.
               * Overlord. You can recruit this model as well as one other model with the LEADER Keyword, so long as they share one of the Keywords KHORNE, NURGLE, SLAANESH, or TZEENTCH. If you do, the other model loses LEADER and this model is your warband leader as long it is in your Warband. A Daemon Prince of Darkness cannot benefit from this ability.


Upgrades
               * Wings. The Daemon Prince can be given wings for an additional +20 credits, granting it 8” movement and FLYING.


Keywords
DAEMON, ELITE, LARGE, LEADER, STRONG, TOUGH

               * A’rgath, The King Of Blades (8 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Melee Weapon). Melee, ignores all -DICE to Hit, DEADLY, VICIOUS 10, CRITICAL, TWO-HANDED
               * Armour Of Scorn (2 Glory, LIMIT: 2, Armour). -1 INJURY MODIFIER.
               * Blood-Drinker Talisman (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Equipment). When the bearer causes one or more BLOOD MARKERS or takes an enemy Out of Action in melee, remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from the bearer.
               * Crimson Crown (4 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Equipment, Headgear). Once during each Turn, if the wearer Downs or takes an enemy Out of Action in melee, it can move up to 6”, and can enter melee as if it Charged, then it can make a single extra melee attack. This movement can cause it to leave melee, but counts as a Retreat except by the model that triggered the movement.
               * Rune Of Brass (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Equipment). When a model uses a psychic power while within 12” of this model, it has PERILOUS ±2. In addition, it must roll twice on the Perils of the Warp table if it suffers Perils of the Warp, and you choose one of the results.
               * Skullreaver (5 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Melee Weapon). Melee, +1 INJURY DICE, TWO-HANDED
               * Mighty Strike: IGNORE ARMOUR
               * Sweeping Blow: SWEEPING

Legion of Excess Battlekit
Legion of Excess Melee Weapons
               * Lashes of Torment (10 credits). Melee, AUTOMATIC 2, ASSAULT, CUMBERSOME, CLEAVE 2, STUN, TWO-HANDED, WHIP 6”. Cannot use both AUTOMATIC and CLEAVE during the same activation.
               * Whips Of Agony (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1). Melee, AUTOMATIC 2, ASSAULT, CRITICAL, CUMBERSOME, CLEAVE 2, STUN, WHIP 12", TWO-HANDED. Cannot use both AUTOMATIC and CLEAVE during the same activation.
Legion of Excess Thrown Weapons
               * Alluring Musk (35 credits, LIMIT: 1). 8”, IGNORE COVER and Long Range, GAS, THROWN. Causes no injury, but does cause a BLOOD MARKER as an attack that uses the GAS Keyword. If the wielder hits a target with this weapon, the model hit is moved in a straight line into the nearest legal space closest to the wielder. This typically ends with the target and the wielder in base contact and in Melee Combat. This movement can place the target in Melee Combat with multiple enemy models. Additionally, no enemy models may Retreat when in Melee Combat with the wielder.
Legion of Excess Equipment
               * Instrument of Slannesh (10 credits, LIMIT: 1). HELD. Counts as an Instrument of Chaos, including for Limits, and as such, unless this model is Down, friendly models within 4” of it can add +1 DICE to their Dash Success Rolls..
               * Rapturous Standard (10 credits, LIMIT: 1). HELD, ICON. While the bearer is not Down, melee attacks made against it and each of its SLAANESH allies within 8” of it have -1 DICE to Hit.
Legion of Excess Campaign Shop
During a campaign, these pieces of battlekit are available only when your Warband is specifically allowed to purchase them.
               * Forbidden Gem (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Equipment). As an Action with no roll required, the equipped model can choose one enemy it can see within 12”. That enemy’s Actions and Abilities cannot target or affect any of that model’s allies until the end of the current Turn.
               * Jewel of Excess (5 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Psyker Only, Equipment). The equipped model has +1 DICE to all PSYCHIC Success Rolls, including attacks.
               * Mark Of Excess (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Equipment). Whenever the equipped model takes an enemy Out of Action with a melee attack, it gains +1 DICE to Hit and +1 INJURY DICE with the next melee attack it makes before the end of its next Activation.
               * Silverstrike (9 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Melee Weapon). Melee, CLEAVE 6, CRITICAL, TWO-HANDED, CUMBERSOME.
               * Slothful Claws (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Equipment). The equipped model has VICIOUS 10 with its Slashing Claws and Daemonic Claws, and STUN on a Critical Hit.
               * Soulstealer (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Melee Weapon). Melee, +1 INJURY DICE, CRITICAL, TWO-HANDED, if the target is taken Out of Action, remove D3 BLOOD MARKERS from the wielder.
Soulstain Psychic Discipline

               * Delightful Agonies (12 credits, Effect). -1 DICE, 18”, Self or One SLAANESH Ally, End of Turn, Injury rolls made against the target have -2 INJURY MODIFIER, which does not stack with Armour or Shields.
               * Hysterical Frenzy (6 credits, Effect). 18”, Self or One SLAANESH Ally, Immediate, the target can move up to half of its movement speed. Tzeentch Psychic Discipline
               * Altered Reality (7 credits, Effect). 6”, Self or One Ally or Enemy, End of Turn, whenever the target makes a Success Roll or an Injury roll is made against it, you can reroll one of the dice.
               * Bolt of Change (15 credits, Attack). 18”, +1 INJURY DICE, PSYCHIC, RISKY. If this attack takes an ELITE enemy Out of Action, you can place a Chaos Spawn Mercenary on the table under your control where that enemy was located. The Chaos Spawn does not count towards Morale or join your Warband, and the original model is treated normally for the post-game sequence. You can have only up to one Chaos Spawn on the table from this or any similar effect at a time.

               * Treason of Tzeentch (1 Glory, Effect). -1 DICE, 18”, One Enemy, Immediate, the target Charges another enemy of your choice within 12” of it that it and the caster can see, then if it Charged successfully, it makes a single melee attack with a melee weapon it is wielding of your choice against that same target. On a failure, the target can instead Charge one of its enemies of its controller’s choice that it can see and make a melee attack if it succeeded on the Charge. Either way, this Charge allows the model to leave close combat, and does not allow free attacks from models it leaves close combat with.
               * Xirat’p’s Sorcerous Barrages (10 credits, Attack). 6”, ASSAULT, PSYCHIC, ARMOUR PIERCING 2, RISKY, can be used in addition to another Shoot Action during the same Activation.

Mercenaries
You can hire the following mercenaries.

               * 0-1 Beast of Nurgle (Cost: 5 Glory) Nurgle Patron Only
               * 0-1 Chaos Spawn (Cost: 4 Glory)

               * Summoned Furie (Cost: 2 Glory)
               * Summoned Bloodletter (Cost: 2 Glory)
               * Summoned Daemonette (Cost: 2 Glory)
               * Summoned Plaguebearer (Cost: 2 Glory)
               * Summoned Blue Horror (Cost: 2 Glory)
               * Summoned Pink Horror (Cost: 5 Glory) (counts as 2 choices)

               * 0-1 Black Legionnaire (Cost: 4 Glory)
               * 0-1 Khorne Berzerker (Cost: 5 Glory) Unavailable to SLAANESH leader
               * 0-1 Mutilator (Cost: 5 Glory)
               * 0-1 Noise Marine (Cost: 5 Glory) Unavailable to KHORNE leader
               * 0-1 Obliterator (Cost: 6 Glory)

               * 0-1 Plague Marine (Cost: 5 Glory) Unavailable to TZEENTCH leader
               * 0-1 Rubric Marine (Cost: 5 Glory) Unavailable to NURGLE leader

               * 0-1 Hell-Forge Heretek (Cost: 5 Glory)
               * 0-1 Negavolt Cultist (Cost: 2 Glory)
Skaven Mercenaries
               * 0-1 Plague Priest (Cost: 4 Glory) Unavailable to TZEENTCH leader

Warband Variant: Followers of Khorne
               * Khorne Alone. You cannot take Chaos Daemons models that do not have the KHORNE Keyword.
               * Khorne Disciples. You can take the following Heretic Astartes models, using the rules for the World Eaters Warband Variant, and Astra Militarum models, using the rules for the Traitor Guard Warband Variant:
               * 0-1 Master of Executions or Slaughterbound.
               * 0-1 Enforcer.
               * Any number of Chaos Cultists.
               * 0-3 Bloodgors.
               * 0-3 in any combination of Possessed and Heretic Astartes (no Havocs).
               * 0-1 Chaos Terminator or Ogryn.
Warband Variant: Followers of Nurgle
               * Nurgle Alone. You cannot take Chaos Daemons models that do not have the NURGLE Keyword.
               * Nurgle Disciples. You can take the following Heretic Astartes models, using the rules for the Death Guard Warband Variant, and Astra Militarum models, using the rules for the Traitor Guard Warband Variant:
               * 0-1 Chaos Sorcerer or Plague Marine Champion.
               * 0-1 Enforcer or Rogue Psyker.
               * Any number of Chaos Cultists.
               * 0-3 in any combination of Poxwalkers and Pestigors.
               * 0-3 Heretic Astartes, with a maximum of 1 Havoc.
               * 0-1 Chaos Terminator or Ogryn.
Warband Variant: Followers of Slaanesh
               * Slaanesh Alone. You cannot take Chaos Daemons models that do not have the SLAANESH Keyword.
               * Slaanesh Disciples. You can take the following Heretic Astartes models, using the rules for the Emperor’s Children Warband Variant, and Astra Militarum models, using the rules for the Traitor Guard Warband Variant:
               * 0-1 Chaos Sorcerer or Lord Kakophonist.
               * 0-1 Enforcer or Rogue Psyker.
               * Any number of Chaos Cultists.
               * 0-3 Slaangors.
               * 0-3 Heretic Astartes, with a maximum of 1 Havoc and 1 Flawless Blade.
               * 0-1 Chaos Terminator or Ogryn.
Warband Variant: Followers of Tzeentch
               * Tzeentch Alone. You cannot take Chaos Daemons models that do not have the TZEENTCH Keyword.
               * Tzeentch Disciples. You can take the following Heretic Astartes models, using the rules for the Thousand Sons Warband Variant, and Astra Militarum models, using the rules for the Traitor Guard Warband Variant:
               * 0-1 Chaos Sorcerer or Tzaangor Shaman.
               * 0-1 Rogue Psyker.
               * Any number of Chaos Cultists.
               * 0-3 Tzaangor (equipped from the Heretic Astartes Armoury).
               * 0-3 Heretic Astartes, with a maximum of 1 Havoc.
               * 0-1 Chaos Terminator or Ogryn.
Warband Variant: Followers of Vashtor
               * Aligned With Heretek. Your Warband Leader must have the UNDIVIDED Keyword. Your patron must be a Heretek Lord.
               * Hellforged. DAEMON models in your Warband have the ARTIFICIAL Keyword. If they are LARGE, they also gain the VEHICLE Keyword. Your DAEMON models have NEGATE GAS.
               * Limited Support. You can recruit a maximum of 1 Flesh Hound, 1 Seeker, 1 Nurgling Swarm, 1 Pink Horror, and 1 Screamer.
               * Haywire. When a DAEMON model in your Warband suffers any STUN MARKERS, it suffers 1 additional STUN MARKER.
               * Hellforged Battlekit. Add the following battlekit to your Armoury:
               * Arc Claw (3 credits, replaces Slashing Claw Only). Melee, STUN, this weapon can replace any Slashing Claw weapon, ignoring normal limits.
               * Hell Cannon (20 credits, LIMIT: 2). 24”, BLAST 2”, SHRAPNEL, TWO-HANDED
               * Flameblade (15 credits, LIMIT: 2, Khorne Only). Melee, +1 INJURY DICE, FIRE, TWO-HANDED
               * Fused Armour (15 credits). -1 INJURY MODIFIER, any model that can take equipment can be equipped with this armour. This armour cannot be removed.
               * Fused Hellplate (40 credits, Elite Only). -2 Armour, any model that can take equipment can be equipped with this armour. This armour cannot be removed.
*Dark Mechanicum
Dark Mechanicum
Dark Mechanicum is an Adeptus Mechanicus Warband Variant that counts as a Chaos Faction.
*Death Guard
Death Guard
Death Guard is a Heretic Astartes Warband Variant. Don’t worry, they have extensive modifications that make them feel like their own Faction!
*Emperor’s Children
Emperor’s Children
Emperor’s Children is a Heretic Astartes Warband Variant. Don’t worry, they have extensive modifications that make them feel like their own Faction!
Heretic Astartes
Heretic Astartes
  Heretic Astartes Special Rules
Dark Pacts. As an Action with a Success Roll, any of your HERETIC ASTARTES models can attempt to make a Dark Pact. On a success, that model gains your choice of +1 DICE to Hit or +1 INJURY DICE with all weapons until the end of its Activation. Whether or not the roll succeeds, the model gains a BLOOD MARKER at the end of its Activation.


Dread Reputation. During a campaign, keep track of your Personal Reputation, Dark Gods Reputation, and Warfleet Reputation, each starting at 4. They cannot be reduced below 1 or increased above 8. They are modified in the following circumstances:
               * At the end of each battle, reduce each Reputation by 1.
               * Add 1 to any one Reputation for every Glorious Deed you perform during a battle.
               * Add D3, distributed as you like between each Reputation, when you win a battle.
               * Add 1 to any one Reputation if you tie in a battle.


While a Reputation is at 7 or 8, you are Favored in that Reputation. While it is at 1 or 2, you are Forsaken in that reputation. You gain benefits and penalties as follows:
               * Favored Personal Reputation. At the end of each battle, if your Warband leader took at least two enemy models Out of Action, it earns an additional 1 XP.
               * Favored Dark Gods Reputation. At the end of each battle, you can purchase one Mark of Chaos for one of your models for free, following normal limitations. If you do, reduce your Dark Gods Reputation by 2.
               * Favored Warfleet Reputation. You have the Extra Dice and Reroll Exploration Skills, stacking with any other instances of those skills.
               * Forsaken Personal Reputation. Any time you lose a battle, after casualties and Trauma rolls, one of your champions challenges for leadership. Choose one of your Elite models that is not your Warband leader and roll a D6. On a 3+, the chosen model must roll on the Elite Trauma chart. On a 1-2, remove your Warband leader from your Warband, and the chosen model becomes your new Warband leader and gains the LEADER and TOUGH Keywords. You can recruit a new model of the type of your previous leader, but that model loses LEADER and TOUGH.
               * Forsaken Dark Gods Reputation. At the end of each battle, choose one of your Elite models and roll a D6. On a 5+, nothing happens. On a 1-4, that model must roll on the Elite Trauma chart.
               * Forsaken Warfleet Reputation. You cannot spend any Glory on battlekit or Mercenaries.


Heretic Astartes Patrons
Your patron can be any of the following Shared Patrons: Chaos Undivided, Chaos Warlord, or Heretek Lord.

Heretic Astartes Unique Battlekit
Heretic Astartes Special Melee Weapons
               * Daemon Weapon. Melee, +2 INJURY DICE, PSYCHIC, MAIN HAND ONLY
               * Fell Dagger. Melee, PSYCHIC, RISKY, remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from the wielder on a hit that causes any BLOOD MARKERS or that takes the target Out of Action.
               * Warp Claws. Melee, includes one weapon in each hand, IGNORE OFF-HAND. While equipped with a Jump Pack, when a model equipped with Warp Claws takes an enemy Out of Action in melee during its Activation, if it is not engaged in melee with any other enemy, it can immediately move up to its speed.
Heretic Astartes Heavy Melee Weapons
               * Helbrute Hammer. Melee, +2 INJURY MODIFIER, HEAVY, TWO-HANDED
               * Power Scourge. Melee, SWEEPING, ARMOUR PIERCING 1, SHRAPNEL, HEAVY, TWO-HANDED
Heretic Astartes Equipment
               * Astartes Bike. The equipped model is mounted on a bike. The rider gains the LARGE Keyword if it is not already LARGE, as well as the VEHICLE Keyword, a movement speed of 10”/Infantry, and it is equipped with a Twin Boltgun, but has only one hand to carry other weapons, though it can hold two-handed weapons with Shield Combo. It also has the Turbo-Boost ability. This battlekit cannot be taken along with Terminator Armour.
               * Twin Boltgun. 24”, +1 DICE, VICIOUS 11, CRITICAL. This weapon can be used alongside any other ranged weapon when the rider takes the Shoot Action.
               * Turbo Boost. The rider has +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls, but it cannot climb sheer surfaces.
               * Icon of Despair. HELD, ICON. While the bearer is not Down, enemies within 4” of it have -1 DICE to all Success Rolls, unless they ignore the FEAR Keyword. This penalty does not stack with the FEAR Keyword.
               * Icon of Excess. HELD, ICON. While the bearer is not Down, it and each of its SLAANESH allies within 4” of it ignore Difficult and Dangerous Terrain, cannot be forced to move by enemy models, and never fall as a result of benign Downed.
               * Icon of Flame. HELD, ICON. While the bearer is not Down, it and each of its TZEENTCH allies within 4” of it gain IGNORE COVER with all ranged attacks.
               * Icon of Vengeance. HELD, ICON. While the bearer is not Down, it and each of its allies within 8” of it have NEGATE FEAR.
               * Icon of Wrath. HELD, ICON. While the bearer is not Down, it and each of its KHORNE allies within 4” of it can move up to 3” after they declare a Charge Action, before determining the target or rolling for charge distance. This movement does not count against the charge distance, but it cannot bring a model into or out of melee combat, and it must bring the model to a location where it can legally declare a Charge.
Marks of Chaos
Marks of Chaos are purchased just like Equipment, but cannot be removed or sold, and a model can have only one of them. A model can take a Mark of Chaos only if specifically allowed.
               * Mark of Darkness. Ranged attacks have -1 DICE to Hit the model.
               * Mark of Khorne. The model’s melee attacks have +1 INJURY MODIFIER. It gains the KHORNE Keyword.
               * Mark of Nurgle. Injury rolls made against the model have -1 DICE. A Bloodbath ignores this effect. The model also gains the NURGLE Keyword, and moves only half as far when Dashing.
               * Mark of Slaanesh. The model has +2” movement speed and +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls. It gains the SLAANESH Keyword.
               * Mark of Tzeentch. The model’s ranged attacks have +1 INJURY MODIFIER. It gains the TZEENTCH Keyword.
Heretic Astartes Campaign Shop
During a campaign, these pieces of battlekit are available only when your Warband is specifically allowed to purchase them.
               * Black Mace (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Melee Weapon). Melee, SWEEPING, +1 INJURY MODIFIER, HEAVY, ARMOUR PIERCING 1, TWO-HANDED
               * Black Rune Of Damnation (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Cultist Only, Equipment). The equipped model and each friendly Cultist within 12” of it has -1 INJURY MODIFIER, stacking with armour up to -3. In addition, when a model uses a psychic power while within 12” of this model, it has PERILOUS ±2, and it must roll twice on the Perils of the Warp table if it suffers Perils of the Warp, and you choose one of the results.
               * Blade Of The Relentless (4 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Melee Weapon). Melee, VICIOUS 11, CRITICAL, ARMOUR PIERCING 2
               * Book of the Reviler (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Word Bearers Only, Dark Apostle or Cultist Only, Equipment). HELD. If a Dark Apostle carries this book, it has +1 DICE to Hit and +1 INJURY DICE with melee attacks. If a model other than a Dark Apostle carries this book, it gains the Malign Sacrifice ability of the Dark Apostle.
               * Eye Of Tzeentch (5 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Psyker with Mark of Tzeentch Only, Equipment). The equipped model has +1 DICE to all PSYCHIC Success Rolls, including attacks.
               * Foecleaver (4 Glory, LIMIT: 1, no Warband Variant Only, Elite Only, Melee Weapon). Melee, +1 INJURY DICE, another +1 INJURY DICE against any model that is part of an Imperium Warband, ARMOUR PIERCING 2, HEAVY, TWO-HANDED
               * Gorget Of Eternal Hate (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Equipment). When the equipped model is taken Out of Action, one enemy within 6” of it suffers D3 BLOOD MARKERS. This equipment is not lost if the equipped model is a casualty.
               * Inferno Tome (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Cultist Only, Equipment). HELD, TWO-HANDED. The bearer gains the FIRETEAM Keyword and can form a Fireteam with a Dark Apostle in your Warband. While the bearer is not Down and is within 6” of the chosen Dark Apostle, that model gain the following ability:
               * Litany of Despair. Enemies within 8” of the Dark Apostle have -1 DICE to all Success Rolls.
               * Intoxicating Elixir (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Mark of Slaanesh Only, Equipment). Once per battle, as an Action that requires no roll, the equipped model can drink it elixir. Until the end of the current Turn, it can make an additional melee attack with one weapon whenever it takes the Fight Action, and injury rolls have -1 INJURY DICE against it.
               * Liber Hereticus (5 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Psyker Only, Equipment). HELD, the first psychic power the bearer uses during each of its Activations does not count towards the Using Multiple Powers rules.
               * Mantle Of Traitors (4 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Equipment). The bearer has +1 DICE to Hit with melee attacks. In addition, once per Turn, it can ignore range and line of sight when targeting a friendly model with an Ability, Action, or psychic power.
               * Orb of Curze (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Nightlords Only, Elite Only, Equipment). After the equipped model activates, you choose the enemy model that activates next, obeying all normal rules.
               * Orbs Of Unlife (5 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Mark of Nurgle Only, Ranged Weapon). 8”, ASSAULT, IGNORE ARMOUR, causes double BLOOD MARKERS, BLAST 3”, IGNORE COVER, IGNORE LONG RANGE, SCATTER, THROWN, FUMBLE
               * Shadesword (4 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Alpha Legion Only, Melee Weapon). Melee, -DICE cannot be applied to Injury rolls, ARMOUR PIERCING 2, PSYCHIC
               * Soulsnare Sigil (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Emperor’s Children Only, Equipment). When the bearer takes an enemy model Out of Action with a melee attack, remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from the bearer.
               * Talisman Of Burning Blood (4 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Mark of Khorne Only, Equipment). Once during each Turn, if the bearer Downs or takes an enemy Out of Action in melee, it can move up to 6”, and can enter melee as if it Charged, then it can make a single extra melee attack. This movement can cause it to leave melee, but counts as a Retreat except by the model that triggered the movement.
               * Warpbreacher (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Iron Warriors Only, Chaos Lord or Warpsmith Only, Equipment). As an Action with a Risky Success Roll, the bearer can pluck a daemon from the warp and infuse a friendly ARTIFICIAL, MECHANICUS, or VEHICLE model with it. On a success, the chosen model has +1 INJURY MODIFIER with all weapons until the end of its next Activation.
               * Warp’s Malice (3 Glory, Ranged Weapon). 12”, VICIOUS 11, ASSAULT, CRITICAL, PISTOL, Scope Capable. On a hit, the wielder has +1 DICE to Hit with the next melee attack it makes against that same target during this Activation with another weapon.
Heretic Astartes Psychic Discipline
               * Dark Prescience (5 credits, Effect). No Range, Self, Next Activation, attacks against the caster suffer -1 DICE to Hit.
               * Death Hex (5 credits, Effect). 12”, One Enemy, End of Turn, Injury rolls against the target have +1 INJURY DICE.
               * Gift of Chaos (3 Glory, Attack). 6”, IGNORE COVER, IGNORE LONG RANGE, IGNORE ARMOUR. If this takes an ELITE enemy Out of Action, you can place a Chaos Spawn Mercenary on the table under your control where that enemy was located. The Chaos Spawn does not count towards Morale or join your Warband, and the original model is treated normally for the post-game sequence. You can have only up to one Chaos Spawn on the table from this or any similar effect at a time.
               * Infernal Gaze (10 credits, Effect). 18”, One Enemy, Immediate, the target gains 1 BLOOD MARKER.
               * Siphon Life (5 credits, Attack). 18”, IGNORE COVER, IGNORE ARMOUR, -1 INJURY DICE, PSYCHIC, RISKY, remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from the caster on a hit that causes any BLOOD MARKERS or that takes the target Out of Action.
               * Warptime (3 credits, Effect). 12”, Self or one Ally, Target Activation, the caster has +1 DICE to Hit with all attacks.

Battlekit
A Chaos Lord must be equipped with Power Armour or Terminator Armour, included in the cost and its statistics above. Otherwise, it can be equipped with any weapons, armour, equipment, or Mark of Chaos from the Heretic Astartes Armoury.


Abilities
               * Black Carapace. The Chaos Lord treats any Down result from the Injury Roll Table as a Minor Hit instead. This does not apply to Down results that already replaced another result, such as with TOUGH.
               * Soul Gorge. When the Chaos Lord takes an enemy Out of Action in melee, you may remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from it.

               * Accursed Crozius. Melee, ARMOUR PIERCING 2. The wielder gains the FEAR Keyword.


Abilities
               * Black Carapace. The Dark Apostle treats any Down result from the Injury Roll Table as a Minor Hit instead. This does not apply to Down results that already replaced another result, such as with TOUGH.
               * Dark Zealotry. As an Action, the Dark Apostle can take a Risky Success Roll with +1 DICE. If successful, all friendly FOLLOWER models that are Down and within 12” of the Dark Apostle may immediately stand up at no penalty or cost.
               * Malign Sacrifice. The Dark Apostle can attack friendly models within 1” of it. Whenever it causes a friendly model to take 1 or more BLOOD MARKERS or takes a friendly model Out of Action, you can choose one other friendly model within 12” of the Dark Apostle that has not yet activated this Turn. That model activates after the Dark Apostle’s Activation ends, and it has +1 DICE to all Success Rolls during that Activation. If the friendly model was taken Out of Action, it also has +1 INJURY DICE with all attacks during that Activation.

A Chaos Space Marine must be equipped with Power Armour, included in the cost and its statistics above. Otherwise, it can be equipped with any weapons, armour, equipment, or Mark of Chaos from the Heretic Astartes Armoury.


Abilities
               * Black Carapace. The Chaos Space Marine treats any Down result from the Injury Roll Table as a Minor Hit instead. This does not apply to Down results that already replaced another result, such as with TOUGH.


Upgrades
               * Havoc. Up to 2 Chaos Space Marines, or 3 in a Warband of 1,200 credits or more, can be upgraded to Havocs for +5 credits each, allowing them to ignore the HEAVY Keyword of one ranged weapon they carry.
               * Shrivetalon. Up to 2 Chaos Space Marines, or 3 in a Warband of 1,200 credits or more, can be upgraded to Shrivetalons for +10 credits each, granting them the FEAR Keyword.
               * Raptor. Up to 2 Chaos Space Marines, or 3 in a Warband of 1,200 credits or more, can be upgraded to Raptors for +5 credits each, allowing them to be equipped with Jump Packs and Warp Claws (purchased separately). While equipped with a Jump Pack, a Raptor gains the SKIRMISHER Keyword.
