Warband Variant: Alpha Legion  
               * Less Corrupt. You cannot include any Dark Apostles, and only up to 1 Possessed, in your Warband, and you cannot purchase any Marks of Chaos.
               * Dedicated to Deception. You cannot use the Dark Pacts special rule. Instead, your HERETIC ASTARTES models have the Lies and Obfuscation ability:
               * Lies and Obfuscation. Enemies treat this model as if they were 2” further away for the purpose of measuring the range of abilities and weapons with a range greater than 1”, as well as for the purpose of declaring Charges, and reduce the Charge distance of any enemy that charges this model by 2”.
               * I Am Alpharius. Your patron must be Alpharius, described below.
               * Hidden in Plain Sight. At the start of the battle, you can give the INFILTRATOR Keyword up to half of your HERETIC ASTARTES models, rounded down, for the duration of the battle.
               * Many Heads of the Hydra. Whenever your Warband leader is taken Out of Action, you can choose any one of your other non-FOLLOWER models on the battlefield. That model gains the LEADER Keyword until the end of the battle, or until it is taken Out of Action, at which point you can choose another model in the same manner.
               * Saboteurs. Up to 2 of your Chaos Space Marines, or 3 in a Warband of 1,200 credits or more, can be upgraded to Saboteurs for +10 credits each. A Saboteur can ignore the HEAVY Keyword of Blasting Charges, has NEGATE MINED, and gains the following ability:
               * Mine Layer. As an Action with a Success Roll with +2 DICE, the Saboteur can mine a piece of terrain they alone are touching, no bigger than 8” x 8” (an 8” section of trench, a wall, a tree, a building etc). If successful, the terrain piece is now MINED.
               * Alpha Legion Battlekit. You can purchase up to 2 Blasting Charges (CONSUMABLE) for 15 credits each, and up to 1 Melta Bombs for 15 credits, as well as the following additional battlekit:
               * Throwing Power Knives (20 credits, LIMIT: 2). 8”, IGNORE LONG RANGE, ASSAULT, ARMOUR PIERCING 2, THROWN
               * Shroud Bombs (5 credits, LIMIT: 4). THROWN. The equipped model has the STEALTH Keyword.
Patron: Alpharius
               * Clandestine. Ranged attacks made against this model automatically miss on a result of 7.
               * Covert Control. At the start of the battle, after deployment, select one enemy model. You can redeploy that model up to 6” in any direction you wish, though not into Melee combat, into impassable terrain, or out of the battlefield. You cannot choose a model that cannot be forced to move.
               * Cult Leader. Friendly Cultist models within 3” of this model have +1 DICE to Hit with all attacks.
               * Headhunter. This model’s attacks cannot have their target changed or have their range reduced, and Injury rolls caused by them cannot have -DICE applied to them.
               * Infiltrate and Subvert. This model gains the ICON Keyword.
               * Master Of Diversion. At the start of each battle, after deployment, you may redeploy this model and up to 2 of your other non-INFILTRATOR models, or 1 INFILTRATOR model.

Warband Variant: Death Guard  
               * Rot Focused. Your Warband cannot include any Dark Apostles, Warpsmiths, Possessed, or Raptors.
               * Nurgle Disciples. All Chaos Lords, Chaos Sorcerers, Cultists, Chaos Space Marines, Chaos Terminators, and Helbrutes in your Warband automatically have a Mark of Nurgle. Their cost is increased by 15 credits each. All of these models besides Cultists also have the Contagion ability.
               * Contagion. Enemy models within 1” of this model that have 1 or more INFECTION MARKERS have -1 DICE to Hit with all attacks (spending all of their INFECTION MARKERS on that attack negates this penalty).
               * Sealed Pact. You cannot use the Dark Pacts special rule. Your patron must be the Nurgle Shared Patron.
               * Spreaders of Disease. Instead of the Dread Reputation special rule, during a campaign, you can alter the way your models’ Contagion Ability functions. During a campaign, in the quartermaster step between battles, you can choose any number of the following modifiers to apply during your next battle, totaling up to a number of Virulence Points equal to the number of battles you have already fought.
               * Death Dance (2 Virulence Points). When an enemy that is affected by Contagion Retreats, your models have +1 DICE to Hit and +1 INJURY DICE against that enemy when making their free attacks.
               * Bleeding Lesions (2 Virulence Points). Whenever one of your models with Contagion is taken Out of Action, each enemy that was within 3” of it is affected by Contagion regardless of distance until the end of the current Turn.
               * Bone Ague (3 Virulence Points). Injury rolls against enemies affected by Contagion cannot be modified by -DICE.
               * Creeping Buboes (5 Virulence Points). Enemies that are affected by Contagion at the start of their Activation remain affected until the end of that Activation.
               * Festering Growths (5 Virulence Points). At the end of each enemy model’s Activation, if it is affected by Contagion, it suffers 1 BLOOD MARKER.
               * Foul Corruption (4 Virulence Points). As an Action with a Risky Success Roll, any model with Contagion can attempt to corrupt one enemy within 7” of it. On a success, that enemy is affected by Contagion regardless of distance until the end of the current Turn.
               * Grey Fever (4 Virulence Points). All Success Rolls made by an enemy affected by Contagion are considered Risky Success Rolls.
               * Green Pox (1-4 Virulence Points). The radius of Contagion increases by 1” for every Virulence Point spent.
               * Haemorrhaging Ulcers (1 Virulence Point). Enemies affected by Contagion cannot add a D6 to their Charge Distance. This applies only if they begin the Charge affected.
               * Oozing Plague (3 Virulence Points). Whenever an enemy model with 1 or more INFECTION MARKERS is taken Out of Action, even if those INFECTION MARKERS were all spent on its final Injury roll, each other enemy model within 3” of that enemy is affected by Contagion regardless of distance until the end of the current Turn.
               * Seeping Cysts (2 Virulence Points).  Enemies affected by Contagion have -2” of movement. This applies only if they begin their movement affected.
               * The Shakes (1 Virulence Point). Enemies that are affected by Contagion have -1 DICE to non-attack Success Rolls.
               * Weeping Sores (5 Virulence Points). When a model with Contagion hits an enemy within 14” with a ranged attack, that model is considered within 3” of it for the purposes of Contagion until the end of the current Turn.
               * Withering Cough (5 Virulence Points). Melee attacks made against enemies affected by Contagion have +1 DICE to Hit.
               * Lord of Rot. Your Chaos Lord can be upgraded in one of the following ways:
               * Shroud of Disease (20 credits, LIMIT: 1). Ranged attacks made against this model or any of its allies within 3” have -1 DICE to Hit.
               * Vector of Disease (10 credits, LIMIT: 1). When this model Charges, or an ally ends a Charge within 3” of it, that model gains +1 INJURY DICE with its melee attacks during that Activation.
               * Virulent Aura (15 credits, LIMIT: 1). The ranged weapons of this model and each of its allies within 3” of it gain the CRITICAL Keyword. Attacks that automatically hit or that already have CRITICAL instead have +1 INJURY DICE.
               * Plague Champions. Up to two of your Chaos Space Marines can be Plague Marine Champions, which gain the ELITE Keyword, and can each be upgraded in one of the following ways. They cannot be upgraded in the normal ways. When one of your Chaos Space Marines is promoted to Elite during a campaign, it can also immediately take one of these upgrades, paying for it normally.
               * Extraction of Fresh Disease (15 credits, LIMIT: 1). When this model takes an enemy model Out of Action in melee, one other enemy within 6” of it suffers 1 INFECTION MARKER.
               * Malicious Calculations (15 credits, LIMIT: 1). BLOOD MARKERS cannot be spent on the attacks of this model or any of its allies within 3” of it.
               * Putrefying Stink (15 credits, LIMIT: 1). Enemy models that charge this model or one of its allies within 3” of it do not add a D6 to their charge roll and must use only their normal movement distance.
               * Tainted Narthecium (10 credits, LIMIT: 1). As an Action with a Success Roll with +1 DICE, this model can attempt to mend the wounds of itself or another NURGLE model within 1” of it. On a success, remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from that model, or 3 BLOOD MARKERS if it was a critical success.
               * Tocsin of Misery (15 credits, LIMIT: 1). At the end of each Turn, each enemy within 3” of this model that has at least 1 BLOOD MARKER gains an additional BLOOD MARKER.
               * The Walking Pox. You can take any number of Poxwalkers, described below, as Troops in your Warband.
               * Blight-Drone Acquisition. You can take a Foetid Blight-Drone, described below, as a Troop in your Warband, but you cannot take both a Foetid Blight-Drone and a Helbrute.
               * Death Guard Battlekit. You cannot purchase any Flamers or Heavy Flamers, but you can purchase up to 2 Mortis Ammunition (CONSUMABLE) for 10 credits each, and add the following battlekit to your Armoury:
               * Blight Grenades (10 credits, LIMIT: 3). 8”, BLAST 2”, IGNORE COVER, IGNORE LONG RANGE, INFECTION MARKERS, FUMBLE
               * Blight Launcher (35 credits, LIMIT: 1). 24”, IGNORE COVER, BLAST 2”, TWO-HANDED, INFECTION MARKERS
               * Corrupted Staff (5 credits, Psyker Only). Melee, +1 INJURY DICE against targets with DAEMON or PSYKER Keyword, INFECTION MARKERS, PSYCHIC, HELD
               * Cursed Plague Bell (15 credits, LIMIT: 1). Melee, +1 INJURY MODIFIER, ARMOUR PIERCING 1 against targets with any INFECTION MARKERS, +1 INJURY DICE against targets with DAEMON or PSYKER Keyword, HEAVY, PSYCHIC, TWO-HANDED
               * Great Plague Blade (30 credits, LIMIT: 1, Elite or Terminator Only). Melee, +1 INJURY DICE, CRITICAL, ARMOUR PIERCING 2, INFECTION MARKERS, HEAVY, TWO-HANDED
               * Heavy Blight Launcher (45 credits, LIMIT: 1). 36”, IGNORE COVER, BLAST 3”, HEAVY, TWO-HANDED, INFECTION MARKERS
               * Heavy Plague Spewer (45 credits, LIMIT: 1). 10”, -1 INJURY DICE, FLAMETHROWER, IGNORE ARMOUR, AUTOMATIC 2, GAS, HEAVY, TWO-HANDED
               * Mischievous Nurgling (3 credits, LIMIT: 3). Once per battle, when an enemy in close combat with this model makes a melee attack, you can cause the attack to be made at -1 DICE to Hit.
               * Plague Blade (7 credits, LIMIT: 2). Melee, INFECTION MARKERS
               * Plague Spewer (30 credits, LIMIT: 2). 8”, FLAMETHROWER, IGNORE ARMOUR, GAS, TWO-HANDED
               * Plaguespurt Gauntlet (10 credits, Wearing Terminator Armour Only). 6”, FLAMETHROWER, IGNORE ARMOUR, -1 INJURY DICE, GAS
               * Poison Vents (10 credits, LIMIT: 3, non-Cultist Elite Only). When an enemy model begins its Activation within 1” of the bearer, if it has any INFECTION MARKERS, it gains an additional INFECTION MARKER, in addition to the one it would normally gain.
               * Death Guard Campaign Shop. Beside Orb Of Unlife, you cannot take battlekit from the Heretic Astartes Campaign Shop, but the following Campaign Shop battlekit is available to you:
               * Billowing Censer (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Equipment). When the bearer or one of its allies with the HERETIC ASTARTES and NURGLE Keywords within 6” is taken Out of Action, one enemy within 6” of that model of your choice suffers 1 BLOOD MARKER.
               * Orb of Decay (7 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Equipment). Once per battle, the bearer can unleash the Orb’s power as an Action with no roll. Each non-NURGLE model within 6” of the bearer must roll on the Injury table. This injury is considered to have the GAS Keyword, and has IGNORE ARMOUR against targets that are not in cover.
               * Plaguebringer (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Melee Weapon). Melee, +1 INJURY DICE, CRITICAL, HEAVY, TWO-HANDED, INFECTION MARKERS. If an enemy is taken Out of Action by this weapon, that enemy’s owner counts as having an additional 1 fewer non-Down models on the battlefield for the purpose of Morale until the end of the current Turn.
               * Plague Skull (6 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Ranged Weapon). 12”, -1 INJURY DICE, AUTOMATIC 2, ASSAULT, GAS
               * Putrid Periapt (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Psyker Only, Equipment). Choose a power from the Contagion Discipline that costs 7 credits when you purchase the tome. The bearer can use that power as if it knew it. In addition, when the bearer takes an enemy model Out of Action with a psychic power, remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from the bearer.
               * Reaper of Glorious Entropy (6 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Melee Weapon). Melee, +2 INJURY DICE, CRITICAL, HEAVY, IGNORE ARMOUR, TWO-HANDED
               * Revolting Stench-Vats (5 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Equipment). Enemy models within 6” of the bearer cannot benefit from any +DICE to its Success Rolls besides its Ranged Skill, its Melee Skill, or its PSYKER X, nor can Injuries they cause benefit from any +DICE besides those provided by their weapons.
               * Suppurating Plate (4 Glory Elite, LIMIT: 1, Elite Wearing Power Armour Only, Equipment). The wearer has an additional -1 INJURY MODIFIER. In addition, whenever an enemy hits the wearer with a melee attack, that enemy suffers 1 BLOOD MARKER. A model with NEGATE GAS is unaffected by the additional BLOOD MARKER.
               * Warp Charm of Nurgle (4 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Equipment). The equipped model’s Contagion Ability’s radius is increased by 3”.
               * Contagion Discipline. Your models cannot choose powers from the Heretic Astartes Psychic Discipline. Instead, you can purchase any of the Contagion Discipline Powers:
               * Entropy (7 credits, Attack). 14”, +1 INJURY DICE, PSYCHIC, RISKY
               * Nurgle’s Rot (2 Glory, Attack). 7”, IGNORE COVER, IGNORE LONG RANGE, IGNORE ARMOUR, INFECTION MARKERS (including from GAS), BLAST 3”, GAS, PSYCHIC, RISKY
               * Poisonous Miasma (14 credits, Effect). 3”, Each Model Besides Self, each target must roll on the Injury table. This injury is considered to have the GAS and IGNORE ARMOUR Keywords.
               * Plague Wind (7 credits, Attack). 14”, IGNORE ARMOUR, GAS, PSYCHIC, RISKY
               * Putrescent Vitality (7 credits, Effect). 7”, Self or One NURGLE Ally, Immediate, remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from the target.
               * Rancid Visitations (7 credits, Effect). -1 DICE, 14”, One Enemy, Immediate, the target gains an INFECTION MARKER.
               * Tallyband. Optionally, when you create your Warband you can choose to make it a Tallyband. If you do, the following additional rules apply:
               * You cannot take any Chaos Cultists or Helbrutes, and you cannot recruit any Summoned Minor Daemon or Summoned Daemon Mercenaries.
               * You can take a maximum of 4 Chaos Space Marines.
               * You can recruit Plague Legion Troop models from the Chaos Daemons faction.
               * If you recruit a Plague Drone, you cannot take a Foetid Blight-Drone.

________________


Warband Variant: Emperor’s Children  
               * Elegance. Your Warband cannot include any Warpsmiths or Shirvetalons.
               * Slaanesh Disciples. All Chaos Lords, Dark Apostles, Chaos Sorcerers, Cultists, Chaos Space Marines, Chaos Terminators, and Helbrutes in your Warband automatically have a Mark of Slaanesh. Their cost is increased by 10 credits each.
               * Sealed Pact. You cannot use the Dark Pacts special rule. Your patron must be the Slaanesh Shared Patron.
               * Combat Elixirs. Instead of the Dread Reputation special rule, during a campaign, you can gather Ingredients during battles and turn them into Combat Elixirs. At the start of each battle, before deployment, your opponent places three 1” Ingredient markers exactly half the battlefield size away from your deployment zone (18” away for 3’x3’ board, 24” away for a 4’x4’ board), but not within 6” of a battlefield edge or another marker, or on Impassable Terrain or completely surrounded by it.


At any point during the battle, one of your models can make a Success Roll to gather an Ingredient marker it is in contact with. On a success, remove the marker and set it aside until the end of the battle.


At the end of the battle, you gain Ingredients based on the number of Ingredient markers you successfully gathered.
               * 1 Ingredient Marker: 1 Common Ingredient
               * 2 Ingredient Markers: D3 Common Ingredients, roll a D6 and on a 5+, 1 Rare Ingredient
               * 3 Ingredient Markers: D3 Common Ingredients, 1 Rare Ingredient


You can also gain Exotic Ingredients, but only under special circumstances:
               * Concentrate of Tormented Souls. You can gain Concentrate of Tormented Souls only via the Favored of Slaanesh Patron Skill.
               * Dilution of False Hope. If your opponent is an Imperial or Outlaw Warband, When you take the first non-ARTIFICAL enemy model Out of Action with a melee attack during each battle, you gain 1 Dilution of False Hope.
               * Distillate of Hatred. When you take the third enemy ASTARTES model Out of Action during each battle, you gain 1 Distillate of Hatred.
               * Essence of Fear. If your opponent has any AELDARI, DRUKHARI, or HARLEQUIN models on the battlefield, the first time they fail a Morale test during each battle, you gain 1 Essence of Fear.
               * Extract of Xeno-Matter. If your opponent is a Xenos Warband, when you take the first enemy model Out of Action with a melee attack during each battle, you gain 1 Extract of Xeno-Matter.
               * Infusion of Traitor’s Blood. If your opponent is a Chaos Warband, when you take the first non-ARTIFICAL, non-DAEMON enemy model Out of Action with a melee attack during each battle, you gain 1 Infusion of Traitor’s Blood.


Keep track of how many of each Ingredient type you have: Common, Rare, and Exotic, each beginning at 0. You can have a maximum of 6 Common, 4 Rare, and 2 of each Exotic Ingredient stored at a time.


During the Trading step between any battles, you can craft Combat Elixirs by spending the required Ingredients. Combat Elixirs function as battlekit, and are always CONSUMBALE. Once an Elixir is crafted, if you do not equip it during a battle, it is lost at the end of that battle. You can also purchase up to two Exotic Ingredients for 1 Glory apiece during each Trading step.
               * Alacrine (Elite Only, 2 Common or 1 Rare + Extract of Xeno-Matter). Whenever an enemy Charges the equipped model, the equipped model can make a single melee attack with a non-HEAVY weapon against that enemy before it takes any further actions.
               * Anfrak Silk (1 Common + 1 Rare or 1 Extract of Xeno-Matter). The equipped model has +2” to its movement during the battle.
               * Cerebresec (Elite Only, 2 Common + 1 Concentrate of Tormented Souls). At the start of each Turn, the equipped model gains 1 BLESSING MARKER.
               * Excelsor X (Elite Only, 4 Common or 1 Dilution of False Hope + 1 Rare). The equipped model has +2 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls during the battle.
               * Heliotrophos (2 Common + 1 Rare). The equipped model has +1 INJURY DICE with melee attacks during the battle.
               * Salviqine Tears (Elite Only, 1 Common + 1 Rare or 1 Essence of Fear). Enemies within 6” of the equipped model treat all of their enemies as if they had the FEAR Keyword.
               * Sanctus Vi (Elite Only, 1 Common + 1 Rare or 1 Concentrate of Tormented Souls + 1 Infusion of Traitor’s Blood). The first -2 of the equipped model’s armour has IMPERVIOUS during the battle.
               * Shivversplit (1 Common + 1 Rare or 1 Essence of Fear). Injury rolls made against the equipped model have -1 DICE during the battle.
               * Skorflense (2 Common or 1 Extract of Xeno-Matter). The equipped model has +1 DICE to Hit with all attacks during the battle.
               * Quail (1 Common + 1 Rare). The equipped model never counts as Down for the purposes of Morale during the battle. In addition, the equipped model has the LEADER Keyword.
               * Thrynicine (Elite Only, 2 Rare or 1 Distillate of Hatred + 1 Rare or 1 Dilution of False Hope + 1 Infusion of Traitor’s Blood). The equipped model has +1 INJURY DICE with all attacks during the battle.
               * Xylocil (1 Common + 1 Rare or 1 Extract of Xeno-Matter). The equipped model gains ARMOUR PIERCING 1 with all melee attacks during the battle.
               * Flawless Blades. Up to 2 of your Chaos Space Marines, or 3 in a Warband of 1,200 credits or more can be upgraded to Flawless Blades for +5 credits each, granting them an additional +1 DICE to Hit with melee attacks. Flawless Blades cannot use any ranged weapons besides Pistols and Grenades.
               * Lord Kakophonist. You can take up to one Lord Kakophonist, described below, as an Elite in your Warband.
               * Emperor’s Children Battlekit. Add the following battlekit to your Armoury:
               * Blastmaster (30 credits, LIMIT: 2). 24”, IGNORE COVER, HEAVY, ARMOUR PIERCING 2, TWO-HANDED
               * Blissblade (12 credits, Elite or Flawless Blade Only). Melee, CRITICAL, ARMOUR PIERCING 1
               * Phoenix Power Spear (15 credits, LIMIT: 2). Melee, +1 INJURY DICE during a Turn in which wielder Charged, CRITICAL, ARMOUR PIERCING 2, TWO-HANDED
               * Rapture Lash (7 credits). Melee, ignores penalty for attacking in Off-Hand
               * Screamer Pistol (15 credits). 12”, IGNORE COVER, ASSAULT
               * Sonic Blaster (15 credits, LIMIT: 4). 18”, IGNORE COVER, ARMOUR PIERCING 1, TWO-HANDED
               * Sonic Shriekers (10 credits, LIMIT: 2, Elite, Chaos Space Marine, or Chaos Terminator Only). The equipped model gains the FEAR Keyword.
               * Twin Screamer Pistols (20 credits, LIMIT: 2). 12”, +1 DICE, IGNORE COVER, ASSAULT
               * Excess Psychic Discipline. Your models cannot choose powers from the Heretic Astartes Psychic Discipline. Instead, they can purchase any of the Excess Discipline:
               * Acquiescence (5 credits, Effect). -1 DICE, 12”, One Enemy, End of Turn, attacks against the target have +1 DICE to Hit and +1 INJURY DICE.
               * Cacophonic Choir (5 credits, Attack). Melee, SWEEPING, IGNORE ARMOUR, PSYCHIC, RISKY
               * Hysterical Frenzy (10 credits, Effect). 12”, Self or One SLAANESH Ally, Immediate, the target can immediately move up to its movement speed. Enemies do not get free attacks if the model leaves close combat with this movement, and the model can move into close combat range as if it Charged.
               * Lash of Submission (5 credits, Attack). 12”, PSYCHIC, RISKY, the target is moved up to 3” in a single direction of the attacker’s choice. This movement can bring the target into or out of melee, and counts as a Charge and/or Retreat as appropriate.
               * Sensory Overload (5 credits, Effect). 12”, One Enemy, Immediate, the target is knocked Down. This is not a result on the Injury chart and does not cause any BLOOD MARKERS.
               * Symphony of Pain (2 Glory, Effect). 6”, One Enemy, Immediate, the target gains D3 BLOOD MARKERS.
               * Carnival of Excess. Optionally, when you create your Warband you can choose to make it a Carnival of Excess. If you do, the following additional rules apply:
               * You cannot take any Chaos Cultists or Helbrutes, and you cannot recruit any Summoned Minor Daemon or Summoned Daemon Mercenaries.
               * You can take a maximum of 4 Chaos Space Marines.
               * You can recruit Legion of Excess Troop models from the Chaos Daemons faction.

Warband Variant: Iron Warriors  
               * Bound to the Machine. You cannot use the Dark Pacts special rule, and you can purchase a maximum of 1 of each Mark of Chaos besides Mark of Darkness. Your patron cannot be Chaos Undivided.
               * Mechanical Horror. You cannot recruit any Dark Apostles, but can recruit up to 2 Warpsmiths.
               * Obliterator. You can recruit up to 1 Obliterator Mercenary as a Troop in your Warband for 215 credits.
               * Heavy Gunners. You can upgrade an additional Chaos Space Marine to a Havoc, but can upgrade only 1 to a Shrivetalon and 1 to a Raptor.
               * Heavier Guns. You can purchase up to 1 Mortar for 50 credits, and the following battlekit is added to your Armoury:

Warband Variant: Night Lords  
               * No Pointless Faith. You cannot recruit any Cultists or Dark Apostles. Your Patron must be Legacy of Curze, described below. When you create your Warband, you can decide to make it an Outlaw Warband instead of a Chaos Warband.
               * In Midnight Clad. You cannot use the Dark Pacts special rule. Instead, all of your HERETIC ASTARTES models gain the STEALTH Keyword.
               * Children of Night. You can purchase a maximum of 1 of each Mark of Chaos besides Mark of Darkness.
               * Terror Tactics. You can upgrade up to 1 additional Chaos Space Marine to a Raptor, and 1 additional one to a Shrivetalon, but can upgrade only a maximum of 1 to a Havoc.
               * Curseclaw. Up to one of your Possessed can be equipped with a Jump Pack.
               * Depradetor. You can upgrade up to 1 of your Raptors to a Depredator for +5 credits, allowing it to ignore the HEAVY Keyword of one melee weapon it wields, including for the purposes of Jump Packs.
               * Warp Talons. You can upgrade up to 2 of your Raptors to gain the DEEP STRIKE Keyword for +10 credits each.
               * Night Lords Battlekit. Add the following additional battlekit to your Armoury:
               * Chain Snare (5 credits, LIMIT: 2). When an enemy in close combat with the equipped model Retreats, the equipped model has +1 DICE to Hit and +1 INJURY DICE for its free attack against that model.
               * Comms Jammer (5 credits, LIMIT: 1). Enemy models within 3” of the bearer cannot be targeted or affected by the abilities, battlekit, actions, or psychic powers of models that are friendly to those enemy models.
               * Grisly Trophy (10 credits, LIMIT: 2). The bearer gains the FEAR Keyword. If it already has the FEAR Keyword, instead that Keyword cannot be ignored by enemies, even if they also have FEAR.
               * Ventrilokar Vox (10 credits, LIMIT: 1). As an Action with a Risky Success Roll, the wearer can trick one enemy model it can see within 6” into moving. On a success, the target must immediately move D3” in the direction of your choice, including falling, provoking free attacks, etc.
               * Terrorchem Vials (20 credits, LIMIT: 1). 6”, -1 INJURY DICE, IGNORE ARMOUR, GAS, IGNORE COVER, IGNORE LONG RANGE, THROWN, FUMBLE, a target hit must immediately move D3” in the direction of your choice, including falling, provoking free attacks, etc.
Patron: Legacy of Curze
               * Dirty Fighter. Once per Turn, when an enemy model misses this model with a melee attack, this model can make a single melee attack against that enemy.
               * Killing Fury. During an Activation in which it Charged, this model has +1 INJURY DICE with melee attacks, and its melee attacks have SWEEPING.
               * Murderous Reputation. This model has +1 DICE to Hit with melee attacks against enemies that are affected by its FEAR Keyword. This skill has no effect for a model without FEAR.
               * Night Haunter’s Curse. As an Action with a Risky Success Roll, this model can choose one enemy model it can see within 6”. On a success, that enemy must roll on the Injury chart. This injury has IGNORE ARMOUR and NONLETHAL.
               * One Piece At A Time. Whenever this model hits with a melee attack, the target also suffers 1 STUN MARKER.
               * One With The Shadows. While this model is in cover from such an attack, enemies cannot make attacks against this model at Long Range.

Warband Variant: Thousand Sons  
               * Exalted. Your Warband cannot include any Chaos Lords, Dark Apostles, Warpsmiths, Raptors, or Possessed.
               * Tzeentch Disciples. All Chaos Sorcerers, Cultists, Chaos Space Marines, Chaos Terminators, and Helbrutes in your Warband automatically have a Mark of Tzeentch. Their cost is increased by 10 credits each.
               * Sealed Pact. You cannot use the Dark Pacts special rule. Your patron must be the Tzeentch Shared Patron.
               * Discover the Arcane. Instead of the Dread Reputation special rule, during a campaign, keep track of your Arcane Points, beginning at 0. At the start of each battle, before deployment, your opponent places three 1” Arcana markers anywhere that is exactly half the battlefield size away from your deployment zone (18” away for 3’x3’ board, 24” away for a 4’x4’ board), but not within 6” of a battlefield edge or another marker, or on Impassable Terrain or completely surrounded by it.


At any point during the battle, one of your PSYKER models can make a Success Roll to try to study an Arcana marker while it is in contact with it. On a success, remove the marker and you earn 1 Arcane Point.


During the quartermaster step between battles, you can empower one of your Elite PSYKER models with arcane knowledge. It gains a benefit depending on how many Arcane Points you have, then reset your Arcane points to 0. The benefit is either an Arcane Relic, which functions as battlekit but cannot be removed or sold, or an Arcane Power, which is an additional psychic power the model knows, which does not count towards its limit. Each model can gain only up to 1 Arcane Relic and up to 1 Arcane Power in this way.
               * 5-9 Arcane Points
               * Arcane Relic: Text Of Warp-Blown Ash. Once per battle, when the bearer would suffer Perils of the Warp, you can choose for it to ignore that Perils of the Warp.
               * Arcane Power: Thief Of Fate. 18”, AUTOMATIC 3, PSYCHIC, RISKY. All attacks must target the same model.
               * 10-14 Arcane Points
               * Arcane Relic: Boonstone. Once per battle, the bearer can gain +2 DICE to any PSYCHIC Success Roll it takes, including an attack.
               * Arcane Power: Visions Of Doom. As an Action with a Risky Success Roll with +1 DICE, the caster can choose one enemy within 18” of it that it can see. On a success, the target suffers -1 DICE to all Success Rolls and Injury rolls have +1 DICE against it until the end of the current Turn.
               * 15+ Arcane Points
               * Arcane Relic: Mesmeric Stave. +1 INJURY DICE against targets with DAEMON or PSYKER Keyword, PSYCHIC, HELD
               * Strike: Melee, +1 INJURY DICE
               * Shoot: 18”, +1 INJURY DICE, ASSAULT
               * Arcane Power: Binding Flames. As an Action with a Risky Success Roll with +1 DICE, the caster can choose itself or one ally within 18” of it that it can see. On a success, until the end of the current Turn, attacks against the target have -1 DICE to Hit and -1 INJURY DICE against the chosen model/
               * Rubric Marines. Your Chaos Space Marines have a speed of 5”, but when activated while within 6” of one of your PSYKER models, they have +1 DICE to all Dash actions during that Activation. They also have NEGATE GAS.
               * Warp Coven. You can recruit up to 2 Chaos Sorcerers, and you must recruit 1 Exalted Sorcerer, described below, as your Warband leader.
               * Tzaangors. You can recruit up to 1 Tzaangor Shaman, described below, as an Elite in your Warband, and any number of Tzaangors, described below, as Troops in your Warband.
               * Mechanical Guardian. You can recruit up to 1 Sekhetar Robot, described below, as a Troop in your Warband.
               * Aspiring Sorcerers. Any model you promote to an Elite gains the PSYKER 1 Keyword. When the model is promoted, choose the Change, Vengeance, or Heretic Astartes Discipline. That model may purchase up to two powers from the chosen discipline. It must be equipped with a PSYCHIC Weapon in order to use any Psychic Power.
               * Thousand Sons Battlekit. You can purchase 1 Cult Icon for 15 credits, each of which can be carried only by a Tzaangor, as well as up to 2 Hand Flamers for 25 credits each, which can be carried only by Elite models, and 1 Meltagun for 40 credits, which can only be carried by a Sekhetar Robot. These count as part of your Armoury. Add the following additional battlekit to your Armoury:

               * Power Claw (15 credits, LIMIT: 1, Sekhetar Robot Only). Melee, +1 INJURY DICE, HEAVY, ARMOUR PIERCING 2
               * Soulreaper Cannon (40 credits, LIMIT: 1). 24”, AUTOMATIC 3, RELOAD, HEAVY, TWO-HANDED
               * Campaign Shop. Beside Eye of Tzeentch, you cannot take battlekit from the Heretic Astartes Campaign Shop, but the following Campaign Shop battlekit is available to you:
               * Athenian Scrolls (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Exalted Sorcerer Only, Equipment). Once during each battle, when the bearer uses a psychic power, it can gain +1 DICE to that Success Roll, as well as each other time it uses that power during the same battle.
               * Change-Wrought Chalice (1 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Tzaangor Shaman Only, Equipment). Choose one power from the Discipline of Change or the Discipline of Vengeance that costs 10 credits or less. The equipped model knows that power in addition to any others.

               * Stave Abominus (5 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Psyker Only, Melee Weapon). Melee, CLEAVE 2, +1 INJURY DICE, +1 INJURY DICE against targets with DAEMON or PSYKER Keyword, PSYCHIC, HELD, TWO-HANDED, CUMBERSOME
               * Umbralefic Crystal (9 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Equipment). Once per battle, as an Action that requires no roll, choose this model or one of its allies within 6” of it. Remove that model from the table, then place it on the table anywhere that is at least 8” away from all enemies.
               * Warpweave Mantle (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Equipment). Whenever an enemy Charges the wearer, it does not roll a die to add to its charge distance.

               * Discipline of Change
               * Baleful Devolution (3 credits, Effect). 12”, One Enemy, Immediate, the target gains 1 STUN MARKER, or D3 STUN MARKERS on a Critical Success.

               * Pyric Flux (10 credits, Effect). 12”, Self or One TZEENTCH Ally, Target Activation, the target’s ranged weapons and psychic power attacks have the FIRE Keyword.

               * Infernal Gateway (10 credits, Attack). 12”, BLAST 3”, CRITICAL, PSYCHIC, RISKY, IGNORE LINE OF SIGHT. This attack can target only the nearest enemy.

               * Temporal Surge (5 credits, Effect). -1 DICE, 6”, Self or One Ally, Immediate, the target can immediately move up to its movement speed. This movement can bring it into or out of close combat, but allows enemies to attack it if it leaves close combat as if it Retreated.
               * Empyric Guidance (7 credits, Effect). 12”, Self or One Ally, Target Activation, the target adds 6” to the range of all non-PSYCHIC ranged weapons it has, or 12” if that weapon already had a range of 18” or higher.
               * Psychic Stalk (5 credits, Effect). 12”, One Enemy, Immediate, the caster can make a single ranged attack against the target with a melee weapon it is wielding, with a range of 12”. This attack automatically hits, and is a Critical Hit if the Success Roll to use this power was a Critical Success. This counts as a use of the Shoot Action.
               * Desecration Of Worlds (2 Glory, Effect). 24”, Point, End of Turn, place a Desecration marker on the battlefield at the target point. The ground within 3” of the center of that marker has DIFFICULT TERRAIN and DANGEROUS TERRAIN. Remove that marker at the end of the Turn. On a failure, the caster must roll on the Injury table.

               * You can take a maximum of 4 Chaos Space Marines.
               * You can recruit Scintillating Legion Troop models from the Chaos Daemons faction.

Warband Variant: Word Bearers  
               * The Faithful. You cannot include a Chaos Lord in your Warband. Instead, you must include at least 1 Dark Apostle or Chaos Sorcerer, and choose one of them to be your Warband Leader. That model gains the LEADER and TOUGH Keywords and does not count towards the normal limit for that type of model.
               * Blessed. You can take up to 4 of each Mark of Chaos besides Mark of Darkness, and your Cultists can take Marks of Chaos. Your patron must be the Chaos Undivided Shared Patron.
               * Gal Vorbak. Your Possessed are known as Gal Vorbak and can each be equipped with a single one-handed ranged weapon. Their Ranged Skill is +1, and they cost +10 credits each. You can recruit up to 5 of them.
               * Malefic Psychic Discipline. Your models select psychic powers from the Malefic Psychic Discipline instead of the Heretic Astartes Discipline, which has the following powers:
               * Cursed Earth (10 credits). -1 DICE, No Range, Self, End of Turn, Injury rolls against the caster and each friendly HERETIC ASTARTES or DAEMON model within 6” of it have -1 DICE.
               * Incursion (4 Glory). 6”, Point, Special, roll a D6 to determine what Mercenary model you summon. On a success, place the summoned model at the chosen point, and it counts as one of your models until the end of the battle. On a failure, your opponent places the model anywhere within 1” of the caster, and it counts as one of their models until the end of the battle. Either way, it can be activated as normal this Turn. Each PSYKER with this power can have only one summoned model on the battlefield at any given time, regardless of who controls it. They can use this power again if their current summoned model is under your control, removing the existing model before placing the new one, but not if it is under your opponent’s control. The summoned model is not added to any Warband and does not count towards Morale.
               * 1. Summoned Furie
               * 2. Summoned Bloodletter
               * 3. Summoned Daemonette
               * 4. Summoned Plaguebearer
               * 5. Summoned Blue Horror
               * 6. Chaos Spawn
               * Infernal Power (10 credits). No Range, Self, End of Turn, Injury rolls against the caster and each friendly HERETIC ASTARTES or DAEMON model within 3” of it have +1 INJURY DICE with all ranged attacks.
               * Mutated Invigoration (10 credits). 18”, Self or One Ally, Target Activation, the target can reroll any one Success Roll it makes before the power ends.
               * Possession (12 credits). No Range, Self, Next Activation, the caster has +1 INJURY DICE with melee attacks, and if it takes an enemy ELITE model Out of Action with a melee attack, you can place a Chaos Spawn Mercenary on the table under your control where that enemy was located. The Chaos Spawn does not count towards Morale or join your Warband, and the original model is treated normally for the post-game sequence. You can have only up to one Chaos Spawn on the table from this or any similar effect at a time.
               * Sacrifice (3 credits). 12”, Two between Self and Allies, Immediate, the first target suffers 1 BLOOD MARKER, then remove D3 BLOOD MARKERS from the second target.
________________


Warband Variant: World Eaters  
               * Martial Power. Your Warband cannot include any Chaos Sorcerers, Warpsmiths, Havocs, or Raptors.
               * Khorne Disciples. All Chaos Lords, Dark Apostles, Cultists, Chaos Space Marines, Possessed, Chaos Terminators, and Helbrutes in your Warband automatically have a Mark of Khorne. Their cost is increased by 10 credits each.
               * Sealed Pact. You cannot use the Dark Pacts special rule. Your patron must be the Khorne Shared Patron.
               * The Skull Harvest. Instead of the Dread Reputation special rule, during a campaign, you must keep track of each of your Elite model’s Claimed Skulls. An Elite model gains Claimed Skulls whenever it takes an enemy model Out of Action in melee: 4 for the enemy leader, 3 for any other ELITE model, 2 for any other TOUGH model, and 1 for any other model. When a non-Elite model takes an enemy Out of Action in melee, the nearest of your Elite models to it gains the Claimed Skulls.


At any time during the quartermaster step between battles, one or more of your models can offer their collected skulls to Khorne. When they do, roll a number of D6 depending on how much XP it has, and reduce its total Claimed Skulls by that amount (minimum 0).
               * 0-3 XP: 1D6
               * 4-9 XP: 2D6
               * 10+ XP: 3D6
        
After this roll, if the model has 0 Claimed Skulls, its offering is deemed unworthy and there is no benefit. If it still has Claimed Skulls, it gains a benefit depending on how many, and then its Claimed Skulls are reset to 0.
               * 1-10 Claimed Skulls: +1 XP
               * 11-20 Claimed Skulls: +2 XP and you gain 1 Glory
               * 21+ Claimed Skulls: +3 XP and you gain 2 Glory
               * Butcher’s Nails. Your Chaos Lord, Chaos Cultist (Jakhal or Goremonger), Chaos Space Marines, and Chaos Terminator models gain the Blood Surge ability, but cannot be equipped with any ranged weapons besides Pistols, THROWN weapons, and Blood Harpoons.
               * Blood Surge. When this model Charges, roll 2D6 instead of 1D6 and then add the highest of the two dice to their charge move.
               * Berzerkers. Up to 2 of your Chaos Space Marines, or 3 in a Warband of 1,200 credits or more, can be upgraded to Berzerkers for +5 credits each, allowing them to ignore the HEAVY Keyword of one melee weapon they carry.
               * Eightbound. Your Possessed cost +10 credits and are known as Eightbound. Replace their Mutated Claws with Mutated Chainblades, and they have the Beacon of Rage ability.
               * Mutated Chainblades. Melee, CRITICAL, RISKY, SHRAPNEL
               * Beacon of Rage. The Eightbound and each of its allies within 3” of it have +1 DICE to Hit any enemy that has 1 or more BLOOD MARKERS.
               * Jakhals. Your Chaos Cultists are known as Jakhals. They have +1” movement speed, +1 Melee Skill (for a total of +0), can use Chain Blades and Chain Glaives, and cost +10 credits. Up to half of them, rounded up, can be upgraded to Goremongers for an additional +5 credits, granting them the INFILTRATOR and SKIRMISHER Keywords.
               * Master of Executions. You can take up to one Master of Executions, described below, as an Elite in your Warband.
               * Slaughterbound. You can take up to one Slaughterbound, described below, as an Elite in your Warband.
               * World Eaters Battlekit. You can purchase up to two Eviscerators at their normal price, up to two Chain Fists for 30 credits each, and add the following additional battlekit to your Armoury:
               * Axe of Dismemberment (35 credits, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only). Melee, +2 INJURY DICE, IGNORE ARMOUR of Down enemies, HEAVY, TWO-HANDED
               * Blood Harpoon (12 credits, LIMIT: 1, Jakhal Only). Melee, ASSAULT, CRITICAL, WHIP 18”
               * Twin Chain Blades (15 credits). Melee, +1 DICE, CRITICAL, RISKY, SHRAPNEL, TWO-HANDED, CUMBERSOME
               * Campaign Shop. Beside Talisman of Burning Blood, you cannot take battlekit from the Heretic Astartes Campaign Shop, but the following Campaign Shop battlekit is available to you:
               * Blood-Soaked Mantle (8 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Equipment). When the bearer takes an enemy model Out of Action in melee, it gains a stacking +1 INJURY DICE to further Injury rolls that it makes in melee for the remainder of the battle.
               * Bloodstep Blade (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Melee Weapon). Melee, +1 INJURY DICE, CRITICAL, MAIN HAND ONLY. The equipped model gains the DEEP STRIKE Keyword. 
               * Gorefather (5 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Melee Weapon). Melee, -1 DICE, +2 INJURY DICE, CRITICAL, HEAVY, RISKY, SHRAPNEL, TWO-HANDED
               * Helm of Brazen Ire (5 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Equipment, Headgear). Injury rolls made against the wearer have -1 INJURY DICE.
               * Infernal Infusion (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Equipment). Once during each battle, you can add 3” to the equipped model’s movement speed for the duration of its Activation.
               * Mighty Skullrack (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Equipment). Friendly Non-Elite models within 6” of the bearer have VICIOUS 11 with melee attacks. The first time the bearer takes an enemy Out of Action in melee during a battle, the range of this ability increases by 9” until the end of the battle.
               * Skullseeker (5 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Ranged Weapon). 12”, VICIOUS 11, -DICE cannot be applied to the Injury roll, IGNORE ARMOUR, CRITICAL, PISTOL, Scope Capable
               * Talisman of Rage (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Equipment). The bearer has +1 INJURY DICE when it scores a Critical Hit in melee, stacking with the normal benefit of a Critical Hit and the CRITICAL Keyword.
               * Witchskull Totem (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Equipment). Injury rolls with the PSYCHIC or IGNORE ARMOUR Keyword have -1 INJURY DICE against the bearer.  In addition, it can Deny the Witch as if it had PSYKER 1.
               * Daemonkin. Optionally, when you create your Warband you can choose to make it a Daemonkin Warband. If you do, the following additional rules apply:
               * You cannot take any Chaos Cultists or Helbrutes, and you cannot recruit any Summoned Minor Daemon or Summoned Daemon Mercenaries.
               * You can take a maximum of 4 Chaos Space Marines.
               * You can recruit Legion of Blood Troop models from the Chaos Daemons faction.

Battlekit
A Master of Executions is always equipped with a Mark of Khorne and Power Armour or Terminator Armour, included in its cost. It can be equipped with any other pistols, THROWN weapons, melee weapons, armour, or equipment from the Heretic Astartes Armoury.


Abilities
               * A Worthy Skull. Whenever the Master of Executions hits an ELITE or TOUGH enemy in melee, it costs 1 fewer BLOOD MARKER to Bloodbath that enemy.
               * Black Carapace. The Master of Executions treats any Down result from the Injury Roll Table as a Minor Hit instead. This does not apply to Down results that already replaced another result, such as with TOUGH.
               * Blood Surge. When this model Charges, roll 2D6 instead of 1D6 and then add the highest of the two dice to their charge move.

*Thousand Sons
Thousand Sons
Thousand Sons is a Heretic Astartes Warband Variant. Don’t worry, they have extensive modifications that make them feel like their own Faction!

Traitor Guard

The Vermintide
The Vermintide
  

Vermintide Special Rules
Warp Tunnels. Your SKAVEN models have the BURROW Keyword.


Conniving Power Struggle. During a campaign, keep track of the Conniving Points of each of your Elite models, beginning at 10 for your Warband leader and 0 for each other Elite, including when a new Elite is recruited or a model is promoted to Elite.


Your Elite models gain Conniving Points under the following circumstances:
               * Crush-Win. Your Warband leader gains D3 Conniving Points whenever you win a battle, or 1 if you tie.
               * Think-Plan. Each model besides your Warband leader automatically gains D3 Conniving Points at the end of each battle, or 3 if you lost the battle.
               * Look-Look. An Elite model gains 1 Conniving Point each time the model performs a Glorious Deed.
               * Kill-Kill Them. An Elite model gains 1 Conniving Point each time the model takes an enemy Elite model Out of Action.
               * Not Me. Whenever one of your Elite models is taken Out of Action, each of your other Elite models within 12” of it gains 1 Conniving Point.


Masterstroke
At the end of each battle, following the exploration step, if your Warband leader has 20 or more Conniving Points and none of your other Elites have more Conniving Points than it, its plans to suppress its allies come to fruition in a masterstroke. You earn 2 Glory and your Warband leader earns 2 additional XP. Then reset your Warband leader to Conniving Points equal to 10 minus 2 for every time it has achieved a masterstroke since it last became your Warband leader, and the rest of your Elites’ Conniving Points to 0.


Power Upset
At the end of each battle, following the exploration step, if any of your Elites besides your warband leader has more Conniving Points than your Warband Leader, that model attempts to take greater control of your Warband in an upset. If two or more of your Elites meet this requirement, only the one with the highest Conniving Points does this. Choose randomly in case of a tie. When this happens, roll a D6 to determine what happens:
               * 1: Banishment. The upsetting Elite model is removed from your Warband.
               * 2: Beaten Down. The upsetting Elite model must roll on the Elite Trauma table.
               * 3-4: Grudging Respect. You earn 1 Glory and the upsetting Elite model earns 1 additional XP.
               * 5-6: Ascension. The upsetting Elite model becomes your new Warband leader. Your previous leader loses the LEADER Keyword and the upsetting Elite model gains it. You earn 2 Glory and the chosen Elite model earns 2 additional XP.


Regardless of the result, reset your Warband leader to 10 Conniving Points, and the rest of your Elites to 0.
Vermintide Patrons
Your patron must be The Great Horned Rat.
Patron: The Great Horned Rat
               * Choking Fumes. Enemies within 1” of this model that do not have NEGATE GAS have -1 DICE to all Success Rolls.
               * Mindless And Rabid. This model gains FEAR.
               * Obscenely Good Fortune. This model begins each battle with D3 BLESSING MARKERS.
               * Scurrying Menace. This model gains the SKIRMISHER Keyword if it does not already have it, and it can move an additional 2” when it uses SKIRMISHER.
               * Truly Ravenous. Once during each of its Activations, when this model inflicts 1 or more BLOOD MARKERS on an enemy with a melee attack, remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from this model.
               * Vermindoom Vanguard. This model has +1 INJURY DICE with its melee attacks.

Vermintide Campaign Shop
During a campaign, these pieces of battlekit are available only when your Warband is specifically allowed to purchase them.
               * Brass Orb (8 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Ranged Weapon). 8”, ASSAULT, IGNORE COVER, IGNORE LONG RANGE, PSYCHIC, THROWN, FUMBLE, can target an ally. Does not cause any injury. Instead, on a hit, the target is removed from the battlefield (it is not taken Out of Action; it counts as Down for Morale purposes). Place a Tear marker at its location. It remains in its current state (Down, markers, etc.). At the start of the next Turn, or the end of the game, whichever comes first, the owner of that model sets it back up anywhere within 3” of the Tear marker, then remove the Tear marker. Once this attack hits, it cannot be used against this battle.
               * Farskitter Cloak (5 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Non-Deathmaster Elite Only, Equipment). As an Action with a Risky Success Roll with +1 DICE, the equipped model can attempt to teleport. On a success, you can pick up the equipped model and deploy it on the battlefield anywhere within 6" of the location it originally occupied (measured from the centre of its base to the centre of its base). If this is impossible for any reason, the equipped model must stay in its original position.
               * Foulhide (6 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Equipment). The equipped model has the TOUGH Keyword.
               * Gnawbomb (4 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Ranged Weapon). 8”, ASSAULT, BLAST 3”, IGNORE COVER, IGNORE LONG RANGE, IGNORE ARMOUR, PSYCHIC, SCATTER, STUN, THROWN, place a Gnawhole marker as close as possible to the final target point. Your models with DEEP STRIKE can be deployed anywhere within 3” of the Gnawhole marker when using that Keyword, regardless of enemies, and they are not adjusted by D3” when doing so. This weapon can be used only once during each battle.
               * Lash of Fangs (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Melee Weapon). Melee, ARMOUR PIERCING 1, ASSAULT, CRITICAL, SHRAPNEL, VICIOUS 11, WHIP 3”, MAIN HAND ONLY
               * Rabid Crown (1 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Equipment, Headgear). Friendly BEAST models within 6” of the wielder have +1 DICE to Hit with all melee attacks.
               * Rustcursed Armour (6 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Armour). -2 to Injury rolls, IMPERVIOUS
               * Shadow Magnet Trinket (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Equipment). Ranged attacks have -1 DICE to Hit the equipped model.
               * Shield of Distraction (4 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Armour). Counts as a Shield, including for Shield Combo, -1 INJURY MODIFIER, HELD. Enemies cannot target the equipped model with melee attacks if there is another legal target for that attack, and enemies cannot declare a Charge against the equipped model if there is another legal target of that Charge within 3” of the equipped model.
               * Staff of Rightful Supremacy (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Psyker Only, Melee Weapon). Melee, PSYCHIC, +1 INJURY DICE against DAEMON and PSYKER models. HELD. The equipped model has +2 DICE to Deny the Witch and enemies have -1 DICE to Deny the Witch against it.

	25-32mm
	

Battlekit
A Clawlord can be equipped with any weapons, armour, or equipment, or from the Vermintide Armoury.


Abilities
               * Lead From the Back. While it is within 3” of a friendly SKAVEN model, if that model is a valid target for such an attack, the Clawlord cannot be chosen as the target of any ranged attack.
               * Scurry Away. Enemies do not get to make a free attack when the Clawlord Retreats.


Keywords
ELITE, LEADER, MASTER, SKAVEN, TOUGH
0-1 Deathmaster (Cost: 85 credits)
Name
	Movement
	Ranged
	Melee
	Armour
	Base
	Deathmaster
	6”/Infantry
	+1
	+2
	0
	25-32mm
	

Battlekit
A Deathmaster can be equipped with any weapons, armour, or equipment, or from the Vermintide Armoury.


Abilities
               * Running Death. The Deathmaster has +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls.
               * Tunneljack. As an Action with a Risky Success Roll with +1 DICE, the Deathmaster can attempt to teleport. On a success, you can pick up the Deathmaster and deploy it on the battlefield anywhere within 6" of the location it originally occupied (measured from the centre of its base to the centre of its base). If this is impossible for any reason, the Deathmaster must stay in its original position. Note that the Deathmaster can redeploy even if it is down or within 1" of an enemy model (in which case the enemy model cannot make a Melee Attack before the Deathmaster is redeployed), and can deploy within 1" of an enemy model. In addition, if the Deathmaster is within 1" of any enemy models that are not LARGE, then the Deathmaster can take 1 of the enemy models with them. If they do so, you must deploy the Deathmaster first and then deploy the enemy model within 1" of the Deathmaster. If you cannot deploy the enemy model within 1" of the Deathmaster, then the enemy model stays in its original position.
               * Scurry Away. Enemies do not get to make a free attack when the Deathmaster Retreats.


Keywords
ELITE, INFILTRATOR, MASTER, SKAVEN, STEALTH

Battlekit

The Warlock may purchase up to three powers from the Ruin Psychic Discipline, and must purchase at least one.

               * Arch-Warlock. You can upgrade the Warlock to an Arch-Warlock at a cost of +10 credits, allowing it to purchase up to five powers, and granting it the PSYKER 2 Keyword instead of PSYKER 1.


Abilities
               * Scurry Away. Enemies do not get to make a free attack when the Warlock Retreats.

Abilities
               * Cowardice. The Charge Action is always a Risky Success Roll for the Skavenslave unless a friendly MASTER SKAVEN model is within 6” of the Skavenslave.
               * Many. When you activate a Skavenslave, activate another unactivated Skavenslave, treating them as a Fireteam, if one is available. The Skavenslave counts for only half of a model for the purpose of Field Strength and Morale. Round up after counting all Skavenslaves.
               * Scurry Away. Enemies do not get to make a free attack when the Skavenslave Retreats.

Clanrat (Cost: 35 credits)
Name

	Melee
	Armour

	6”/Infantry
	+0
	+0
	0

Battlekit
A Clanrat can be equipped with any weapons, armour, or equipment from the Vermintide Armoury.


Abilities
               * Cowardice. The Charge Action is always a Risky Success Roll for the Clanrat unless another friendly MASTER SKAVEN model is within 6” of the Clanrat.
               * Scurry Away. Enemies do not get to make a free attack when the Clanrat Retreats.
               * Strength in Numbers. The Clanrat has +1 DICE to Hit with melee attacks made against any enemy that has at least 2 of the Clanrat’s SKAVEN allies within 1” of it. In addition, it ignores its Cowardice Ability if the target of its Charge is already in melee with at least 1 of the Clanrat’s SKAVEN allies.


Upgrades
               * Night Runner. Up to 2 Clanrats, or 3 in a Warband worth 1,200 or more credits, can be upgraded to Night Runners at a cost of +15 credits each, granting them the INFILTRATOR and STEALTH Keywords, and the Slinking Advance Ability.
               * Slinking Advance. The Night Runner can Charge an enemy it cannot see, and when it does so, it ignores its Cowardice Ability.
               * Packmaster. Up to 1 Clanrat can be upgraded to a Packmaster at a cost of +5 credits, granting it the MASTER Keyword.
               * Plague Monk. Up to 2 Clanrats, or 3 in a Warband worth 1,200 or more credits, can be upgraded to Plague Monks at a cost of +10 credits each, granting them the Frenzied Assault Ability.
               * Frenzied Assault. The Plague Monk ignores its Cowardice Ability, and it rolls 2D6 instead of 1D6 when Charging, taking the highest result.
               * Globodier. Up to 2 Clanrats, or 3 in a Warband worth 1,200 or more credits, can be upgraded to Globodiers at a cost of +10 credits each, granting them the Lobber Ability.
               * Lobber. Increase the range of any THROWN weapon the Globodier is equipped with by 4”. It has +1 DICE to Hit with any such weapon, but attacks made beyond the weapon’s normal range, before this benefit, are made at -1 DICE.


Keywords
NO PROMOTION, SKAVEN, SKIRMISHER

Warband Variant: Plague Vermin  
               * Rats of Nurgle. All of your non-mercenary models gain the NURGLE Keyword. Your Patron must be the Nurgle Shared Patron.
               * Priesthood of Rot. You cannot recruit a Clawlord or Warlock. Instead, you can recruit up to 2 Plague Priest mercenaries, which count as Elites in your Warband instead of mercenaries. You must recruit at least 1 as your Warband leader, which gains the LEADER and TOUGH Keywords.


Plague Priests cost you 60 credits each, but are not automatically equipped with any battlekit and do not automatically know any psychic powers. They can be equipped with any weapons, armour, or equipment from the Vermintide Armoury, but must be equipped with at least one PSYCHIC weapon. They can use Plague Monk Only battlekit. They may purchase up to four psychic powers from the Ruin Psychic Discipline and/or the Contagion Discipline found in the Death Guard Warband Variant of the Heretic Astartes Faction, and must purchase at least one.
               * Plague Monasteries. You cannot upgrade any Clanrats to Nightrunners or Packmasters, but you can upgrade any number to Plague Monks, and your Storm Vermin can use Plague Monk Only battlekit.
               * Plague Spreaders. Instead of the Warp Tunnels special rule, when one of your models makes an attack with the GAS Keyword, you can choose to replace that Keyword with INFECTION MARKERS during that attack.
               * Plague Vermin Battlekit. You cannot take any Flamers, Hand Flamers, or Heavy Flamers, but add the following battlekit to your Armoury:
               * Hand Plague Spewer (10 credits, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only). 6”, FLAMETHROWER, IGNORE ARMOUR, -1 INJURY DICE, GAS
               * Light Plague Censer (7 credits, Plague Priest Only). Melee, GAS, -1 INJURY DICE. Enemies within 1” of the wielder that do not have NEGATE GAS have -1 DICE to all Success Rolls (does not stack).
               * Heavy Plague Spewer (45 credits, LIMIT: 1). 10”, -1 INJURY DICE, FLAMETHROWER, IGNORE ARMOUR, AUTOMATIC 2, GAS, HEAVY, TWO-HANDED
               * Plague Spewer (30 credits, LIMIT: 2). 8”, FLAMETHROWER, IGNORE ARMOUR, GAS, TWO-HANDED
*World Eaters
World Eaters
World Eaters is a Heretic Astartes Warband Variant. Don’t worry, they have extensive modifications that make them feel like their own Faction!
Xenos Factions
Xenos Factions
Aeldari
Aeldari
  

Aeldari Special Rules
Battle Focus. At the start of each Turn, for every 300 credits in your Warband’s credit limit, you gain 1 Focus Point. You can spend these points on any of the following effects. At the end of the Turn, all unspent Focus Points are lost.
               * Fall Back. When one of your AELDARI models Retreats, you can prevent enemies from making free attacks against it.
               * Flitting Shadows. Before a ranged attack is made against one of your AELDARI models, you can give it STEALTH until the end of that Activation.
               * Opportunity Seized. After an enemy Retreats from one of your AELDARI models, that AELDARI model can immediately move up to 3” if it is not in close combat with any enemies. This movement cannot bring it within 1” of any enemy.
               * Star Engines. When one of your AELDARI VEHICLE models moves, you can increase its movement speed by 3” until the end of that movement.
               * Sudden Strike. When one of your AELDARI models Charges, you can reroll the charge die.
               * Swift as the Wind. You can add +1 DICE to any Dash Success Roll one of your AELDARI models makes.


Guiding Fate. During a campaign, when you create your Warband and after each of your Threads of Fate have ended, successfully or not, generate 3 Threads of Fate by rolling on the following table (rerolling all duplicates), and keep track of your Threat, beginning at 1 (or resetting to 1).
               1. Cleanse The Corruption
               2. Destined Device
               3. Enemy Of My Enemy
               4. Humble Beginnings
               5. Isha’s Bounty
               6. Rising Nemesis


Each Thread of Fate has three stages: Dawning, Waning, and Frayed. Each begins at Dawning, and advances to the next stage every time it escalates. If a Frayed Thread of Fate escalates, it ends in failure instead.


At the start of any battle, you can choose one of your Threads of Fate that has not ended. That Thread of Fate is active during that battle, and if you complete all of the requirements of its current stage by the end of the battle, it is ended successfully and you can lower your Threat by an amount based on the current stage of that Thread of Fate: 3 for Dawning, 2 for Waning, 1 for Frayed, to a minimum of 0. If you do not complete the requirements, that Thread of Fate escalates.


At the end of each battle, after lowering Threat if you successfully ended a Thread of Fate, increase your Threat by D3, or by 3 if you lost the battle. If your Threat ever reaches 13 or higher, all Threads of Fate automatically end in failure, even if you ended any of them successfully before.


Once all of your Threads of Fate have ended, successfully or in failure, you gain rewards and/or penalties based on your current Threat.
               * 0-4 Threat: Brief Respite
               * You gain 3 Glory
               * Each of your Elite models earns 1 additional XP
               * One of your Elite models earns the Patron Skill of your choice, separate from any skills earned via XP.
               * 5-8 Thread: Watchful
               * You gain 2 Glory
               * Each of your Elite models earns 1 additional XP
               * 9-12 Threat: Beset
               * You gain 1 Glory
               * One of your Elite models earns 1 additional XP
               * 13+ Threat: Endure the Inevitable
               * Each of your Elite models must roll on the Elite Trauma table.
Cleanse The Corruption
Dawning Requirements: Whispers of Warning
               * At the start of the battle, choose one of your non-Elite AELDARI models. That model is set up anywhere within 6” of the center of the battlefield. It cannot contribute to any rule that earns VP during that mission except by surviving or taking enemies Out of Action. At the end of the battle, if that model is in your deployment zone, you successfully end this Thread of Fate.
Waning Requirements: Follow the Trail
               * At the start of the battle, your opponent must place two 1” Trail markers on the battlefield, each within 6” of the center of the battlefield, and exactly 9” apart. At the end of each Turn, if you have more models within 3” of a marker than your opponent, roll a D6. On a 5+, remove that marker from the battlefield. If both markers are removed, you successfully end this Thread of Fate.
Frayed Requirements: Into Peril
               * At the end of the battle, if at least 3 of your AELDARI models are wholly within your opponent’s deployment zone, and at least one of the models is Elite, you successfully end this Thread of Fate.
Destined Device
Dawning Requirements: Buried Provenance
               * At the start of the battle, your opponent places a 1” Device marker on the battlefield, exactly 6” away from your deployment zone. At the end of the battle, if your opponent has more models within 3” of the Device marker than you, they earn 1 Glory. If you have more models than your opponent within 3” of the Device marker at the end of the battle, you successfully end this Thread of Fate.
Waning Requirements: Unknowing Bearer
               * At the start of the battle, after deployment, your opponent chooses three of their models that are on the battlefield, then randomly selects one of them. That model is carrying the device. If the model carrying the device is taken Out of Action, you successfully end this Thread of Fate
Frayed Requirements: Abandoned
               * If you have more models within 3” of the center of the battlefield than your opponent at the end of the battle, roll a D6. On a 5+, you successfully end this Thread of Fate.
Enemy Of My Enemy
Dawning Requirements: Unknowing Ally
               * At the start of the battle, after deployment, your opponent chooses three of their models that are on the battlefield, then randomly selects one of them. If that model was not taken Out of Action by the end of the mission, you successfully end this Thread of Fate.
Waning Requirements: Subtle Persuasions
               * At the start of the battle, after deployment, your opponent chooses three of their models that are on the battlefield. As an Action with a Success Roll, any of your AELDARI PSYKERS within 9” of one of those models can attempt to influence it. On a success, that model is influenced. If all three are influenced, you successfully end this Thread of Fate.
Frayed Requirements: Gift of Life
               * At the start of the battle, place a 1” Injured Warrior marker anywhere in your deployment zone. At the end of each Turn, if you do not have a model within 3” of that marker, remove it. At the end of the battle, if the marker is still on the battlefield, you successfully end this Thread of Fate.
Humble Beginnings
Dawning Requirements: Destined for Greatness
               * At the start of the battle, choose one of your non-Elite AELDARI models, which must be a Guardian if possible. If your opponent takes that model Out of Action during the battle, they earn 1 Glory. If that model was not taken Out of Action by the end of the mission, you successfully end this Thread of Fate.
Waning Requirements: Identity Blurs
               * At the start of the battle, choose two of your non-Elite AELDARI models, which must be Guardians if possible. If your opponent takes both of those models Out of Action during the battle, they earn 1 Glory. If both models were not taken Out of Action by the end of the mission, you successfully end this Thread of Fate. If only one was taken Out of Action, roll a D6 and on a 4+, you successfully end this Thread of Fate.
Frayed Requirements: Fallen Hero
               * At the start of the battle, choose one of your AELDARI PSYKER models. As an Action with a Success Roll, while that model is within 6” of the center of the battlefield, that model can attempt to claim the fallen hero’s spirit stone. On a success, you successfully end this Thread of Fate.
Isha’s Bounty
Dawning Requirements: Author of Destruction
               * At the start of the battle, after deployment, your opponent chooses one of their models that is on the battlefield, which must be TOUGH if possible. If that model is taken Out of Action before the end of the third Turn, you successfully end this Thread of Fate.
Waning Requirements: World Spirit
               * At the start of the battle, place six 1” World Spirit markers on the battlefield, each 9” away from the center of the battlefield, and equidistant from each other. At the end of the battle, if you have more models within 3” of a World Spirit marker than your opponent, you control that marker. If you control at least three of the markers, you successfully end this Thread of Fate.
Frayed Requirements: Precious Remnants
               * At the end of the battle, if one of your Elite models is wholly within your opponent’s deployment zone, one of your Elite models is wholly within your own deployment zone, and one of your Elite models is anywhere else on the battlefield, you successfully end this Thread of Fate.
Rising Nemesis
Dawning Requirements: Sow Dissent
               * If your opponent loses the battle due to a failed Morale test, you successfully end this Thread of Fate.
Waning Requirements: Sever the Head
               * At the start of the battle, secretly choose three of your opponent’s models. You must choose Elite models if possible, and one of them must be their Warband leader if possible. If you take all three of these models Out of Action, you successfully end this Thread of Fate.
Frayed Requirements: Seal the Webway
               * At the start of the battle, your opponent must place two 1” Webway markers on the battlefield, each within 12” of the center of the battlefield, and at least 6” apart. As an Action with a Success Roll, any of your models within 8” of one of these markers can attempt to seal it. On a success, it becomes Partially Sealed, or it becomes Sealed and is removed from the battlefield if it was already Partially Sealed. If you Seal both Webway markers, you successfully end this Thread of Fate.
Aeldari Patrons
Your patron can be an Autarch Council, a Phoenix Lord, or a Seer Council.
Patron: Autarch Council
               * Ambush of Blades. During an Activation in which it Charged, this model’s melee attacks have ARMOUR PIERCING 1, stacking with other sources of ARMOUR PIERCING, and if such an attack scores a Critical Hit, the injury roll has +1 INJURY DICE, in addition to the normal bonus for a Critical Hit and the CRITICAL Keyword.
               * Enduring Resolve. This model can Deny the Witch as if it had PSYKER 2. If it is already a PSYKER, it instead has +1 DICE when Denying the Witch.
               * Falcon’s Swiftness. This model’s movement speed is increased by 2”.
               * Mark of the Incomparable Hunter. This model has +1 INJURY DICE against enemy Elite models.
               * Master of Ambush. At the start of each battle, after deployment, you may redeploy this model and up to 2 of your other non-INFILTRATOR models, or 1 INFILTRATOR model.
               * To Their Final Breath. Enemies cannot Retreat from this model.
Patron: Phoenix Lord
When you select this Patron, choose one of the following: Dark Reaper, Dire Avenger, Fire Dragon, Howling Banshee, Shining Spear, Striking Scorpion, Swooping Hawk, Warp Spider.
               * Aspect Armoury. While this model is in your Warband, increase the LIMIT of one piece of battlekit you are allowed to take by 1, so long as it is limited to the Aspect of your choosing or Elite or that Aspect. Each piece of battlekit can be chosen only once.
               * Aspect Mastery (LIMIT: 1). While this model is in your Warband, you can recruit one additional Aspect Warrior of the Aspect you chose for this Patron.
               * Combat Specialization. This model gains your choice of either +1 Ranged Skill or +1 Melee Skill.
Dark Reaper
               * Doom Incarnate. Whenever this model takes an enemy Down or Out of Action, your opponent has -1 DICE to Morale tests at the end of that Turn.
               * Dark Reaper Training (Non-Vehicle Non-Aspect Warrior Only). This model can equip Dark Reaper Only battlekit and gains the Inescapable Accuracy and Reaper Arsenal Abilities of the Dark Reaper Aspect Warrior.
               * Harvester of Souls (Dark Reaper or Dark Reaper Training Only). This model and each ally within 6” of it has CRITICAL with all attacks.
Dire Avenger
               * Dire Avenger Training (Non-Vehicle Non-Aspect Warrior Only). This model can equip Dire Avenger Only battlekit and gains the Bladestorm Ability of the Dire Avenger Aspect Warrior.
               * Hand of Asuryan (Dire Avenger or Dire Avenger Training Only). Once during each battle, this model can choose to score a Critical Hit instead of rolling to Hit with a ranged attack, so long as that attack is not being rolled with -DICE (i.e. it does not have more -DICE than +DICE).
               * Shield of Grace. While this model is equipped with two non-HEAVY melee weapons, all attacks have -1 DICE to Hit it.
Fire Dragon
               * Burning Lance (Fire Dragon or Fire Dragon Training Only). Increase the range of this model’s TWO-HANDED ranged weapons by 6”, and the range of all of its other ranged weapons by 3”.
               * Fire Dragon Training (Non-Vehicle Non-Aspect Warrior Only). This model can equip Fire Dragon Only battlekit and gains the Assured Destruction Ability of the Fire Dragon Aspect Warrior.
               * Unquenchable Resolve. BLOOD MARKERS cannot be spent on the Success Rolls of attacks this model makes, and it has +1 DICE to Hit with all attacks if it has 3 or more BLOOD MARKERS.
Howling Banshee
               * Howling Banshee Training (Non-Vehicle Non-Aspect Warrior Only). This model can equip Howling Banshee Only battlekit and gains the Acrobatic Ability of the Howling Banshee Aspect Warrior.
               * Storm of Silence (Howling Banshee or Howling Banshee Training Only). This model is always treated as having rolled the maximum result when determining Charge distance.
               * Whirling Death. This model’s melee attacks have SWEEPING.
Shining Spear
               * Celestial Hunter. This model has +1 DICE to Hit and +1 INJURY DICE on all attacks it makes against a model with TOUGH.
               * Seventh Sky Strike (Shining Spear or Shining Spear Training Only). While equipped with a Jetbike, when this model Charges one or more enemies, one of those enemies must roll on the Injury chart.
               * Shining Spear Training (Non-Vehicle Non-Aspect Warrior Only). This model can equip Shining Spear Only battlekit and gains the Aerobatic Grace Ability of the Shining Spear Aspect Warrior while equipped with a Jetbike.
Striking Scorpion
               * Death by a Thousand Stings. When this model scores a Critical Hit with a melee weapon, it can choose to suffer -1 INJURY DICE for the injury roll. If the target is not taken Out of Action by that attack, this model can make another attack with the same weapon against that enemy. This can be done only once during each Activation.
               * Shadow Hunter (Striking Scorpion or Striking Scorpion Training Only). While this model is in cover from such an attack, enemies cannot make attacks against this model at Long Range.
               * Striking Scorpion Training (Non-Vehicle Non-Aspect Warrior Only). This model can equip Striking Scorpion Only battlekit and gains the Shadow Strike Ability of the Striking Scorpion Aspect Warrior.
Swooping Hawk
               * Cloudstrider. This model gains +1” movement speed and the SKIRMISHER Keyword.
               * Cry of the Wind (Swooping Hawk or Swooping Hawk Training Only). This model gains DEEP STRIKE while equipped with Swooping Hawk Wings.
               * Swooping Hawk Training (Non-Vehicle Non-Aspect Warrior Only). This model can equip Swooping Hawk Only battlekit and gains the Flyover Ability of the Swooping Hawk Aspect Warrior while equipped with Swooping Hawk Wings.
Warp Spider
               * Empyric Ambush (Warp Spider or Warp Spider Training Only). After this model uses its Flickerjump Ability, it has +1 DICE and +1 INJURY DICE with melee attacks until the end of its Activation.
               * Warp Spider Training (Non-Vehicle Non-Aspect Warrior Only). This model can equip Warp Spider Only Equipment and gains the Flickerjump Ability of the Warp Spider Aspect Warrior.
               * Whispering Web. This model’s other allies have VICIOUS 9 with all attacks made against enemies that this model has hit with a ranged attack during the same Turn.
Patron: Seer Council
               * An Eye on Distant Events. This model’s ranged attacks have IGNORE LONG RANGE and it gains the STEALTH Keyword.
               * Fate’s Messenger. This model treats any Down result from the Injury Roll Table as a Minor Hit instead. This does not apply to Down results that already replaced another result, such as with TOUGH.
               * Fate Reader. As an Action with a Success Roll, this model can read fate. On a success, it gains a BLESSING MARKER.
               * Forewarned. Enemy models must be set up at least 16” away from this model when using the DEEP STRIKE or INFILTRATOR Keywords.
               * Presentiment of Dread. This model gains the FEAR Keyword.
               * Seer of the Shifting Vector. Once during each battle, you can reroll one Success Roll this model makes, or an injury roll made against it.
