Aeldari Equipment
               * Aeldari Helmet (5 credits). The model has NEGATE SHRAPNEL. Counts as Headgear.
               * Banshee Mask (10 credits, Autarch or Howling Banshee Only). The wearer gains the FEAR Keyword. Counts as Headgear.
               * Blood of Isha (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only). The model gains REGENERATE 1.
               * Cloak of Shadow (10 credits, Warlock Only). The wearer gains the STEALTH Keyword.
               * Flip Belt (10 credits, LIMIT: 2). The wearer automatically succeeds on Climb and jump Success Rolls, and does not suffer Injury rolls due to falling. It also has +1 DICE to Diving Charge Success Rolls and does not fall Down or suffer an Injury roll due to failing a Diving Charge.
               * Ghosthelm (5 credits, Psyker Only). When the wearer is affected by Perils of the Warp, before rolling to determine the effect, roll a D6. On a 4+, the Perils of the Warp do not occur. Counts as Headgear.
               * Guardian Platform (20 credits, non-Ranger Guardian Only, Limit: 1). The bearer of this platform gains the LARGE and TOUGH Keywords. When the model’s TOUGH Keyword is used, the Guardian Platform is removed for the remainder of the battle, causing the model to lose all of its benefits. When you purchase this equipment, choose one:
               * Serpent’s Scale. The bearer and any ally within 3” of it treat Down Injury results as Minor Hits instead. This does not apply to Down results that already replaced another result, such as with TOUGH.
               * Weapon Platform. The Weapon Platform can be equipped with one TWO-HANDED ranged weapon. The equipped model is considered to be equipped with that weapon in addition to any others, and it ignores that weapon’s HEAVY Keyword. When it takes the Shoot Action, it can attack with this weapon in addition to any other attacks it makes.
               * Isitha Kasra (15 credits, LIMIT: 1). HELD. Any friendly models within 4” of the musician who is not Down can add +1 DICE to their Dash Success Rolls.
               * Jetbike (25 credits, Shining Spear Only). The equipped model is mounted on a jetbike. It gains 10” movement, FLYING, and the following abilities:
               * Pilot. The equipped model gains the VEHICLE Keyword and has only one hand for melee weapons, but can hold one two-handed melee weapon with Shield Combo.
               * Weapon Mount. The equipped model can ignore the HEAVY Keyword of one ranged weapon it wields.
               * Jetbike Shimmershield (15 credits, Shining Spear Only). The bearer treats any Down result from the Injury Roll Table as a Minor Hit instead. This does not apply to Down results that already replaced another result, such as with TOUGH.
               * Mandiblasters (7 credits, Striking Scorpion Only). When this model Charges one or more enemies, one of those enemies suffers a BLOOD MARKER.
               * Shimmershield (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only). The bearer treats any Down result from the Injury Roll Table as a Minor Hit instead. This does not apply to Down results that already replaced another result, such as with TOUGH.
               * Spirit Stone (6 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Psyker Only). The first psychic power the equipped model uses during each of its Activations does not count towards the Using Multiple Powers rules.
               * Swooping Hawk Wings (25 credits, Autarch or Swooping Hawk Only). The wearer has a movement speed of 10” and FLYING.
Aeldari Campaign Shop
During a campaign, these pieces of battlekit are available only when your Warband is specifically allowed to purchase them.
               * Aegis Of Eldanesh (8 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Autarch Only, Armour). -2 INJURY MODIFIER, IMPERVIOUS. In addition, all Injury rolls against it have -1 INJURY DICE.
               * Avenging Blade (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Dire Avenger Only, Melee Weapon). Melee, +1 INJURY DICE, IGNORE ARMOUR, MAIN HAND ONLY
               * Banner of Asuryan (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Autarch Only, Equipment). ICON, At the start of each Turn, if the bearer is not Down or Out of Action, you gain 1 additional Focus Point.
               * Cloak Of The Shadewalker (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Equipment). Ranged attacks have -1 DICE to Hit the bearer.
               * Cronescream (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Howling Banshee Only, Equipment). As an Action with a Success Roll, the bearer can unleash a scream at one enemy within 1” of it. On a success, that enemy suffers 1 BLOOD MARKER.
               * Dragon’s Fury (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Fire Dragon Only, Equipment). When an enemy successfully Charges the bearer, that enemy suffers 1 BLOOD MARKER. A model with NEGATE FIRE is immune.
               * Exile Glaive (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite or Ranger Only, Melee Weapon). Melee, +1 INJURY DICE, BLOCK, ARMOUR PIERCING 2, TWO-HANDED, Shield Combo
               * Firesabre (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Melee Weapon). Melee, +1 INJURY DICE, CRITICAL, FIRE
               * Khaine’s Lance (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Shining Spear Only, Equipment). Whenever the equipped model successfully charges one or more enemies, one of them suffers 1 BLOOD MARKER.
               * Phoenix Gem (8 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Equipment). When the bearer would be taken Out of Action, you can instead remove it from the battlefield and it suffers no effect from that Injury (even from Keywords like GAS). At the end of the Turn, place it back on the battlefield as close as possible to its previous position.
               * Phoenix Plume (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Swooping Hawk Only, Equipment). Injury rolls made against the wearer have -1 INJURY DICE.
               * Rune of Faolchú (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Equipment). As a Success Roll, the bearer can choose one enemy it can see within 18”. On a success, attacks made against that enemy have VICIOUS 11 until the end of the current Turn.
               * Shadowsting (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Striking Scorpion Only). Melee, +1 INJURY DICE, CRITICAL, ARMOUR PIERCING 1, RISKY, SHRAPNEL, TWO-HANDED
               * Shard Of Anaris (5 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Melee Weapon). Melee, CLEAVE 3, CRITICAL, MAIN HAND ONLY
               * Shrine Skull (1 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Dark Reaper Only, Equipment). Whenever the bearer takes at least one enemy Out of Action with a ranged attack, your opponent has -1 DICE to Morale tests at the end of the current Turn.
               * Spider’s Bite (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Warp Spider Only, Melee Weapon). Melee, +1 INJURY DICE, CRITICAL, ARMOUR PIERCING 2, TWO-HANDED
               * Sunstorm (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Windrider, Shining Spear, or Skyrunner Only, Equipment). The model has +2” to its movement speed.
               * Weeping Stones (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Equipment). Once per battle, the bearer can reroll any one Success Roll it makes, or one Injury roll made against it.
Aeldari Psychic Disciplines
Runes of Battle Discipline
               * Conceal/Reveal (5 credits, Effect). 18”, One Model, End of Turn, choose one:
               * Conceal: The target gains STEALTH.
               * Reveal: Attacks against the target have +1 DICE to Hit.
               * Embolden/Horrify (7 credits, Effect). 18”, One Model, End of Turn, choose one:
               * Embolden: The target gains NEGATE FEAR and rolls 2d6 instead of 1d6 to determine charge bonus, taking the highest result.
               * Horrify: The target cannot Charge. A target with FEAR or that is otherwise unaffected by FEAR is immune.
               * Enhance/Drain (7 credits, Effect). 18”, One Model, End of Turn, choose one:
               * Enhance: The target has +1 DICE to Hit with all attacks.
               * Drain: The target has -1 DICE to Hit with all attacks.
               * Protect/Jinx (7 credits, Effect). 18”, One Model, End of Turn, choose one:
               * Protect: The target’s Armour improves by -1, to a maximum of -3.
               * Jynx: The target's armour worsens by 1, to a minimum of 0.
               * Quicken/Restrain (10 credits, Effect). 18”, One Model, End of Turn, choose one:
               * Quicken: The target has +3” movement and +1 DICE to all Dash Actions.
               * Restrain: The target has -3” movement and -1 DICE to all Dash Actions.
               * Empower/Enervate (1 Glory, Effect). 18”, One Model, End of Turn, choose one:
               * Empower: The target has +1 INJURY DICE with all attacks.
               * Enervate: The target has -1 INJURY DICE with all attacks.
Runes of Fate Discipline
               * Guide (10 credits, Effect). 18”, One Ally, Immediate, the target can take the Standard Move or Retreat Action. If the Success Roll result for this power was a 10 or higher, the target has +2” movement during that action.
               * Doom (7 credits, Effect). 18”, One Enemy, End of Turn, Injury rolls against the target have +1 INJURY DICE. If the Success Roll result for this power was a 10 or higher, those injuries have +2 INJURY DICE instead.
               * Fortune (2 Glory, Effect). 18”, Self or One Ally, Immediate, the target gains 1 BLESSING MARKER. If the Success Roll result for this power was a 10 or higher, the target gains D3 BLESSING MARKERS instead.
               * Executioner (15 credits, Attack). 18”, +1 INJURY DICE, VICIOUS 10, CRITICAL, PSYCHIC, RISKY. If the target is taken Out of Action by this attack, you can repeat the attack against another target within 3” of it. You can make a maximum of 3 attacks in this way (the original attack and two extras).
               * Will of Asuryan (3 credits, Effect). 18”, Self or One Ally, Target Activation, the chosen model has +1 DICE to all Success Rolls that are not attacks, Dash, or psychic powers. If the Success Roll result for this power was a 10 or higher, you can choose an additional target to be affected.
               * Mind War (10 credits, Attack). 18”, CRITICAL, VICIOUS 10, IGNORE ARMOUR, PSYCHIC, RISKY, -1 INJURY DICE against a model that is unaffected by FEAR.
Runes of Fortune Discipline
               * Fateful Divergence (7 credits, Effect). 18”, Self or One Ally, End of Turn, Injury rolls against the target have -1 INJURY DICE.
               * Witch Strike (7 credits, Effect). Melee, Self, Target Activation, the next melee attack the caster makes causes an additional BLOOD MARKER if it caused any BLOOD MARKERS at all.
               * Ghostwalk (3 credits, Effect). 18”, Self or One Ally, Target Activation, the target has BURROW.
               * Crushing Orb (20 credits, Attack). 18”, AUTOMATIC 3, ARMOUR PIERCING 1, PSYCHIC, RISKY. All attacks must target the same model.
               * Focus Will (2 Glory, Effect). No Range, Self, Target Activation, add +1 DICE to the next PSYCHIC Success Roll the caster makes. This power is not RISKY, and it does not count towards Using Multiple Powers, but it is still subject to Perils of the Warp.
               * Impair Senses (15 credits, Effect). -1 DICE, 18”, One Enemy, End of Turn, the target cannot have line of sight to any model or point further than 6” away from it.

Battlekit
An Autarch can be equipped with any weapons, armour, or equipment from the Aeldari Battlekit list.


Abilities
               * Path of Command. Other friendly AELDARI models within 3” of the Autarch have +1 DICE to Hit with all attacks.
               * Superlative Strategist. When the Autarch’s Activation ends, you can choose any one friendly model that has not yet activated this Turn. That model’s Activation begins.


Keywords
AELDARI, ELITE, LEADER, TOUGH

Battlekit
A Seer can be equipped with any weapons, armour, or equipment from the Aeldari Battlekit list. It must be equipped with exactly one PSYCHIC weapon.


When you recruit the Seer, select two Aeldari Psychic Disciplines. The Seer may purchase up to five powers from those Disciplines, and must purchase at least two.

AELDARI
0-6 Aspect Warrior (Cost: 65 Credits)
Name
	Movement
	Ranged
	Melee
	Armour
	Base
	Aspect Warrior
	7”/Infantry
	+2
	+2
	0
	28-32mm
	

Battlekit
An Aspect Warrior can be equipped with any weapons, armour, or equipment from the Aeldari Battlekit list.


When you recruit the Aspect Warrior, you must choose one of the following Aspects:
               * Dark Reaper (LIMIT: 2). 
               * Inescapable Accuracy. The Dark Reaper ignores penalties to its attack Success Rolls besides Cover, Long Range, and BLOOD MARKERS (including INFECTION and STUN), as well as penalties or limits to the range of its ranged weapons.
               * Reaper Arsenal. The Dark Reaper can ignore the HEAVY Keyword of one ranged weapon it is carrying.
               * Dire Avenger (LIMIT: 2). 
               * Bladestorm. The Dire Avenger has VICIOUS 10 with all attacks.
               * Fire Dragon (LIMIT: 2). 
               * Assured Destruction. When the Fire Dragon makes a ranged attack with a weapon that has IGNORE ARMOUR, if the target would otherwise have an armour modifier from any source other than a shield, you may set one die of the injury roll to a 6 before the roll is made. The rest of the dice for the injury roll are rolled as normal.
               * Howling Banshee (LIMIT: 2). 
               * Acrobatic. Enemy models never get free attacks against the Howling Banshee when it Retreats, and the Howling Banshee can move out of Melee Combat using its Standard Move, Charge, or Dash.
               * Shining Spear (LIMIT: 2).
               * Aerobatic Grace. Ranged attacks made against the Shining Spear have -1 DICE to Hit.
               * Jetbike. The Shining Spear must take the Jetbike Equipment, paying for it normally.
               * Striking Scorpion (LIMIT: 2). 
               * Shadow Strike. The Striking Scorpion gains INFILTRATOR and can declare a Charge against an enemy it cannot see.
               * Swooping Hawk (LIMIT: 2). 
               * Flyover. The Swooping Hawk can ignore enemy models when moving, so long as it ends that movement in a legal location. Once per turn, after it moves through an enemy, it can make a single attack with a THROWN weapon it is wielding, which does not count towards its normal action limits (including number of ASSAULT attacks that can be made along with a Charge/Fight), against that enemy, regardless of range or intervening terrain.
               * Wings. The Swooping Hawk must take the Swooping Hawk Wings Equipment, paying for it normally.
               * Warp Spider (LIMIT: 2). 
               * Flickerjump. As an Action with a Risky Success Roll with +1 DICE, the Warp Spider can attempt to teleport to any location within 6” of it that is not impassable terrain or entirely surrounded by impassable terrain. On a success, remove the Warp Spider from the table, then place it at the chosen location. On a failure, the Warp Spider must roll on the Injury chart.


Keywords
AELDARI

Battlekit
A Windrider is equipped with Mesh Armour, included in its cost and statistics above. It can be equipped with any weapons or equipment from the Aeldari Battlekit list. It has only one hand for melee weapons, but can hold one two-handed melee weapon with Shield Combo.


Abilities
               * Swift Demise. The Windrider has +1 DICE to Hit with ranged attacks made against the closest possible target.
               * Weapon Mount. The Windrider can ignore the HEAVY Keyword of one ranged weapon it wields.


Keywords
AELDARI, FLYING, VEHICLE

	+1
	+1
	-2

Battlekit
A Wraith can be equipped with any weapons and/or a Force Shield from the Aeldari Battlekit list.


Abilities
               * Malevolent Souls. When the Wraith is taken Out of Action, before removing it from the table, it can make a single melee attack against an enemy it is in close combat with. BLOOD MARKERS cannot be spent on this attack's Success Roll.
               * Psychic Guidance. While at least one friendly AELDARI PSYCHIC model is within 3” of the Wraith, the Wraith has +1 DICE to Hit with all attacks.


Keywords
AELDARI, LARGE, NO PROMOTION, STRONG, TOUGH, VEHICLE

Warband Variant: Corsairs  
                  * Outlaws. Your Warbands counts as an Outlaw Warband in addition to a Xenos (Aeldari) Warband. Your Patron must be the Pirate Lord Shared Patron.
                  * Pirate Life. You cannot recruit any Wraiths, but up to 5 of your Guardians can be upgraded to Rangers.
                  * Void Dreamers. Your Seer and Warlocks must choose their Psychic Powers from the Void Discipline, which has the following powers:
                  * Freezing Grasp (7 credits). 18”, -1 INJURY DICE, PSYCHIC, RISKY. A target hit moves at half speed and cannot stand up from Down or Retreat during its next Activation.
                  * Lightning Strike (5 credits). 24”, FIRE, PSYCHIC, RISKY
                  * Soul Channel (5 credits). As an Action with a Risky Success Roll with +1 DICE, the caster can attempt to increase the speed of itself or one ally within 6” of it. On a success, that model has +2” to its movement speed during its next Activation, or this Activation if the target is the caster.
                  * Soul Heal (10 credits). As an Action with a Risky Success Roll, the caster can attempt to heal itself or an ally within 6” of it. On a success, remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from the chosen model, or 3 BLOOD MARKERS if the Success Roll was a Critical Success.
                  * Warding Shield (7 credits). As an Action with a Risky Success Roll with +1 DICE, the caster can attempt to ward itself or an ally it can see within 12” of it. On a success, the next Injury roll made against the target during this Turn has -2 INJURY DICE and cannot ignore any of the target’s Armour.
                  * Warp Fold (2 Glory). As an Action with a Risky Success Roll with +1 DICE, the caster can choose 2 other friendly models within 12” of it that it can see. On a success, swap the positions of those two models.
                  * Path of the Outcast. Your Aspect Warriors are called Voidscarred and must choose one of the following Outcast Aspects instead of the normal Aspect options:
                  * Malevolent (LIMIT: 2). 
                  * Frightening. The Malevolent has the FEAR Keyword.
                  * Ravager (LIMIT: 2). 
                  * Powerful. The Ravager gains the STRONG Keyword.
                  * Shade Runner (+5 credits, LIMIT: 1). 
                  * Slicing Blink. As an Action with a Risky Success Roll with +1 DICE, the Shade Runner can teleport to any location within 6” of it. Then draw a line between its starting position and where it ended this teleport. Up to one model of your choice along that line must roll on the Injury Table.
                  * Starstorm (LIMIT: 2). 
                  * Pistoleer. The Voidstorm has the following benefits while armed with Ranged Weapons with the PISTOL Keyword:
                  * If it is equipped with 2 Weapons with the PISTOL Keyword, it can take a Shoot ACTION with one and then immediately take a Shoot ACTION with the other.
                  * Add the IGNORE OFF-HAND WEAPON Keyword to any weapons that have the PISTOL Keyword.
                  * Voidstorm (LIMIT: 2). 
                  * Blademaster. The Voidstrom can ignore the MAIN HAND ONLY Keyword of melee weapons it wields.
                  * Bladed Stance. When an enemy Charges the Voidstorm, if the Voidstorm has not Activated this Turn, it can immediately take the Fight Action against that enemy before it takes any further actions. This can be done only once per Turn, and the Voidstorm counts as having already taken the Fight Action during its Activation this Turn.
                  * Repurposed Weapons. You can purchase up to 2 Wraith Cannons, 2 Ghost Blades, and 1 Force Shield, which can be equipped by any of your models rather than only Wraiths.
                  * Limited Arsenal. You cannot purchase any Death Spinners, Dragon Fusion Guns, Dragon’s Breath Flamers, Firepikes, Spinneret Rifles, Death Weavers, Dragon Fusion Pistols, Reaper Launchers, Tempest Launchers, Banshee Blades, Biting Blades, Chainsabres, Scorpion Chainswords, Banshee Masks, Guardian Platforms, or Swooping Hawk Wings.

                  * Exiles. You cannot recruit any Autarchs or Aspect Warriors, and your Patron cannot be a Phoenix Lord. In addition, your Windriders lose the VEHICLE Keyword and gain the MOUNTED Keyword.
                  * Dragonlord. You must include a Dragonlord, described below, as the leader of your warband.
                  * Dragon Knights. You can include up to 6 Dragon Knights, described below, as Troops in your Warband.
                  * Knightly Lances. You can purchase any number of Laser Lances and Star Lances.
                  * Rustic Lifestyle. You cannot purchase any Death Spinners, Dragon Fusion Guns, Dragon’s Breath Flamers, Firepikes, Spinneret Rifles, Death Weavers, Dragon Fusion Pistols, Reaper Launchers, Tempest Launchers, Banshee Blades, Biting Blades, Chainsabres, Scorpion Chainswords, Haywire Grenades, Banshee Masks, Flip Belts, Guardian Platforms, or Swooping Hawk Wings.

Warband Variant: Ynnari  
                  * Followers of Ynnead. Your Patron must be Ynnead, the Whispering God.
                  * Combined Forces. You can take models from the Aeldari Drukhari, and Harlequins Factions, and your warband counts as a Xenos (Aeldari), Xenos (Drukhari), and Xenos (Harlequins) Warband. The following restrictions apply:
                  * You can take 1 between Autarch, Archon, or Troupe Master.
                  * You can take either a Seer or a single Warlock
                  * You can take up to 1 Succubus.
                  * You can take either a Death Jester or Shadowseer.
                  * You can take a maximum of 2 Aspect Warriors.
                  * You can take a total of 2 models between Incubus and Wyches.
                  * You can take a maximum of 2 Players.
                  * You can take a total of 2 models between Windriders (Max 1), Reavers (Max 1), and Skyweavers (Max 1).
                  * You can take a maximum of 1 Wraith.
                  * You cannot take any Haemonculi, Wracks, Grotesques, or Solitaires, nor can you recruit any Mandrake Mercenaries.


Your Drukhari and Harlequins models can equip Ynnari Only battlekit from the Aeldari Campaign Shop.
                  * Strength From Death. Instead of the Strands of Fate special rule, whenever a model is taken Out of Action, friendly or enemy, one of your AELDARI, DRUKHARI, or HARLEQUIN models of your choice within 6” of it gains a BLESSING MARKER. A model that already has 1 or more BLESSING MARKERS cannot be chosen.
                  * Souls for Ynnead. Instead of the Guiding Fate special rule, you can collect Soul Points. Keep track of your current Soul Points, beginning at 0. You gain 1 Soul Point whenever one of the following occurs, each once per battle.
                  * You take an enemy Warband leader Out of Action.
                  * You take at least 6 enemy models Out of Action (stacking with the above).
                  * You take the enemy Elite model with the most XP Out of Action (stacking with the above). If there is a tie for most XP, choose one at the start of the battle.
                  * At least one of your Elite models scores any VP during a battle.
                  * At least one of your PSYKER models successfully uses a power from the Revenant Discipline at least 4 times during the battle.


During any quartermaster step, you can spend Soul Points on any of the following Soul Bonds:
                  * Dread Revival (20 Soul Points). Remove one Battle Scar, and its accompanying Trauma, from one of your Elite models.
                  * Knowledge of the Lost (5 Soul Points). One of your Elite models gains 1 XP.
                  * Safe Housing (15 Soul Points). Add a Wraith to your roster. You can add a second Wraith to your roster in this way.
                  * Summon the Yncarne (35 Soul Points). Add The Yncarne, described below, to your Warband roster.
                  * Soul-Bonded Relic (15 Soul Points). You can select any one of the following relics and add it to your reserves.
                  * Soulblast Blade (LIMIT: 1). Melee, CRITICAL, ARMOUR PIERCING 2. If the target is taken Out of Action, each other enemy within 3” suffers 1 BLOOD MARKER.
                  * Soulguard Plate (LIMIT: 1, Armour). -2 to Injury rolls. The wearer has +1” to its movement speed.
                  * Soulsight Crown (LIMIT: 1, Psyker Only, Headgear). The first time during each Turn that the wearer succeeds at a PSYCHIC Success Roll to use a psychic power, remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from it.
                  * Soulsever Pistol (LIMIT: 1). 12”, +1 INJURY DICE, ARMOUR PIERCING 1 on Critical Hit, PISTOL
                  * Teachings of Ynnead (15 Soul Points). One of your PSYKER models learns the Gaze of Ynnead power, ignoring its normal limit to the number of powers it can learn.
                  * Death Begets Death. You cannot spend BLESSING MARKERS on Success Rolls besides attacks or Dash, nor can you spend them on Injury rolls made against your models.
                  * Death Not Pain. Your models with Pain(X) abilities cannot spend BLOOD MARKERS to fuel them, but can instead spend BLESSING MARKERS from any of your own models in the same way.
                  * Revenant Psychic Discipline. Instead of any other Disciplines, your models must choose their powers from the Revenant Discipline, which had the following powers:
                  * Gaze of Ynnead (3 Glory, Attack). 18”, treats a Down result as Out of Action (even if the target would normally replace Down with another result), PSYCHIC, RISKY
                  * Storm of Whispers (10 credits, Attack). 6”, Each Enemy, Immediate, each target must roll on the Injury table.
                  * Word of the Phoenix (10 credits, Effect). -1 DICE, 18”, Self or One Ally, Immediate, remove D3 BLOOD MARKERS from the target.
                  * Unbind Souls (5 credits, Effect). 18”, One Enemy, End of Turn, melee attacks have +1 INJURY DICE against the target.
                  * Shield of Ynnead (5 credits, Effect). No Range, Self, Next Activation, the caster and each friendly model within 3” of it treat Down results as Minor Hits (does not affect Down results that replace Out of Action via TOUGH Keyword)
                  * Ancestor’s Grace (7 credits, Effect). 18”, Self or One Ally, Target Activation, that target has +1 DICE to Hit with all attacks and +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls.
Patron: Ynnead, the Whispering God
                  * Favoured Of Ynnead. When this model takes an enemy Out of Action or Downs an enemy, it can immediately move up to half its movement speed. This movement can cause it to enter close combat, and it does not trigger free attacks as with Retreat.
                  * Fear Of The Grave. This model gains FEAR.
                  * Lord of Rebirth. This model gains REGENERATE 1.
                  * Master Of Death. When this model scores a Critical Hit, the injury has +1 INJURY DICE, in addition to the normal bonus for a Critical Hit and the CRITICAL Keyword, as well as ARMOUR PIERCING 1, stacking with other sources of ARMOUR PIERCING.
                  * Walker Of Many Paths. This model has +1 DICE to Hit with all attacks.
                  * Warden Of Souls. When this model benefits from Strength From Death, it gains 2 BLESSING MARKERS instead of 1.

Battlekit
The Yncarne is equipped with its Swirling Soul Energy and Vilith-zhar, the Sword of Souls, as well as armour plating, included in its cost and statistics above. You cannot equip it with any other weapons, armour, or equipment. It knows all of the powers from the Revenant Discipline.
                  * Swirling Soul Energy. 6”, automatically hits each enemy within range, ASSAULT
                  * Vilith-zhar, the Sword of Souls. Melee
                  * Piercing Strike: IGNORE ARMOUR, +1 INJURY DICE, -DICE cannot be applied to the Injury roll
                  * Sweeping Blow: SWEEPING, IGNORE ARMOUR


Abilities
                  * Explosion of Energy. When The Yncarne is taken Out of Action, each other model, enemy and friendly, within 3” must roll on the Injury table. Models that are in cover roll this injury with -1 DICE.
                  * Inevitable Death. The Yncarne is never placed normally using its DEEP STRIKE Keyword. Instead, whenever any model, friendly, enemy, or neutral, is taken Out of Action, you can immediately place The Yncarne as close as possible to that model’s previous location. It is not adjusted by D3”. It is activated as normal during that Turn, but it cannot take the Charge Action. You can still deploy The Yncarne normally instead of using its DEEP STRIKE Keyword.

Drukhari
Drukhari
  

Drukhari Special Rules
Power From Pain. Some of your models have Pain(X) abilities. In order to use one of these abilities, you must spend X BLOOD MARKERS from among non-ARTIFICIAL enemy models. If there is not enough blood, you cannot use the ability.


Each of your DRUKHARI models always have the following Pain(X) abilities:
                  * Pain(1): Gain +1 DICE to a Dash Success Roll. Limit once per Success Roll.
                  * Pain(1): Gain +1 DICE to a Hit with any one attack. Limit once per Success Roll.


Territorial Dominance. During a campaign, after you create your Warband, choose one Archon, Haemonculus, or Succubus model to be your Ascendant Lord. Keep track of your Raid Spoils, beginning at 0. If your Ascendant Lord leaves your Warband for any reason, choose a different Archon, Haemonculus, or Succubus model to become your new Ascendant Lord, and reset your Raid Spoils to 0 (but they inherit all Territory).


At the start of each battle, before deployment, your opponent places a 1” Spoils marker anywhere that is exactly half the battlefield size away from your deployment zone (18” away for 3’x3’ board, 24” away for a 4’x4’ board), but not within 6” of a battlefield edge or on Impassable Terrain or completely surrounded by it


As an Action with a Success Roll, any of your models in contact with this marker can attempt to recover that marker. On a success, you recover the spoils. At the end of each battle, you earn D3 Raid Spoils if you recovered the spoils, and another D3 if you won the battle.


During any Trading step, you can purchase Territories for 1 Raid Spoil each. When you do, choose one of the following at random. You can instead spend 4 Raid Spoils to choose any result. While you control 3 or more territories of the same type, you gain their special ability.
                  1. Arena
                  * 3 or more: At the end of each battle, one Succubus or Elite Wych in your Warband earns 1 additional XP.
                  2. Docks
                  * 3 or more: Your opponent must place Spoils markers closer to your deployment zone than to their own.
                  3. Gang Territory
                  * 3 or more: At the end of each battle, during casualties, choose one:
                  * One Elite Reaver in your Warband earns 1 additional XP.
                  * Reroll one casualty roll for a Reaver.
                  4. Incubi Shrine
                  * 3 or more: At the end of each battle, during casualties, choose one:
                  * One Elite Incubus in your Warband earns 1 additional XP.
                  * Reroll one casualty roll for an Incubus.
                  5. Noble Spire
                  * 3 or more: While your Ascendant Lord is on the battlefield, your opponent has -1 DICE to all Morale tests.
                  6. Raidcraft Workshop
                  * 3 or more: At the end of each battle, during casualties, you can reroll any one casualty or Elite Trauma roll.
                  7. Scourge Spire
                  * 3 or more: At the end of each battle, during casualties, choose one:
                  * One Elite model with Scourge Wings in your Warband earns 1 additional XP.
                  * Reroll one casualty roll for a model with Scourge Wings.
                  8. Shadow District
                  * 3 or more: You can hire Mandrake Mercenaries for 3 Glory each.
                  9. Sprawl Slum
                  * 3 or more: Each time you spend a Raid Spoils point, roll one D6: on a 6, that Raid Spoils point is refunded.
                  10. Toxin Distillery
                  * 3 or more: Once per battle, you can give all melee attacks made by one of your DRUKHARI models GAS during one of its Activations.
                  11. Trading District
                  * 3 or more: Once per quartermaster step, you can purchase one piece of battlekit from your Campaign Shop.
                  12. Weapon Forge
                  * 3 or more: Weapons that cost 20 credits or more cost you 5 fewer credits. This affects both their cost to purchase and their cost for the purposes of credit limit.


You also gain another special benefit depending on your Ascendant Lord’s model type:
                  * Archon. If you win a battle against another Drukhari Warband, and your Ascendant Lord is an Archon, you can select one of their Territories. You gain that Territory and they lose it. If you win a battle against any other Warband, you earn 1 additional Raid Spoil.
                  * Haemonculus. If your Ascendant Lord is a Haemonculus, you can spend 1 Raid Spoils to ignore the result of any Elite Trauma or casualty roll for a Haemonculus, Wrack, Grotesque, Cronos, or Talos.
                  * Succubus. If your Ascendant Lord is a Succubus, it costs you only 2 Raid Spoils to choose an Arena Territory result.
Drukhari Patrons
Your patron must be Commorragh.
Patron: Commorragh
                  * Ancient Evil. While this model is on the battlefield and not Down, your opponent has -1 DICE to Morale tests. This effect does not stack.
                  * Deathly Perfectionist. Select any skill you want from the Ranged Skills.
                  * Famed Savagery. During Activations that this model has Charged, it gains +1 DICE to Hit and +1 INJURY DICE with melee attacks.
                  * Hatred Eternal. Injury rolls made against this model as a result of ranged attacks or psychic powers have -1 INJURY DICE.
                  * Labyrinthine Cunning (LIMIT: 1). After you activate this model, you decide the next 3 models to activate, following the normal limits (i.e. the second will usually be your own model again). If you choose a model in a Fireteam, the model's owner decides if it will activate as a Fireteam or individually.
                  * Soul Thirst. Whenever this model takes an enemy Out of Action, remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from it.

                  * Drukhari Helmet (5 credits). The model has NEGATE SHRAPNEL. Counts as Headgear.
                  * Goblet of Spite (10 credits, LIMIT: 1, Succubus or Wych Only). Whenever an enemy Charges, if the bearer is within 12” and visible to that enemy, it must choose a model carrying a Goblet of Spite as at least one of its targets for the Charge.
                  * Gruesome Talismens (15 credits). When a model equipped with Gruesome Talismans fails a Risky Success Roll, that model may use this item. If it does, its Activation is not ended. Can be used once per battle.
                  * Hellion Skyboard (30 credits, LIMIT: 2, Succubus or Wych Only). The wearer has a movement speed of 10” and FLYING.
                  * Hell Mask (7 credits, LIMIT: 2, Wrack Only). The wearer gains the FEAR Keyword. Counts as Headgear.
                  * Scourge Wings (25 credits, LIMIT: 2, Archon or Kabalite Warrior Only). The wearer has a movement speed of 8” and the FLYING, INFILTRATOR, and SKIRMISHER Keywords.
                  * Shadow Field (2 Glory, LIMIT: 2, Elite Only). The bearer treats any Down result from the Injury Roll Table as a Minor Hit instead. This does not apply to Down results that already replaced another result, such as with TOUGH.
                  * Soul Seeker Ammunition (5 credits, LIMIT: 2, CONSUMABLE). AMMUNITION (IGNORE COVER). Splinter Pistol, Splinter Rifle, Tormentor Helm, or Hellglaive only.
Drukhari Campaign Shop
During a campaign, these pieces of battlekit are available only when your Warband is specifically allowed to purchase them.
                  * Animus VItae (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Ranged Weapon). 6”, IGNORE ARMOUR, BLAST 3”, SCATTER, THROWN, can be used only once per battle.
                  * Casket of Suffering (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Non-Haemonculus, Non-Wrack Elite Only, Equipment). Injury rolls made against the bearer have -1 INJURY DICE.
                  * Djin Blade (8 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Archon Only, Melee Weapon). Melee, CLEAVE 2, +2 INJURY DICE, CRITICAL, TWO-HANDED
                  * Helm of Spite (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Equipment, Headgear). The equipped model can Deny the Witch as if it had PSYKER 2. If it succeeds, the enemy PSYKER automatically suffers Perils of the Warp (if it would already, it suffers them only once).
                  * Master Clone Field (5 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Equipment). All attacks made against the wearer have -1 DICE to Hit.

Genestealer Cults
Genestealer Cults
  

Genestealer Cults Special Rules
Cult Ambush. At the start of the battle, before deployment, choose up to half of your GENESTEALER CULTS models, rounded up, to be waiting to Ambush. Those models gain DEEP STRIKE, but are deployed with this Keyword in a special way.


After normal deployment, place four 32mm Cult Ambush markers on the battlefield, each at least 8” away from any enemy model, and not within line of sight of any enemy. Your models in waiting to Ambush can be deployed using their DEEP STRIKE keyword only within 3” of one of your Cult Ambush markers, and more then 1” away from all enemies, instead of the normal restrictions, and are not adjusted by D3”.


Infiltration. During a campaign, when you create your Warband, or when you gain control of an Institution, choose one of the following Institutions to be your target or roll randomly. You cannot choose an Institution that you already Control.
                  1. Community
                  * Conditions: Gain an Infiltration Point if fewer than half of your models, rounded down, were taken Out of Action.
                  * Reward: Permanently increase your Model Limit for each battle by 2.
                  2. Industry
                  * Conditions: Gain an Infiltration Point if you won the battle, or scored at least ⅔ of the number of VP as your opponent.
                  * Reward: Your credit limit is permanently increased by 50, and you earn 100 credits.
                  3. Military
                  * Conditions: Gain an Infiltration Point if you took at least half of the enemy models, rounded up, Out of Action.
                  * Reward: Each of your Elite models earns an additional 1 XP.
                  4. Politics
                  * Conditions: Gain an Infiltration Point if one of your Elite models scored a Glorious Deed.
                  * Reward: Your Warband leader can choose to gain a Patron Skill the next time it has enough XP to learn a Campaign Skill, instead of rolling.
                  5. Religion
                  * Conditions: Gain an Infiltration Point if you succeeded on a Morale test, and/or if your opponent failed one.
                  * Reward: You earn 2 Glory.
                  6. Resources
                  * Conditions: At the start of the battle, your opponent places a 1” Resource marker on the battlefield, at least 6” closer to your deployment zone than their own. At the end of the battle, if your opponent has more models within 3” of the Resource marker than you, they earn 50 credits. If you have more models than your opponent within 3” of the Resource marker at the end of the battle, you gain an Infiltration Point.
                  * Reward: During this or any future quartermaster step, you can purchase up to two total pieces of battlekit from your Campaign Shop.


Keep track of your Infiltration Points, beginning at 0. At the end of each battle, you gain 1 Infiltration Point if you satisfied the conditions listed for your target Institution.


Once you have 3 Infiltration Points, you gain Control of your target Institution and permanently gain that Institution’s Reward, then reset your Infiltration Points to 0 and choose a new target Institution. If you have fewer than 3 Infiltration Points, you can choose to abandon your current Institution target and choose another, but your Infiltration Points reset to 0 if you do.
Genestealer Cults Patrons
Your patron must be The Four-Armed Emperor.
Patron: The Four-Armed Emperor
                  * Alien Majesty. This model gains FEAR.
                  * Biomorph Adaptation. This model has +1 INJURY DICE with melee attacks.
                  * Born Survivor. Injury rolls made against this model as a result of melee attacks are made at -1 INJURY DICE.
                  * Focus of Adoration. When this model is hit, it can choose one non-Elite ally within 1” of it. That model becomes the target of the hit instead, even if it would normally be out of range.
                  * Preturnatural Speed. When an enemy Charges this model, if this model has not Activated this Turn, it can immediately take the Fight Action against that enemy before it takes any further actions. This can be done only once per Turn, and this model counts as having already taken the Fight Action during its Activation this Turn.
                  * Shadow Stalker. While this model is in cover from such an attack, enemies cannot make attacks against this model at Long Range.

Genestealer Cults Unique Battlekit
Genestealer Cults Special Melee Weapons
                  * Bonesword. Melee, +1 INJURY DICE, CRITICAL
                  * Lash Whip. Melee, BLOCK, WHIP 3”
                  * Toxin Injector Claw. Melee, CRITICAL, GAS, MAIN HAND ONLY
Genestealer Cults Equipment
                  * Atalan Bike. The equipped model is mounted on a bike. The rider gains the LARGE Keyword if it is not already LARGE, as well as the VEHICLE Keyword, a movement speed of 10”/Infantry, but has only one hand to carry other weapons, though it can hold two-handed weapons with Shield Combo. It also has the Demolition Run and Outrider abilities. If it is selected for your Cult Ambush special rule, it must also be placed within 8” of a table edge.
                  * Demolition Run. The rider can ignore the HEAVY Keyword of Blasting Charges.
                  * Outrider. The rider has +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls, but it cannot climb sheer surfaces.
                  * Atalan Quad. The equipped model is mounted on a quad bike. The rider gains the LARGE Keyword if it is not already LARGE, as well as the VEHICLE Keyword, a movement speed of 10”/Infantry, and an additional -1 INJURY MODIFIER (does not stack with a shield), but it has only one hand to carry other weapons, though it can hold two-handed weapons with Shield Combo. It also has the Built-In Weapon and Turbo-Boost abilities. It cannot be chosen for your Cult Ambush special rule.
                  * Built-In Weapon. The rider can be equipped with up to 1 additional non-Grenade ranged weapon, which does not take any hands. The rider ignores the HEAVY Keyword of this weapon, and it can be used alongside any other ranged weapon when the rider takes the Shoot Action.
                  * Turbo Boost. The rider has +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls, but it cannot climb sheer surfaces.
Genestealer Cults Campaign Shop
During a campaign, these pieces of battlekit are available only when your Warband is specifically allowed to purchase them.
                  * Amulet Of The Voidwyrm (7 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Equipment). Once per battle, before an Injury roll is made for the equipped model, you can cause that roll to automatically be a Minor Hit. This choice is made after BLOOD MARKERS are spent.
                  * Crouchling (5 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Equipment). Whenever the owner makes a PSYCHIC attack or takes a PSYCHIC Success Roll, it can add +1 DICE to that roll. Cannot be combined with a Psychic Familiar.
                  * Crown of Ascendancy (6 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Patriarch Only, Equipment, Headgear). The first psychic power the Patriarch uses during each of its Activations does not count towards the Using Multiple Powers rules. In addition, while this model is not Out of Action, you have an additional +1 DICE to Morale tests.
                  * Dagger Of Swift Sacrifice (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Primus Only, Melee Weapon). Melee, +2 INJURY MODIFIER
                  * Elixir of Dark Vistas (1 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Leader Only, CONSUMABLE, Equipment). The equipped model earns an additional 2 XP at the end of the battle.
                  * Gift From Beyond (4 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Ranged Weapon). 48”, +1 DICE, +1 INJURY DICE, CRITICAL, RISKY, Scope Capable. Enemy models cannot change the target of this attack.
                  * Oppressor’s Bane (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Brood Brothers Only, Ranged Weapon). 15”, +1 INJURY DICE against a target with a Crossfire token, ASSAULT, PISTOL, Scope Capable
                  * Pennant of Ascension (5 Glory, LIMIT: 1, model with Cult Icon Only, Equipment). Friendly models within 6” of the bearer have +1 DICE to Hit with all attacks.
                  * Pervasion Veil (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, non-Patriarch, non-Vehicle Only, Equipment). The equipped model gains a movement speed of 8” and FLYING.
                  * Stratodais (1 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Nexos Only, Equipment). The bearer has +1 DICE and can move up to two Cult Ambush markers when using its Cult infiltration Ability.
                  * Unwilling Orb (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Psyker Only, Equipment). The bearer can Deny The WItch an additional time during each Turn. The first time it Denies The Witch does not count towards the number of powers the bearer has used for the purposes of Using Multiple Powers that Turn.
                  * Voice Of The Liberator (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Clamavus Only, Equipment). Increase the range of the bearer’s Voice of New Truths Ability by 4”.
Broodmind Psychic Discipline
                  * Mass Hypnosis (3 Glory, Effect). 6”, Each Enemy, each target must immediately move D3” in the direction of your choice. Each can move in a different direction. This movement can bring the target into or out of melee, and counts as a Charge and/or Retreat as appropriate.
                  * Mental Onslaught (7 credits, Attack). 12”, IGNORE ARMOUR, PSYCHIC, RISKY, STUN
                  * Might From Beyond (5 credits, Effect). -1 DICE, 12”, Self or One Ally, Target Activation, the target has +1 DICE to Hit and +1 INJURY DICE with all melee attacks.
                  * Mind Control (10 credits, Effect). 12”, One Enemy, Immediate, the target must move D6” in the direction of your choice. This movement can bring the target into or out of melee, and counts as a Charge and/or Retreat as appropriate.
                  * Psionic Blast (5 credits, Attack). 12”, IGNORE COVER, IGNORE LONG RANGE, BLAST 3”, STUN, PSYCHIC, RISKY
                  * Psychic Stimulus (5 credits, Effect). 12”, One Ally, Immediate, the target can take a Standard Move or Retreat Action.

Warband Variant: Brood Brothers  
                  * Military Infiltration. You can recruit up to 1 Commissar, 6 Guardsmen, 3 Veteran Guardsmen (no Drop Troopers), and 2 Heavy Weapons Teams from the Astra Militarum Faction. Up to 1 of those Veteran Guardsmen can be a Lieutenant, gaining the ELITE Keyword.
                  * Military Cult. You cannot recruit a Clamavus or a Nexos.
                  * Military Strategy. You gain the Voice of Command rule from the Astra Militarum Faction. Note that only MILITARUM models can give or receive Orders, and only GENESTEALER CULT models can benefit from your Cult Ambush rule.
                  * Crossfire. Whenever one of your MILITARUM models makes a ranged attack against an enemy model, give that enemy a Crossfire token. When one of your GENESTEALER CULTS models makes a ranged attack against an enemy with a Crossfire token, that attack has +1 DICE to Hit. A model can have only one Crossfire token, and all Crossfire tokens are removed at the end of each Turn.

Warband Variant: The Final Day  
                  * Arrival of the Star Children. You can recruit up to 5 Gaunts (no Neurogaunts, maximum 1 Barbgaunt), 2 Tyranid Warriors, 2 Raveners, and 1 Zoanthrope from the Tyranids Faction, and you can recruit Von Ryan’s Leapers Mercenaries. Your patron can be The Hive Mind from the Tyranids Faction.
                  * Hive Mind Expansion. You gain the Synapse rule from the Tyranids Faction. This and any other rule that applies to your GENESTEALER CULT models applies to your TYRANID models (but not vice versa).
                  * Psychic Parasitism. Each TYRANID model in your Warband can, by succeeding on a Success Roll as an Action, sap the life force out of a friendly non-TYRANID GENESTEALER CULTS model within 6” of it. On a success, you can move 1 BLOOD MARKER from the TYRANID model to the GENESTEALER CULTS model.
                  * No More Hiding. You do not have the Cult Ambush rule.
                  * Converted to Biomass. You can recruit only up to 3 Acolytes and 2 Aberrants.
                  * Out With the Old. You can purchase only up to 1 of each of Mining Lasers, Seismic Cannons, Heavy Rock Drills, Heavy Rock Saws, and Blasting Charges.

Warband Variant: Malstrain  
                  * Monstrous. You cannot recruit a Primus, Clamavus, Magos, or Nexos, any Acolytes, and only up to one Aberrant.
                  * Malstrain Leadership. You can recruit up to one Coalesce and up to one Malstrain Alpha, described below, as Elites in your Warband. You must recruit at least one, and choose one of them to be your Warband leader. That model gains the LEADER Keyword.
                  * Malstrain Genestealers. You can recruit any number of Genestealers as normal Troops in your Warband for 75 credits each. They lose the INFILTRATOR Keyword, but gain the FEAR and LARGE Keywords, and can be equipped with any equipment from your Wild Mutations special rule. You can give up to two of them the ELITE Keyword.
                  * WIld Mutation. The following battlekit is available to your Coalesce, Malstrain Alpha, Genestealers, and Malstrain Tyramites.
                  * Acid Blood (10 credits, LIMT: 1, Elite Only). Whenever an enemy model inflicts one or more BLOOD MARKERS on this model with a melee attack, that model suffers one BLOOD MARKER.
                  * Balemind Membrane (1 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Psyker Only). Enemies cannot Deny the Witch when the equipped model uses a psychic power.
                  * Dermic Symbiosis (5 credits). The model has NEGATE SHRAPNEL.
                  * Enhanced Senses (5 credits, LIMIT: 3). The model can declare a Charge against an enemy it cannot see.
                  * Membranous Mobility (5 credits, LIMIT: 2). The model gains +1 DICE on Climb, Jump, and Diving Charge Success Rolls.
                  * Metamorphic Regrowth (3 Glory, LIMIT: 2). At the start of the model’s Activation, you can remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from it.
                  * Temperature Adaptation (5 credits). The model has NEGATE FIRE.
                  * Toxin Sacs (5 credits, LIMIT: 3). The model’s melee attacks cause INFECTION MARKERS instead of BLOOD MARKERS.

Harlequins
Harlequins
  

Harlequins Special Rules
Luck of the Laughing God. Once per battle for every 600 credits in your Warband’s limit, you can reroll any one Success Roll one of your HARLEQUIN models makes, or an Injury roll made against one of your HARLEQUIN models.


At the start of each battle, you can roll a number of D6s of your choice, up to a maximum of 6. If all of these rolls have a different result, you can use this special rule to reroll one additional roll for each of them. If any of them have the same result, you gain no additional rerolls.


Grand Performances. During a campaign, when you form your Warband, and each time you complete or abandon a Tale, select one of the Tales listed below. When you do, you begin the Rehearsal Phase of that Tale and can begin to satisfy its Requirements. You can complete these Requirements over the course of any number of battles. At any time before you complete all of your current Tale’s Rehearsal Phase requirements, you can abandon that Tale and begin the Rehearsal Phase of a different Tale.


Once you have completed all of the requirements of the Rehearsal Phase, you gain that Tale’s Rehearsal Rewards. Following this, you can begin the Grand Performance of that Tale at the start of any battle, though you do not have to. During that battle, you can gain Accolade points as described by the Tale’s Grand Performance section. At the end of that battle, after casualties, the Tale is complete, and you earn Grand Performance Rewards depending on how many Accolade points you earned during the battle.
Tale: Cegorach’s Lament
Rehearsal Phase Requirements:
                  * Your HARLEQUIN models must take at least 20 enemy models Out of Action during Activations in which the HAELEQUIN model took the Retreat Action.
                  * A HARLEQUIN PSYKER model must succeed at at least 10 PSYCHIC Success Rolls when using powers from the Phantasmancy Discipline.


Rehearsal Phase Rewards:
                  * One Troupe Master, Death Jester, Shadowseer, or Solitaire in your Warband can immediately purchase an additional Pivotal Role upgrade that is available to it for its normal price.


Grand Performance Accolades:
                  * Gain 1 Accolade point if you roll 4 or more D6 for a Luck of the Laughing God roll at the start of the battle.
                  * Gain 1 Accolade point at the end of the battle for each of your Elite HARLEQUIN models that earned you any VP during that battle and that were not taken Out of Action.
                  * Gain 3 Accolade points if your opponent’s Warband leader was taken Out of Action by a Solitaire model during the battle. Gain 4 Accolade points instead of 3 if that Solitaire model has a Pivotal Role upgrade.


Grand Performance Rewards:
                  * 10+ Accolade Points: Masterpiece of Theater
                  * You earn 3 Glory.
                  * Each of your Elite models earns an additional 1 XP
                  * You can remove a Battle Scar, and its accompanying Trauma, from one of your Elite models.
                  * 4-9 Accolade Points: Accomplished Performance
                  * You earn 2 Glory.
                  * Up to two of your Elite models each earns an additional 1 XP
                  * You can remove a Battle Scar, and its accompanying Trauma, from a Solitaire in your Warband.
                  * 0-3 Accolade Points: A Little Amatuerish
                  * You earn 1 Glory.
                  * One of your Elite models earns an additional 1 XP
Tale: The Forging of Anaris
Rehearsal Phase Requirements:
                  * At the end of at least 4 battles, one of your HARLEQUIN models must be within 6” of the middle of the battlefield.
                  * Your HARLEQUIN models must take at least 10 enemy models that are worth any Glory Out of Action.


Rehearsal Phase Rewards:
                  * One Troupe Master, Death Jester, Shadowseer, or Solitaire in your Warband can immediately purchase an additional Pivotal Role upgrade that is available to it for its normal price.


Grand Performance Accolades:
                  * Gain 1 Accolade point at the end of the battle for each of your Elite HARLEQUIN models that earned you any VP during that battle and that were not taken Out of Action.
                  * Gain 1 Accolade point for each Elite HARLEQUINS model that took at least 1 enemy Out of Action during the battle. Gain 2 Accolade points instead if that Elite HARLEQUIN model has a Pivotal Role upgrade.
                  * Gain 3 Accolade points if your Warband leader ends the battle having used its TOUGH Keyword but was not taken Out of Action.


Grand Performance Rewards:
                  * 10+ Accolade Points: Masterpiece of Theater
                  * You earn 3 Glory.
                  * Each of your Elite models earns an additional 1 XP
                  * Up to twice during any quartermaster steps, you can purchase any one piece of Glory Shop battlekit, paying for it normally.
                  * 4-9 Accolade Points: Accomplished Performance
                  * You earn 2 Glory.
                  * Up to two of your Elite models each earns an additional 1 XP
                  * Once during any quartermaster step, you can purchase any one piece of Glory Shop battlekit, paying for it normally.
                  * 0-3 Accolade Points: A Little Amatuerish
                  * You earn 1 Glory.
                  * One of your Elite models earns an additional 1 XP
Tale: The Trials of Khaine
Rehearsal Phase Requirements:
                  * Your HARLEQUIN models must take at least 4 enemy Warband leader models Out of Action.
                  * Your HARLEQUIN models must take at least 30 enemy models Out of Action, counting TOUGH models as 2.


Rehearsal Phase Rewards:
                  * One Troupe Master, Death Jester, Shadowseer, or Solitaire in your Warband can immediately purchase an additional Pivotal Role upgrade that is available to it for its normal price.


Grand Performance Accolades:
                  * Gain 1 Accolade point each time one of your HARLEQUIN models takes an enemy model Out of Action.
                  * Gain 1 Accolade point if one of your HARLEQUIN models takes the enemy Warband leader Out of Action (in addition to the 1 gained from the above). Gain 2 Accolade points instead if that HARLEQUIN model has a Pivotal Role upgrade.


Grand Performance Rewards:
                  * 10+ Accolade Points: Masterpiece of Theater
                  * You earn 5 Glory.
                  * Each of your Elite models earns an additional 1 XP
                  * 4-9 Accolade Points: Accomplished Performance
                  * You earn 3 Glory.
                  * Up to two of your Elite models each earns an additional 1 XP
                  * 0-3 Accolade Points: A Little Amatuerish
                  * You earn 1 Glory.
                  * One of your Elite models earns an additional 1 XP
Harlequins Patrons
Your patron must be Cegorach, the Laughing God.
Patron: Cegorach, the Laughing God
When you select this patron, choice one: Light, Dark, or Twilight.
                  * A Foot in the Future. When this model declares a Charge, before rolling for charge distance, it can make a single melee attack against the target of that Charge as if they were in close combat. If that attack causes the enemy to suffer any BLOOD MARKERS, the Charge succeeds automatically, and you can place this model anywhere within 1” of the target.
                  * The Final Joke. When this model is taken Out of Action, before removing it from the table, it can make a single melee attack against an enemy it is in close combat with. BLOOD MARKERS cannot be spent on this attack's Success Roll.
                  * Fractal Storm. This model’s armour improves by -1, stacking up to -3.
                  * Player of the Light (Light Only). This model has +2” movement speed.
                  * Player of the Dark (Dark Only). When this model hits with a melee attack, the target suffers 1 additional STUN MARKER.
                  * Player of the Twilight (Twilight Only). Once per battle, you can reroll any one Success Roll this model makes, or an Injury roll made against this model, without spending a use of your Luck of the Laughing God special rule.
                  * Skystrider. This model gains FLYING.
                  * Webway Walker. This model gains the DEEP STRIKE Keyword.
Harlequins Battlekit
Harlequins Ranged Weapons
                  * Hallucinogen Grenade Launcher (25 credits, LIMIT: 2). 18”, BLAST 3”, TWO-HANDED, each target hit must move D3” in the direction of its opponent’s choice.

Harlequins Armour
                  * Holo Suit (10 credits). All attacks have -1 DICE to Hit against the wearer.
Harlequins Equipment
                  * Bio-Explosive Ammunition (10 credits, LIMIT: 3). AMMUNITION (special). Shuriken Catapult, Shuriken Rifle, or Shuriken Pistol only. If an attack with the weapon takes an enemy Out of Action, it explodes, and each model within 2” of it must roll on the Injury chart with -1 DICE. This Injury is considered to have the SHRAPNEL Keyword.
                  * Flip Belt (5 credits). The wearer automatically succeeds on Climb and Jump Success Rolls, and does not suffer Injury rolls due to falling. It also has +1 DICE to Diving Charge Success Rolls and does not fall Down or suffer an Injury roll due to failing a Diving Charge.
Harlequins Campaign Shop
During a campaign, these pieces of battlekit are available only when your Warband is specifically allowed to purchase them.
                  * Crescendo (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Ranged Weapon). 12”, ASSAULT, PISTOL, ARMOUR PIERCING 1, IGNORE ARMOUR on Critical Hit
                  * Cegorach’s Rose (4 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Melee Weapon). Melee, +1 INJURY DICE, ARMOUR PIERCING 2, MAIN HAND ONLY
                  * Laughing God’s Eye (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Equipment). Injury rolls caused by attacks that hit automatically, or caused by anything other than an attack, against the bearer and each of its allies within 6” of it have -1 INJURY DICE.
                  * Mask Of Secrets (5 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Equipment). Enemy models within 6” of the bearer cannot benefit from any +DICE to their Success Rolls besides their Ranged Skill, Melee Skill, and PSYKER X, nor can Injuries they cause benefit from any +DICE besides those provided by their weapons.
                  * Raiment Of The Laughing God (9 Glory, LIMIT: 1, no Pivotal Role Upgrade Only, Equipment). The bearer has +1 DICE to all Success Rolls. In addition, at the start of the battle, when you roll for your Luck of the Laughing God special rule, you can reroll any number of those dice once.
                  * Serpent’s Tail (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Melee Weapon). Melee, +1 INJURY DICE, VICIOUS 10, CRITICAL, MAIN HAND ONLY
                  * Starmist Raiment (4 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Armour). -2 INJURY MODIFIER
                  * Storied Sword (5 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Melee Weapon). Melee, +2 DICE, CRITICAL, ARMOUR PIERCING 1, melee attacks against the wielder have -1 DICE
                  * Suit Of Hidden Knives (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Equipment). The wearer is automatically equipped with an additional Blade at no additional cost. Whenever it takes the Fight Action, it can make an extra attack with this Blade with no off-hand penalties.
Phantasmancy Psychic Discipline
                  * Fog of Dreams (15 credits, Effect). 6”, Self or One Ally, Special, the target can hide if any piece of scenery it is touching can block line of sight in any direction, even partially, regardless of whether an enemy currently has a clear line of sight. Enemies cannot target the affected model with ranged attacks or Charges. Weapons with the Keyword BLAST it as normal if it is in the radius of the weapon. This effect ends if the affected model moves from its exact position in any way, it makes a Ranged Attack, or an enemy model comes within 1.5” of it.
                  * Shards of Light (7 credits, Attack). 18”, ASSAULT, ARMOUR PIERCING 1, SHRAPNEL, PSYCHIC, RISKY.
                  * Treacherous Illusion (2 Glory, Effect). 8”, One Enemy, Immediate, the target must make an attack with one weapon it is wielding of your choice against the target of your choice, and it can make a ranged attack in this way even if it is within 1” of an enemy. On a failure, the target can instead make an attack with any of its weapons against any target of its choice.
                  * Twilight Pathways (5 credits, Effect). 12”, One Ally, Immediate, the target can take a Standard Move or Retreat Action.
                  * Veil of Tears (7 credits, Effect). 18”, Self or One Ally, End of Turn, all attacks against the target have -1 DICE to Hit.
                  * Webway Dance (15 credits, Effect). -1 DICE, 6”, Self or One Ally, Immediate, remove the target from the table, then place it on the table anywhere that is at least 8” away from all enemies.

1 Troupe Master (Cost: 95 credits +15 for battlekit)

Battlekit
The Troupe Master must be equipped with a Holo Suit and a Flip Belt, included in its cost. It can otherwise be equipped with any weapons or equipment from the Harlequins Battlekit list.


Abilities
                  * Swift. The Troupe Master has +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls.


Upgrades:
When you recruit the Troupe Master, you can choose one of the following Pivotal Role upgrades for it:
                  * Darkness’ Bite (+10 credits). When the Troupe Master takes the Fight Action, it can make one additional attack with one of its weapons.
                  * Prince of Light (+10 credits). The Troupe master and all friendly HARLEQUINS models within 6” of it can declare a Charge against an enemy they cannot see.
                  * Twilight’s Grasp (+15 credits). At the end of each of its Activations, each enemy in close combat with the Troupe master suffers 1 BLOOD MARKER.


Keywords
ELITE, FEAR, HARLIQUIN, LEADER, TOUGH

Battlekit
The Death Jester must be equipped with a Holo Suit and a Flip Belt, included in its cost. It can otherwise be equipped with any weapons or equipment from the Harlequins Battlekit list.


Abilities
                  * Deadly Hunter. The Death Jester can ignore the HEAVY Keyword of one ranged weapon it wields.
                  * Swift. The Death Jester has +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls.


Upgrades:
When you recruit the Death Jester, you can choose one of the following Pivotal Role upgrades for it:
                  * Harvester of Torment (+20 credits). Once during each of its Activations, after the Death Jester makes a ranged attack that does not have the BLAST Keyword, it can repeat that attack against one other enemy within 3” of the original target.
                  * Humbling Cruelty (+10 credits). Whenever the Death Jester hits an enemy with a non-BLAST ranged attack, that enemy is Shaken until the end of its next Activation. (A Shaken model treats all Success Rolls and RISKY, not counting those that require no roll.)
                  * The Jest Inescapable (+10 credits). The Death Jester’s non-BLAST ranged attacks have their range increased by 12”.


Keywords
ELITE, FEAR, HARLIQUIN

Harlequin Mercenaries
You can hire the following mercenaries:
                  * 0-1 Corsair Shade Runner (Cost: 4 Glory)
                  * 0-1 Corsair Soul Weaver (Cost: 4 Glory)
Leagues of Votann
Leagues of Votann
  

Leagues of Votann Special Rules
Eye of the Ancestors. Whenever an enemy takes one of your non-ARTIFICIAL VOTANN models Out of Action, give that enemy 1 Grudge token. Any given model can have up to 2 Grudge tokens. Depending on how many Grudge tokens a model has, apply the following effects, which are cumulative.
                  * 1 Grudge Token. Your VOTANN models have +1 DICE to Hit that model with any of their attacks.
                  * 2 Grudge Tokens. Your VOTANN models have +1 INJURY DICE that model with any of their attacks.


Acquisitions. Keep track of the amount of each of the following Resources you have: Raw Minerals, Biomatter, Energy Sources, and Galactic Intel. At the end of each battle, you earn an amount of resources based on the credit limit of that battle:
                  * 899 or lower: D3x10
                  * 900-1199: D6x10
                  * 1200-1499: 2D6x10
                  * 1500+: 3D6x10


Roll a D6 to determine what type of resource you earn:
                  * 1. Raw Minerals
                  * 2. Biomatter
                  * 3. Energy Sources
                  * 4. Galactic Intel
                  * 5-6. Resource of your choice


During any Trade step, you can spend Resources to purchase any of the following Assets, which permanently apply to your Warband. You can purchase each Asset only once.
                  * Enhance Kindred Fleet (50 Raw Minerals, 50 Biomatter, 50 Energy Sources). At the end of each battle, you gain an additional D3x10 Energy Sources. In addition, you gain the Circle Back Exploration Skill.
                  * Equip Prospectors (50 Raw Minerals, 50 Biomatter, 50 Energy Sources, 50 Galactic Intel). Each time you roll to randomly determine a type of resource, you can roll two dice and choose one of the results to apply. In addition, you gain the Seek Exploration Skill.
                  * Establish Trade Routes (50 Raw Minerals, 50 Biomatter, X 50 Galactic Intel). Once per quartermaster step, you can trade up to 30 of one type of Resource for the same amount of one other Resource. In addition, you can sell battlekit for full price.
                  * Expand Mining Operations (100 Biomatter, 100 Energy Sources). At the end of each battle, you gain an additional D3x10 Raw Minerals. In addition, you gain the Extra Dice Exploration Skill.
                  * Honor Mercenary Contracts (50 Raw Minerals, 50 Biomatter, 50 Galactic Intel). Whenever one of your Mercenaries is a casualty, you regain half of its Glory cost, rounded down.
                  * Maintain Core Systems (50 Raw Minerals, 50 Energy Sources, 100 Galactic Intel). At the end of each battle, you gain an additional D3x10 Galactic Intel. In addition, you gain the Careful Exploration Skill.
                  * Settle Kindred Colony (50 Raw Minerals, 100 Biomatter, 50 Energy Sources). At the end of each battle, you gain an additional D3x10 Biomatter. In addition, you gain the Duplicate Exploration Skill.
                  * Supply the Guilds (100 Raw Minerals, 50 Biomatter, 50 Energy Sources). At the end of each battle, you earn D6x10 credits.
Leagues of Votann Patrons
Your patron can be an Ancestor Core or a Guildmaster.
Patron: Ancestor Core
                  * A Long List. When this model takes an enemy that has one or more Grudge tokens Out of Action, you can give a Grudge token to any other enemy it can see, either within 18” of it or within 6” of the enemy model.
                  * Ancestral Power (Psyker Only). The range of all of this model’s psychic powers is increased by 6”.
                  * Bequest of the Votann. At the beginning of the battle, select any one Action, Ability, or psychic power aside from Cast, Charge, Fight, or Shoot. During this battle, the chosen type of effect, used by another model, cannot target, affect, or modify this model in any way.
                  * Grim Demeanor. This model and its VOTANN allies within 8” of it have IGNORE FEAR.
                  * Kinbond. This model may form a FIRETEAM with any one non-ELITE model of the Warband. In addition, the first time one of the models fails a Risky Success Roll during a Fireteam Activation, the Activation does not end.
                  * Warrior Lord. This model’s melee attacks and Injury rolls caused by their melee attacks cannot have -DICE applied to them.
Patron: Guildmaster
                  * Ancestral Bearing. This model’s Actions, Abilities, and psychic powers can treat its allies as if they were 6” closer than they are.
                  * Experienced Eye (LIMIT: 1). The cost of any suit of armour or equipment worth 15 credits or more is reduced by 5 credits if the model with this skill is part of the Warband.
                  * Guild Affiliate. At the end of each battle, while this model is in your Warband, you earn an additional 2D6x10 credits.
                  * Luck Has. Need Keeps. Toil Earns. This model gains the ICON Keyword.
                  * Master Armourer. This model’s armour increases by -1 if it is wearing any armour at all. This does not stack with shields, and stacks with other armour only up to -3.
                  * Unrelenting Toil. This model has +1 DICE to all Success Rolls besides attacks, Dash, or using psychic powers.

Leagues of Votann Unique Melee Weapons
                  * Concussion Gauntlet. Melee, STUN, MAIN HAND ONLY. If the wielder hits a target with this weapon that is not LARGE, the model hit is moved in a straight line D3” directly away from the wielder, even if it is Down.
                  * Heavy Concussion Gauntlet. Melee, +1 INJURY MODIFIER, HEAVY, STUN, MAIN HAND ONLY. If the wielder hits a target with this weapon that is not LARGE, the model hit is moved in a straight line D3+1” directly away from the wielder, even if it is Down.
                  * Twin Concussion Gauntlets. Melee, +1 DICE, CUMBERSOME, STUN, TWO-HANDED. If the wielder hits a target with this weapon that is not LARGE, the model hit is moved in a straight line D3” directly away from the wielder, even if it is Down.
                  * Twin Heavy Concussion Gauntlets. Melee, +1 DICE, +1 INJURY MODIFIER, CUMBERSOME, HEAVY, STUN, TWO-HANDED. If the wielder hits a target with this weapon that is not LARGE, the model hit is moved in a straight line D3+1” directly away from the wielder, even if it is Down.
Leagues of Votann Unique Thrown Weapons
                  * Throwing Plasma Knives. 8”, IGNORE LONG RANGE, ASSAULT, ARMOUR PIERCING 1, THROWN
Leagues of Votann Equipment
                  * Ancestral Crest. The equipped model begins each battle with 2 BLESSING MARKERS.
                  * Exoarmour Grenade Launcher. The range of Frag Grenades and Krak Grenades the equipped model attacks with is increased by 4”.
                  * Grey Crest. Attacks made against the wearer have -1 DICE to Hit.
                  * Mass Driver Accelerators. When this model charges, roll 2D6 instead of 1D6 and then add the highest of the two dice to their charge move.
                  * Multispectral Visor. The model ignores penalties to its attack Success Rolls besides Cover, Long Range, and BLOOD MARKERS (including INFECTION and STUN), as well as penalties or limits to the range of its ranged weapons.
                  * Preymark Crest. This model and each friendly VOTANN model within 6” of it ignore Long Range penalties when making ranged attacks. Counts as Headgear.
                  * Rampart Crest. Injury rolls made against the wearer have -1 DICE. Counts as Headgear.
                  * Rollbar Searchlight. This model ignores penalties for attacking at Long Range with its ranged weapons.
                  * Weavefield Crest. The wearer and each of its allies within 6” of it treat Down Injury results as Minor Hits instead. This does not apply to Down results that already replaced another result, such as with TOUGH.
                  * Teleport Crest. The model gains the DEEP STRIKE Keyword. Counts as Headgear.

Necrons
Necrons
  

Necron Special Rules
Reanimation Protocols. The first time each NECRON model you control would be taken Out of Action (or the second if it is TOUGH), it is treated as the Down result instead. Mark it as Reanimating. If the model has not been activated this Turn, it is considered to have done so. A Reanimating model cannot stand up or move for any reason.


At the start of a Reanimating model’s Activation, it can make a Success Roll with +1 DICE. On a success, it reanimates: it is no longer Reanimating and can stand and act normally. On a failure, it remains Down and Reanimating, and its Activation ends. Once one of your models reanimates, you cannot make this roll for any of your other models during the same Turn.


While a model is Reanimating, it can be attacked as normal, and Injury rolls made against it have an additional +1 DICE and IGNORE ARMOUR.


At the end of the battle, each of your models that is still Reanimating makes one more Success Roll with +1 DICE, and on a failure, it is taken Out of Action.


Dynastic Epithets. At the end of each battle, if your Warband leader scored at least one Glorious Deed and was not taken Out of Action, choose a new appropriately dramatic Epithet for it, inspired by that battle and its deed or a generally noble-sounding title. When you do, choose one of the following Dynastic Abilities for that model to gain. A model can have only up to five of these Abilities, but it can have any number of Epithets.
                  * Arch-Machinator. At the start of each mission, after deployment, you may redeploy up to 2 of your non-INFILTRATOR models, or 1 INFILTRATOR model.
                  * Arkane Collector. You can purchase one piece of equipment from the Campaign Shop, paying for it normally, but only this model can equip it.
                  * Dynastic Embodiment. You can recruit 1 additional Royal Warden.
                  * Lord Of Legions. Whenever one of your Necron Warriors is a casualty, you can recover one piece of battlekit from it.
                  * Loyal Servant. You immediately earn 1 Glory upon gaining this Dynastic Ability.
                  * Martial Perfectionist. You can purchase one weapon from the Campaign Shop, paying for it normally, but only this model can equip it.
                  * Restorer Of Empires. At the end of each battle, you earn D6x10 credits.
                  * Unflinchingly Regressive. At the end of each battle, if all enemy models were taken Out of Action, you earn 1 Glory (this is not a Glorious Deed).
Necron Patrons
Your patron must be a Dynasty Overlord.
Patron: Dynasty Overlord
                  * Enduring Will. This model treats any Down result from the Injury Roll Table as a Minor Hit instead. This does not apply to Down results that already replaced another result, such as with TOUGH.
                  * Eternal Madness. This model’s melee attacks and Injury rolls caused by their melee attacks cannot have -DICE applied to them.
                  * Honorable Combatant. This model has +1 DICE to Hit and +1 INJURY DICE with melee attacks against Elite enemies, but must attack an Elite target when making a melee attack if possible.
                  * Immortal Pride. Injuries with IGNORE ARMOUR or ARMOUR PIERCING 2 have -1 INJURY DICE against this model.
                  * Implacable Conqueror. When this model Charges, or a friendly NECRON model begins a Charge within 6” of it, roll 2D6 instead of 1D6 and then add the highest of the two dice to the charge move.
                  * Thrall of the Silent King. Once per game, this model can re-roll all the dice or any single die for one Success Roll, or an Injury roll it causes.

If it is not Charging, it still must end this movement outside of close combat.
