0-1 Cryptek (Cost: 105 Credits + cost of staff)
Name
	Movement
	Ranged
	Melee
	Armour
	Base
	Cryptek
	5”/Infantry
	+1
	+0
	-2
	40-50mm
	

Battlekit
A Cryptek can be equipped with any weapons or equipment from the Necrons Battlekit list. It must be equipped with exactly one Staff. Its necrodermis provides it with -2 Armour, included above.


When you recruit a Cryptek, you must choose one of the following Disciplines:
                  * Chronomancer. 
                  * Chronometron. As an Action with a Success Roll with +1 DICE, the Chronomancer can choose itself or one ally within 6” of it that it can see. On a success, all attacks against that model have -1 DICE to Hit until the end of the current Turn.
                  * Timesplinter. As an Action with a Success Roll, the Chronomancer can attempt to relocate an ally it can see within 6” of it. If successful, remove that model from the battlefield and place it back on the battlefield anywhere within 6” of the Chronomancer that is not on or entirely surrounded by Impassable Terrain.
                  * Ethermancer. 
                  * Ether Crystal. As an Action with a Success Roll, the Ethermancer can conjure a storm. On a success, place a Storm Marker on the battlefield at any point the Ethermancer can see within 24”. The area within 3” of the center of that marker counts as Dangerous and Difficult Terrain. The Ethermancer can have only one Storm Marker on the battlefield at a time. Remove any existing Markers before placing a new one.
                  * Lightning Field. As an Action with a Success Roll with +1 DICE, the Ethermancer can choose itself or one ally within 6” of it that it can see. On a success, until the end of the current Turn, after each time an enemy model makes a melee attack against that model, the attacker suffers a STUN MARKER.
                  * Geomancer. 
                  * Harp of Dissonance. As an Action with a Success Roll, the Geomancer can choose one enemy it can see within 6”. On a success, the target must roll on the Injury table.
                  * Seismic Crucible. As an Action with a Success Roll with +1 DICE, the Geomancer can cause the earth to shake around it. On a success, each other model within 6” of the Geomancer must roll on the Injury table, ignoring Armour and with +1 INJURY DICE, suffers STUN MARKERS instead of BLOOD MARKERS from that Injury roll and treats results of Out of Action as Down instead.
                  * Plasmancer. 
                  * Harbinger of Destruction. As an Action with a Success Roll, the Plasmancer can choose itself or one ally within 12” of it that it can see. On a success, that model has +1 DICE to Hit and to +1 DICE Injure with its next Ranged Attack it makes.
                  * Living Lightning. As an Action with a Success Roll with +1 DICE, the Plasmancer can choose one enemy within 8” of it. On a success, that enemy gets D3 STUN MARKERS.
                  * Psychomancer. 
                  * Harbinger of Despair. As an Action with a Success Roll , choose one enemy the Psychomancer can see within 24” that has not activated this Turn. Your opponent cannot activate that model until they have activated D3 other models, or until it is the last left to activate this Turn.
                  * Veil of Darkness. As an Action with a Success Roll with +1 DICE, the Psychomancer can attempt to vanish and reappear elsewhere. If successful, choose a location within 8” that is not on or entirely surrounded by Impassable Terrain. Remove the Psychomancer from the battlefield and place it back on the battlefield at that point.
                  * Technomancer. 
                  * Canoptek Repair. As an Action with a Success Roll, the Technomancer can attempt to repair itself or an ally with the NECRON Keyword it can see within 6”. If it succeeds, remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from that model, or 3 BLOOD MARKERS if the Success Roll was a Critical Success.
                  * Reinforce Metal. As an Action with a Success Roll with +1 DICE, the Technomancer can choose itself or one ally within 6” of it that it can see. On a success, all Injury rolls made against that model have -1 DICE until the end of the current Turn.


Abilities
                  * Hover. The Cryptek does not suffer any Injuries due to falling. It also does not fall Down or suffer an Injury roll due to failing a Diving Charge.


Keywords
ELITE, LARGE, NECRON, NEGATE GAS

                  * Enemies do not get free attacks the next time the target Retreats this Turn.

                  * The target stands up from Down.
                  * Relentless March. The Royal Warden and each friendly NECRON model within 4” of it have +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls. This ability does not stack.

Warband Variant: Court of the Flayer King  
                  * Hated. You cannot include any Necron Lords or Tomb Blades in your Warband. You can include a maximum of 4 Necron Warriors and 2 Immortals in your Warband.
                  * Flayer Hordes. You can recruit any number of Flayed Ones as normal Troops in your Warband at a cost of 70 credits each. You do not have to include them in every battle, and they lose NO PROMOTION.
                  * Ascendant Flayer. You must include a Flayer King, described below, as the leader of your Warband.
                  * Flayer Virus. Your patron must be The Flayer Virus, described below. Your Crypteks, Royal Wardens, Necron Warriors, and Immortals, as well as any non-ARTIFICAL NECRON Mercenary you recruit, are infected with the Flayer Virus. They have the FEAR Keyword and gain the following  ability:
                  * Flesh Hunger. This model has +1 DICE to Hit any model that has any BLOOD MARKERS in melee. If it begins its Activation within 8” of an enemy it can see that has at least one BLOOD MARKER, it must attempt to Charge that enemy as its first Action.
                  * Flayer Technology. If you recruit a Cryptek, it must choose the Fleshmancer Discipline instead of any others. This Discipline provides the following abilities:
                  * Madness of the Blood. As an Action with a Success Roll with +1 DICE, the Fleshmancer can incite madness in one model, enemy or ally, that it can see within 6” of it. On a success, that model must move D3” in the direction of your choice, including falling, entering or leaving close combat, etc.
                  * Eviscerating Call. As an Action with a Success Roll, the Fleshmancer can call to the flesh and blood, or the equivalent, within one enemy model within 12” that it can see. On a success, that enemy must roll on the Injury table, ignoring Armour.
Patron: The Flayer Virus
                  * Blood-Fueled Cruelty. When an enemy in close combat with this model Retreats, this model has +1 DICE to Hit and +1 INJURY DICE for its free attack against that model.
                  * Blood Rites. When this model causes a Bloodbath, you can remove D3 BLOOD MARKERS from it.
                  * Deathless Rise. This model has +2 DICE to Success Rolls made to Reanimate.
                  * Dimensional Breach. The model gains the DEEP STRIKE Keyword.
                  * Shadow of Drazak. All attacks made against this model have -1 DICE to Hit.
                  * Storm of Flensing Blades. During Activations that this model has Charged, it gains +1 DICE to Hit with melee attacks. In addition, this model’s attacks always have IGNORE OFF-HAND.

Battlekit
An Apprentek is always equipped with a Staff of Light, included in its cost and ignoring the normal restrictions. It can be equipped with any equipment from the Necrons Battlekit list. Its necrodermis provides it with -2 Armour, included above.


When you recruit an Apprentek, assign it to one of your Crypteks that has not had an Apprentek assigned to it. Depending on the Crytpek’s Discipline, the Apprentek has one of the following abilities. If the Cryptek it is assigned to is removed from your Warband, you can assign the Apprentek to another Cryptek, and it cannot be fielded until you do.
                  * Chronomancer: Chronometron. As an Action with a Success Roll, the Apprentek can choose itself or one ally within 6” of it that it can see. On a success, all attacks against that model have -1 DICE to Hit until the end of the current Turn.
                  * Ethermancer: Lightning Field. As an Action with a Success Roll, the Ethermancer can choose itself or one ally within 6” of it that it can see. On a success, until the end of the current Turn, after each time an enemy model makes a melee attack against that model, the attacker suffers a STUN MARKER.
                  * Geomancer: Seismic Crucible. As an Action with a Success Roll, the Geomancer can cause the earth to shake around it. On a success, each other model within 6” of the Geomancer must roll on the Injury table, ignoring Armour and with +1 INJURY DICE, suffers STUN MARKERS instead of BLOOD MARKERS from that Injury roll and treats results of Out of Action as Down instead.
                  * Plasmancer: Living Lightning. As an Action with a Success Roll, the Apprentek can choose one enemy within 8” of it. On a success, that enemy gets D3 STUN MARKERS.
                  * Psychomancer: Veil of Darkness. As an Action with a Success Roll, the Apprentek can attempt to vanish and reappear elsewhere. If successful, choose a location within 8” that is not on or entirely surrounded by Impassable Terrain. Remove the Psychomancer from the battlefield and place it back on the battlefield at that point.
                  * Technomancer: Reinforce Metal. As an Action with a Success Roll, the Apprentek can choose itself or one ally within 6” of it that it can see. On a success, all Injury rolls made against that model have -1 DICE until the end of the current Turn.

Battlekit
A Cryptothrall is equipped with its Scouring Eye and Scythed Limbs, included in its cost. Its necrodermis provides it with -1 Armour, included above. It cannot be equipped with any other weapons or equipment.
                  * Scouring Eye. 6”
                  * Scythed Limbs. Melee, CLEAVE 2


Abilities
                  * Bodyguard. If any ally within 1” of the Cryptothrall is hit by a ranged or melee weapon (excluding BLAST weapons), you can choose to redirect the hit to the Cryptothrall instead. Determine the injuries exactly as if the weapon just hit the Cryptothrall.


Keywords
NECRON, NEGATE GAS, NO PROMOTION
Orks
Orks
  



Ork Special Rules
Waaagh! Once per battle, at the start of any Turn, you can declare a Waaagh! For the duration of that Turn, the Waaagh! Is active, providing the following benefits:
                  * When one of your ORK models charges, roll 2D6 instead of 1D6 and then add the highest of the two dice to their charge move.
                  * Your ORK models’ melee attacks have +1 DICE to Hit.
                  * Injuries rolled against any of your ORK models have -1 DICE.


Stomp ‘Em Good. During a campaign, keep track of your Stompin’ Points, beginning at 0 with a maximum of 50. You gain Stompin’ Points for the following accomplishments:
                  * Braggin’ Rights. +1 Stompin’ Point if you scored any Glorious Deeds during a battle.
                  * Winnin’. +2 Stompin’ Points if you win a battle.
                  * Da Boss. +2 Stompin’ Points if you take the enemy Warband leader Out of Action.
                  * Legand. +5 Stompin’ Points each time one of your Elite models reaches 15 XP.
                  * New Warboss. -20 Stompin’ Points (minimum 0) if your Warband leader is removed from your Warband for any reason.
                  * Momentum. At the end of each battle, you gain Stompin’ Points depending on the Stage your WAAAGH! is in (see below).
                  * Speshul. At the start of each battle, choose one of the following special ways you can gain Stompin’ Points:
                  * Overwhelming Aggression. At the end of the battle, if there are more of your ORK models on the battlefield than there are enemy models, you gain 2 Stompin’ Points.
                  * Scrag Da Killiest Git. At the start of the battle, identify the enemy model with the highest cost in credits, counting each 1 Glory as 30 credits. If you take that enemy model Out of Action during the battle, you gain 2 Stompin’ Points.
                  * Scrap ‘Em. If you took at least one enemy VEHICLE model Out of Action during the battle, you gain 2 Stompin’ Points.
                  * Show ‘Em How It’s Done. If your Warband leader took a TOUGH enemy model Out of Action, you gain 2 Stompin’ Points.


Depending on how many Stompin’ Points you have, your WAAAGH! is in one of the following Stages. Each Stage has special rules you must follow, and is updated after all Stompin’ Points are added from a battle.
                  * 0-9 Stompin’ Points: Gathering WAAAGH!
                  * Stompin’ Points. At the end of each battle, you gain 1 Stompin’ Point.
                  * Orky Ructions. You cannot suffer Orky Ructions.
                  * 10-24 Stompin’ Points: Escalating WAAAGH!
                  * Stompin’ Points. At the end of each battle, you gain 1 Stompin’ Point.
                  * Orky Ructions. After each battle, take a Ruction test, with -1 DICE if you lost that battle.
                  * ‘Eads Together. One of your Elite models earns an additional 1 XP at the end of each battle.
                  * 25-49 Stompin’ Points: Thunderous WAAAGH!
                  * Stompin’ Points. At the end of each battle, you gain 2 Stompin’ Points.
                  * Orky Ructions. After each battle, take a Ruction test, with -1 DICE if you lost that battle.
                  * ‘Eads Together. One of your Elite models earns an additional 1 XP at the end of each battle.
                  * Bestest Bossloot. You can purchase any battlekit from your Campaign Shop (paying for it normally), but any battlekit purchased in this way can be equipped only by your Warband leader.
                  * 50 Stompin’ Points: All-Conquering WAAAGH!
                  * Stompin’ Points. You do not gain any Stompin’ Points at the end of each battle. You are at the limit!
                  * Orky Ructions. After each battle, take a Ruction test with -1 DICE, or -2 DICE if you lost that battle.
                  * ‘Eads Together. One of your Elite models earns an additional 1 XP at the end of each battle.
                  * Bestest Bossloot. You can purchase any battlekit from your Campaign Shop (paying for it normally), but any battlekit purchased in this way can be equipped only by your Warband leader.
                  * Massive WAAAGH! At the end of each battle, you earn 2 Glory.
Orky Ructions
At the end of each battle, after casualties, depending on your current WAAAGH! Stage, you might be required to take a Ruction test. To do so, make a Success Roll with +1 DICE, modified further as described in your current Stage. If the Success Roll succeeds, nothing happens. If it fails, one of the following occurs:
                  * If the result on the dice did not show doubles, you must choose to either suffer a Minor Ruction, or reduce your Stompin’ Points by 10 (minimum 0). If you choose a Minor Ruction, roll a D6 and consult the following table:
                  1. Unruly Mobs. Choose two of your Elite models. Randomly choose one to roll on the Elite Trauma table. The other earns 1 XP.
                  2. Reckless Meks. If you have at least one ORK VEHICLE model in your Warband, choose one at random. Otherwise, choose one Big Mek or Mek in your Warband at random. The chosen model must roll a casualty test, or on the Elite Trauma table if it is Elite. If you have no ORK VEHICLES, Big Meks, or Meks, nothing happens.
                  3. Impatient Irritability. During your next battle, when you use your Waaagh! special rule, randomly select one of the three benefits. You do not gain that benefit during this battle.
                  4. Thievin' Gits. Randomly select one piece of battlekit in your roster that costs Glory. If you do not have any such pieces of battlekit, randomly choose the piece of battlekit in your roster that costs the most credits, rolling randomly for a tie. You cannot use the chosen piece of battlekit during your next battle.
                  5. Rowdy Yoofs. During the next Exploration phase, you earn half as many credits from your roll as normal. This has no effect if you Reinforce, and does not affect any credits gained from your Exploration’s result; only the roll.
                  6. Confrazzled By Mind Monsters. One random ORK PSYKER model from your Warband must roll on the Elite Trauma chart.
                  * If the result on the dice showed doubles, you must choose to either suffer a Major Ruction, or reduce your Stompin’ Points by 15 (minimum 0). If you choose a Major Ruction, roll a D6 and consult the following table:
                  1. Ruckus In Da Ranks. One random Elite model in your Warband suffers a Battle Scar (with no associated Trauma).
                  2. External Combustion Test. Each ORK VEHICLE, Mek, or Big Mek in your Warband must roll a casualty test, or on the Elite Trauma table if it is Elite.
                  3. Last Boss Standin’. Randomly choose one of your non-LEADER Elite models, then roll a D6. On a 1-2, your Warband leader gains a Battle Scar. On a 3-6, the chosen model gains a Battle Scar. Repeat this process until one of them is killed. These Battle Scars have no associated Trauma.
                  4. Unstable Discharge. Reduce your Stompin’ Points by 10, then one random ORK PSYKER model from your Warband must roll on the Elite Trauma chart.
                  5. Fizzlin’ Momentum. During your next battle, when you use your Waaagh! special rule, randomly select one of the three benefits. You only gain that benefit during this battle, and not the other two.
                  6. Lost In Da Warp. You must Reinforce during this post-battle phase. You cannot Explore, nor can you choose to do neither.
Ork Patrons
Your patron must be Gork or Mork.
Patron: Gork
                  * ‘Ard As Nails. BLOOD MARKERS on this model cannot be spent to add +DICE to Injury rolls against this model. Other bonuses to the Injury rolls apply, and a Bloodbath against this model works as normal.
                  * Big Crumpin’. Each time this model takes an enemy model out of Action, this model gains a BLESSING MARKER.
                  * Big Gob. This model’s Actions, Abilities, Skills, and psychic powers can treat its allies as if they were 6” closer than they are.
                  * Big Muscles. Select any skill you want from the Melee & Strength Skills.
                  * Brutal But Kunnin’. The first time during each of its Activations that this model misses with a melee attack, it can repeat that attack against the same target.
                  * Ded Choppy (LIMIT: 1). While this model is in your Warband, the cost of any melee weapon worth 10 credits or more is reduced by 5 credits.
Patron: Mork
                  * Dakkalad (LIMIT: 1). While this model is in your Warband, the cost of any ranged weapon worth 10 credits or more is reduced by 5 credits.
                  * Killy Git. Each time this model takes an enemy model out of Action, this model gains a BLESSING MARKER.
                  * Kunnin’ But Brutal. When an enemy Charges this model, if this model has not Activated this Turn, it can immediately take the Fight Action against that enemy before it takes any further actions. This can be done only once per Turn, and this model counts as having already taken the Fight Action during its Activation this Turn.
                  * Loud Gob. This model’s Actions, Abilities, Skills, and psychic powers can treat its allies as if they were 6” closer than they are.
                  * Sneaky Git. Select any skill you want from the Stealth & Speed Skills.
                  * Stomp ‘Em. When this model causes a Down enemy to roll on the Injury chart with a melee attack, you may set 1 Die to 6 and roll the rest as normal.

This does not apply to Down results that already replaced another result, such as with TOUGH. Due to its bulk the wearer rolls only a D3 for extra charge distance instead of a D6 (even if STRONG).
Ork Equipment
                  * Ammo Runt (5 credits, LIMIT: 3, Assistant). Once per battle, the equipped model can continue its Activation even after failing a Risky Success Roll, so long as that Success Roll was an attack. Its attacks lose RISKY for the remainder of the turn. Max 1 Assistant per model.
                  * 'Ard Hat (5 credits, Headgear). The wearer has NEGATE SHRAPNEL.
                  * Bosspole (20 credits, Elite Only, LIMIT: 1). Unless this model is Down, friendly models within 4” of it can add +1 DICE to their Dash Success Rolls.
                  * Distraction Grot (3 credits, Kommando Only, LIMIT: 1, Assistant, CONSUMABLE). Once during the battle, when the equipped model is hit by an attack, the Injury roll has -1 DICE. The grot is not consumed if it is not used. Max 1 Assistant per model.
                  * Grot Gunner (20 credits, Warbike Only, LIMIT: 1, Assistant). The equipped model has +1 Ranged Skill instead of its normal skill when attacking with the Built-In Shoota of its Warbike. Max 1 Assistant per model.
                  * Grot Oiler (10 credits, Mega Armour or Deff Dread Only, LIMIT: 1, Assistant). Once per battle, as an Action with no roll required, remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from the equipped model. Max 1 Assistant per model.
                  * Grot Orderly (5 credits, Painboss Only, LIMIT 1, Assistant). Once per battle, the Painboss can add +2 DICE to one Sawbonez Success Roll it makes. Max 1 Assistant per model.
                  * Iron Gob (15 credits, LIMIT: 3, Elite or Nob Only). The equipped model gains the FEAR Keyword.
                  * Jump Rokkit (5 credits, Stormboy Only). Once during its Activation, when the wearer moves (any single Movement or Dash Action), it can use this ability to gain a 12” movement speed and FLYING during that movement. After this movement, it must make a Risky Success Roll, modified depending on how far they moved. On a failure, the wearer is knocked Down and must roll on the Injury table.
                  * 0-3”: +2 DICE.
                  * 4-6”: +1 DICE.
                  * 7-9”: No modifier.
                  * 10-12”: -1 DICE.
                  * Personal Tellyporta (10 credits, Mek Only). The user gains the DEEP STRIKE Keyword.
                  * Squig Bomb (20 credits, Squig Only). A squig equipped with this device can trigger an explosive at any point during its Activation. This does not require a Success Roll. When detonated, the explosion is treated as a BLAST 3” weapon targeting the Squig model. Models within 1” of the Squig hit by the explosion roll their injuries with +1 DICE. The squig is automatically a casualty.
                  * Waaagh! Banner (1 Glory). HELD, ICON. While the bearer is not Down, it and each of its allies within 8” of it have NEGATE FEAR.
                  * Warbike (45 credits, LIMIT: 3, Elite or Boy Only, Max 1 Elite on bike). The equipped model is mounted on a bike. The rider gains the LARGE Keyword if it is not already LARGE, as well as the VEHICLE Keyword, a movement speed of 10”/Infantry, but has only one hand to carry other weapons, though it can hold two-handed weapons with Shield Combo. It also has the Built-In Shoota and Drive-by Dakka abilities. This battlekit cannot be taken along with Mega Armour.
                  * Built-In Shoota. The rider is equipped with a Dakkagun in addition to its other weapons. This takes up no hands, and it can be used alongside any other ranged weapon when the rider takes the Shoot Action.
                  * Dakkagun. 18”, AUTOMATIC 3, -1 INJURY DICE
                  * Drive-by Dakka. The rider has +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls, but it cannot climb sheer surfaces.
Ork Campaign Shop
During a campaign, these pieces of battlekit are available only when your Warband is specifically allowed to purchase them.
                  * Beasthide Mantle (4 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Da Big Hunt Only, Equipment). The wearer has -1 INJURY MODIFIER, stacking with armour up to -3, and gains the STEALTH Keyword.

                  * Da Dead Shiny Shoota (4 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Ranged Weapon). 18”, -1 DICE, AUTOMATIC 5, HEAVY
                  * Da Killa Klaw (5 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Melee Weapon). Melee, +2 INJURY DICE, IGNORE ARMOUR, CRITICAL, HEAVY, RISKY
                  * Da Krushin’ Armour (7 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Armour). Counts as Mega Armour, including for limits, and provides all the same benefits. In addition, when the wearer successfully Charges one or more enemies, those enemies each suffer 1 BLOOD MARKER.
                  * Da Souped Up Shokka (5 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Big Mek Only, Ranged Weapon). 72”, +1 INJURY DICE, IGNORE COVER, IGNORE ARMOUR, STUN, HEAVY, TWO-HANDED, RISKY, DANGEROUS
                  * Headwoppa’s Killchoppa (5 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Melee Weapon). Melee, +2 INJURY DICE, VICIOUS 10, CRITICAL, HEAVY, TWO-HANDED
                  * Scorched Gitbonez (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Psyker Only, Equipment). The equipped model has +1 DICE to Hit with PSYCHIC attacks. In addition, when it suffers Perils of the Warp, you can reroll the result once.
                  * Supa-Cybork Body (6 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Equipment). The equipped model has the benefits of each of the following pieces of Cyberteknika Equipment: Cranial, Motive, Ocular, Sindexterous, Torsonic, Vascular.
                  * Gitstoppa Rounds (1 Glory, LIMIT: 2, Equipment). AMMUNITION (CRITICAL, VICIOUS 10). Shoota, Slugga, Twin Slugga, Big Shoota, or Dakkagun only. Note that this equipment does not have CONSUMABLE.
                  * Wazgit’s Kopper Skullkap (4 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Psyker Only, Equipment, Headgear). The wearer has VICIOUS 11 with Attack psychic powers. On a Critical Hit with an Attack psychic power, the wearer can repeat the attack once.
Ork Psychic Powers
                  * Da Jump (2 Glory, Effect). -1 DICE, 6”, Self or One ORK Ally and Point, Immediate, choose a point anywhere on the battlefield that is at least 8” away from all enemies, and that is not Impassable Terrain or completely surrounded by it, then roll 1D6 and consult the following table:
                  * 1. The target must roll on the Injury table.
                  * 2. Your opponent removes the target from the table, then places it anywhere on the table that is not Impassable Terrain or completely surrounded by it.
                  * 3. Remove the caster from the table, then place it at the chosen point.
                  * 4-6. Remove the target from the table, then place it at the chosen point.
                  * Da Krunch (7 credits, Attack). 18”, -1 DICE, +1 INJURY DICE, BLAST 2”, PSYCHIC, RISKY
                  * ’Eadbanger (7 credits, Attack). 18”, -1 DICE, +1 INJURY DICE, ARMOUR PIERCING 1, PSYCHIC, RISKY, STUN
                  * Eyez of Mork (5 credits, Attack). 18”, -1 DICE, IGNORE ARMOUR, PSYCHIC, RISKY
                  * Fists of Gork (3 credits, Effect). -2 DICE, 6”, Self or One ORK Ally, Target Activation the target has +1 INJURY MODIFIER with all melee attacks.
                  * Jabbin’ Fingerz (7 credits, Effect). -1 DICE, 18”, One Enemy, Immediate, the target gets D3 STUN MARKERS.
                  * Roar of Mork (10 credits, Effect). -1 DICE, 18”, One Enemy, Immediate, the target must move D6” directly away from the caster. This movement can bring the target into or out of melee, and counts as a Charge and/or Retreat as appropriate.
                  * Warpath (2 Glory, Effect). -1 DICE, 6”, Self or One ORK Ally, Target Activation, the target can make an additional attack with one melee weapon when it takes the Fight Action.

Battlekit
The Warboss can be equipped with any weapons, armour, or equipment from the Orks Armoury.


Abilities
                  * Da Biggest and da Best. While the Waaagh! Is active, the Warboss can make an additional attack with one weapon when it takes the Fight Action.
                  * Might Is Right. Other friendly ORK models within 3” of the Warboss have +1 to Hit with melee weapons.


Upgrades
                  * Painboss. At a cost of +30 credits, you can upgrade the Warboss to a Painboss. A Painboss has the following additional abilities, but cannot wear Mega Armour and its melee Skill is reduced to +2.
                  * Bioniks. While the Painboss is in your Warband, you can purchase Cyberteknika Equipment for the following prices. You cannot keep such equipment in your reserves, and must equip it immediately.
                  * Cranial: 5 credits
                  * Ocular: 10 credits
                  * Sindexterous: 10 credits
                  * Motive: 5 credits
                  * Torsonic (LIMIT: 2): 25 credits
                  * Vascular (LIMIT: 2): 20 credits
                  * Dok’s Toolz. Injury rolls made against the Painboss have -1 DICE.
                  * Sawbonez. As an Action with a Risky Success Roll, the Painboss can attempt to heal itself or an ORK ally that does not have the ARTIFICIAL or VEHICLE Keyword it can see within 3”. If it succeeds, remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from that model, or 3 BLOOD MARKERS if the Success Roll was a Critical Success.


Keywords
ELITE, LARGE, LEADER, ORK, STRONG, TOUGH

Battlekit
The Weirdboy must be equipped with a Weirdboy Staff, included in its cost above It can be equipped with any other weapons, armour, or equipment from the Orks Armoury. It can take up to four powers from the Ork Psychic Powers list, and must take at least one.


Abilities
                  * Deadly Demise. When the Weirdboy is taken Out of Action, each other model, enemy and friendly, within 3” must roll on the Injury table. Models that are in cover roll this injury with -1 DICE.
                  * Waaagh! Energy. Increase the X value of the Weirdboy’s PSYKER Keyword by 1 for each friendly ORK model within 6” of it, to a maximum of 3.

Orks Troops

You cannot include more Gretchin in your army than you have non-Gretchin, non-Squig models.

Battlekit
A Gretchin can be equipped with a single Grot Blasta and any melee weapons or equipment from the Orks Armoury.


Abilities
                  * Cowardice. The Charge Action is always a Risky Success Roll for the Gretchin.
                  * Expendable. The Gretchin is not counted as part of your Warband for the purposes of Morale.
                  * Small. A Gretchin cannot carry any HEAVY battlekit. If it somehow gains the STRONG Keyword, the two cancel out.

                  * Ya Filthy Grots! Friendly Gretchin within 6” of the Runtherd ignore their Cowardice ability and have +2 DICE to Hit with non-PISTOL melee weapons.

                  * Mek. Up to 2 Boys, or 3 in a Warband of 1,200 credits or more, can be upgraded to Meks for +5 credits each, allowing it to ignore the HEAVY property of one Kustom Mega-Blasta, Shokka Pistol, Shokk Attack Gun, Traktor Blasta, or Zzap Gun.
                  * Stormboy. Up to 2 Boys, or 3 in a Warband of 1,200 credits or more, can be upgraded to Stormboys for free, allowing them to use Jump Rokkits. A Stomboy must be equipped with a Jump Rokkit in order to be deployed.

Slann
Slann
  
  

Slann Special Rules
Cold Blooded. While your Warband is Shaken due to failing a Morale test, your models’ Success Rolls are not automatically Risky. Your Warband still makes a tactical retreat if it fails another Morale test while Shaken.


Divination of The Stars. During a campaign, your Warband is subject to goals dictated by the stars. Keep track of your Divination Points, beginning at 0. When you first create your Warband, randomly select two different Divinations from the following list. These are your Divinations for as long as the Warband exists.
                  1. Aeon Expedition. At the end of each battle, if one of your Elite models is in your opponent’s Deployment Zone, you earn 1 Divination Point.
                  2. Burning Bright. The first time during each battle that one of your Elite models scores a Glorious Deed, you earn 1 Divination Point.
                  3. Celestial Reclaimers. At the start of each battle, before deployment, your opponent places a 1” Star Relic marker exactly half the battlefield size away from your deployment zone (18” away for 3’x3’ board, 24” away for a 4’x4’ board), but not within 6” of a battlefield edge or on Impassable Terrain or completely surrounded by it. As an Action without a Success Roll, any of your models in contact with the marker can pick up that marker. On a success, remove the marker and you earn 1 Divination Point.
                  4. Divine Warriors. If you take at least half of the enemy models Out of Action, rounded up, during a battle, you earn 1 Divination Point.
                  5. Grand Triangulation. At the start of each battle, roll 3D6 and record each die’s result. At any point, if you control 3 SLANN models that are not Down, those models are not in your Deployment Zone, and they form a triangle with sides exactly 6” (+/- 0.25”) plus the result of each die, you earn 1 Divination Point. (For instance, if you rolled 2, 5, and 3, your models would need to be 8”, 11”, and 9” apart.)
                  6. Resonance Study. At the start of each battle, secretly note one enemy ELITE or TOUGH model. At the end of the battle, reveal your choice, and if that model was not Taken Out of Action, you earn 1 Divination.


Spending Divination Points
You can spend Divination Points in the following ways:
                  * Control Fate. When one of your Elite models would roll on the Elite Trauma table, you can spend 1 Divination to roll twice and choose one of the results. You must choose to do this before rolling.
                  * Expanded Consciousness. You can spend 2 Divination to grant one of your Elite models 1 XP.
                  * Fated Companion. When recruiting Mercenaries, you can spend Divination to reduce the Glory cost by 1 for every 3 Divination you spend.
                  * Star Destiny. After rolling Exploration Dice, you can reroll any one Exploration Die by spending 1 Divination. You can continue to do this as long as you have Divination to spend.
Slann Patrons
Your patron must be a Great Mage.
Patron: Great Mage
                  * Dominating Mind (Psyker Only). This model has +1 DICE to Deny the Witch and enemies have -1 DICE to Deny the Witch against it.
                  * Higher State of Consciousness (LIMIT: 1). After you activate this model, you decide the next 3 models to activate, following the normal limits (i.e. the second will usually be your own model again). If you choose a model in a Fireteam, the model's owner decides if it will activate as a Fireteam or individually.
                  * Lord of Celestial Resonance. This model begins the game with D3 BLESSING MARKERS.
                  * Thickly Scaled Hide. Injury rolls made against this model have -1, stacking with Armour and similar effects up to -3.
                  * Vengeful Defender. This model has +1 DICE and +1 INJURY DICE when making melee attacks against a model that Charged during this Turn.
                  * Wrath of Aeons (Psyker Only). This model has +1 INJURY DICE to injuries caused by its psychic powers.

In addition, as an Action with a Success Roll with +1 DICE, the carrier can attempt to heal itself or a friendly model within 6” of it. Slann Campaign Shop
During a campaign, these pieces of battlekit are available only when your Warband is specifically allowed to purchase them.
                  * Blade of Realities (4 Glory, Elite Only, Melee Weapon). Melee, +1 INJURY DICE against ELITE or TOUGH models, IGNORES ARMOUR, MAIN HAND ONLY
                  * Bloodrage Pendant (5 Glory, Elite Only, Equipment). When the equipped model makes a Melee Attack, it can make an additional Melee Attack with a -1 DICE penalty to Hit using a single melee weapon it is equipped with. This attack has -2 DICE to Hit instead if it is TWO-HANDED (even if it is held in one hand by a STRONG model.)
                  * Cloak of Feathers (4 Glory, Priest or Skirmisher Only, Equipment). The equipped model gains a movement speed of 10” and FLYING.
                  * Crystalline Skull (6 Glory, Elite Only, Equipment). Keep track of the number of psychic powers the equipped model has successfully cast, to a maximum of 10. Once during each battle, as an Action with no roll, if it has successfully cast at least one psychic power, it can choose one enemy it can see within 12”. That enemy must roll on the Injury table with +1 INJURY DICE for every 2 psychic powers the caster has successfully cast.
                  * Incandescent Rectrices (3 Glory, Elite Only, Equipment). The model gains REGENERATE 1.
                  * Prism of Amyntok (1 Glory, Elite Only, Equipment). As an Action with a Success Roll, the equipped model can choose one enemy it can see within 12”. On a success, that model gains 1 BLOOD MARKER. Once this Action succeeds, it cannot be used again during this battle.
                  * Relocation Orb (4 Glory, Elite Only, Equipment). Once during each battle, when the equipped model suffers 1 or more BLOOD MARKERS, you can remove it from the battlefield then place it anywhere on the battlefield within 12” of its previous position, at least 3” away from all enemy models.
                  * Spacefolder’s Stave (6 Glory, Elite Only, Melee Weapon). Melee, +1 DICE, +1 INJURY DICE against targets with DAEMON or PSYKER Keyword, PSYCHIC, HELD, MAIN HAND ONLY. Before deployment each battle, the equipped model and up to two non-ELITE SLANN models in your Warband gain the DEEP STRIKE Keyword for the duration of the battle. When using their DEEP STRIKE Keyword, they must be deployed all at once, each within 3” of the others.
                  * Throne of the Lost Gods (8 Glory, Mage Chief Only, Equipment). Counts as a Hover Palanquin, including for limits, providing all of the same benefits. The equipped model gains +4” to its movement and Injury rolls made against it have -1 INJURY DICE.
                  * Zoetic Dial (3 Glory, Elite Only, Equipment). At the start of the battle, secretly note a Turn number besides 1. At the start of the Turn you chose, reveal your choice and remove all BLOOD MARKERS from the equipped model.

                  * Brutal Blows. When the Brute makes a melee attack with a HEAVY melee weapon, it can target two enemy models it is in melee combat with. The attack’s Success Roll is made only once, hitting both on a success.
                  * Heavy Scales. Injury rolls made against the Brute have -1 (included above), stacking with Armour up to -3.


Keywords
LARGE, SLANN, STRONG

T'au Empire
T'au Empire
  

T’au Empire Special Rules
For the Greater Good. When one of your models with the MARKERLIGHT keyword would make a ranged attack against an enemy, you can declare that it is attempting to mark that model instead of shooting it. Instead of taking an attack Success Roll, the target simply gains a Markerlight token. You cannot make multiple attacks or attack multiple targets with a weapon that normally allows you to in this way. The single Markerlight replaces all attacks with that weapon.


Ranged attacks against enemy models with at least one Markerlight token have +1 DICE to Hit. Your models can spend Markerlight tokens as if they were BLOOD MARKERS when making injury rolls for their ranged attacks. Remove all Markerlight tokens at the end of each Turn.


Expanding The Empire. During a campaign, when you create your Warband, or when you Convert a Demographic, choose one of the following Demographics to be your target (or roll randomly). You cannot choose a Demographic you have already Converted.
                  1. Brutal
                  * Conditions: Gain an Influence Point if you took at least half of the enemy models, rounded up, Out of Action.
                  * Reward: You earn 2 Glory.
                  2. Convicted
                  * Conditions: Gain an Influence Point if you succeeded on a Morale test, and/or if your opponent failed one.
                  * Reward: Each of your Elite models earns an additional 1 XP.
                  3. Downtrodden
                  * Conditions: At the start of the battle, your opponent places a 1” Supplies marker on the battlefield, at least 6” closer to your deployment zone than their own. At the end of the battle, if your opponent has more models within 3” of the Supplies marker than you, they earn 50 credits. If you have more models than your opponent within 3” of the Supplies marker at the end of the battle, you gain an Influence Point.
                  * Reward: Permanently increase your Model Limit for each battle by 2.
                  4. Logical
                  * Conditions: Gain an Influence Point if fewer than half of your models, rounded down, were taken Out of Action.
                  * Reward: Your credit limit is permanently increased by 50, and you earn 100 credits.
                  5. Prosperous
                  * Conditions: Gain an Influence Point if you won the battle, or scored at least ⅔ of the number of VP as your opponent.
                  * Reward: Your credit limit is permanently increased by 50, and you earn 100 credits.
                  6. Superstitious
                  * Conditions: Gain an Influence Point if one of your Elite models scored a Glorious Deed.
                  * Reward: Your Warband leader can choose to gain a Patron Skill the next time it has enough XP to learn a Campaign Skill, instead of rolling.


Keep track of your Influence Points, beginning at 0. At the end of each battle, you gain 1 Influence Point if you satisfied the conditions listed for your target Demographic.


Once you have 3 Influence Points, you have Converted your target Demographic and permanently gain that Demographic’s Reward, then reset your Influence Points to 0 and choose a new target Demographic. If you have fewer than 3 Influence Points, you can choose to abandon your current Demographic target and choose another, but your Influence Points reset to 0 if you do.
T’au Empire Patrons
Your patron must be an Ethereal Supreme.
Patron: Ethereal Supreme
                  * Academy Luminary. The model gains one of the Invocations listed for the Ethereal model. If it is an Ethereal, it cannot take the same Invocation twice.
                  * Exemplar Of The Kauyon. At the start of each battle, after deployment, you may redeploy this model and up to 2 of your other non-INFILTRATOR models, or 1 INFILTRATOR model.
                  * Exemplar Of The Mont’ka. This model has +1 INJURY DICE with ranged attacks that are not made at Long Range (regardless of the penalty actually applying).
                  * Precision Of The Hunter. All ranged attacks this model makes need 1 less BLOOD MARKER to cause a Bloodbath.
                  * Through Boldness, Victory. As an Action with no roll required, this model can choose itself or an ally within 12”. The chosen model can immediately move up to 3” towards any one enemy it can see. This movement can bring the model into melee combat, but counts as a Retreat if it moves away from an enemy.
                  * Through Unity, Devastation. This model and each ally within 6” of it gain CRITICAL on ranged attacks against targets with any Markelight tokens.

If successful, select any piece of terrain of up to 8"x8" size within 12" the model can see (a house, 8" woods, a wall etc.). This terrain piece is destroyed and it is removed from the table. Any models inside/touching/on top of this terrain must roll immediately on the Injury Chart with +1 INJURY DICE. If they are not taken Out of Action they are placed on the ground in their previous spots. The Destabilizer can be used successfully only once per battle.

                  * Solid-Image Projection Unit (4 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Battlesuit Only, Equipment). Injury rolls made against the equipped model have -1 INJURY DICE.

Battlekit
The Ethereal can be equipped with any weapons, armour, or equipment from the T’au Battlekit list.


When you recruit the Ethereal, choose one of the following Invocation for it to know:
                  * Power of Tides. As an Action with a Risky Success Roll, the Ethereal can choose itself or one T’AU ally within 6” that it can see. That model has +1 INJURY DICE with the next attack it makes.
                  * Sense of Stone. As an Action with a Risky Success Roll, the Ethereal can choose itself or one T’AU ally within 6” that it can see. That model has an additional -1 INJURY MODIFIER, stacking up to -3, and the first -1 of its armour has IMPERVIOUS, until the end of the current Turn.
                  * Storm of Fire. As an Action with a Risky Success Roll, the Ethereal can choose itself or one T’AU ally within 6” that it can see. That model has +1 DICE to Hit and ignores Long Range and Cover with its next ranged attack.
                  * Unifying Mantra. The Ethereal and its T’AU allies within 12” of it have NEGATE FEAR.
                  * Wisdom of the Guides. As an Action with no Success Roll, the Ethereal can choose one enemy that it can see that has not yet activated this Turn. The Ethereal’s Activation immediately ends and the chosen model’s Activation begins.
                  * Zephyr’s Grace. As an Action with a Risky Success Roll, the Ethereal can choose itself or one T’AU ally within 6” that it can see. That model has +1 DICE the next time it makes a Dash Success Roll, and it has +3” movement during that Dash.


Abilities
                  * Coordinated Leadership. After you deploy your models, choose up to two pairs of other T’AU models in your Warband. Those pairs form FIRETEAMS so long as the Ethereal is on the battlefield.
                  * Failure Is Not an Option. While the Ethereal is on the battlefield and not Down, you have an additional +1 DICE to Morale Tests.


Keywords
ELITE, LEADER, MARKERLIGHT, T’AU

Battlekit
A T’au Drone is equipped with light armour plating, included in its price and its statistics above. It cannot be equipped with any weapons, armour, or equipment, except those provided by its Type. It is always considered to be equipped with a Close Combat Weapon.


When you recruit a T’au Drone, you must choose one of the following Types:
                  * Grav-Inhibitor Drone (+5 credits, LIMIT: 1). The Grav-Inhibitor Drone is equipped with a Grav-Inhibitor Field.
                  * Grav-Inhibitor Field. Enemies that Charge the Grav-Inhibitor Drone or one of its allies within 3” of it cannot add the normal D6 (or D3 or other replacement) to their Charge distance.
                  * Guardian Drone (+5 credits, LIMIT: 2). The Guardian Drone is equipped with a Guardian Shield Generator.
                  * Guardian Shield Generator. The Guardian Drone and any ally within 3” of it treat Down Injury results as Minor Hits instead. This does not apply to Down results that already replaced another result, such as with TOUGH.
                  * Gun Drone (+20 credits). The drone is equipped with Twin Pulse Carbines.
                  * Twin Pulse Carbines. 20”, +1 DICE, ASSAULT
                  * Marker Drone (+5 credits, LIMIT: 2). The Marker Drone has the MARKERLIGHT Keyword, and is treated as wielding a ranged weapon with a range of 24” for the purpose of placing Markerlight tokens.
                  * Missile Drone (+15 credits, LIMIT: 2). The Missile Drone is equipped with a Missile Pod.
                  * Pulse Accelerator Drone (+0 credits, LIMIT: 1). The Pulse Accelerator Drone is equipped with a Pulse Accelerator.
                  * Pulse Accelerator. The “Pulse” weapons of friendly models within 3” of the Pulse Accelerator Drone have +6” to their range.
                  * Recon Drone (+60 credits, LIMIT: 1). The Recon Drone is equipped with a Burst Cannon, and heavy armour plating that provides it -2 armour instead of -1, and has +1 DICE to Hit with all attacks (for a total of +1 to Ranged and +0 to Melee) and the INFILTRATOR and LARGE Keywords.
                  * Shield Drone (+5 credits). The Shield Drone is equipped with an Energy Shield and has the Interpose ability.
                  * Interpose. If any ally within 1” of the Shield Drone is hit by a ranged or melee weapon (excluding BLAST weapons), you can choose to redirect the hit to the Shield Drone instead. Determine the injuries exactly as if the weapon just hit the Shield Drone.
                  * Sniper Drone (+25 credits, LIMIT: 3). The Sniper Drone is equipped with a Longshot Pulse Rifle.
                  * Longshot Pulse Rifle. 48”, +1 DICE
                  * Tactical Support Turret (+15 credits). The Tactical Support Turret stands on a 40mm base and has the LARGE keyword. It is equipped with a Smart Missile System, but cannot move or be forced to move (its movement speed counts as 0”).


Abilities
                  * Drone Control. Select one non-ARTIFICIAL, non-KROOT, T’AU model in your Warband at the start of the battle. The T’au Drone forms a FIRETEAM with that model.


Keywords
ARTIFICIAL, FLYING, NO PROMOTION, T’AU
0-4 Kroot Carnivore (Cost: 40 Credits)
Name
	Movement
	Ranged
	Melee
	Armour
	Base
	Kroot Carnivore
	6”/Infantry
	+0
	+1
	0
	25-28mm
	

Battlekit
A Kroot Carnivore can be equipped with any melee weapons, Kroot Only weapons, or Kroot Only equipment from the T’au Empire Battlekit list. Up to half of your Kroot Carnivores, rounded down, can be equipped with a single other weapon from the T’au Empire Battlekit list.


Abilities
                  * Long Stride. The Kroot Carnivore has +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls.


Upgrades
Your Kroot Carnivores can be upgraded in one of the following ways:
                  * Farstalker. Up to half of your Kroot Carnivores, rounded up, can be upgraded to Farstalkers for +10 credits each, granting them the INFILTRATOR and SKIRMISHER keywords.
                  * Kroot Gunner. Up to 1 Kroot Carnivore can be upgraded to a Kroot Gunner for +5 credits, allowing it to ignore the HEAVY Keyword of one ranged weapon it carries.


Keywords
KROOT, STEALTH, T’AU
0-2 Stealth Battlesuit (Cost: 110 Credits)
Name
	Movement
	Ranged
	Melee
	Armour
	Base
	Stealth Battlesuit
	6”/Flying
	+1
	+0
	-2
	32mm
	

Battlekit
The Stealth Battlesuit is equipped with Battlesuit Plating, included in its price. It can be equipped with any single Battlesuit Only weapon, and any Battlesuit Only equipment from the T’au Battlekit list. It is always considered to be equipped with a Close Combat Weapon if it has two free hands in melee.


Abilities
                  * Cloaking Field. As an Action with a Risky Success Roll with +1 DICE, the Stealth Battlesuit can hide if any piece of scenery the model is touching can block line of sight in any direction, even partially, regardless of whether an enemy currently has a clear line of sight. If successful, enemies cannot target the Stealth Battlesuit with ranged attacks or Charges. Weapons with the Keyword BLAST it as normal if it is in the radius of the weapon. This effect ends if the Stealth Battlesuit moves from its exact position in any way, it makes a Ranged Attack, or an enemy model comes within 1.5” of it.
                  * Homing Beacon. Allies with DEEP STRIKE or INFILTRATOR can be deployed fully within 3” of this model, even if they are too close to an enemy and/or are visible to an enemy. They still cannot be deployed within close combat range of any enemy. A model deployed with DEEP STRIKE in this way is not adjusted by D3” as normal.


Keywords
FLYING, INFILTRATOR, MARKERLIGHT, STEALTH, T’AU, VEHICLE
0-1 Crisis Battlesuit (Cost: 135 Credits)

Battlekit
The Crisis Battlesuit is equipped with Battlesuit Plating, included in its price. It can be equipped with any other Battlesuit Only weapons, armour, or equipment from the T’au Battlekit list. It is always considered to be equipped with a Close Combat Weapon if it has two free hands in melee.


Abilities
                  * Battlesuit Armaments. While equipped with two ranged weapons, the Crisis Battlesuit can make a Shoot Action with each of them during its Activation. It can replace either or both of these with an attempt to place a Markerlight token, using the replaced weapon’s range.


Keywords
FLYING, LARGE, MARKERLIGHT, STRONG, T’AU, TOUGH, VEHICLE

You can recruit the following mercenaries:
                  * 0-1 Demiurg Pioneer (Cost: 6 Glory)
                  * 0-1 Gue’vesa Weapon Team (Cost: 3 Glory +1 with Mortar)

                  * 0-2 Kroot Hound (Cost: 2 Glory) No Variant or Auxiliary Cadre only
                  * 0-1 Krootox Rampager (Cost: 5 Glory) no Variant or Auxiliary Cadre only

Warband Variant: Auxiliary Cadre  
                  * Separate Battleforce. You cannot take any Ethereals or Commanders, and you can recruit only as many Fire Warriors as you have T’au Auxiliaries and Mercenaries combined. Choose one of your other Elite models to be your Warband leader. That model gains the LEADER and TOUGH Keywords.
                  * Auxiliary Leadership. You can recruit up 1 Vespid Stingwing for 130 credits, which is a Vespid Strain Leader that gains the ELITE Keyword and has +2 Ranged Skill and +2 Melee Skill and does not count towards the limit of Vespid Stingwings.
                  * Farsight Auxiliaries. Your patron can be an Ethereal Supreme or a Farsight Leader, found in the Farsight Enclaves Warband Variant below.
                  * Auxiliary Soldiers. You can recruit any number of T’au Auxiliary, described below, as Troops in your Warband.
                  * Auxiliary Fireblade. You can optionally choose one of the Species from the T’au Auxiliary described below when you recruit a Cadre Fireblade, paying the listed cost.
                  * Auxiliary Support. Your Warband begins with 11 Glory, but only 500 credits, and your Credit Threshold is 200 credits lower. Whenever you Reinforce during the campaign, you also gain 4 Glory. In a one-off game, you instead have 200 fewer credits and 7 more Glory.
                  * Imperial Mercenaries. You can recruit the following Imperial Mercenaries:
                  * 0-1 Canid Companion (Cost: 2 Glory)
                  * 0-1 Deathworld Veteran (Cost: 4 Glory)
                  * 0-1 Ogryn Bodyguard (Cost: 4 Glory)
                  * 0-1 Penal Legionnaire (Cost: 3 Glory)
                  * 0-1 Ratling Sniper (Cost: 3 Glory)
                  * 0-1 Rejuvenat Adept (Cost: 2 Glory)
                  * 0-1 Sanctioned Psyker (Cost: 3 Glory)
                  * Outlaw Mercenaries. You can recruit the following Outlaw Mercenaries:
                  * 0-1 Beastman Bounty Hunter (Cost: 3 Glory)
                  * 0-1 Piscean Ambusher (Cost: 3 Glory)
                  * 0-1 Zoat Archivist (Cost: 5 Glory)
                  * Minor Drone Support. You can recruit up to 1 T’au Drone for every 2 other models in your Warband, including Mercenaries, regardless of Keywords.
T’au Auxiliary (Cost: 30 Credits +cost of species)
Name

Battlekit
A T’au Auxiliary can be equipped with any weapons, armour, or equipment from the T’au Empire Battlekit list.


When you recruit the T’au Auxiliary, choose one of the following Species:
                  * Demiurg (-5 credits). The Demiurg has -1” to its movement speed and the following ability:
                  * Heavily Armoured. The first -1 of the Demiurg’s Armour, if any, has IMPERVIOUS.
                  * Gue'vesa (+0 credits). The Gue’vesa has no special modifications.
                  * Hrenian (+10 credits). The Hrenian has +1 Ranged Skill.
                  * Morralian (+10 credits). The Morralian has +1 Melee Skill.
                  * Tarellian (+10 credits). The Tarellian has +2” movement speed.
                  * Thraxian (+10 credits). The Thraxian has the following ability:
                  * Multi-Armed. The Thraxian has an additional hand for both melee and ranged combat. It can attack with an additional weapon whenever it takes the Fight or Shoot Action, without off-hand penalties.


Upgrades
                  * Veteran. Up to 3 of your T’au Auxiliaries can be upgraded to Veterans for +20 credits, granting them an additional +1 Ranged Skill and +1 Melee Skill.


Keywords
MARKERLIGHT, T’AU
Warband Variant: Farsight Enclave  
                  * Without Ethereals. You cannot recruit any Ethereals. You must recruit a Commander as the leader of your Warband, which gains the LEADER Keyword.
                  * Heroes of the Enclave. You can recruit a second Commander, but it costs 30 additional credits.
                  * Aggressive Footing. Your models can place Markerlight tokens only while the target is within half the maximum range of their weapon, but when they do so, they apply 2 Markerlight tokens instead of 1.
Patron: Farsight Leader
Commander Farsight or another high-ranking member of the Farsight Enclave.
                  * Aggressive Tactician. At the start of the battle, after deployment, this model can move up to 6” if it was not deployed as an INFILTRATOR.
                  * A Ghost Walks Among Us. Select any skill you want from the Stealth & Speed Skills.
                  * Blooded Through War. This model gains the FEAR Keyword.
                  * Hero of the Enclaves. If any ally within 1” of this model is hit by a ranged or melee weapon (excluding BLAST weapons), you can choose to redirect the hit to this model instead. Determine the injuries exactly as if the weapon just hit this model.
                  * Master of the Killing Blow. This model counts Markerlight tokens as 2 BLOOD MARKERS for the purpose of Bloodbaths.
                  * Tactical Acumen (LIMIT: 1). You can increase the number of models that are normally limited (such as Crisis Battlesuits) by 1 while this model is in your Warband. This does not include ELITE Models.

Warband Variant: Retaliation Cadre  
                  * Battlesuit Cadre. You cannot recruit any Ethereals, Cadre Fireblades, Kroot Shapers, Fire Warriors, or Kroot Carnivores. You must recruit a Commander as the leader of your Warband, which gains the LEADER Keyword.
                  * Farsight Cadres. Your patron can be an Ethereal Supreme or a Farsight Leader, found in the Farsight Enclaves Warband Variant above.
                  * Battlesuit Focus. You can recruit up to 2 Crisis Battlesuits, or 3 in a Warband worth 1,200 or more credits, and up to one of them gains the ELITE keyword. You can also recruit up to 3 Stealth Battlesuits, and up to one of them gains the ELITE keyword.
                  * Broadside Battlesuit. You can recruit up to 1 Broadside Battlesuit, described below, as a Troop in your Warband.
                  * Drone Support. You can include up to 2 T’au Drones for each other model in your Warband.

Warband Variant: Kroot Kinband  
                  * Kroot Alone. You cannot include any Ethereals, Commanders, Cadre Fireblades, Fire Warriors, Crisis Battlesuits, or Stealth Battlestuits in your Warband, but you can recruit any number of Kroot Carnivores, and up to 2 of them, or 3 in a Warband worth 1,200 or more credits, can be Kroot Gunners. Your Patron must be a Shaper Chief.
                  * Skirmish Fighters. Instead of the For the Greater Good special rule, enemy models have -1 DICE to Hit your KROOT models with ranged attacks.
                  * Kroot Elite. You can recruit up to 3 Kroot Shapers, each with a different Path, and up to 1 Kill Broker, described below, as an Elite in your Warband. One of your Elite models must be your Warband leader, gaining the LEADER and TOUGH Keywords. Your leader can equip weapons that are not Kroot Only, and can purchase the following battlekit:
                  * Beastly Mount (80 credits, Leader Only). The leader is mounted on a beast such as a Knarloc or Kalamandra. The model gains the LARGE, MOUNTED, and SKIRMISHER Keywords, the Mount’s Bite weapon, Thick Hide armour, and the Beastly Stride and Thunderous Pounce abilities, but has only one hand to carry other weapons, though it can hold two-handed weapons with Shield Combo.
                  * Mount’s Bite. Melee, DEADLY, does not take up any hands, and the wielder can make an attack with this weapon as well as any other melee weapon it is equipped with without any off-hand penalties
                  * Thick Hide. -1 to Injury rolls.
                  * Beastly Stride. The rider has +1 DICE to all Climb, Jump, and Diving Charge Success Roll.
                  * Thunderous Pounce. Roll 2D6 instead of 1D6 when the rider Charges, using the highest result. In addition, when the rider successfully charges an enemy, that enemy suffers 1 BLOOD MARKER.
                  * Kroot Hounds. You can recruit up to 3 Kroot Hound Mercenaries for 50 credits each as Troops in your Warband. You still cannot change their battlekit in any way.
                  * Krootox Riders. You can recruit up to 2 Krootox Riders, described below, or up to 3 if your Warband is worth 1,200 credits or more, as Troops in your warband.
                  * Kroot Weapons. You can purchase up to 2 Dvorgite Skinners, 2 Repeater Cannons, 2 Tanglecannons, and 3 Blast Javelins.
                  * Minor Drone Support. You can recruit up to 1 T’au Drone for every 2 other models in your Warband, regardless of Keywords.
Patron: Shaper Chief
                  * Master of the Hunt. This model and each of its KROOT allies within 6” of it have IGNORE COVER when making ranged attacks against enemies within 12” of them.
                  * Pack Leader. When this model Charges, or one of its KROOT allies begins a Charge within 6” of it, you can reroll the Charge dice once.
                  * Nomadic Hunter. This model and each of its KROOT allies within 6” of it have +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls during an Activation in which they hit with a ranged attack.
                  * Predator’s Pounce. While Charging, this model ignores Difficult and Dangerous Terrain, and it automatically passes any Climb, Jump, or Diving Charge Success Roll.
                  * Prey Slayer. This model has +1 DICE to Hit and +1 INJURY DICE enemies that are Down.
                  * Technological Trophy. You can purchase any one weapon that does not cost Glory for this model from the following Warbands’ Armoury/Battlekit List: any Imperial or Outlaw Warband, Chaos Cult, Heretic Astartes, Aeldari, Drukhari, Genestealer Cults, Harlequins, or Leagues of Votann. This weapon costs 10 fewer credits. Only this model can carry that weapon, but it can be repurchased if lost. All other limits, such as a weapon being restricted to specific types of model, still apply.
0-1 Kill Broker (Cost: 75 Credits)
Name

	Ranged
	Melee

	Base

	6”/Infantry
	+2
	+2
	0
	25-28mm
	

Battlekit
The Kill Broker can be equipped with any melee weapons, Kroot Only ranged weapons, or Kroot Only equipment from the T’au Empire Battlekit list.


Abilities
                  * Call the Kill. As an Action with no roll, the Kill Broker can choose one enemy it can see to be its mark until the end of the current Turn. Whenever one of your KROOT models attacks that model this Turn, the attack has ARMOUR PIERCING 1, stacking with all other sources of ARMOUR PIERCING.
                  * Long Stride. The Kill Broker has +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls.
                  * Victory Shriek. Whenever the Kill Broker’s mark is taken Out of Action, it can immediately use its Call the Kill ability again, then up to one other KROOT ally within 6” of it gains +1 INJURY DICE with all attacks until the end of its next Activation.


Keywords
ELITE, KROOT, STEALTH, T’AU

Battlekit
The Krootox Rider is equipped with its thick hide and two Krootox Fists, included in its price, which do not take up any hands. It can make an attack with each of its fists as well as any other melee weapon it is equipped with, treating only the second fist attack as off-hand. It can be equipped with any other Kroot Only weapons or equipment from the T’au Empire Battlekit list, but it has only one hand to carry other weapons, though it can hold two-handed weapons with Shield Combo.
                  * Krootox Fists. Melee, +1 INJURY MODIFIER


Abilities
                  * Long Stride. The Krootox Rider has +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls.


Upgrades
                  * Rampager. Up to 1 of your Kroot Riders can be upgraded to a Rampager for +10 credits, gaining the following ability:
                  * Linebreaker. When the Rampager successfully charges an enemy, that enemy suffers a BLOOD MARKER.
                  * Thunderer. Up to 1 of your Kroot Riders can be upgraded to a Thunderer for +10 credits, gaining the following ability:
                  * Weapon Mount. The Thunderer can be equipped with a single TWO-HANDED ranged weapon, and ignores the HEAVY Keyword for that weapon.


Keywords
LARGE, KROOT, LIMITED POTENTIAL, MOUNTED, T’AU, TOUGH
Tyranids
Tyranids
  

Tyranids Special Rules
Shadow in the Warp. Enemy Warbands roll Morale Tests with -1 DICE when fighting a Tyranid Warband. Orks, Necrons, and T’au Empire Warbands as well as other Tyranid Warbands ignore this penalty.


In addition, while a Tyranid Warband is participating in a battle, Perils of the Warp do not apply to either side. Instead, models have a cumulative -2 DICE for each psychic power they have used during a given turn, instead of -1 DICE.


Synapse. Some Tyranid models have the SYNAPSE Keyword. While within 4” of a model with SYNAPSE, including itself, a model with the TYRANID Keyword is considered Within Synapse Range. A model Within Synapse Range has +1 DICE to Dash Actions.


Retrieve Biomass. During a campaign, whenever one of your TYRANID models is killed, whether from a casualty roll, receiving its third Battle Scar, or any other reason, you regain half of its credit cost, rounded up, and half of its Glory cost, rounded down.


In addition, if your opponent rolls the Captured result on the Elite Trauma table for any of their models, you cannot return that model to them, but you gain 50 credits if that model is not ARTIFICIAL, DAEMON, or NECRON. If it is also not a VEHICLE and has the LARGE Keyword, you earn 100 credits instead. You earn half as many credits if the model is MECHANICUS.
Tyranids Patrons
Your patron must be The Hive Mind.
Patron: The Hive Mind
                  * Adaptive Biology. The model has NEGATE FIRE and NEGATE GAS.
                  * Alien Cunning. Enemy models never get free attacks against the model when it Retreats, and the model can move out of Melee Combat using its Standard Move, Charge, or Dash.
                  * Direct Guidance (Synapse Only). When the model activates, you can choose up to one other friendly non-Elite TYRANID model that is Within Synapse Range of it that has not yet activated during this Turn. Activate that model alongside this model as if they had the FIRETEAM Keyword. That model has +1 DICE to Hit with all attacks during this Activation.
                  * Heightened Reflexes. Once during each Turn, when an enemy model ends its movement within 6” of this model, this model can make a single ranged attack against that enemy.
                  * Synaptic Lynchpin. The model gains the SYNAPSE Keyword. If it already has SYNAPSE, instead allies are Within Synapse Range while they are within an additional 2” of this model.
                  * Synaptic Tendrils. The first time this model fails a Risky Success Roll during each of its Activations, its Activation continues instead of ending.

Causes no injury, but does cause a BLOOD MARKER as an attack that uses the SHRAPNEL Keyword. If the wielder hits a target with this weapon that is not LARGE, the model hit is moved in a straight line into the nearest legal space closest to the wielder, even if it is Down. This typically ends with the target and the wielder in base contact and in Melee Combat. This movement can place the target in Melee Combat with multiple enemy models. Additionally, no enemy models may Retreat when in Melee Combat with the wielder.

Takes no hands, but max 1 tail weapon per model.
Tyranids Equipment
                  * Abhorrent Pheromones (15 credits, Elite Only). The model gains FEAR.
                  * Adrenal Glands (10 credits, LIMIT: 3, Gaunt Only). The model gains +1 DICE on Dash Success Rolls.
                  * Acid Blood (10 credits, LIMT: 1, Elite Only). Whenever an enemy model inflicts one or more BLOOD MARKERS on this model with a melee attack, that model suffers one BLOOD MARKER.
                  * Acid Maw (10 credits, LIMIT: 2, Gaunt Only). The model’s melee attacks gain ARMOUR PIERCING 1.
                  * Balemind Membrane (1 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Psyker Only). Enemies cannot Deny the Witch when the equipped model uses a psychic power.
                  * Bioplasma Discharger (4 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only). The model’s melee weapons gain the FIRE Keyword.
                  * Bonded Exoskeleton (10 credits, Elite with -2 or higher Armour Only). -1 of the model’s Armour gains IMPERVIOUS.
                  * Dermic Symbiosis (5 credits). The model has NEGATE SHRAPNEL.
                  * Enhanced Senses (5 credits, LIMIT: 3). The model can declare a Charge against an enemy it cannot see.
                  * Flesh Hooks (5 credits, LIMIT: 2, Elite or Tyranid Warrior Only). Enemies cannot Retreat while within 1” of this model.
                  * Membranous Mobility (5 credits, LIMIT: 2). The model gains +1 DICE on Climb, Jump, and Diving Charge Success Rolls.
                  * Metamorphic Regrowth (3 Glory, LIMIT: 2). At the start of the model’s Activation, you can remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from it.
                  * Temperature Adaptation (5 credits). The model has NEGATE FIRE.
                  * Toxin Sacs (2 Glory, LIMIT: 2). The model’s melee attacks cause INFECTION MARKERS instead of BLOOD MARKERS.
Tyranids Campaign Shop
During a campaign, these pieces of battlekit are available only when your Warband is specifically allowed to purchase them.
                  * Gestation Sac (7 Glory, LIMIT: 1, LARGE Elite Only, Equipment). As an Action with a Risky Success Roll, the equipped model can attempt to birth warriors. On a success, place two new Hormagaunt models within 1” of the equipped model, each equipped with 2 Scything Talon. These models do not count towards your number of models for the purpose of Morale, and they can be activated this Turn as normal. They are not added to your Warband. Once this Success Roll succeeds, it cannot be taken again for the remainder of the battle.
                  * Norn Crown (8 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Warband Leader Only, Equipment). Allies are Within Synapse Range while they are within 12” of this model. In addition, each other TYRANID model within 12” of the wearer has +1 DICE to Hit with all melee attacks.
                  * The Passenger (3 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Equipment). The equipped model has +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls.
                  * Pathogenesis (4 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Equipment). The equipped model has +1 DICE to Hit with all ranged attacks, and the range of all of its ranged weapons that have at least a range of 18” are increased by 6”.
                  * Resonance Barb (6 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Psyker Only, Equipment). When you purchase this battlekit, choose one Tyranid psychic power that costs 7 credits or fewer. The equipped model knows the chosen power in addition to any others. In addition, the equipped model has +1 DICE to all PSYCHIC Success Rolls, including attacks.
                  * Shardgullet (4 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Ranged Weapon). 36”, +1 INJURY DICE, IGNORE ARMOUR, ASSAULT, HEAVY, TWO-HANDED
                  * Spirit-Leech Cortex (1 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Psyker Only, Equipment). When the equipped model takes an enemy Out of Action with a PSYCHIC attack, you can remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from it.
                  * Dirgeheart Of Kharis (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Equipment). Enemies within 3” of the equipped model treat all of their enemies as if they had the FEAR Keyword.
                  * Maw-Claws Of Thyrax (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Equipment). Whenever the equipped model takes an enemy Out of Action with a melee weapon, you can remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from it.
                  * Reaper Of Obliterax (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Melee Weapon). Melee, VICIOUS 11, +1 INJURY DICE, Injury roll cannot benefit from any -DICE, CRITICAL, HEAVY
                  * The Ymgarl Factor (5 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Equipment). The equipped model begins each battle with 2 BLESSING MARKERS.
Tyranids Psychic Powers
                  * Catalyst (5 credits, Effect). 6”, Self or One Ally, Immediate, remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from the target.
                  * Node Lash (5 credits, Effect). 12”, Self or One Ally, Target Activation, the target has +1 DICE to Hit with all attacks.
                  * Null Zone (3 credits, Effect). 6”, Self or One Ally, End of Turn, the target cannot be targeted or affected by PSYCHIC weapons or abilities, and PSYCHIC Success Rolls cannot be used while within 6” of it.
                  * Paroxysm (5 credits, Effect). 18”, One Enemy, Target Activation, the target has -1 DICE to all actions.
                  * Psychic Scream (5 credits, Attack). Melee, IGNORE ARMOUR, PSYCHIC, RISKY, STUN
                  * Synaptic Pulse (12 credits, Attack). 18”, IGNORE COVER, IGNORE ARMOUR, BLAST 2”, PSYCHIC, RISKY, STUN
                  * The Horror (5 credits, Effect). 6”, One Enemy, Immediate, the target must move D3” directly away from the caster. This movement can bring the target into or out of melee, and counts as a Charge and/or Retreat as appropriate.
                  * Warp Blast (7 credits, Attack). 18”, IGNORE COVER, BLAST 3”, SHRAPNEL, PSYCHIC, RISKY
                  * Warp Lance (5 credits, Attack). 24”, ARMOUR PIERCING 1, PSYCHIC, RISKY
Tyranids Elites

Battlekit
A Hive Tyrant can be equipped with any weapons or equipment from the Tyranids Battlekit list, and it can take up to two powers from the Tyranids Psychic Powers list. Its super dense carapace provides it with -3 Armour, included in its statistics above.


Abilities
                  * Many-Limbed. The Hive Tyrant has +1 DICE to Hit with melee attacks, except with Tail Weapons, if it is not equipped with any ranged weapons besides Tail Weapons and/or THROWN weapons.
                  * Onslaught (PSYCHIC). The Hive Tyrant and allies with the TYRANID Keyword in Synapse Range of it add the CRITICAL Keyword to all of their ranged attacks.
                  * Will of the Hive Mind. Allies are Within Synapse Range while they are within 6” of this model.


Upgrades
                  * Winged. For an additional 10 credits, the Hive Tyrant can be Winged, granting it 8” movement and FLYING.


Keywords
ELITE, FEAR, LARGE, LEADER, PSYKER 1, STRONG, SYNAPSE, TOUGH, TYRANID

Battlekit
A Lictor can be equipped with any melee weapons or equipment from the Tyranids Battlekit list. 


Abilities
                  * Camouflage. Ranged attacks have -1 DICE to hit the Lictor.
                  * Feeder Tendrils. The first time a Lictor causes an enemy to suffer a BLOOD MARKER in melee combat during each of its Activations, you can remove one BLOOD MARKER from the Lictor.
                  * Pheromone Trails. Allies with DEEP STRIKE or INFILTRATOR can be deployed fully within 3” of this model, even if they are too close to an enemy and/or are visible to an enemy. They still cannot be deployed within close combat range of any enemy. A model deployed with DEEP STRIKE in this way is not adjusted by D3” as normal.
                  * Vanguard Predator. The Lictor has +1 DICE to all Dash and Diving Charge Success Rolls. It automatically succeeds at all Climb and Jump Success Rolls.

Battlekit
A Gaunt can be equipped with any weapons or equipment from the Tyranids Battlekit list.


When you recruit a Gaunt, you must choose one of the following Strains:
                  * Barbgaunt (+15 credits, LIMIT: 2). Barbgaunts have -1 Armour and can ignore the HEAVY Keyword of one ranged weapon they wield.
                  * Gargoyle (+5 credits, LIMIT: 3). Gargoyles have 8” movement and FLYING, but they count as having no hands for the purpose of melee weapons. They can still use melee weapons that require no hands, such as Blinding Venom.
                  * Hormagaunt. Hormagaunts have 8” movement speed and +1 Melee Skill, but they cannot be equipped with ranged weapons.
                  * Neurogaunt (LIMIT: 3). While Within Synapse Range, Neurogaunt’s have the SYNAPSE Keyword, but they cannot equip ranged weapons besides THROWN Weapons.
                  * Termagant. Termagants have the SKIRMISHER Keyword.


Abilities
                  * Skittering. The Gaunt has +1 DICE to all Climb, Jump, and Diving Charge Success Rolls.


Keywords
NO PROMOTION, TYRANID

Warband Variant: Assimilation Swarm  
                  * Assimilators. You cannot take a Hive Tyrant. Instead, you must include a Malanthrope, described below, as your Warband leader.
                  * Rear Guard. You cannot take any Raveners or Lictors.
                  * Toxic Retinue. You can recruit up to 2 Venomthrope Mercenaries for 85 credits each, which count as Troops in your Warband instead of Mercenaries, and can be equipped with any equipment from the Tyranids Battlekit list. One of them can be given the ELITE Keyword, becoming an Elite in your Warband.
                  * Heavy Spores. The range of your Venomthropes’ Foul Spores ability is extended to 9”.
                  * Biomass Processors. You can recruit up to 4 Ripper Swarms, and they gain the following ability:
                  * Feed The Swarm. As an Action with a Risky Success Roll, the Ripper Swarm can attempt to heal itself or a TYRANID ally within 1” of it. On a success, you can remove one BLOOD MARKER from the target, or if it is Down, you can instead allow it to stand up.

                  * Remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from it.
                  * It has +1 INJURY DICE with melee attacks until the end of its next Activation.
                  * It has +1 DICE to Hit with ranged attacks until the end of its next Activation.
                  * It has your choice of NEGATE FIRE, NEGATE GAS, or NEGATE SHRAPNEL, and attacks with a Keyword negated by this have -1 INJURY DICE it, until the end of the battle.
