Warband Variant: Vanguard Onslaught  
                  * Vanguard. You cannot recruit a Hive Tyrant, but you can recruit up to 2 Lictors. You must recruit at least 1 Lictor, and one of them must be your Warband leader, gaining the ELITE, LEADER, and SYNPASE Keywords.
                  * Forerunners. You cannot recruit any Ripper Swarms, and only up to 2 Tyranid Warriors, but you can recruit up to 3 Von Ryan’s Leapers Mercenaries for 120 credits each. They count as Troops in your Warband instead of Mercenaries, and can be equipped with any additional equipment from the Tyranids Battlekit list. Their Enhanced Senses do not count towards any limits.
Outlaw Factions
Outlaw Factions


Necromunda Gang
Necromunda Gang
  

Necromunda Gang Special Rules
Gang. When creating a Necromunda Gang Warband, you must choose one of the gangs listed below. You can take the special models and battlekit from that gang, and follow any additional special rules it has, but you cannot take any models or battlekit from any of the other gangs.


If a piece of battlekit appears in both the Necromunda Gang Shared Armoury and your Gang’s Armoury, use the cost and LIMIT from your Gang’s Armoury.


Fast Learners. You roll an additional Promotion Die at the end of each battle. In addition, when one of your models is promoted to Elite, it immediately gains a Campaign Skill. Choose one of the available Skill Charts and roll 2D6 on it. Reroll results of 2 or 12. The model has that skill in addition to any it learns via XP.
Necromunda Gang Patrons
Your patron can be a Gang Overlord. If your Warband is an Imperium Warband, your patron can be a Wealthy Noble. If your Warband is an Outlaw Warband, your patron can be a Sump Lord.
Patron: Gang Overlord
                  * Crushing Blow. When this model Downs an enemy with a melee attack (even if it is already Down), that enemy suffers an additional BLOOD MARKER.
                  * Gang Territory. This model gains the Set Dice Exploration Skill.
                  * Overpower. Select any skill you want from the Melee & Strength Skills.
                  * Mighty Leap. This model automatically succeeds when it takes a Jump Success Roll, and has +1 DICE to Diving Charge.
                  * Fearsome. This model gains the FEAR Keyword.
                  * True Grit. Injury rolls made against this model as a result of falling, Dangerous Terrain, or non-attack Abilities or psychic powers have -2 INJURY DICE.
Patron: Sump Lord
                  * Catfall. This model does not suffer Injury rolls as a result of falling, and it does not fall Down or suffer an Injury roll due to failing a Diving Charge.
                  * Down and Dirty. This model ignores the penalties to Success Roll rolls from being Down, and Injury rolls never treat it as Down, except for the cost of Bloodbath.
                  * In the Shadows. Select any skill you want from the Stealth & Speed Skills.
                  * Iron Jaw. Injury rolls made against this model as a result of melee attacks have -1 INJURY DICE.
                  * Lie Low. While this model is in cover against such an attack, enemies cannot make ranged attacks at Long Range against it.
                  * Tunnel Runner. This model gains the Seek and Circle Back Exploration skills.
Patron: Wealthy Noble
                  * Counter Attack. When an enemy makes a melee attack against this model, this model can make a melee attack against that enemy afterwards, if it is not taken Out of Action. It can do so only once during each model’s Activation.
                  * Expedition Funding. This model gains the Extra Dice Exploration Skill twice.
                  * Firearm Procurement (LIMIT: 1). The cost of any ranged weapon worth 20 ducats or more is reduced by 5 ducats if the model with this skill is part of the Warband.
                  * Hotshot. Select any skill you want from the Ranged Skills.
                  * Inspirational. This model and its allies within 8” have the NEGATE FEAR Keyword.
                  * Team Work. This model may form a FIRETEAM with any one non-ELITE model of the Warband. In addition, the first time one of the models fails a Risky Success Roll during a Fireteam Activation, the Activation does not end.

Necromunda Gang Unique Battlekit
Necromunda Gang Equipment
                  * Caryatid. Once per battle, when the owner is hit by an attack, you can force the attacker to reroll that attack at -2 DICE.
                  * Ridgerunner. The equipped model is mounted on a bike. The rider gains the LARGE Keyword if it is not already LARGE, as well as the VEHICLE Keyword, a movement speed of 10”/Infantry, but has only one hand to carry other weapons, though it can hold two-handed weapons with Shield Combo. It also has the Outrider ability.
                  * Outrider. The rider has +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls, but it cannot climb sheer surfaces.
                  * Stim-Slug Cache. Once during each battle, at the start of any of its Activations, the equipped model can gain one of the following benefits. Once used, at the end of the Turn, the equipped model must roll on the INJURY table, with IGNORE ARMOUR and -1 INJURY DICE.
                  * Remove D3 BLOOD MARKERS from the model.
                  * The model gains +2” movement speed and +1 DICE to Hit with all attacks.
Necromunda Gang Chem Equipment
A model can equip only one Chem Equipment at a time.
                  * Frenzon. The model has +1 DICE to Hit in melee, but at the start of each Activation, if there are any enemy models within 12” that it can see, its first action must be to Charge the closest enemy.
                  * Ghast. At the start of the battle, roll for a random Shared Psychic Discipline (or a random House Delaque Psychic Discipline if your gang is House Delaque), and two different random Powers from that Discipline. The model gains those powers and the PSYKER 1 Keyword until the end of the battle, but its psychic powers have PERILOUS ±2.
                  * ‘Slaught. The model gains +1 INJURY DICE in melee. If a model has ever been equipped with this Chem Equipment and is not equipped with it during a battle, add +1 DICE to Injury rolls against that model.
                  * Spur. The model gains +1” movement speed. At the end of each of the model’s Activations, it must make a Success Roll. On a failure, the Spur has worn off and ceases to function.
                  * Stinger Mould. The model can ignore the effects of one Trauma it has suffered for the duration of the battle.
Necromunda Gang Campaign Shop
During a campaign, these pieces of battlekit are available only when your Warband is specifically allowed to purchase them.


Alliances. Alliances provide you with passive abilities that apply without being equipped to any model, and each grants you a set of mercenaries called a Delegation, which can only be acquired by purchasing these Alliances from the Campaign Shop. You must have room in your Warband for any ELITE models. You can purchase only one Alliance at a time, and if you sell the Alliance, its cost is considered to cost 1 Glory plus the cost of all of the Delegation Mercenaries still in your Warband. If one or more of the Delegation mercenaries is killed or otherwise removed from your Warband, you can purchase replacements at its normal cost, without the need for another Campaign Shop purchase.
                  * Cold Traders Alliance (8 Glory, Outlaw Warband Only). Delegation of a Cold Trader, a Bosun, and two Void-Born Scum. Add the following Xenos weapons to your Armoury, but you can purchase only a total of up to 6 of these weapons, and only up to 1 of each.
                  * From the Aeldari Battlekit List: Avenger Shuriken Catapult, Death Spinner, Mist Staff, Plasma Grenades, Shuriken Cannon, Shuriken Catapult, Shuriken Pistol.
                  * From the Drukhari Battlekit List: Agonizer, Splinter Cannon, Splinter Pistol, Splinter Rifle.
                  * From the Leagues of Votann Battlekit List: Ion Blaster, Ion Pistol, Magna Rail Rifle, Volkanite Disintegrator.
                  * From the Necrons Battlekit List: Gauss Blaster, Gauss Cannon, Gauss Flayer, Gauss Reaper, Tesla Cannon, Voidblade.
                  * From the T’au Empire Battlekit List: Bladestave, EMP Grenades, Honor Blade, Photon Grenades, Pulse Blaster, Pulse Carbine, Pulse Rifle, Rail Rifle.
                  * Fallen Houses Alliance (5 Glory, Outlaw Warband Only). Delegation of a Rebel Lord. You earn an additional 1D6x10 credits at the end of each battle.
                  * House Catallus Alliance (7 Glory). Delegation of a Masked Killer and a Mindfrayed. Your models ignore the Captured and Robbed result on the Elite Trauma table.
                  * House Greim Alliance (8 Glory). Delegation of a Krieg Mester and a Jagerkin. You can purchase any of the following Ammunition Equipment, and that equipment loses CONSUMABLE while you have this Alliance.
                  * Dum-Dum Ammunition (5 credits, LIMIT: 2). AMMUNITION (CRITICAL). Autogun, Autopistol, Stub Pistol, or Heavy Stubber only.
                  * Frag Ammunition (5 credits, LIMIT: 1). AMMUNITION (BLAST 2”, SHRAPNEL, -1 INJURY DICE). Bolt Carbine, Boltgun, Bolt Rifle, Bolt Sniper Rifle, Assault Bolter, Bolt Pistol, or Heavy Bolt Pistol, or the Bolter or Bolt Pistol mode of a combined weapon only.
                  * Manstopper Ammunition (10 credits, LIMIT: 2). AMMUNITION (+1 INJURY MODIFIER against any target that has no armour, including shields). Autogun, Autopistol, Stub Pistol, or Heavy Stubber only.
                  * Phosphor Ammunition (5 credits, LIMIT: 1). AMMUNITION (IGNORE COVER). Autogun, Automatic Shotgun, Shotgun, Autopistol, Stub Pistol, or Heavy Stubber only.
                  * Radium Ammunition (10 credits, LIMIT: 1). AMMUNITION (GAS). Autogun, Automatic Shotgun, Shotgun, Autopistol, Stub Pistol, or Heavy Stubber only.
                  * Shock Ammunition (5 credits, LIMIT: 2). AMMUNITION (STUN). One Autogun, Automatic Shotgun, Shotgun, Autopistol, Stub Pistol, or Heavy Stubber only.
                  * Vengeance Ammunition (10 credits, LIMIT: 1). AMMUNITION (ARMOUR PIERCING 1). Bolt Carbine, Boltgun, Bolt Rifle, Bolt Sniper Rifle, Assault Bolter, Bolt Pistol, or Heavy Bolt Pistol, or the Bolter or Bolt Pistol mode of a combined weapon only.
                  * House Ko’iron (9 Glory). Delegation of a Prima Materis and two Frateris Bodyguards. At the start of each battle, before deployment, you can choose one of your non-Mercenary models. That model counts as being equipped with a Holy Relic during that battle.
                  * House Ran Lo Alliance (7 Glory). Delegation of an Auditor and a Gelt-Scrivener. At the end of each battle, after Exploration, if your opponent earned more credits from this battle, from exploration and any other sources, you earn half the difference in credits. Models gained from Reinforcing do not count as earned credits.
                  * House Ty Alliance (7 Glory). Delegation of an Onmyoto Telepath and an Onmyoto Null. Choose one other PSYKER model in your Warband, then randomly select a Shared Psychic Power (roll a D6 for Discipline and a D6 for power). That model learns that power without paying for it, which does not count towards its normal limits or towards your credit limit for a mission. It loses this power if you sell this Alliance. If you have no PSYKER models, you can give this benefit to any one PSYKER you later recruit, or to a model that gains the PSYKER Keyword.
                  * House Ulanti Alliance (8 Glory). Delegation of an Ulanti Courtier and a Mirror Mask. You earn an additional 1D6x10 credits at the end of each battle.
                  * Imperial House Alliance (7 Glory, Imperial Warband Only). Delegation of two Enforcer Bodyguards. You have the Circle Back Exploration Skill.
                  * Imperial Imposters Alliance (5 Glory, Outlaw Warband Only). Delegation of a Master Charlatan. Your warband counts as an Imperium Warband in addition to its other type(s) for the purpose of recruiting Mercenaries, and the first such Mercenary you recruit costs 1 less Glory. If you sell this Alliance, all of your Imperium Mercenaries count as part of the Delegation, but the first counts as costing 1 less Glory.
                  * Mercator Gelt: The Guild of Coin Alliance (8 Glory, non-Outlaw Warband Only). Delegation of a Master of Coin, a Skinflint, and two Grovellers (one with a Longlas). You have the Seek Exploration Skill.
                  * Mercator Munda: The Iron Guild Alliance (6 Glory, non-Outlaw Warband Only). Delegation of five Guild Dregs. You can purchase weapons from the Venators Armoury as if it were your Gang’s Armoury.
                  * Mercator Nautica: The Water Guild Alliance (8 Glory, non-Outlaw Warband Only). Delegation of a Master Nautican, a Syphonite, and a Subnautican. At the end of each battle, you can reroll one Casualty or Elite Taruma roll for one of your models.
                  * Mercator Pallidus: The Corpse Guild Alliance (8 Glory, non-Outlaw Warband Only). Delegation of a Pale Consort, a Bone Scrivener, and two Corpse Grinders (not to be confused with the Corpsegrinder Cult). For each non-ARTIFICIAL non-VEHICLE non-NECRON model killed, enemy or friendly, during the Trauma Step after a battle you participated in, you earn credits equal to half the model’s credit cost (rounded down), or 10 credits per 1 Glory it cost. This counts only the model’s base cost, and not any equipment if it was purchased separately. You earn half as many credits from MECHANICUS models.
                  * Mercator Pyros: The Promethium Guild Alliance (8 Glory, non-Outlaw Warband Only). Delegation of a Pyrocaen Lord, a Pyromagir, and two Cynders. Add the following weapons to your Armoury: Hand Flamer (20 credits, LIMIT: 2), Flamer (30 credits, LIMIT: 3), Heavy Flamer (55 credits, LIMIT: 1), Plasma Pistol (20 credits, LIMIT: 2), Plasma Gun (30 credits, LIMIT: 1), Plasma Cannon (60 credits, LIMIT: 1). Use the higher LIMIT if the weapon already appears in your Armoury. If you sell this contract, you must sell any of these weapons no longer allowed by your normal Armoury.
                  * Mercator Sanguis: The Slave Guild Alliance (8 Glory, non-Outlaw Warband Only). Delegation of a Chain Lord, a Shackleman, and two Pit Fighters. After rolling on both skill tables during Advancement, each of your Elite models can reroll one of the results. This applies only to Advancement, and not to skill gained any other way.
                  * Narco Lords Alliance (6 Glory, Outlaw Warband Only). Delegation of five Narco Scum. You can ignore the CONSUMABLE property of Chem Equipment, and increase the LIMIT of each Chem Equipment from the Necromunda Gang Shared Armoury by 1.
                  * Psi-Syndica Alliance (5 Glory, Outlaw Warband Only). Delegation of a Mind-Locked Wyrd. One of your models of your choice gains the PSYKER 1 Keyword. Randomly select a Shared Discipline. That model can purchase up to two powers from that Discipline, but can use it only while equipped with a PSYCHIC weapon. It loses this Keyword and its powers if you sell this Alliance.
                  * Rogue Factoria Alliance (8 Glory, Outlaw Warband Only). Delegation of a Factoria Overseer, a Work Party Boss, and three Factoria Workers. You can purchase Counterfeit versions of any weapon you have access to, or any weapon from the Venators Armoury, that costs 15 or more credits and that do not have the RISKY Keyword. These weapons cost 5 fewer credits, gain the DANGEROUS and RISKY Keywords.


Hangers On. Hangers on function just like equipment. You can use a model to represent them, but you do not need to. A model can be equipped with only up to 1 hanger on at a time. A hanger on must be equipped to a model fielded during a battle in order to use any of its pre- or post-battle effects.
                  * Agitator (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Outlaw Only). Once per battle, the equipped model or an ally within 12” of it can reroll a Dash Success Roll. In addition, at the start of the battle, you can boast that you are going to win. If you win the battle, you gain 1 Glory. If you lose the battle, you gain 1 fewer Glory from that battle, if you gained any. On a tie, nothing happens.
                  * Ammo-Jack (2 Glory, LIMIT: 3). Once per battle, the equipped model can reroll one ranged attack Success Roll it makes.
                  * Cadaver Merchant (1 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Outlaw Only). Once per battle, you can give the equipped model FEAR when it is attacked in melee. This keyword lasts until the end of the current Turn. In addition, at the end of each battle, you earn 10 additional credits for every model that was a casualty, on either side, during that battle.
                  * Chem Dealer (1 Glory, LIMIT: 1). Once during each battle, the equipped model can gain the effects of one chem equipment from the Necromunda Gang Armoury for one entire Activation. If you choose ‘Slaught, the drawback applies only during this battle. In addition, one chem equipment equipped by the same model loses CONSUMABLE while both remain equipped.
                  * Data-Skrivener (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1). Once during each battle, the equipped model can gain +1 DICE on any one Success Roll that is not an attack or Dash Success Roll. In addition, you have the Lucky Exploration Skill.
                  * Dome Runner (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1). Once during each battle, when a ranged attack misses it, the equipped model can take a Standard Move or Retreat Action, but can move only up to 3”. In addition, you have the Seek Exploration Skill.
                  * Fixer (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Imperial Only). Once during each battle, when this model falls Down, it can immediately stand up again. In addition, you have the Circle Back Exploration Skill.
                  * Gang Lookout (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1). Once during each battle, if your opponent would activate the first model during a Turn, you can activate this model first. In addition, you have the Reroll Exploration Skill.
                  * Heretek (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Outlaw Only). Once during each Battle, the equipped model can add the GAS, FIRE, or SHRAPNEL Keyword to one attack it hits with.
                  * ’Narker (1 Glory, LIMIT: 2). One per battle, you can set the result of one Charge roll made against this model to 1. In addition, you have the Reroll Exploration skill.
                  * Propagandist (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1). Once per battle, you can gain +1 DICE to a Morale roll. In addition, at the end of each battle, roll a D6. On a 5-6, you gain 1 Glory.
                  * Proxy (2 Glory, LIMIT: 1, Outlaw Only). Once during each battle, when this model falls Down, it can immediately stand up again. In addition, you have the Duplicate Exploration Skill.
                  * Rogue Doc (2 Glory, LIMIT: 2). Once per battle, during its Activation, you can remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from the equipped model.
                  * Slopper (1 Glory, LIMIT: 1). At the end of the battle, you can reroll one Casualty or Elite Taruma roll (for any model in your Warband).
                  * Underhive Trader (1 Glory, LIMIT: 1). You have the Extra Dice Exploration Skill.

Battlekit
The Juve can be equipped with any Basic Ranged, Pistol, or Basic Melee weapons, armour besides Heavy Armour, or any equipment, all from the Necromunda Gang Armoury or your chosen Gang’s Armoury.


Abilities
                  * New Kid. After a Juve has participated in at least two missions, or if it scores a Glorious Deed or takes an enemy Elite model Out of Action, you can upgrade it to a Ganger for free at the end of a battle, and it can choose the Veteran Specialty. It uses its new cost when calculating how many credits worth of models you can bring to a mission, and you can purchase any upgrades for it so long as you do not surpass any limits.

Ganger (Cost: 35 Credits)
Name

	Armour

	+0 or +1

	25-32mm
	

Battlekit
The Ganger can be equipped with any weapons, armour, or equipment from the Necromunda Gang Armoury or your chosen Gang’s Armoury.


When you recruit a Ganger, you must choose one of the following Specialties:
                  * Bruiser. The Ganger has +1 Melee and +0 Ranged.
                  * Shooter. The Ganger has +1 Ranged and +0 Melee.
                  * Veteran. The Ganger has +1 Ranged and +1 Melee. You can select this option only if the Ganger was upgraded from a Juve.


Upgrades
                  * Heavy. Up to 2 of your Gangers can be upgraded to Heavies for +5 credits each, allowing them to ignore the HEAVY Keyword of one piece of battlekit they carry. They can still carry only one piece of HEAVY battlekit each, and it still takes up the normal number of hands.

House Gangs
Gang: House Cawdor  
                  * Allegiance. Your Warband counts as either an Imperial or Outlaw Warband, your choice upon Warband creation.
                  * Path of Faith. You have a pool of Miracles, beginning at 0 at the start of each battle. You gain Miracles in the following circumstances:
                  * Slay. Whenever one of your GANGER models takes an enemy Elite model Out of Action, you gain 1 Miracle.
                  * Martyr. Whenever one of your Elite GANGER models is taken Out of Action, you gain 1 Miracle.
                  * Hold. Whenever you succeed at a Morale test, you gain 1 Miracle.


You can spend Miracles as if they were BLESSING MARKERS on any of your GANGER models.
                  * Leader. Your Gang Leader is known as either a Word-Keeper or a Priest, costs +10 credits, and gains the following ability:
                  * The Path We Follow. As an Action with a Risky Success Roll, the Gang Leader can pray for divine intervention. On a success, you gain 1 Miracle.
                  * Champions. Each of your Champions is either a Deacon or Firebrand, gaining the corresponding ability below:
                  * Deacon: During a turn in which this model has Charged, it can make a single additional melee attack with one weapon when it takes the Fight Action, but it suffers -1 DICE to Hit with all melee attacks during that action if it does.
                  * Firebrand: The first time during each Turn that this model takes an enemy model Out of Action, you gain 1 Miracle, in addition to any others you would gain.
                  * Gangers. Your Gangers are known as Brethren. When promoted to Elite, the Ganger gains either the Deacon or Firebrand ability listed for Champions above.
                  * Juves. Your Juves are known as Bonepickers. Up to two of them can be upgraded to Zealots for a cost of +5 credits each. If they are upgraded to a Ganger later, they retain this upgrade, including its cost and limits.
                  * Zealot: Fanatical. The Zealot is immune to the effects of the FEAR keyword.
                  * Ridge Walkers. You cannot purchase any Ridgerunners.

House Cawdor Unique Battlekit
                  * Asbestos Mandilion. The equipped model gains NEGATE FIRE.
                  * Autogun Polearm. BLOCK, CUMBERSOME, TWO-HANDED
                  * Strike: Melee
                  * Shoot: 24”, AUTOMATIC 2, -1 INJURY DICE, ASSAULT
                  * Blunderbuss Polearm. BLOCK, CUMBERSOME, TWO-HANDED
                  * Strike: Melee
                  * Shoot: 10”, SHRAPNEL

Battlekit
The Bomb Delivery Rat is equipped with its Bomb and nothing else. It is always considered to be equipped with a Close Combat Weapon.


Abilities
                  * Expendable. The Bomb Delivery Rat is not counted as part of your Warband for the purposes of Morale.
                  * Kaboom. The Bomb Delivery Rat can trigger its bomb at any point during its Activation or when an enemy comes within 3” of it. This does not require a Success Roll. When detonated, the explosion is treated as a BLAST 3” weapon targeting the rat model. Models within 1” of the rat hit by the explosion roll their injuries with +1 DICE. The rat is automatically a casualty.
                  * Skitter. The Bomb Delivery Rat has +1 DICE to all Climb, Dash, Jump, and Diving Charge Success Rolls.
                  * Tiny. Injury rolls made against the Bomb Delivery Rat have +1 DICE.

                  * Unsanctioned Psyker. The Master of Shadow gains the PSYKER 2 Keyword. Select one House Dalaque Psychic Discipline. The Master of Shadow may purchase up to four powers from that Discipline, and must purchase at least one. It must be equipped with exactly one PSYCHIC Weapon.

                  * Phantom: Unsanctioned Psyker. The Phantom gains the PSYKER 1 Keyword. Select one House Dalaque Psychic Discipline. The Phantom may purchase up to three powers from that Discipline, and must purchase at least one. It must be equipped with exactly one PSYCHIC Weapon.
                  * Gangers. Your Gangers are known as Ghosts. When promoted to Elite, the Ganger gains either the Nacht-Ghul or Phantom ability listed for Champions above.
                  * Juves. Your Juves are known as Shadows. Up to two of them can be upgraded to Psy-Gheists for +5 credits each. If a Psy-Gheist is later upgraded to a Ganger, it retains this upgrade and purchased powers, including their costs and limits.
                  * Psy-Gheist: Unsanctioned Psyker. Gains the PSYKER 1 Keyword. Select one House Dalaque Psychic Discipline. The Psy-Gheist may purchase up to two powers from that Discipline, and must purchase at least one. It must be equipped with exactly one PSYCHIC Weapon.

House Delaque Unique Battlekit
                  * Flechette Pistol. 12”, +1 DICE if shot from behind any terrain obstacles, -1 INJURY DICE, ASSAULT, PISTOL, SHRAPNEL
                  * Digital Laser. 6”, PISTOL, -1 INJURY DICE, takes no hands, can be used in addition to any other attacks the equipped model makes, but can be used only once per battle.
                  * Displacer Field. Attacks against a Spyker with this equipment have -1 DICE to Hit.
                  * Web Gauntlet. Melee, +1 INJURY DICE, IGNORE ARMOUR, NONLETHAL, STUN MARKERS, STUN. Enemies cannot Retreat from the wielder.
Darkness Psychic Discipline
                  1. A Perfect Void (3 credits, Effect). No Range, Self, End of Turn, Injury rolls against the caster have -1 INJURY MODIFIER, stacking with armour up to -3.
                  2. Cacophony of Silence (5 credits, Effect). No Range, Self, Next Activation, the caster and any allies within 6” of it have the STEALTH Keyword.
                  3. Cloak of Whispers (5 credits, Effect). 12”, Self or One Ally, Target Activation, enemy models do not get free attacks against the target when it Retreats, and the target can move out of Melee Combat using its Standard Move, Charge, or Dash.
                  4. Eternal Slumber (1 Glory, Attack). 12”, +2 INJURY DICE, IGNORE ARMOUR, PSYCHIC, RISKY. This attack can only be used on a Down enemy.
                  5. Penumbral Mirror (10 credits, Effect). 12”, One Ally and One Enemy that are within 12” of one another, Immediate, remove the target ally from the battlefield then place it on the battlefield anywhere within 3” of the target enemy.
                  6. Sight Blight (3 credits, Effect). 18”, One Enemy, Target Activation, the target has -1 DICE to Hit with all attacks until the end of its next Activation.
Delusion Psychic Discipline
                  1. Deceitful Thoughts (5 credits, Effect). 8”, One Enemy, Immediate, the target must move D3” in the direction of your choice. This movement can bring the target into or out of melee, and counts as a Charge and/or Retreat as appropriate. This power cannot be used while in close combat.
                  2. Ghost and Shadow (3 credits, Effect). 18”, Up to Three Enemies, End of Turn, the targets cannot make attacks that are not made during their Activation.
                  3. Opprobrious Curse (10 credits, Effect). -1 DICE, 3”, Each Enemy, each target must roll on the Injury table, with IGNORE ARMOUR.
                  4. Seen Unseen (5 credits, Effect). 24”, One Enemy, Target Activation, the target counts as an “enemy model” to all of its allies, and it treats those allies as “enemy models.” This only affects what targets it can choose, which models its abilities affect, etc. For instance, it must Retreat in order to move away from its former allies, though they do not have to attack it if it does if they do not want to.
                  5. Spatial Psychosis (5 credits, Effect). 18”, One Enemy, Immediate, the target falls Down. This is not a result on the Injury chart and does not cause any BLOOD MARKERS.
                  6. Suicidal Embrace (2 Glory, Effect). 8”, One Enemy, the target must make an attack with one melee weapon of your choice that it is wielding against itself.
Madness Psychic Discipline
                  1. Existential Barrage (3 credits, Effect). Melee, One Enemy, Immediate, the target must move D6” direct away from the caster. This movement can bring the target into or out of melee, and counts as a Charge and/or Retreat as appropriate.
                  2. Terrible Truths (5 credits, Effect). No Range, No Target, End of Turn, your opponent has -1 DICE to their Morale Test at the end of this Turn, if any.
                  3. Psychoteric Lure (7 credits, Effect). 18”, One Enemy, Immediate, the target must move D6” directly towards the caster. This movement can bring the target into or out of melee, and counts as a Charge and/or Retreat as appropriate.
                  4. Cyclopean Gaze (5 credits, Effect). 18”, One Enemy, Special, if the target has not yet activated this Turn, it cannot be chosen to activate this Turn until D3 of its owner’s other models have been activated, or no models not under a similar effect are available.
                  5. Craven Howl (5 credits, Effect). -1 DICE, 18”, One Down Enemy, Target Activation, the Target cannot stand up from Down.
                  6. Unrememberable (1 Glory, Effect). No Range, Self, Special, enemies cannot target the caster with ranged attacks or Charges. Weapons with the Keyword BLAST affect it as normal if it is in the radius of the weapon. This effect ends if the caster moves from its exact position in any way, it makes a Ranged Attack, or an enemy model comes within 1.5” of it.

House Escher Unique Battlekit
If another faction gains access to House Escher Chem Equipment for any reason, they treat it as CONSUMABLE.
                  * Bad Blood. When this model is hit in melee, if any BLOOD MARKERs were suffered, the attacker suffers 1 BLOOD MARKER as well. A model with NEGATE GAS ignores this effect.
                  * Brain Lock. The model cannot be affected by PSYCHIC attacks or abilities.
                  * Cutter. The equipped model is mounted on a hover bike. The rider gains the LARGE Keyword if it is not already LARGE, as well as the VEHICLE Keyword, and a movement speed of 10” and FLYING, but it has only one hand to carry other weapons, though it can hold two-handed weapons with Shield Combo.                   * Turbo Boost. The rider has +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls.
                  * Hyper. The model has +2” movement and +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls, but suffers -1 DICE to Hit with all attacks.
                  * Jolt. The model treats the first Down result of the battle as a Minor Hit.
                  * Night Night. The model does not need to make a Casualty roll at the end of battle if it was taken Out of Action. An elite model instead rolls twice on the Elite Trauma table and you choose one of the results.
                  * Nightshade Chem-Thrower. 10”, AUTOMATIC 2, -1 INJURY DICE, FLAMETHROWER, IGNORE ARMOUR, -1 INJURY DICE, GAS
                  * Puke. The model has NEGATE GAS.
                  * Venom Claw. Melee, GAS, MAIN HAND ONLY
                  * Wide-Eye. The model ignores penalties to its attack Success Rolls besides Cover, Long Range, and BLOOD MARKERS (including INFECTION and STUN), as well as penalties or limits to the range of its ranged weapons.

Gang: House Orlock  
                  * Allegiance. Your Warband counts as either an Imperial or Outlaw Warband, your choice upon Warband creation.
                  * Legendary Names. Keep track of how many Glorious Deeds each of your GANGER models has scored. If they are ELITE, they gain additional skills, chosen in the same manner as when gained through XP, when they have scored 2, 4, 7, 10, 14, and 18 Glorious Deeds. If a model is promoted to ELITE, it immediately gains the appropriate number of skills.
                  * Leader. Your Gang Leader is known as a Road Champion and gains the following ability:
                  * Living Legend. Whenever the Road Champion scores a Glorious Deed, you gain twice as much Glory.
                  * Champions. Each of your Champions is either a Arms Master or Road Sergeant, gaining the corresponding ability below:
                  * Arms Master: Rule of Iron. The Arms Master gains the LEADER Keyword.
                  * Road Sergeant: Gas Up. The Road Sergeant and GANGER allies within 4” of it have +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls.
                  * Gangers. Your Gangers are known as Gunners, and cannot choose the Bruiser Speciality. When promoted to Elite, the Ganger gains either the Watcher or Stormcaller ability listed for Champions above.
                  * Juves. You Juves are known as Greenhorns. Up to two of them can be upgraded to Wreckers for +5 credits each. If a Wrecker is later upgraded to a Ganger, it retains this upgrade, including its cost and limits.
                  * Wrecker: Jump Booster. Once during its Activation, when the Wrecker (any single Movement or Dash Action), it can use this ability to gain a 12” movement speed and FLYING during that movement. After this movement, it must make a Risky Success Roll, modified depending on how far they moved. On a failure, the Wrecker is knocked Down and must roll on the Injury table.
                  * 0-3”: +2 DICE.
                  * 4-6”: +1 DICE.
                  * 7-9”: No modifier.
                  * 10-12”: -1 DICE.
                  * Road Warriors. You can purchase up to 3 Ridgerunners.

Other Gangs
Gang: Ash Waste Nomads  
                  * Summoning. At the start of each battle, roll a number of D6s equal to the number of your Elite models participating. Set those dice aside, keeping their results. As an Action with no Success Roll, your Elite models can spend one of these dice to summon a creature to aid them. The benefit depends on the result showing on the spent die:
                  * 1: Styr'ghar. Remove the model from the table, then place it on the table anywhere that is 12” or further from enemy models.
                  * 2: Dae'ghar. Until the end of the current Turn, enemies treat the ground within 5” of the model as Dangerous Terrain.
                  * 3: Tyr. Each enemy within 3” of the model when summoned cannot make attacks against enemies that Retreat from them, and they have -1 DICE to hit with all attacks, each until the end of the current Turn.
                  * 4: Char'ghar. This model’s attacks have FIRE during this Activation.
                  * 5: Vau'ghar. This model or any one other chosen ally can make an additional attack the next time it takes the Fight Action this Turn.
                  * 6: Myr'ghar. This model’s melee attacks ignore Armour during this Activation.
                  * Leader. Your Gang Leader is known as a Chieftain and gains the following ability:
                  * Swift. The Chieftain’s movement speed is increased by 1”.
                  * Champions. Each of your Champions is either a Watcher or Stormcaller, gaining the corresponding ability below:
                  * Watcher: Accurate. The Watcher has an additional +1 to its Ranged skill, for a total of +2.
                  * Stormcaller (LIMIT: 1): Call the Storm. Ranged attacks made against the Stormcaller or any of its allies within 6” of it have -1 DICE.
                  * Gangers. Your Gangers are known as Warriors. When promoted to Elite, the Ganger gains either the Watcher or Stormcaller ability listed for Champions above.
                  * Juves. You Juves are known as Scouts. Up to two of them can be upgraded to Dust Runners for a cost of +5 credits each. If they are upgraded to a Ganger later, they retain this upgrade, including its cost and limits.
                  * Dust Runner: Swift. The Dust Runner’s movement speed is increased by 2”.
                  * Beast Riders. You cannot purchase any Ridgerunners.
                  * Ash Waste Chems. You cannot purchase any Chem Equipment from the Necromunda Gang Shared Armoury.

In addition, the first 3” of this move must be in a straight line directly away from its starting position, if it is possible for it to do so. If the model is Down at the start of the Activation, it will stand up if it can do so and must then attempt to move 3” in a straight line away from its starting position.

                  * Venom Sacs. This model’s attacks with GAS have +1 INJURY DICE and RISKY.

Gang: Corpsegrinder Cult  
                  * Chaos Cult. Your Warband counts as a Chaos Warband instead of an Outlaw Warband, and generally recruits mercenaries as such, except you cannot recruit any Daemon, Heretic Astartes (besides Khorne Berzerker), Dark Mechanicum, or Skaven Mercenaries, but you can recruit Hive and Hive Beast Outlaw Mercenaries. Your patron can be a Gang Overlord or the Khorne Shared Patron.
                  * Blessed By The Lord Of Skin & Sinew. As an Action with a Risky Success Roll, any of your models can attempt to invoke the dark gods. This Success Roll is made at +1 DICE if the model has taken an enemy Out of Action during this battle, or +2 DICE if it has taken an enemy ELITE Out of Action during the battle. Juves roll at an additional -1 DICE. On a success, remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from the model and it gains +1 DICE to Hit and to Injure with all melee attacks during this Activation. On a failed roll, it must roll on the Injury chart.
                  * Leader. Your Gang Leader is known as a Butcher, and gains the following abilities:
                  * First to the Frey. When the Butcher charges, roll 2D6 instead of 1D6 and then add the highest of the two dice to their charge move.
                  * Champions. You can recruit up to 3 Champions. They are known as either Cutters or Rippers, gaining the corresponding ability below:
                  * Cutter: Dervish. The Cutter ignores penalties for attacking with off-hand weapons.
                  * Ripper: Powerful. The Ripper gains the STRONG Keyword.
                  * Gangers. Your Gangers are known as Skinners. When promoted to Elite, the Ganger gains the Cutter or Ripper ability listed for Champions above.
                  * Juves. Your Juves are known as Initiates. Up to three of them can be upgraded to Fresh Meat for a cost of +5 credits each. If they are upgraded to a Ganger later, they retain this upgrade, including its cost and limits.
                  * Fresh Meat: Delicious. When the Fresh Meat is taken Out of Action, one of your GANGER models of your choice gains a BLESSING MARKER.

Gang: Delegation  
                  * Delegates. Choose any one Alliance from the Necromunda Gang Campaign Shop besides Imperial House Alliance, Iron Guild Alliance, or Narco Lords Alliance. You have the benefits of that Alliance. You recruit the Delegation for that Alliance, but those models cost credits instead of Glory, as described below, and you must pay for them normally. They come with no equipment, but you can purchase equipment for them normally, and they can be equipped with any weapons, armour, or equipment from the Delegation Armoury or the Necromunda Gang Shared Armoury.


You cannot purchase any Alliances from the Campaign Shop.


Your Gang Leader is replaced as the leader of your Warband with a model that depends on your Delegation. That model gains the LEADER Keyword. Your choice also determines if your Warband counts as an Outlaw Warband or an Imperium Warband.

                  * Refractor Field. The equipped model treats Down results as Minor Hits (does not affect Down results that replace Out of Action via TOUGH Keyword). Cannot be combined with a Displacer Field.

                  * Sling Gun. 24”, ARMOUR PIERCING 1 on Critical Hit, TWO-HANDED




Gang: Ironhead Squat Prospectors  
                  * Kin. Your warband counts as a Xenos (Leagues of Votann) Warband, and your non-Mercenary GANGER models gain the VOTANN Keyword. It also counts as your choice of an Outlaw Warband or an Imperium Warband. Your patron cannot be a Gang Overlord, but it can be a Guildmaster, described in the Leagues of Votann Warband.
                  * Squats. Your Gang Leader, Gang Champion, Ganger, and Juve models have -1” to their movement speed, but the first -1 of their Armour, if any, has IMPERVIOUS, and they cost 10 fewer credits.
                  * Leader. Your Gang Leader is known as a Charter Master and gains the following ability:
                  * Prospecting. The Charter Master has the INFILTRATOR Keyword. At the start of each battle, you can give the INFILTRATOR Keyword to up to two other non-ELITE models in your Warband for that battle. They are deployed at the same time as your Charter Master, and must begin within 3” of your Charter Master.
                  * Champions. Each of your Champions is a Drill Master and gain the following ability:
                  * Heavy Drilling. The Drill Master has the STRONG Keyword.
                  * Gangers. Your Gangers are known as Drill-Kyn. When promoted to Elite, the Ganger gains the Heavy Drilling ability listed for Champions above.
                  * Juves. You Juves are known as Diggers, and have +0 Ranged Skill and +0 Melee Skill, but cost 10 additional credits (for a total of +0). Up to two of them can be upgraded to Burrowers for +5 credits each. If a Burrower is later upgraded to a Ganger, it retains this upgrade, including its cost and limits.
                  * Burrower: Drill Up. The Burrower gains the DEEP STRIKE (TUNNEL) Keyword.
                  * Trikers. You cannot purchase any Ridgerunners.

                  * Svenotar Trike. The equipped model is mounted on a trike. It also has the Built-In Weapon and Turbo-Boost abilities.
                  * Built-In Weapon. The rider can be equipped with up to 1 additional non-Grenade ranged weapon, which does not take any hands. The rider ignores the HEAVY Keyword of this weapon, and it can be used alongside any other ranged weapon when the rider takes the Shoot Action.

Gang: Palanite Enforcers  
                  * Imperial Authority. Your Warband counts as an Imperium Warband instead of an Outlaw Warband. Your patron must be the Administratum Prefectus Shared Patron.
                  * Ruthless Efficiency. Your GANGER models do not roll to randomly select a target when making ranged attacks against enemies that are engaged in close combat, instead always targeting the chosen target.
                  * Leader. Your Gang Leader is known as a Captain and gains the following ability:
                  * Mark For Justice. As an Action with no roll required, the Captain can choose one enemy it can see. Until the end of the current Turn, your GANGER models have VICIOUS 10 with all attacks against that enemy.
                  * Champions. Each of your Champions is a Sergeant and gains the following ability:
                  * Heavy Hitter. The Sergeant has an additional +1 to its Melee skill (for a total of +2).
                  * Gangers. Your Gangers are known as Patrolmen. When promoted to Elite, the Ganger gains the Heavy Hitter ability listed for Champions above.
                  * Juves. You Juves are known as Rookies. Up to two of them can be upgraded to Haunts for +5 credits each, and up to two of them can be upgraded to Hotshots for +5 credits each. If a Hotshot is later upgraded to a Ganger, it retains this upgrade, including its cost and limits.
                  * Haunt: Sanctioned Psyker. Gains the PSYKER 1 and NO PROMOTION Keywords, and loses the New Kid ability. Select either the Telepathy or Telekinesis Shared Psychic Discipline. The Haunt may purchase up to three powers from that Discipline, and must purchase at least one. It must be equipped with exactly one PSYCHIC Weapon.
                  * Hotshot: Headstrong. When the Hotshot charges, roll 2D6 instead of 1D6 and then add the highest of the two dice to their charge move.

                  * Robust. When the Chief rolls on the Elite Trauma table, you can roll twice and choose either result.
                  * Champions. You can recruit up to 3 Champions. Your Champions are known as Hunters, Shamans, or Totem Warriors, gaining the corresponding ability below:
                  * Hunter: Accurate. The Hunter has an additional +1 Ranged Skill (for a total of +2).

                  * Gangers. Your Gangers are known as Braves. Up to to of your Ganger can be upgraded to Shaman Adepts for free, as long as you have a Shaman in your Warband, granting them the following abilities:
                  * Shamanistic Promotion. If your Shaman is removed from your Warband for any reason, you can replace it with one of your Shaman Adepts. The new Shaman gains all of the rules and statistics of a Shaman, but is otherwise considered the same model.
                  * Wyrd in Training. A Shaman Adept gains PSYKER 0, must take exactly one power from the Spirit Lore Psychic Discipline, and must be equipped with a PSYCHIC weapon in order to use any powers.
                  * Juves. Your Juves are known as Youngbloods.

                  1. Bloated. The model gains the LARGE keyword and treats Down results as Minor Hits (does not affect Down results that replace Out of Action via TOUGH Keyword).
                  2. Camouflage. The model gains the STEALTH Keyword.
                  3. Horns of the Beast. When the Mutant successfully Charges one or more enemies, one of them gets a BLOOD MARKER.
                  4. Extra Legs. The Mutant cannot be forced to move by any effect an enemy model causes.
                  5. Mutant Limb. The Mutant gains the following weapon: Melee, +1 INJURY DICE, HELD
                  6. Extra Arm. The Mutant gains an extra arm, which can carry melee and ranged weapons as normal. It can make an additional attack with any weapon in that arm without off-hand penalties. If it cannot normally be equipped, this hand can be equipped with weapons, a shield, or any battlekit that takes a single hand from the Chaos Cult Armoury.

                  * Disintegration Matrix. 12”, ARMOUR PIERCING 2
                  * Heavy Hunting Rig. -3 INJURY MODIFIER, but does not stack with a Shield. The model gains the LARGE and VEHICLE Keywords.
                  * Hunting Rig. -2 INJURY MODIFIER. The model gains the VEHICLE Keyword.
                  * Laser Gauntlet. 12”, ASSAULT, takes up no hands.
                  * Mirror Shield. Counts as a Shield, including for Shield Combo, -1 INJURY MODIFIER, and has the following weapon built in:
                  * Melee. Causes no injury. On a hit, the target cannot make attacks against enemies that Retreat from it until the end of the current Turn.
                  * Monomolecular Sword. Melee, ARMOUR PIERCING 2
                  * Naga Blade. Melee, +1 INJURY DICE, CRITICAL
                  * Sthenian Hunting Rig. -3 INJURY MODIFIER, but does not stack with a Shield. The model gains the LARGE and VEHICLE Keywords. This armour has the following weapon built in:
                  * Sthenian Claws. Melee, CRITICAL. Takes no hands and can be used to make an extra attack when the wielder takes the Fight Action.
                  * Yeld Hunting Rig. -2 INJURY MODIFIER. The model gains 8” movement and the FLYING, INFILTRATOR, STEALTH, and VEHICLE Keywords.

	8”/Flying
	N/A
	-1
	0
	25mm
	

Battlekit
The Caryatid Prime cannot be equipped with any weapons, armour, or equipment. It is always considered to be equipped with a Close Combat Weapon.


Abilities
                  * Precognition (Psychic). All attacks against the Caryatid Prime, and its owner if the Caryatid Prime is within 6” of it, are made at -1 DICE to Hit.
                  * Pet. Select one GANGER model in your Warband at the start of the battle. The Caryatid Prime forms a FIRETEAM with that model. That model is its owner.

Name

	Melee
	Armour
	Base
	Mind-Locked Wyrd
	6”/Infantry

	+0
	-1
	25-32mm
	

Battlekit
The Mind-Locked Wyrd is equipped with Standard Armour and a Force Rod. You cannot change its battlekit in any way.
                  * Force Rod. Melee, +1 INJURY DICE against targets with DAEMON or PSYKER Keyword, PSYCHIC, HELD, MAIN HAND ONLY


The Mind-Locked Wyrd knows up to 30 credits worth of powers of your choice from the following list, to a maximum of five total powers, chosen when you recruit it. They are free and do not count towards your credit limit.
                  * Assail (10 credits, Attack). 12”, ASSAULT, +1 INJURY MODIFIER, PSYCHIC, SHRAPNEL, RISKY
                  * Bio Lightning (5 credits, Attack). 12”, +1 INJURY DICE, PSYCHIC, RISKY
                  * Cacophony of Silence (5 credits, Effect). No Range, Self, Next Activation, the caster and any allies within 6” of it have the STEALTH Keyword.
                  * Craven Howl (5 credits, Effect). -1 DICE, 18”, One Down Enemy, Target Activation, the Target cannot stand up from Down.
                  * Crush (15 credits, Attack). 24”, IGNORE ARMOUR, PSYCHIC, RISKY
                  * Cyclopean Gaze (5 credits, Effect). 18”, One Enemy, Special, if the target has not yet activated this Turn, it cannot be chosen to activate this Turn until D3 of its owner’s other models have been activated, or no models not under a similar effect are available.
                  * Flame Breath (20 credits, Attack). 12”, AUTOMATIC 2, -1 INJURY DICE, IGNORE COVER, IGNORE LONG RANGE, IGNORE ARMOUR, FIRE, PSYCHIC, RISKY
                  * Opprobrious Curse (10 credits, Effect). -1 DICE, 3”, Each Enemy, each target must roll on the Injury table, with IGNORE ARMOUR.
                  * Penumbral Mirror (10 credits, Effect). 12”, One Ally and One Enemy that are within 12” of one another, Immediate, remove the target ally from the battlefield then place it on the battlefield anywhere within 3” of the target enemy.
                  * Psychic Shriek (5 credits, Attack). Melee, IGNORE ARMOUR, PSYCHIC, RISKY, STUN
                  * Puppet Master (10 credits, Effect). -1 DICE, 12”, One Enemy, Immediate, the target must move D6” in the direction of your choice. This movement allows free attacks as if it were a Retreat Action. This power cannot be used while in close combat.
                  * Seen Unseen (5 credits, Effect). 24”, One Enemy, Target Activation, the target counts as an “enemy model” to all of its allies, and it treats those allies as “enemy models.” This only affects what targets it can choose, which models its abilities affect, etc. For instance, it must Retreat in order to move away from its former allies, though they do not have to attack it if it does if they do not want to.


Keywords
ELITE, FEAR, GANGER, PSYKER 2, TOUGH

                  * Shock Stave. Melee, BLOCK, STUN, TWO-HANDED
                  * Refractor Field. The Pyrocean Lord treats Down results as Minor Hits (does not affect Down results that replace Out of Action via TOUGH Keyword).


The Onmyodo Telepath knows the following psychic powers:
                  1. Invisibility (Effect). No Range, Self, Special, if any piece of scenery the caster is touching can block line of sight in any direction, even partially, regardless of whether an enemy currently has a clear line of sight, then enemies cannot target the caster with ranged attacks or Charges. Weapons with the Keyword BLAST affect it as normal if it is in the radius of the weapon. This effect ends if this caster moves from its exact position in any way, it makes a Ranged Attack, or an enemy model comes within 1.5” of it.
                  2. Psychic Shriek (Attack). Melee, IGNORE ARMOUR, PSYCHIC, RISKY, STUN
                  3. Puppet Master (Effect). -1 DICE, 12”, One Enemy, Immediate, the target must move D6” in the direction of your choice. This movement allows free attacks as if it were a Retreat Action. This power cannot be used while in close combat.
                  4. Terrify (Effect). Melee, One Enemy, Immediate, the target must move D6” directly away from the caster. This movement can bring the target into or out of melee, and counts as a Charge and/or Retreat as appropriate. A model that ignores the FEAR Keyword is immune.


Abilities
                  * Onmyoto Coven. The Onmyoto Telepath forms a Fireteam with an Onmyoto Null, if both are fielded.
                  * Telepath Control Collar. When the Onmyodo Telepath uses its first psychic power during each of its Activations, you can choose whether it has PSYKER 1, or PSYKER 2 and PERILOUS ±2 with all powers, until you make this choice again.

ARTIFICIAL, GANGER, NEGATE FEAR, NO PROMOTION, TOUGH
Pirate Crew
Pirate Crew
  

Pirate Crew Special Rules
Backgrounds. Whenever you recruit a PIRATE model, choose one of the following Backgrounds for it, paying the additional cost if any:
                  * Beastman (+0 credits). When the Beastman successfully Charges one or more enemies, give one of those enemies a BLOOD MARKER. The Beastman gains the BEASTMEN Keyword.
                  * Dark Eldar (+5 credits). The Dark Eldar has +1” movement speed. It can spend 1 BLOOD MARKER from among non-ARTIFICIAL enemy models to gain +1 DICE to a Hit with any one attack or +1 DICE to Dash. Limit once per Success Roll. The Dark Eldar can take battlekit from the Drukhari Battlekit list in addition to the Pirate Crew Armoury. It can use Incubus or Wych Only battlekit. The Dark Eldar gains the DRUKHARI Keyword.
                  * Death Worlder (+0 credits). The Death Worlder has NEGATE FIRE and NEGATE GAS.
                  * Eldar Corsair (+5 credits). The Eldar Corsair has +1” movement speed, and +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls. It can take battlekit from the Aeldari Battlekit list in addition to the Pirate Crew Armoury, but limited by the Limited Arsenal special rule of the Corsairs Warband Variant. The Eldar Corsair gains the AELDARI Keyword.
                  * Feral Worlder (+0 credits). The Feral Worlder gains the STEALTH Keyword, it Ignores Difficult Terrain, and it has +1 DICE to all Climb Success Rolls.
                  * Forge Worlder (+0 credits). The first Down result the Forge Worlder suffers each battle is ignored. (Does not apply to the TOUGH Keyword.)
                  * Gretchin Freebooter (-5 credits). The Gretchin Freebooter has +1 Ranged Skill, -1 Melee Skill, and the ORK and STEALTH Keywords. The Gretchin Freebooter cannot carry any HEAVY battlekit, and Charging is always a Risky Success Roll for it. If it gains the STRONG Keyword, the two cancel out. It can also take Grot Blastas, melee weapons, armour, and equipment from the Ork Battlekit list in addition to the Pirate Crew Armoury. It gains the ORK Keyword.
                  * Hive Worlder (+0 credits). Friendly models ignore the Hive Worlder when making ranged attacks against enemies it is in melee with.
                  * Kroot (+5 credits). The Kroot has +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls and the STEALTH Keyword. It can take Kroot Only battlekit from the T’au Empire Battlekit list in addition to the Pirate Crew Armoury. The Kroot gains the KROOT Keyword.
                  * Kin (-5 credits). The Kin has -1” movement speed, but the first -1 of its Armour, if any, has IMPERVIOUS. The Kin can take battlekit from the Leagues of Votann Battlekit list in addition to the Pirate Crew Armoury. It gains the VOTANN Keyword.
                  * Noble (+0 credits). Other friendly models have +1 DICE to Hit with melee attacks against targets the Noble is also in melee with. This effect does not stack.
                  * Ogryn (+35 credits, or +5 for Pirate Captain). The Ogryn gains the LARGE, LIMITED POTENTIAL, STRONG, and TOUGH Keywords.
                  * Ork Freebooter (+0 credits). The Ork Freebooter has +1 Melee Skill and -1 Ranged Skill. It can take battlekit from the Ork Battlekit list in addition to the Pirate Crew Armoury. It can use Kommando-only, Mek-only, and Stormboy-only battlekit. The Ork Freebooter gains the ORK Keyword.
                  * Piscean (+0 credits). The Piscean has the SKIRMISHER Keyword and ignores all penalties to its attack Success Rolls besides Cover, Long Range, and BLOOD MARKERS (including INFECTION and STUN), as well as penalties or limits to the range of its ranged weapons.
                  * Ratling (+0 credits). The Ratling has +1 Ranged Skill, -1 Melee Skill, the SKIRMISHER Keyword, and +1 DICE to all Climb Success Rolls. It cannot carry any HEAVY battlekit. If it gains the STRONG Keyword, the two cancel out.
                  * Slanni Brave (-5 credits). The Slanni Brave has VICIOUS 11 with melee attacks against a target with 1 or more BLOOD MARKERS. It also gains the SLANN Keyword. It can take battlekit from the Slanni Battlekit list in addition to the Pirate Crew Armoury.
                  * Slanni Brute (+20 credits). When the Slanni Brute makes a melee attack with a HEAVY melee weapon, it can target two enemy models it is in melee combat with. The attack’s Success Roll is made only once, hitting both on a success. It also gains the LARGE, SLANN, and STRONG Keywords, +1 Melee Skill, and Injury rolls made against it have -1, stacking with Armour up to -3. It can take battlekit from the Slanni Battlekit list in addition to the Pirate Crew Armoury.
                  * Slanni Skirmisher (-5 credits). The Slanni Skirmisher has +2 DICE to Climb and Jump Success Rolls, and it never suffers injury rolls from falling. It also has +1 DICE to Diving Charge Success Rolls and does not fall Down or suffer an Injury roll due to failing a Diving Charge. The Slanni Skirmisher gains the SKIRMISHER and SLANN Keywords and has -1 Melee Skill. It can take battlekit from the Slanni Battlekit list in addition to the Pirate Crew Armoury, and counts as a Skirmisher for that purpose.
                  * Tarellian (+0 credits). The Tarellian has +2” movement speed.
                  * Voidborn (+0 credits). The Voidborn begins each battle with a BLESSING MARKER.
                  * Zoat (+40 credits). The Zoat gains the FEAR and LARGE Keywords. It cannot wear non-shield armour, but it has armoured scales that provide it with -2 Armour.


If any of your models can take battlekit from another Armoury, you must obey the highest LIMIT among armories a model has access to for each piece of battlekit.


Specialties. Whenever you recruit a PIRATE model, you may optionally choose one of the following Specialties for it, paying the additional cost:
                  * Cannoneer (+15 credits, LIMIT: 1). When the Cannoneer makes a ranged attack with a TWO-HANDED weapon during its Activation, it has +1 INJURY DICE. This applies only to the first attack if it can attack multiple times in one Activation.
                  * Duelist (+10 credits, LIMIT: 2). Whenever an enemy Charges the Duelist, the Duelist can make a single melee attack with a non-HEAVY weapon against that enemy before it takes any further actions.
                  * Grenadier (+5 credits). Increase the range of THROWN weapons used by the Grenadier by 4”. Attacks made beyond the weapon’s normal range, before this benefit, are made at -1 DICE.
                  * Grot Boss (+5 credits, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only, Gretchin Freebooter or Ork Freebooter Only). Gretchin Freebooters within 6” of the Grot Boss, including the Grot Boss itself if it is a Gretchin, can Charge as normal.
                  * Heavy (+10 credits, LIMIT: 3, non-Ogryn Only). The Heavy gains the STRONG Keyword.
                  * Heretek (+10 credits, LIMIT: 2, Death Worlder, Forge Worlder, Hive Worlder, Noble, or Voidborn Only). The Heretek gains the MECHANICUS Keyword and has an additional hand for both melee and ranged combat. It can attack with an additional weapon whenever it takes the Melee or Shoot Action, without off-hand penalties. A Heretek can also choose the Mechanic Specialty without counting towards its limit of Specialties, following all other limits.
                  * Hunter (+5 credits, LIMIT: 1). This model has +1 DICE to Hit enemies that have already been Activated this turn.
                  * Knife Fighter (+10 credits, LIMIT: 3). The Knife Fighter has +1 INJURY DICE with Close Combat Weapons and Throwing Knives.
                  * Mechanic (+10 credits, LIMIT: 1). As an Action with a Success Roll, the Mechanic can attempt to repair one ARTIFICIAL, MECHANICUS, or VEHICLE ally, or an ally with -2 or better Armour (excluding shields) within 1” of it. On a success, remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from that ally, or 3 BLOOD MARKERS if the Success Roll was a Critical Success.
                  * Pistoleer (+10 credits, LIMIT: 2). The Pistoleer has the following benefits while armed with Ranged Weapons with the PISTOL Keyword:
                  * If it is equipped with 2 Weapons with the PISTOL Keyword, it can take a Shoot ACTION with one and then immediately take a Shoot ACTION with the other.
                  * Add the IGNORE OFF-HAND WEAPON Keyword to any weapons that have the PISTOL Keyword.
                  * Psyker (+5 credits +cost of powers, LIMIT: 1, Elite Only). The Psyker gains the PSYKER 1 Keyword. Select one Shared Psychic Discipline. The Psyker may purchase up to three powers from that Discipline. It can only use Psychic Powers while wielding a PSYCHIC weapon. This Specialty is modified in the following ways, depending on the model’s Keywords:
                  * AELDARI: Must choose Runes of Battle, Runes of Fate, or Runes of Fortune instead of a Share Discipline.
                  * ORK Must choose powers from the Ork Psychic Powers list described in the Orks faction instead of a discipline.
                  * SLANNI: Must choose Celestial Discipline or Primal Discipline instead of a Shared Discipline.
                  * VOTANN: May choose the Skeinwrought Discipline instead of a Shared Discipline.
                  * Reaver (+10 credits, LIMIT: 3). Once during each Turn, if the Reaver Downs or takes an enemy Out of Action in melee, it can move up to 3”, and can enter melee as if it Charged, then it can make a single extra melee attack.
                  * Rigger (+5 credits). The Rigger has +1 DICE to all Climb, Jump, and Diving Charge Success Rolls, as well as Success Rolls to avoid falling off of raised terrain.
                  * Sapper (+10 credits, LIMIT: 1). The Sapper has NEGATE MINED. As an Action with a Success Roll with +1 DICE, the Sapper can mine a piece of terrain they alone are touching, no bigger than 8” x 8” (an 8” section of trench, a wall, a tree, a building etc). If successful, the terrain piece is now MINED.
                  * Sneak (+10 credits, or +5 for Feral Worlder, Gretchin Freebooter, or Kroot, LIMIT: 3). The Sneak gains the INFILTRATOR and STEALTH Keywords.
                  * Tactician (+10 credits, LIMIT: 1). The Tactician gains the FIRETEAM Keyword, and can form a fireteam with any one other model in your Warband, chosen at the start of each battle.
                  * Void Terror (+10 credits, LIMIT: 1, Non-Zoat Elite Only). The Void Terror gains the FEAR Keyword.


Black Market Acquisitions. You can purchase battlekit from the Campaign Shop without needing permission from Exploration or other rules to do so, so long as its cost is at most an amount of Glory equal to the number of battles you have already fought (or 1 for every 100 credits over 700 your Warband is Worth for a one-off battle). In addition, you can purchase battlekit from the Campaign Shop of any other Faction besides Chaos Daemons, Orks (except for ORK models), Necrons, and Tyranids. You must follow all other limits, but replace any Faction Keyword in their descriptions with PIRATE.


Pirate Crew Patron
Your patron must be the Pirate Lord Shared Patron.

Pirate Crew Armoury
Basic Ranged Weapons
Autogun                20 credits (LIMIT: 2)

Pirate Crew Unique Battlekit
                  * Crackthorn Whip. Melee, CRITICAL, STUN, VICIOUS 10, WHIP 3”, MAIN HAND ONLY
                  * Cyber Parrot. Once per battle, when an enemy in close combat with this model makes a melee attack, you can cause the attack to be made at -1 DICE to Hit.
                  * Pirate Bike. The equipped model is mounted on a bike. The rider gains the LARGE Keyword if it is not already LARGE, as well as the VEHICLE Keyword, a movement speed of 10”/Infantry, but has only one hand to carry other weapons, though it can hold two-handed weapons with Shield Combo. It also has the Outrider abilities.
                  * Outrider. The rider has +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls, but it cannot climb sheer surfaces.
                  * Pirate Trophy. The equipped model gains the FEAR Keyword.

Warband Variant: Chaos Pirates  
                  * Chaos Aligned. Your Warband counts as a Chaos Warband instead of an Outlaw Warband. Your Patron can optionally be the Chaos Warlord or Heretek Lord Shared Patron.
                  * Chaotic Backgrounds. You cannot select the Dark Eldar, Eldar Corsair, Gretchin Freebooter, Ork Freebooter, Slanni Brave, Slanni Brute, or Slanni Skirmisher Backgrounds, but you can select the following new Backgrounds in addition to the others that are available:
                  * Bloodgor (+5 credits). The Bloodgor has +1 Melee Skill and rolls 2D6 instead of 1D6 to determine Charge distance, taking the highest result. It gains the BEASTMEN and KHORNE Keywords. A Bloodgor with the Psyker Specialty must choose the Pyromancy Psychic Discipline.
                  * Fellgor (+5 credits). The Fellgor has +1 Melee Skill and when it is taken Out of Action, before removing it from the table, it can make a single melee attack against an enemy it is in close combat with. BLOOD MARKERS cannot be spent on this attack's Success Roll.  It gains the BEASTMEN Keyword.
                  * Pestigor (+5 credits). The Pestigor has +1 Melee Skill and treats Down results as Minor Hits (does not affect Down results that replace Out of Action via TOUGH Keyword), but has -1” movement speed. It gains the BEASTMEN and NURGLE Keywords. A Pestigor with the Psyker Specialty may choose the Warprot Psychic Discipline from the Chaos Daemons faction instead of a Shared Psychic Discipline.
                  * Skaven (-5 credits). Enemies do not get to make a free attack when the Skaven Retreats. It gains the SKAVEN Keyword, and can take battlekit from the Vermintide Armoury in addition to the Pirate Crew Armoury. A Skaven with the Psyker Specialty may choose the Ruin Psychic Discipline from the Vermintide faction instead of a Shared Psychic Discipline.
                  * Slaangor (+5 credits). The Slaangor has +1 Melee Skill and the first time during each Turn that it suffers any BLOOD MARKERS, if it is not Down, it can move up to 3”, and can move into or out of melee in this way, treating it as a Retreat or Charge as appropriate. It gains the BEASTMEN and SLAANESH Keywords. A Slanngor with the Psyker Specialty may choose the Soulstain Psychic Discipline from the Chaos Daemons faction instead of a Shared Psychic Discipline.
                  * Tzaangor (+5 credits). The Slaangor has +1 Melee Skill, +1 Ranged Skill, and the BEASTMEN, SKIRMISHER, and TZEENTCH Keywords. A Tzaangor with the Psyker Specialty may choose the Tzeentch Psychic Discipline from the Chaos Daemons faction instead of a Shared Psychic Discipline.
                  * Chaotic Specialties. You can choose any of the following Specialties in addition to those normally available.
                  * Fluxbray (+10 credits, LIMIT: 1, Beastmen Only). The model gains an extra arm, which can carry melee weapons as normal. It can make an additional attack with any weapon in that arm without off-hand penalties.
                  * Herder (+5 credits, LIMIT: 1). Other BEASTMEN allies within 3” of the Herder have VICIOUS 10 with all melee attacks.
Mercenaries
Mercenaries
Mercenaries are separated into four main categories: Imperial, Chaos, Xenos, and Outlaw. By default, a Warband of the corresponding type can take any Mercenaries from the appropriate category, except for Xenos who can only take Mercenaries that match their exact Faction.


Mercenaries are further categorized into sub-categories, but besides Xenos, this is for organizational purposes only.


Many Mercenaries have more specific limits or requirements, and some Mercenaries can be taken by Warbands of other categories.
Imperial Mercenaries
Imperial Mercenaries
Assassinorum Mercenaries
All are unavailable to Officio Assassinorum.
Max 1 per Warband:
                  * 0-1 Adamus Assassin (Cost: 6 Glory)
                  * 0-1 Callidus Assassin (Cost: 6 Glory)
                  * 0-1 Culexus Assassin (Cost: 6 Glory)

                  * 0-1 Vindicare Assassin (Cost: 6 Glory)

Assassinorum Mercenaries
Assassinorum Mercenaries
A Warband can only include a maximum of 1 of the following Mercenaries. An Officio Assassinorum Warband cannot take any of these Mercenaries.
0-1 Adamus Assassin (Cost: 6 Glory)
Name
	Movement
	Ranged
	Melee
	Armour
	Base

	8”/Infantry
	+2
	+2
	-1
	32mm
	

Battlekit
The Adamus Assassin is equipped with its Synskin Bodyglove, included in its statistic above, as well as a Nemesii Blade, a Needlespine Blaster, and Filter Plugs. You cannot change its battlekit in any way.
                  * Synskin Bodyglove. -1 INJURY MODIFIER, IMPERVIOUS
                  * Nemesii Blade. Melee, CRITICAL, ARMOUR PIERCING 1
                  * Needlespine Blaster. 12”, PISTOL, when used to make a ranged attack, the wielder can make one attack with each profile
                  * Spine: GAS
                  * Bolt: VICIOUS 11, CRITICAL
                  * Filter Plugs. The model has NEGATE GAS.


Abilities
                  * Death’s Artisan. The Adamus Assassin has +1 INJURY DICE with any attack it makes against an enemy with the TOUGH Keyword.
                  * Decapitation Strike. When the Adamus Assassin makes an attack with its Nemesii Blade during its Activation, if it has made no other melee attacks during this Activation, it can grant that attack +2 INJURY DICE. If it does, its Activation immediately ends after the attack.
                  * Fleet. The Adamus Assassin has +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls.
                  * Lightning Reflexes. Attacks made against the Adamus Assassin have -1 DICE to Hit.


Keywords
ASSASSINORUM, ELITE, INFILTRATOR, STEALTH, TOUGH

                  * Synskin Bodyglove. -1 INJURY MODIFIER, IMPERVIOUS

                  * Psyk-Out Grenades. 8”, IGNORE ARMOUR, +1 INJURY DICE against targets with DAEMON or PSYKER Keyword, BLAST 2”, IGNORE COVER, IGNORE LONG RANGE, PSYCHIC, ASSAULT, STUN, THROWN, FUMBLE
                  * Life-Draining Touch. Melee, IGNORE ARMOUR, +1 INJURY DICE against targets with DAEMON or PSYKER Keyword, PSYCHIC. If this attack causes any BLOOD MARKERS or takes the target Out of Action, remove one BLOOD MARKER from the attacker.
                  * Combat Helmet. The model has NEGATE SHRAPNEL.

Battlekit
The Eversor Assassin is equipped with a Synskin Bodyglove, included in its statistic above, an Executioner’s Pistol, Melta Bombs, a Neuro Gauntlet, and a Power Weapon. You cannot change its battlekit in any way.
                  * Synskin Bodyglove. -1 INJURY MODIFIER, IMPERVIOUS
                  * Needlespine Blaster. 12”, PISTOL, when used to make a ranged attack, the wielder can make one attack with each profile below
                  * Spine: GAS
                  * Bolt: VICIOUS 11, CRITICAL
                  * Melta Bombs. 6”, IGNORE ARMOUR, +1 INJURY DICE, ASSAULT, IGNORE COVER, IGNORE LONG RANGE, RISKY, THROWN, FUMBLE
                  * Neuro Gauntlet. Melee, IGNORE ARMOUR, STUN, causes 2 additional STUN MARKERS instead of 1 due to the STUN Keyword.
                  * Power Weapon. Melee, ARMOUR PIERCING 2, MAIN HAND ONLY


Abilities
                  * Deadly Demise. When the Eversor Assassin is taken Out of Action, each other model, enemy and friendly, within 3” must roll on the Injury table. Models that are in cover roll this injury with -1 DICE.
                  * Fleet. The Eversor Assassin has +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls.
                  * Frenzon. When the Eversor Assassin Charges, roll 2D6 instead of 1D6 and then add the highest of the two dice to its charge move.
                  * Lightning Reflexes. Attacks made against the Eversor Assassin have -1 DICE to Hit.
                  * Overkill. Once during each of its Activations, when the Eversor Assassin takes an enemy Out of Action or knocks it Down with a melee attack, the assassin can immediately move up to its movement speed, and can enter close combat as if Charging during this movement. If it is then within 1” of another enemy, it can make an additional melee attack against that enemy.


Keywords
ASSASSINORUM, ELITE, FEAR, INFILTRATOR, STEALTH, TOUGH
0-1 Vanus Infocyte (Cost: 6 Glory)
Name
	Movement
	Ranged
	Melee
	Armour
	Base
	Vanus Infocyte
	8”/Infantry
	+2
	+2
	-1
	32mm
	

Battlekit
The Vanus Infocyte is equipped with its Synskin Bodyglove, included in its statistic above, as well as two Sympatic Dataspikes, two Las Pistols, and an Augury Scanner. You cannot change its battlekit in any way.
                  * Synskin Bodyglove. -1 INJURY MODIFIER, IMPERVIOUS
                  * Sympatic Dataspike. Melee, CRITICAL, ARMOUR PIERCING 2, STUN
                  * Laspistol. 12”, PISTOL
                  * Augury Scanner. Enemy models must be set up at least 16” away from this model when using the DEEP STRIKE or INFILTRATOR Keywords.


Abilities
                  * Ambidextrous. The Vanus Infocyte ignores penalties for making off-hand attacks, and has the following benefits while armed with Ranged Weapons with the PISTOL Keyword:
                  * If it is equipped with 2 Weapons with the PISTOL Keyword, it can take a Shoot ACTION with one and then immediately take a Shoot ACTION with the other.
                  * Add the IGNORE OFF-HAND WEAPON Keyword to any weapons that have the PISTOL Keyword.
                  * Autotomic Servo-Limbs. Enemy models never get free attacks against the Vanus Infocyte when it Retreats, and the Vanus Infocyte can move out of Melee Combat using its Standard Move, Charge, or Dash.
                  * Fleet. The Vanus Infocyte has +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls.
                  * Lightning Reflexes. Attacks made against the Vanus Infocyte have -1 DICE to Hit.
                  * Noospheric Interloper. After you activate the Vanus Infocyte, you decide the next 3 models to activate, following the normal limits (i.e. the second will usually be your own model again). If you choose a model in a Fireteam, the model's owner decides if it will activate as a Fireteam or individually.


Keywords
ASSASSINORUM, INFILTRATOR, STEALTH, TOUGH
0-1 Venenum Assassin (Cost: 6 Glory)
Name

	32mm
	

Battlekit
The Venenum Assassin is equipped with its Synskin Bodyglove, included in its statistic above, as well as a Hookfang, Toxin Injector, and Poison Globes. You cannot change its battlekit in any way.
                  * Synskin Bodyglove. -1 INJURY MODIFIER, IMPERVIOUS
                  * Hookfang. Melee, GAS, INFECTION MARKERS (including from GAS Keyword)
                  * Toxin Ejector. 8”, AUTOMATIC 2, -1 INJURY DICE, FLAMETHROWER, IGNORE ARMOUR, GAS
                  * Poison Globes. 8”, IGNORE ARMOUR, ASSAULT, BLAST 3”, GAS, IGNORE COVER, IGNORE LONG RANGE, THROWN, FUMBLE


Abilities
                  * Fleet. The Venenum Assassin has +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls.
                  * Lightning Reflexes. Attacks made against the Venenum Assassin have -1 DICE to Hit.
                  * Unnatural Conditioning. Instead of gaining an INFECTION MARKER at the start of the Venenum Assassin’s Activation if it already has one, instead remove one INFECTION MARKER from it.


Keywords

Ecclesiarchy Mercenaries
Ecclesiarchy Mercenaries
If your Warband is not Adepta Sororitas, Adeptus Ministorum, or Inquisition, you can recruit only a maximum of 2 of these mercenaries.
0-2 Arco-Flagellant (Cost: 3 Glory)
An Adeptus Mechanicus or Necromunda Gang (Ironhead Squat Prospectors) Warband cannot take this Mercenary.
Name
	Movement
	Ranged
	Melee
	Armour
	Base
	Arco-Flagellant
	8”/Infantry
	N/A
	+1
	0
	25-28mm
	

Battlekit
The Arco-Flagellant is equipped with two Arco-Flails. You cannot change its battlekit in any way.
                  * Arco-Flails. Melee, IGNORE OFF-HAND, ARMOUR PIERCING 1, STUN


Abilities
                  * Penance Paid. When the Arco-Flagellant is taken Out of Action, you can give any one of your other models 1 BLESSING MARKER. When the Arco-Flagellant becomes a casualty, you earn 1 Glory.
                  * Stimm Injectors. When an Arco-Flagellant charges, roll 2D6 instead of 1D6 and then add the highest of the two dice to its charge move. In addition, Injury rolls made against the Arco-Flagellant have -1 DICE.


Keywords

0-1 Death Cult Assassin (Cost: 4 Glory)
An Adeptus Mechanicus, Adeptus Ministorum, or Necromunda Gang (Ironhead Squat Prospectors) Warband cannot take this mercenary.
Name
	Movement
	Ranged
	Melee
	Armour
	Base
	Death Cult Assassin
	8”/Infantry
	+1
	+1
	-1
	25mm
	

Battlekit
The Death Cult Assassin is equipped with Standard Armour, a Dartmask, a Power Weapon, a Blade, a Grapnel Launcher, and Sindexterous Cyberteknika. You cannot change its battlekit in any way.
                  * Dartmask. 6”, IGNORE LONG RANGE, GAS, ASSAULT
                  * Power Weapon. Melee, ARMOUR PIERCING 2, MAIN HAND ONLY
                  * Blade. Melee, CRITICAL
                  * Grapnel Launcher. A model with this launcher adds +1 DICE to any Climb Success Roll rolls.
                  * Sindexterous Cyberteknika. The model ignores the penalty for attack with its off-hand. In addition, the model ignores the penalties of the following Traumas (but not the accompanying Scar) and cannot gain them: Hand Wound, Lost Arm.


Abilities
                  * Bladed Stance. When an enemy Charges the Death Cult Assassin, if the Death Cult Assassin has not Activated this Turn, it can immediately take the Fight Action against that enemy before it takes any further actions. This can be done only once per Turn, and the Death Cult Assassin counts as having already taken the Fight Action during its Activation this Turn.
