0-1 Dialogus (Cost: 4 Glory)
Only an Adepta Sororitas, Adeptus Ministorum (Ecclesiastic Militant Force), or Inquisition (Ordo: Hereticus) Warband can take this mercenary.
Name
	Movement
	Ranged
	Melee
	Armour
	Base
	Dialogus
	6”/Infantry
	+1
	+1
	-2
	32-40mm
	

Battlekit
The Dialogus is equipped with Power Armour, included in its statistics above, as well as a Bolt Pistol, and a Staff of Declamation. You cannot change its battlekit in any way.
                  * Bolt Pistol. 12”, VICIOUS 11, CRITICAL, PISTOL
                  * Staff of Decelmation. Melee, STUN


Abilities
                  * Endless Crusade. Once per battle, the Dialogus can use this ability as an Action with no roll required. Once used, until the end of the current Turn, the Dialogus and each friendly SORORITAS model within 6” of it have +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls.
                  * Laud Hailer. At any point during its Activation, the Dialogus can take a Risky Success Roll with +1 DICE. If successful, all friendly models that are Down and within 6” of the Dialogus may immediately stand up at no penalty or cost.
                  * Stirring Rhetoric. If you have the Miracles special rule, whenever you spend a Miracle on a Success Roll for the Dialogus or an ally within 6” of it, you can set one die’s value to 6 before rolling instead of rolling it.


If you have the Battle Hymns special rule, the range of that rule is 9” instead of 6” for the Dialogus.


If you have the Vows of Atonement special rule, Injury roll made against your Repentia, Arco-Flagellants, and Penitent Engines within 9” of the Dialogus have -1 INJURY DICE.


Keywords
ORATOR, SORORITAS

	-2
	32-50mm
	

Battlekit
The Hospitaller is equipped with Power Armour, included in its statistics above, as well as a Bolt Pistol and Surgeon’s Tools. You cannot change its battlekit in any way.
                  * Bolt Pistol. 12”, VICIOUS 11, CRITICAL, PISTOL
                  * Surgeon’s Tools. Melee, SHRAPNEL


Abilities
                  * Medicus Ministorum. As an Action with a Success Roll with +1 DICE, the Hospitaller can heal itself or one friendly model within 1” of it. This Success Roll has an additional +1 DICE for every 2 BLOOD MARKERS on the target. On a success, remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from the target, or 3 BLOOD MARKERS on a Critical Success. You can choose to instead remove 3 BLOOD MARKERS if you spend 1 Miracle.


This Ability also automatically removes 3 BLOOD MARKERS if you have the Battle Hymns or the Vows of Atonement special rule and the Hospitaler is within 6” of a friendly ORATOR, Connoness, or Dogmata model, or within 9” of a friendly Dialogus.

                  * Eviscerator. Melee, +1 INJURY DICE, CRITICAL, HEAVY, RISKY, SHRAPNEL

                  * Warrior of Faith. The Questkeeper can ignore the HEAVY Keyword of one melee weapon it wields. This does not allow it to carry more than one piece of HEAVY battlekit, and it must still use the normal number of hands.

0-1 Saint Celestine (Cost: 8 Glory + 2 Glory to deploy)
Only an Adepta Sororitas Warband can take this mercenary. You must pay 2 Glory to Deploy this model.
Name
	Movement
	Ranged
	Melee
	Armour
	Base
	Saint Celestine
	8”/Flying
	+0
	+3
	-2
	40mm
	

Battlekit
Saint Celestine is equipped with Armour of Saint Kathrine, The Ardent Blade, and 2 Geminae Superia. You cannot change her battlekit in any way.
                  * Armour of Saint Kathrine. -2, IMPERVIOUS
                  * The Ardent Blade. TWO-HANDED
                  * Slash: Melee, CLEAVE 2, CRITICAL, IGNORE ARMOUR on a Critical Hit
                  * Smite: 8”, FIRE, FLAMETHROWER, IGNORE ARMOUR
                  * Geminae Superia. Each Geminae adds +1 INJURY DICE to all of Saint Celestine’s attacks.


Abilities
                  * Healing Tears. As an Action with a Success Roll with +1 DICE, Saint Celestine can heal herself or one friendly model within 6” of her. On a success, remove D3 BLOOD MARKER from the target, or 3 BLOOD MARKERS on a Critical Success. You can choose to instead remove 3 BLOOD MARKERS if you spend 1 Miracle. You must choose before rolling the D3 on a Critical Success.


This Ability also automatically removes 3 BLOOD MARKERS if you have the Vows of Atonement special rule and Celestine is within 3” of a friendly ORATOR, Connoness, or Dogmata model.
                  * Lifewards. Up to twice per battle, when Saint Celestine is taken Out of Action for any reason, treat the result as No Effect instead, but she loses 1 Giminae Superia each time.
                  * Living Saint. Injuries rolled against Saint Celestine have -1 DICE. In addition, she can never be a casualty.
                  * Miraculous Intervention. Saint Celestine cannot be deployed normally, and cannot be deployed using her DEEP STRIKE keyword until the third Turn of the battle. In order to deploy her, you must pay 2 Glory.
                  * Saintly Blessings. The first -1 worth of Armour of other friendly SORORITAS models within 6” of Celestine have IMPERVIOUS, and those models have NEGATE FEAR.


Keywords
DEEP STRIKE, FLYING, NEGATE FEAR, SORORITAS, NO PROMOTION

0-1 Canid Officer (Cost: 2 Glory)
Only an Astra Militarum Warband can take this Mercenary.
Name
	Movement
	Ranged
	Melee
	Armour
	Base
	Canid Officer
	8”/Infantry
	N/A
	+0
	0
	25-28mm
	

Battlekit
The Canid Officer is equipped with its Bite. You cannot change its battlekit in any way.
                  * Bite. Melee


Abilities
                  * Bark Orders. The Canid Officer outranks common soldiers. It can give Orders as if it were an ELITE model, but with a range of only 3”, even if it is promoted to Elite.
                  * Canid Swiftness. The Canid Officer has +1 DICE to all Dash and Jump Success Rolls
                  * Fetch. The Canid Officer can drag along any Down model that is not LARGE within 1” when it moves, moving at half speed with the other model following directly behind it, both with normal movement or when dashing. If this takes that model out of melee combat, this does not trigger a free attack. The model can be moved by this Ability even though it is Down.
                  * Pet. Select one other non-ARTIFICIAL, non-BEAST model in your Warband at the start of the battle. The Canid Officer forms a FIRETEAM with that model. That model is its owner (though it may outrank that model if it is not ELITE).


Keywords

	+2
	+2
	-2
	32mm
	

Battlekit
The Deathwatch Veteran is equipped with Power Armour, a Boltgun, a Xenophase Sword, a Close Combat Weapon, and a Combat Helmet. You cannot change its battlekit in any way.
                  * Boltgun. 24”, VICIOUS 11, CRITICAL
                  * Xenophase Sword. Melee, IGNORE ARMOUR, CRITICAL
                  * Close Combat Weapon. Melee
                  * Combat Helmet. The model has NEGATE SHRAPNEL.


When you recruit the Deathwatch Veteran, you must choose two ammo types for its Special Issue Ammunition ability. It cannot use any of the other options.


Abilities
                  * Black Carapace. The Deathwatch Veteran treats any Down result from the Injury Roll Table as a Minor Hit instead. This does not apply to Down results that already replaced another result, such as with TOUGH.
                  * Special Issue Ammunition. At the start of each battle, choose two of the following ammunition types. Once during each of its Activations, when the Deathwatch Veteran attacks with its Boltgun, it can use one of the two ammo types you chose.
                  * Derevenant Shells. AMMUNITION (special). On a hit, after the normal Injury roll, the target must roll on the Injury table again with -2 INJURY DICE. Other properties of the weapon do not apply during this injury roll.
                  * Dragonfire Bolts. AMMUNITION (FIRE).
                  * Hellfire Rounds. AMMUNITION (GAS).
                  * Inertial Fusion Bolts. AMMUNITION (special). Injury rolls caused by these attacks cannot be modified by -DICE.
                  * Kraken Bolts. AMMUNITION (ARMOUR PIERCING 1).
                  * Metal Storm Shells. AMMUNITION (SHRAPNEL).
                  * Tempest Bolts. AMMUNITION (+1 INJURY DICE against targets with the ARTIFICIAL, MECHANICUS, or VEHICLE Keyword).
                  * Thermic Acceleration Rounds. AMMUNITION (special). Attacks ignore all penalties to attack Success Rolls besides Cover, Long Range, and BLOOD MARKERS (including INFECTION and STUN), as well as penalties or limits to their range.

                  * Suffer Not the Unclean to Live. The model has +1 INJURY DICE with melee attacks against non-ARTIFICIAL non-VEHICLE enemies during an Activation in which it Charged. However, whenever the model Charges, it must charge the closest possible enemy model it can see even if the actual route to reach it is longer than to reach another.
                  * Uphold the Honour of the Emperor. Injury rolls against the model have -1 INJURY DICE, but attack Success Rolls made against it have +1 DICE.
                  * Abhor the Witch, Destroy the Witch. The model has +1 INJURY DICE against PSYKER enemies with melee attacks, and has +2” movement so long as it moves in the most direct path towards an enemy PSYKER (it can avoid Difficult and Dangerous terrain). However, while an enemy PSYKER is within 8” of the model, the model has -1 DICE to all Success Rolls besides melee attacks.
                  * Accept Any Challenge, No Matter the Odds. The model's melee attacks gain ARMOUR PIERCING 1 on a Critical Hit. However, the model cannot Retreat.

                  * Precognition (Effect). 6”, Self or Ally, 1 Target, End of Turn, all attacks against that model have -1 DICE to Hit.
                  * Prescience (Effect). 6”, Self or Ally, 1 Target, Immediate, the target gains a BLESSING MARKER.

                  * Fetch. The Canid Companion can drag along any Down model that is not LARGE within 1” when it moves, moving at half speed with the other model following directly behind it, both with normal movement or when dashing. If this takes that model out of melee combat, this does not trigger a free attack.

Battlekit
The Inquisitor is equipped with Power Armour, a Plasma Pistol, a Power Weapon, Filter Plugs, and a Rosarius. You cannot change its battlekit in any way.
                  * Plasma Pistol. 
                  * Normal Shot: 12”, ARMOUR PIERCING 1
                  * Overload: 12”, +1 INJURY DICE, ARMOUR PIERCING 2, RISKY, DANGEROUS
                  * Power Weapon. Melee, ARMOUR PIERCING 2, MAIN HAND ONLY
                  * Filter Plugs. The model has NEGATE GAS.
                  * Rosarius. The Inquisitor treats Down results as Minor Hits (does not affect Down results that replace Out of Action via TOUGH Keyword).


When you recruit the Lone Inquisitor, choose one of the following Ordos:
                  * Ordo: Hereticus. The Lone Inquisitor gains the FEAR Keyword and is equipped with a Hand Flamer instead of its Plasma Pistol.
                  * Hand Flamer. 6”, FLAMETHROWER, IGNORE ARMOUR, -2 INJURY DICE, FIRE, PISTOL, loses FLAMETHROWER when attacking in melee
                  * Ordo: Malleus. The Lone Inquisitor gains the PSYKER 1 Keyword and knows the following powers. Replace its Power Weapon with a Force Weapon.
                  * Force Weapon. Melee, +1 INJURY DICE, Additional +1 INJURY DICE against targets with DAEMON or PSYKER Keyword, PSYCHIC, HELD
                  * Foreboding (Effect). -1 DICE, 6”, Self or Ally, 1 Target, End of Turn, whenever an enemy attempts to Charge the target, the target can make either a ranged attack before that Charge, or a melee attack afterwards, against the Charging model in either case.
                  * Mental Fortitude (Effect). No Range, No Targets, End of Turn, you have +1 DICE to your Morale Test at the end of this Turn, if any.
                  * Precognition (Effect). 6”, Self or Ally, 1 Target, End of Turn, all attacks against that model have -1 DICE to Hit.
                  * Terrify (Effect). Melee, Enemy, 1 Target, Immediate, the target must move D6” directly away from the caster. This movement allows free attacks as if it were a Retreat Action. A model that ignores the FEAR Keyword is immune.
                  * Ordo: Xenos. Replace the Lone Inquisitor’s Plasma Pistol with an Avenger Shuriken Catapult and a Scorpion Chainsword
                  * Avenger Shuriken Catapult. 18”, AUTOMATIC 2, ASSAULT, ARMOUR PIERCING 1 on Critical Hit, TWO-HANDED
                  * Scorpion Chainsword. Melee, CRITICAL, RISKY, SHRAPNEL
                  * Ordo: Minoris. The Lone Inquisitor gains the STRONG Keyword and is equipped with a Heavy Bolter and a Heavy Power Weapon instead of its Plasma Pistol and Power Weapon.
                  * Heavy Bolter. 36”, +1 INJURY DICE, VICIOUS 11, CRITICAL, HEAVY
                  * Heavy Power Weapon. Melee, +1 INJURY DICE, HEAVY, ARMOUR PIERCING 2, TWO-HANDED

                  * Biomancy
                  * Bio Lightning (Attack). 12”, +1 INJURY DICE, PSYCHIC, RISKY
                  * Endurance (Effect). 6”, Self or Ally, 1 Target, Immediate, remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from the chosen model.
                  * Iron Arm (Effect). No Range, Self, 1 Target, Next Activation, injury rolls against the caster have -1 INJURY MODIFIER, stacking with armour up to -3.
                  * Divination
                  * Foreboding (Effect). -1 DICE, 6”, Self or Ally, 1 Target, End of Turn, whenever an enemy attempts to Charge the target, the target can make either a ranged attack before that Charge, or a melee attack afterwards, against the Charging model in either case.

                  * Prescience (Effect). 6”, Self or Ally, 1 Target, Immediate, the target gains a BLESSING MARKER.
                  * Pyromancy
                  * Fire Shield (Effect). 6”, Self or Ally, 1 Target, Immediate and End of Turn, each enemy in close combat with the target immediately suffers 1 BLOOD MARKER, and each time an enemy enters close combat with the target and after each time an enemy attacks it in melee, that enemy suffers 1 BLOOD MARKER. An enemy with NEGATE FIRE is immune to this power.
                  * Spontaneous Combustion (Effect). 12”, Enemy, 1 Target, Immediate, the target suffers 2 BLOOD MARKERS. A target with NEGATE FIRE is immune to this power.
                  * Technomancy
                  * Blessing of the Machine God (Effect). 6”, Self or Ally, Target Activation, the target has +1 INJURY DICE with one weapon of your choice.
                  * Machine Flense (Attack). 12”, IGNORE ARMOUR, -1 INJURY DICE non-ARTIFICIAL, non-VEHICLE models, PSYCHIC, RISKY, STUN
                  * Reforge (Effect). 6”, Self or Ally, immediate, if the target has the ARTIFICIAL, MECHANICUS, or VEHICLE Keyword, or is a model with at least -2 worth of Armour (besides shields), remove D3 BLOOD MARKERS from it.
                  * Telekinesis
                  * Assail (Attack). 12”, ASSAULT, +1 INJURY MODIFIER, PSYCHIC, SHRAPNEL, RISKY
                  * Shockwave (Effect). -1 DICE, 6”, Enemy and Ally, Each Target, Immediate, each target must roll on the Injury table, with +1 INJURY DICE. This injury has IGNORE ARMOUR, NONLETHAL, and STUN MARKERS. The targets are then each pushed D3” directly away from the caster unless they are LARGE.
                  * Telekine Dome (Effect). No Range, Self, End of Turn, Injury rolls against the caster and each ally within 3” of it have -1 DICE.
                  * Telepathy
                  * Psychic Shriek (Attack). Melee, IGNORE ARMOUR, PSYCHIC, RISKY, STUN
                  * Puppet Master (Effect). -1 DICE, 12”, Enemy, 1 Target, Immediate, the target must move D6” in the direction of your choice. This movement allows free attacks as if it were a Retreat Action. This power cannot be used while in close combat.
                  * Terrify (Effect). Melee, Enemy, 1 Target, Immediate, the target must move D6” directly away from the caster. This movement allows free attacks as if it were a Retreat Action. A model that ignores the FEAR Keyword is immune.

                  * Wings. The Daemon Prince can be given wings for an additional +1 Glory, granting it 8” movement and FLYING.

	Ranged
	Melee
	Armour
	Base
	Blessed Blade
	6”/Infantry
	N/A
	+1
	-1
	32mm
	

Battlekit
Each Blessed Blade is equipped with Standard Armour and a Commune Blade. You cannot change its battlekit in any way.
                  * Commune Blade. Melee, +1 INJURY DICE, VICIOUS 11, CRITICAL, TWO-HANDED


Abilities
                  * Attuned In Purpose. Blessed Blades come in pairs. They are always in a FIRETEAM with each other. While they are both in close combat with the same enemy, they both have +1 DICE to Hit with melee attacks against that enemy. If one of them is a casualty or is otherwise removed from your Warband, the other cannot be fielded until you recruit a replacement, which costs 1 Glory. They share Promotion Dice, and are promoted to Elite as if one model, taking only one Elite slot in your Warband. They earn XP together and learn skills together. They suffer Trauma and Battle Scars individually.
                  * Cut Them Down. When an enemy in close combat with the Blessed Blade Retreats, the Blessed Blade has +1 DICE to Hit and +1 INJURY DICE for its free attack against that model.

                  * Spreading Plague. If this model takes an enemy Out of Action in melee, and that model is a casualty during the post-battle sequence, add another Plague Zombie to your Warband. You can exceed the normal limit of 3 Plague zombies in this way.

                  * Force Weapon. Melee, +1 INJURY DICE, Additional +1 INJURY DICE against targets with DAEMON or PSYKER Keyword, PSYCHIC, HELD
                  * Laspistol. 12”, PISTOL


Abilities
                  * Power Unleashed. The Unsanctioned Psyker has PERILOUS ±2 with all psychic powers.


When you recruit the Unsanctioned Psyker, choose one of the following disciplines. The Unsanctioned Psyker knows all of the powers listed below under that discipline.
                  * Biomancy
                  * Bio Lightning (Attack). 12”, +1 INJURY DICE, PSYCHIC, RISKY
                  * Endurance (Effect). 6”, Self or Ally, 1 Target, Immediate, remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from the chosen model.
                  * Iron Arm (Effect). No Range, Self, 1 Target, Next Activation, injury rolls against the caster have -1 INJURY MODIFIER, stacking with armour up to -3.
                  * Divination
                  * Foreboding (Effect). -1 DICE, 6”, Self or Ally, 1 Target, End of Turn, whenever an enemy attempts to Charge the target, the target can make either a ranged attack before that Charge, or a melee attack afterwards, against the Charging model in either case.
                  * Precognition (Effect). 6”, Self or Ally, 1 Target, End of Turn, all attacks against that model have -1 DICE to Hit.
                  * Prescience (Effect). 6”, Self or Ally, 1 Target, Immediate, the target gains a BLESSING MARKER.
                  * Pyromancy
                  * Fire Shield (Effect). 6”, Self or Ally, 1 Target, Immediate and End of Turn, each enemy in close combat with the target immediately suffers 1 BLOOD MARKER, and each time an enemy enters close combat with the target and after each time an enemy attacks it in melee, that enemy suffers 1 BLOOD MARKER. An enemy with NEGATE FIRE is immune to this power.
                  * Spontaneous Combustion (Effect). 12”, Enemy, 1 Target, Immediate, the target suffers 2 BLOOD MARKERS. A target with NEGATE FIRE is immune to this power.
                  * Technomancy
                  * Blessing of the Machine God (Effect). 6”, Self or Ally, Target Activation, the target has +1 INJURY DICE with one weapon of your choice.
                  * Machine Flense (Attack). 12”, IGNORE ARMOUR, -1 INJURY DICE non-ARTIFICIAL, non-VEHICLE models, PSYCHIC, RISKY, STUN
                  * Reforge (Effect). 6”, Self or Ally, immediate, if the target has the ARTIFICIAL, MECHANICUS, or VEHICLE Keyword, or is a model with at least -2 worth of Armour (besides shields), remove D3 BLOOD MARKERS from it.
                  * Telekinesis
                  * Assail (Attack). 12”, ASSAULT, +1 INJURY MODIFIER, PSYCHIC, SHRAPNEL, RISKY
                  * Shockwave (Effect). -1 DICE, 6”, Enemy and Ally, Each Target, Immediate, each target must roll on the Injury table, with +1 INJURY DICE. This injury has IGNORE ARMOUR, NONLETHAL, and STUN MARKERS. The targets are then each pushed D3” directly away from the caster unless they are LARGE.
                  * Telekine Dome (Effect). No Range, Self, End of Turn, Injury rolls against the caster and each ally within 3” of it have -1 DICE.
                  * Telepathy
                  * Psychic Shriek (Attack). Melee, IGNORE ARMOUR, PSYCHIC, RISKY, STUN
                  * Puppet Master (Effect). -1 DICE, 12”, Enemy, 1 Target, Immediate, the target must move D6” in the direction of your choice. This movement allows free attacks as if it were a Retreat Action. This power cannot be used while in close combat.
                  * Terrify (Effect). Melee, Enemy, 1 Target, Immediate, the target must move D6” directly away from the caster. This movement allows free attacks as if it were a Retreat Action. A model that ignores the FEAR Keyword is immune.

As an Action with a Risky Success Roll with +1 DICE, the caster or one ally it can see within 6” of it can hide if any piece of scenery it is touching can block line of sight in any direction, even partially, regardless of whether an enemy currently has a clear line of sight. If successful, enemies cannot target the affected model with ranged attacks or Charges. Weapons with the Keyword BLAST it as normal if it is in the radius of the weapon. This effect ends if the affected model moves from its exact position in any way, it makes a Ranged Attack, or an enemy model comes within 1.5” of it.
                  * Treacherous Illusion. As an Action with a Risky Success Roll with +1 DICE, choose one weapon carried by an enemy model within 8” of the caster. On a success, the model carrying that weapon must make an attack with it against the target of your choice. On a failure, the model can instead make an attack with any of its weapons against any target of its choice. This power cannot be used while in close combat.


Abilities
                  * Swift. The Shadowseer has +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls.

If it ends this movement in close combat, it can take an additional Fight Action, allowing it to attack with both of its Blades.

0-3 Hired Gun (Cost: 2-3 Glory)
Name
	Movement
	Ranged
	Melee
	Armour
	Base
	Hired Gun
	6”/Infantry
	+0 or +1
	+0 or +1
	0
	40mm
	

Battlekit
The Hired Gun can be equipped with any weapons, armour, or battlekit from the Necromunda Gang Shared Armoury or the Venators Gang Armoury each found in the Necromunda Gang Faction, except only up to 1 can use any Hunting Rig. You can purchase battlekit from these lists for the Hired Gun, but that battlekit cannot be used by any models other than Hired Guns unless you normally have access to the Armoury it is from or it is available to you from another Armoury for the same or lower price. You can still sell it normally.


When you recruit the Hired Gun, you must choose one of the following Specialties:
                  * Bruiser. The Hired Gun has +1 Melee and +0 Ranged.
                  * Shooter. The Hired Gun has +1 Ranged and +0 Melee.


When you recruit the Hired Gun, choose up to 2 of the following Specialties for it, or up to 4 if you paid 3 Glory.
                  * Agile. The Hired Gun’s movement speed increases by 1”, and it has +1 DICE to all Climb, Jump, and Diving Charge Success Rolls.
                  * Brute. The Hired Gun gains the STRONG Keyword.
                  * Deep Hunter. The Hired Gun ignores Dangerous and Difficult Terrain.
                  * Duelist. Whenever an enemy Charges the Hired Gun, the Hired Gun can make a single melee attack with a non-HEAVY weapon against that enemy before it takes any further actions.
                  * Engineer. As an Action with a Success Roll, the Hired Gun can attempt to repair one ARTIFICIAL, MECHANICUS, or VEHICLE ally, or an ally with -2 or better Armour (excluding shields) within 1” of it. On a success, remove 1 BLOOD MARKER from that ally.
                  * Executioner. Once during each Turn, if the Hired Gun Downs or takes an enemy Out of Action in melee, it can move up to 3”, and can enter melee as if it Charged, then it can make a single extra melee attack.
                  * Explosives Expert. The Explosives Expert has NEGATE MINED. As an Action with a Success Roll with +1 DICE, the Hired Gun can mine a piece of terrain they alone are touching, no bigger than 8” x 8” (an 8” section of trench, a wall, a tree, a building etc). If successful, the terrain piece is now MINED.
                  * Grenadier. Increase the range of THROWN weapons used by the Hired Gun by 4”. It has +1 DICE to Hit with these weapons, but attacks made beyond the weapon’s normal range, before this benefit, are made at -1 DICE.
                  * Knife Fighter. The Hired Gun has +1 INJURY DICE with Close Combat Weapons and Throwing Knives.
                  * Massive (counts as 3 choices). The Hired Gun gains the LARGE and TOUGH Keywords.
                  * Master of Stealth. The Hired Gun gains the INFILTRATOR and STEALTH Keywords.
                  * Pistoleer. The Hired Gun has the following benefits while armed with Ranged Weapons with the PISTOL Keyword:
                  * If it is equipped with 2 Weapons with the PISTOL Keyword, it can take a Shoot ACTION with one and then immediately take a Shoot ACTION with the other.
                  * Add the IGNORE OFF-HAND WEAPON Keyword to any weapons that have the PISTOL Keyword.
                  * Runner. The Hired Gun has +1 DICE to all Dash Success Rolls.
                  * Sniper. When the Hired Gun makes a ranged attack with a TWO-HANDED weapon, it has +1 INJURY DICE.
                  * Psyker (LIMIT: 1). The Hired Gun gains the PSYKER 1 Keyword. Select one Shared Psychic Discipline. The Hired Gun may purchase up to three powers from that Discipline (at normal cost). It can only use Psychic Powers while wielding a PSYCHIC weapon.
                  * Tactician. The Hired Gun gains the FIRETEAM Keyword, and can form a fireteam with any one other model in your Warband, chosen at the start of each battle.
                  * Terrifying. The Hired Gun gains the FEAR Keyword.


Keywords
GANGER

                  * Bludgeon. Melee, -1 DICE


Abilities
                  * Temporary Hire. At the end of a battle in which the Hive Scum participated, if it was not a casualty, remove it from your roster and you regain your spent 1 Glory.

ELITE, FEAR, LARGE, TOUGH
Missions
Missions


Meatgrinder
Meatgrinder
  

                  * Forces
No special restrictions apply to the models the players can include in their Forces in this scenario.


Additionally, each player will need 4-6 small, expendable models.
                  * The Battlefield
The battlefield is 36”x36”. The players roll-off and the winner sets up the terrain for the game, with the following additional rules.
Arena
The area within 8” of the center of the battlefield should contain only sparse terrain, with no Impassible Terrain and only a few pieces of cover. It should not include any Difficult or Dangerous Terrain. This area is the Arena.
Objective
Place a single 1” objective marker in the center of the battlefield.
                  * Deployment
The player that did not set up the terrain chooses which Deployment Zone will be theirs. The other Deployment Zone is their opponent’s. The players then alternate deploying their models one at a time, starting with the player who has more models in their Warband (roll-off if both players have the same number of models). Models must be set up wholly within their own Deployment Zone. If a player runs out of models to set up, the other player sets up all their remaining models, one after another until they have none left. Once the players have set up their models, deployment ends and the game begins.
Infiltrators
Infiltrators can deploy normally or by using their special deployment rules.
                  * Endless Reinforcements
At the start of the game, after deploying normal models, each player deploys D3+3 Fodder models within their Deployment Zone. Roll only once, and each player deploys the same number.
At the start of each Turn after the first, each player redeploys all of their Fodder that have been taken Out of Action, within their deployment zone. The player with the initiative places first, and players alternate.
These models have the following statistics:
Fodder
Name
	Movement
	Ranged
	Melee
	Armour
	Base
	Fodder
	6”/Infantry
	-1
	-1
	0
	25mm
	

Battlekit
The Fodder is equipped with a Close Combat Weapon and a Simple Ranged Weapon.
                  * Close Combat Weapon. Melee
                  * Simple Ranged Weapon. 12”


Abilities
                  * Expendable. The Fodder is not counted as part of your Warband for the purposes of Morale.
                  * Masses. When you activate the Fodder, you must also activate another Fodder as if they were a Fireteam, if there is another of your Fodder that has not been activated this Turn.
                  * Vulnerable. Injury rolls made against the Fodder are always considered a Bloodbath. Besides the NONLETHAL Keyword, nothing can cause an Out of Action result against the Fodder to become any other result (Tough, Reanimation Protocols, etc).


Keywords
The Fodder has your Faction Keyword
Game Length
At the end of the fourth Turn, one player rolls a D6. On a 1 or 2, the game ends immediately. On a 3 or more, the game will end at the end of the fifth Turn.
                  * Victory Conditions
A player wins this scenario immediately if there are no enemy models on the battlefield or if the opposing Warband flees (typically due to failing a Morale Check). Otherwise, the player with more Victory Points at the end of the game is the winner.
Victory Points
At the end of each Turn, each player earns 1 VP for each of their models within 3” of the Objective that is not Down.
                  * Players earn 1 VP for each scored Glorious Deed, as normal.
                  * Glorious Deeds
Chew Through. A friendly model takes at least 4 enemy Fodder Out of Action.
Gladiator. A friendly model takes at least 2 non-Fodder enemies Out of Action during a single Activation, while it and each of those enemies are in the Arena.
Kaboom! A friendly model hits at least 5 models, enemy or friendly, with a BLAST attack.
King of the Hill. A friendly model ends three Activations in a row within 3” of the Objective.
Pile of Bodies. At the end of a Turn, there are no Fodder models on the battlefield belonging to either player. Both players score this Glorious Deed.
Resist and Bite. A friendly model that began its Activation Down takes an enemy model Out of Action during that Activation.
Sniper. A friendly model takes an enemy ELITE model Out of Action with a Ranged Weapon Attack that has the Long Range modifier.
Toxic Crossing
Toxic Crossing
  

                  * Forces
No special restrictions apply to the models the players can include in their Forces in this scenario.
                  * The Battlefield
The battlefield is 36”x36”. The players roll-off and the winner sets up the terrain for the game, with the following additional rules.
River
Include a 3-4"-wide toxic river through the center of the map with 2D3 crossings. At least one crossing should be between platforms that are 3" tall or higher. The river should be symmetrical, with ends that are somewhat off-center. It can be curved or straight.
The river counts as both Difficult and Dangerous Terrain, and injury rolls caused by it have the GAS Keyword. BURROWING movement cannot ignore the river.
Models that are Downed within 1” of the river must roll to avoid falling into it as if they were at the edge of a cliff.
                  * Deployment
The player that did not set up the terrain chooses which Deployment Zone will be theirs. The other Deployment Zone is their opponent’s. The players then alternate deploying their models one at a time, starting with the player who has more models in their Warband (roll-off if both players have the same number of models). Models must be set up wholly within their own Deployment Zone. If a player runs out of models to set up, the other player sets up all their remaining models, one after another until they have none left. Once the players have set up their models, deployment ends and the game begins.
Infiltrators
Infiltrators must be placed on their owner’s side of the river.
                  * Escaping
Each side has an extraction point. They are directly opposite each other, on the edge of the battlefield at the centre of the opponent's Deployment Zone. If a model finishes a move in contact with its extraction point, it is immediately removed from the battlefield. Models are also immediately extracted if they move into contact with an enemy model that is within 1" of their extraction point. Note that you cannot choose for a model to not be extracted.
A model with DEEP STRIKE cannot be deployed using that Keyword anywhere within 12” of its owner’s Escape Point.
Game Length
The battle lasts for five turns.
                  * Victory Conditions
A player wins this scenario immediately if the opposing Warband flees (typically due to failing a Morale Check). Otherwise, the player with more Victory Points at the end of the game is the winner.
Victory Points
At the end of the game, each player earns VP equal to the credit values (including battlekit) of their models that escaped the battlefield, divided by 100, plus the Glory cost of those models divided by 3, rounding down after adding them together.
                  * Players earn 1 VP for each scored Glorious Deed, as normal.
                  * Glorious Deeds
Cast them Down. A friendly model causes an enemy model to Fall from a height of at least 3" (e.g. by taking it Down near a ledge or by forcing it off a ledge in some way).
Into the River. A friendly model takes an enemy that is wholly within the river Out of Action, that was not wholly within the river at the start of your model's activation.
Parkour. A friendly model succeeds on two Jump and/or Climb Success Rolls during a single Activation. Does not count automatic success.
Speedrun. A friendly model escapes the battlefield before the end of the third Turn.
Toxic Fording. A friendly model crosses the entire river, beginning entirely on one side and ending entirely on the other, in one activation. It must move over the river terrain and not avoid it with a jump, FLYING movement, a bridge, etc.
Whirlwind. A friendly model takes two enemies Out of Action with melee attacks in a single Turn.
Industrial Zone
Industrial Zone
  

                  * Forces
No special restrictions apply to the models the players can include in their Forces in this scenario.
                  * The Battlefield
The players roll-off and the winner sets up the terrain for the game, with the following additional rules.
Machinery
Place four pieces of terrain that can be entered and/or stood on, such as buildings, as shown. These terrain pieces are Machines. Place a Power Supply marker in the center of each.
A player controls a Machine terrain piece if there are more friendly models on, in, or within 1" of the terrain piece than there are enemy models. If one player has any models on the terrain piece and their opponent does not, then the player with models on the terrain piece controls it even if their opponent has more models within 1" of the Objective.
Powered Junk
Place three 1” Powered Junk Markers as shown. A model within 1” of a Powered Junk Marker can attempt to harvest it with the Harvest Action:
                  * Harvest. The model makes a Risky Success Roll. On a success, it picks up a Power Cell Marker from that Powered Junk, as described below. On a failure, the model gains a STUN MARKER and place a Power Cell Marker within 1” of it. Regardless of the outcome, the Powered Junk Marker is removed.


Power Cells
As an Action with no Success Roll, a model within 1” of a Power Cell Marker can pick up that marker. Put the Power Cell Marker in contact with the model’s base to show it is carrying it. The only thing a model carrying a cell can do is take Move, Retreat, Dash, or Power On Actions – it cannot do anything else while it has the power cell.
                  * At the end of its Activation, you can say that a model that is carrying a power cell will either drop it or hand it to a friendly model that is within 1". When a model drops the power cell, place it within 1" of the model and not in contact with any other models. When a model hands the power cell on, place the Power Cell Marker in contact with the other model’s base.
                  * If a model carrying a cell is taken Down or Out of Action, or is chosen as the target of a Melee Attack, it immediately drops the power cell as described above.


Powering Machines
A model that is on top of or within a Machine can take the Power On or Power Off Actions.
                  * Power On. A model carrying a Power Cell Marker can take this action, which requires no Success Roll. Place its Power Cell Marker on top of that terrain piece’s Power Source, and that marker cannot be picked up again without a Power Off Action. While the Machine has this marker on it, it is Powered.
                  * Power Off. A model within 1” of a Power Source that has a Power Cell Marker on it can take the Power Off Action. The Machine ceases to be Powered, and the model must make a Risky Success Roll. On a success, it picks up the Power Cell Marker. On a failure, it suffers a STUN MARKER and place the Power Cell Marker within 1” of it. A model cannot take this Action if it is within 1” of any enemies or if there is an enemy within 1” of the Power Source.
                  * Deployment
The player that did not set up the terrain chooses which Deployment Zone will be theirs. The other Deployment Zone is their opponent’s. The players then alternate deploying their models one at a time, starting with the player who has more models in their Warband (roll-off if both players have the same number of models). Models must be set up wholly within their own Deployment Zone. If a player runs out of models to set up, the other player sets up all their remaining models, one after another until they have none left. Once the players have set up their models, deployment ends and the game begins.
Infiltrators
Infiltrators can deploy normally or by using their special deployment rules, but must be placed at least 8” away from any Powered Junk Markers.
                  * Industrial Accidents
Dangerous Machinery. The machinery here is dangerous and constantly malfunctioning. Injury rolls made as a result of a Fall or due to Dangerous Terrain have +1 INJURY DICE.


Accidents. At the start of each Turn, if at least one Machine is Powered, one of the players must roll a D6. On a roll of 1-3 nothing happens, but on a 4-6, an Industrial Accident takes place. Roll a D3 and look up the roll on the table below to see what happens.
                  1. Power Surge. Each model on or within a Powered Machine terrain piece or within 3” of a Powered Junk Marker must roll on the Injury chart. This injury has the STUN MARKERS and STUN Keywords.
                  2. Pouring Smoke. During this Turn and the next, Ranged Attacks made by or against models within any Powered Machine terrain piece or within 3” of a Powered Junk Marker have their range halved.
                  3. Steam Vent. During this Turn, the area within 3” of a Powered Machine’s Power Source or a Powered Junk Marker is considered Dangerous Terrain. Injuries from this terrain have the FIRE Keyword.
Game Length
This scenario lasts five Turns.
                  * Victory Conditions
A player wins this scenario immediately if there are no enemy models on the battlefield or if the opposing Warband flees (typically due to failing a Morale Check). Otherwise, the player with more Victory Points at the end of the game is the winner.
Victory Points
At the end of each round after the first, each player earns 1 VP for each Machine they control, or 3 VP if it is Powered.
Players earn 1 VP for each scored Glorious Deed, as normal.
                  * Glorious Deeds
Accident Master. Satisfy one of the following (this deed can still be scored only once):
                  * Spend at least 2 STUN Markers on an enemy Success Roll during a Turn in which it was subjected to Power Surge.
                  * A friendly model takes an enemy Out of Action while suffering penalties for Long Range and while one or both models are within Pouring Smoke.
                  * A friendly model moves into Dangerous Terrain caused by Steam Vent from outside and takes an enemy model Out of Action while within that terrain in the same Activation.
Bloodletting. An attack made by a friendly model results in the sixth BLOOD MARKER being placed beside an enemy model.
Hold Your Ground. A Warband is the first to pass a Morale Check in this game. In a campaign game, treat this Glorious Deed as having been performed by 1 ELITE model from the Warband that has the LEADER Keyword if you have one available.
Into the Gears. A friendly model causes an enemy model that is on a Machine to Fall and the Fall results in it being taken Out of Action.
Stolen Power. A friendly model successfully uses the Power Off Action and ends that activation outside of the Machine it used the action in.
Suicidal Bravery. A friendly model successfully charges two models with the same charge move.
Escort Supplies
Escort Supplies
  

                  * Forces
Before picking their Forces, the players must decide who is the attacker and who is the defender in this scenario. In a campaign, the player with the most models in their Warband (ignoring their Threshold Values or Field Strength) is the attacker, and their opponent is the defender. If both players have the same number of models, and in one-off games, the players roll-off and the winner must decide who will be the attacker and who will be the defender.
The defender will additionally need three transports: light vehicle models such as trucks or APCs.
                  * The Battlefield
The battlefield is 48”x48”. The players roll-off and the winner sets up the terrain for the game, with the following additional rules.
Roads
Make sure there are three open roads from one side of the battlefield to the other, at least as wide as the transports.
                  * Deployment
The defender’s deployment zone is the green zone at the bottom of the shown map, while the attacker’s is the red area at the top. The players then alternate deploying their models one at a time, starting with the player who has more models in their Warband (roll-off if both players have the same number of models). Models must be set up wholly within their own Deployment Zone. If a player runs out of models to set up, the other player sets up all their remaining models, one after another until they have none left. Once the players have set up their models, deployment ends and the game begins.
Infiltrators
Infiltrators can deploy normally or by using their special deployment rules. The attacker’s Infiltrators cannot be deployed within 12” of any transport.
                  * The Transport
At the start of the game, before deployment, the defender places a transport in the center of each of the three roads, touching the table edge. Keep track of the Supply inside each transport, beginning at 3.
Transports count as models that belong to the defender, as well as Impassable Terrain that provides cover as normal. A model can climb on them as normal for terrain, and moves with it, but when a transport moves, each model on top of it must make a Success Roll to avoid falling off before the transport moves. A model that falls off also falls Down and must roll on the Injury table.
The transports cannot be forced to move in any way, their movement cannot be reduced or prevented, they cannot fall Down for any reason, and they have -3 Armour. They have the ARTIFICIAL and VEHICLE Keywords unless the defender is Tyranids.
Whenever a Downed or Out of Action result is scored against a transport, treat it as a Minor Hit, one Supply is removed from the transport, and the attacker places a Crate Marker anywhere within 3” of that transport. The crate can be placed on one of the attacker’s models, in which case that model is carrying the crate (see below). If the transport has 0 Supply, instead nothing happens.
A transport is never activated. Instead, at the end of each Turn, move each transport 10” along its road, towards the opposite edge of the table from the one it began at (this counts as Activation for the purpose of ongoing effects). Transports can move through any model. When a transport moves onto or through a model, that model must roll on the Injury chart, treating any Minor Hit result as Down instead. If a transport would end its movement overlapping any other model, move that model to the nearest open space after it rolls on the Injury Chart.
At the end of the fifth Turn, the transports move off the battlefield and the game ends (after they run over any models in the way).
                  * Supplies
As an Action with no Success Roll, a model within 1” of a Supply Crate Marker can pick up that marker. Put the Supply Crate Marker in contact with the model’s base to show it is carrying it. The only thing a model carrying a crate can do is take Move or Retreat Actions - it cannot do anything else while it has the crate.
                  * At the end of its Activation, you can say that a model that is carrying a crate will either drop it or hand it to a friendly model that is within 1". When a model drops the crate, place it within 1" of the model and not in contact with any other models. When a model hands the crate on, place the Supply Crate Marker in contact with the other model’s base.
                  * The model can destroy the crate as an Action that requires no Success Roll. If it does, remove the Supply Crate Marker from the game.
                  * If a model carrying a crate is taken Down or Out of Action, or is chosen as the target of a Melee Attack, it immediately drops the crate as described above.
                  * If a model belonging to the attacker that is carrying a crate finishes its Activation in contact with any edge of the battlefield, it can escape with the crate; remove the model and the Supply Crate Marker from the battlefield. The model is still considered part of the Warband for the purposes of Morale Checks.
                  * If a model belonging to the defender that is carrying a crate finishes its Activation in contact with a transport that has less than 3 Supply, it can return the crate to that transport. Remove the Supply Crate Marker from the battlefield and increase the Supply of that transport by 1.
Game Length
The battle lasts until the transports leave the battlefield at the end of the fifth Turn.
                  * Victory Conditions
A player wins this scenario immediately if there are no enemy models on the battlefield or if the opposing Warband flees (typically due to failing a Morale Check). Otherwise, the player with more Victory Points at the end of the game is the winner.
Victory Points
                  * The defender earns 2 VP for each Supply on a transport when it moves off the battlefield.
                  * The attacker earns 4 VP for each of their models that escaped with a Supply Crate Marker.
                  * The attacker earns 3 VP for each of their models on the battlefield that is carrying a Supply Crate Marker at the end of the game.
                  * The attacker earns 2 VP for each destroyed Supply Crate Marker.
                  * The attacker earns 1 VP for each Supply Crate Marker on the battlefield that is not carried by any model at the end of the game.
Players earn 1 VP for each scored Glorious Deed, as normal.
                  * Glorious Deeds
Beep Beep! Make an enemy move into the area in front of a transport due to a friendly model’s Action, and the transport moves through or onto that model at the end of the Turn.
Empty The Coffers (Attacker Only). At least one transport has 0 Supplies at the end of the second Turn. In a campaign game, treat this Glorious Deed as having been performed by 1 ELITE model from the Warband that has the LEADER Keyword if you have one available.
Fickle Luck. Spend 2 or more BLOOD MARKERS on a single enemy’s Success Roll (INFECTION MARKERS also count, but STUN MARKERS do not).
Personal Revenge. A friendly model uses a Bloodbath to take an enemy model Out of Action that had taken a friendly model Out of Action earlier in the game.
Shotgun! A model that began its Activation on top of a transport takes an enemy model Out of Action without moving off of that transport during that Activation.
Take Backsies. A friendly model takes an enemy model that is carrying a Supply Crate Marker Out of Action and picks up that crate in the same Activation.
Well Guarded (Defender Only). Each transport has 3 Supplies at the end of the second Turn. In a campaign game, treat this Glorious Deed as having been performed by 1 ELITE model from the Warband that has the LEADER Keyword if you have one available.
Fragile Bounty
Fragile Bounty
  

                  * Forces
No special restrictions apply to the models the players can include in their Forces in this scenario.
                  * The Battlefield
The players roll-off and the winner sets up the terrain for the game, with the following additional rules.
Objectives
Place five 1” objective markers, one in the center of the battlefield, and the other four symmetrically (mirrored between table halves, opposite quarters, or both), each at least 10” away from other objectives.
Controlling Objectives
A player controls an objective if they have more models within 3” of that objective that are not Down than their opponent.
                  * Deployment
The player that did not set up the terrain chooses which Deployment Zone will be theirs. The other Deployment Zone is their opponent’s. The players then alternate deploying their models one at a time, starting with the player who has more models in their Warband (roll-off if both players have the same number of models). Models must be set up wholly within their own Deployment Zone. If a player runs out of models to set up, the other player sets up all their remaining models, one after another until they have none left. Once the players have set up their models, deployment ends and the game begins.
Infiltrators
Infiltrators can deploy normally or by using their special deployment rules. They cannot be deployed within 6” of an objective.
                  * Fragile Spoils
Keep track of the Durability of each Objective, beginning at 6 Durability for each Objective besides the center objective, and 10 Durability for the center objective.
Whenever one of the following occurs, remove 1 Durability from an Objective. Once an Objective reaches 0 Durability, remove it from the battlefield.
                  * A model passes within 3” of the Objective while Charging.
                  * A model passes within 3” of the Objective while Dashing.
                  * A model is Downed or taken Out of Action while within 3” of the Objective.
                  * Consume Bounty
A model within 1” of an Objective whose owner controls that Objective can take the Consume Action.
                  * Consume. Reduce the Durability of the Objective by 1. This model gains a Blessing Marker.
Game Length
At the end of the fourth Turn, one player rolls a D6. On a 1 or 2, the game ends immediately. On a 3 or more, the game will end at the end of the fifth Turn.
                  * Victory Conditions
A player wins this scenario immediately if there are no enemy models on the battlefield or if the opposing Warband flees (typically due to failing a Morale Check). Otherwise, the player with more Victory Points at the end of the game is the winner.
Victory Points
At the end of each round, each player earns VP equal to the current Durability of each objective they control, except for the center objective during the first Turn.
Players earn 3 VP for each scored Glorious Deed, instead of the normal 1.
                  * Glorious Deeds
Bloody Spoils. A friendly model takes a Down enemy Out of Action with a Bloodbath while that enemy is within 3” of an Objective.
Cast Them Down. A friendly model causes an enemy model to Fall from a height of at least 3" (e.g. by taking the enemy model Down near a ledge, or by forcing it off a ledge in some way).
Elite Hunter. A friendly model takes two enemy models with the ELITE Keyword Out of Action.
Preservation. A friendly model controls an objective that has not lost any Durability at the end of the fourth or fifth turn.
Ruin. A friendly model causes 3 or more Durability to be lost from a single Objective during a single Activation.
Show of Mercy. A friendly model Retreats from Melee Combat with an enemy that is Down.
Spec Ops
Spec Ops
  

                  * Forces
No special restrictions apply to the models the players can include in their Forces in this scenario.
                  * The Battlefield
The players roll-off and the winner sets up the terrain for the game, with the following additional rules.
Objectives
Place five 1” objective markers, one in the center of the battlefield, and the other four in the center of each table quarter, as shown.
Table Sections
The battlefield is divided into five sections: everything within 1/6 the width of the table counts as the center area, and the four quarters of the table excluding that center area as the quarter areas. Deployment zones are opposite quarter areas.
                  * Deployment
The player that did not set up the terrain chooses which Deployment Zone will be theirs. The other Deployment Zone is their opponent’s. The players then alternate deploying their models one at a time, starting with the player who has more models in their Warband (roll-off if both players have the same number of models). Models must be set up wholly within their own Deployment Zone. If a player runs out of models to set up, the other player sets up all their remaining models, one after another until they have none left. Once the players have set up their models, deployment ends and the game begins.
Infiltrators
Infiltrators can deploy normally or by using their special deployment rules.
                  * Tactical Missions
At the start of the game, before deployment, each player secretly chooses three of the following Tactical Missions. At the end of any Turn after the first, a player can reveal one or more of their Missions for which they have satisfied the requirements to score that Mission, earning VP as described. Once revealed, a Mission cannot be scored again by that player.
Home Field
Requirement. Control the objective in your own Deployment Zone.
Reward. 1 VP
Focused Control
Requirement. Control the objective in the center of the battlefield.
Reward. 3 VP
Takeover
Requirement. Control the objective in your opponent’s Deployment Zone.
Reward. 4 VP
Split Decision
Requirement. Control one or both objectives that are not the center objective or an objective in a Deployment Zone.
Reward. 1 VP, or 2 VP if you control both
Big Game
Requirement. Took an enemy ELITE model Out of Action during this Turn.
Reward. 2
Assassinate
Requirement. Took the enemy Leader Out of Action during this Turn.
Reward. 3
Efficiency
Requirement. More enemy models than friendly models were taken Out of Action this Turn.
Reward. 2
Keep Safe
Requirement. No friendly models were taken Out of Action this Turn.
Reward. 2
Surround
Requirement. At least one friendly model is in each of the four quarter areas, but none in the center area.
Reward. 3
Game Length
At the end of the fourth Turn, if any Tactical Missions remain unrevealed, one player rolls a D6. On a 1 or 2, the game ends immediately. On a 3 or more, the game will end at the end of the fifth Turn. If all Tactical Missions are revealed, the game ends immediately.
                  * Victory Conditions
A player wins this scenario immediately if there are no enemy models on the battlefield or if the opposing Warband flees (typically due to failing a Morale Check). Otherwise, the player with more Victory Points at the end of the game is the winner.
Victory Points
Players earn VP based on their successful Tactical Missions.
Players earn 1 VP for each scored Glorious Deed, as normal.
                  * Glorious Deeds
Death From Above. A friendly model takes an enemy model Out of Action with a Melee Attack that has the Diving Charge modifier.
Hold your Ground. A Warband is the first to pass a Morale Check in this game. In a campaign game, treat this Glorious Deed as having been performed by 1 ELITE model from the Warband that has the LEADER Keyword if you have one available.
In and Out. You reveal and score all of your Tactical Missions before the fourth Turn begins. If both players qualify, then neither scores this Glorious Deed. In a campaign game, treat this Glorious Deed as having been performed by 1 ELITE model from the Warband that is on the battlefield.
Live Dangerously. Retreat from Melee Combat twice with one model during the game.
Sniper. A friendly model takes an enemy ELITE model Out of Action with a Ranged Weapon Attack that has the Long Range modifier.
Total Control. Control 3 or more Objectives at the end of a Turn with all of your Tactical Missions revealed on previous Turns.
High Speed
High Speed
  

                  * Forces
No special restrictions apply to the models the players can include in their Forces in this scenario.
                  * The Battlefield
The players roll-off and the winner sets up the terrain for the game, with the following additional rules.
Traffic
All terrain is considered to be moving platforms: vehicles, icebergs, stampeding beasts, etc. Terrain should be thick, but be mostly made up of many smaller pieces, the majority of which should allow models to stand on them. Cover, raised areas, and even full buildings are also appropriate, either on top of other raised terrain, or assumed to be somehow moving as well.
At least one piece of terrain must be equidistant between deployment zones, and at least four other terrain pieces must be wholly within 6” of the center line of the battlefield.
Choose a direction traffic is moving.
Objective
Place five 1” objective markers, one on a piece of terrain that is equidistant between deployment zones, one on each of the two terrain pieces wholly within 6” of the center line of the battlefield that are closest to one player's deployment zone, and one on each of the two terrain pieces wholly within 6” of the center line of the battlefield that are closest to the other player's deployment zone.
A terrain piece with one of these markers is considered an Objective terrain piece. The exact location of the marker on that terrain piece is unimportant.
Controlling Vehicles
A player controls any piece of terrain if there are more friendly models on the terrain piece than there are enemy models.
                  * Deployment
The player that did not set up the terrain chooses which Deployment Zone will be theirs. The other Deployment Zone is their opponent’s. The players then alternate deploying their models one at a time, starting with the player who has more models in their Warband (roll-off if both players have the same number of models). Models must be set up wholly within their own Deployment Zone, and cannot be deployed on the ground level. If a player runs out of models to set up, the other player sets up all their remaining models, one after another until they have none left. Once the players have set up their models, deployment ends and the game begins.
Infiltrators
Infiltrators can deploy normally or by using their special deployment rules, but cannot be deployed on Objective terrain pieces.
                  * Smog
The vehicles here produce massive amounts of exhaust. The range of any ranged attack made during this mission is reduced by 6”, to a minimum of 12” or the weapon’s normal maximum, whichever is lower.
                  * Traffic
Terrain pieces are always moving, but the battlefield follows them. Whenever a model moves onto or falls onto the ground below from above, or begins and ends its Activation there, it must immediately make a Success Roll, falling Down on a failure, and either way, it is moved 9” directly opposite the direction traffic is moving. It stops if it collides with any terrain, and if it does, it immediately suffers an Injury roll with +1 INJURY DICE. If it collides with the edge of the battlefield, it is immediately taken Out of Action.


The ground is also considered Difficult Terrain unless a model is moving in the opposite direction that traffic is moving, at up to a 45 degree angle.
  

Jumping
During this mission, any model can jump between terrain pieces that are up to 1” away without requiring a Jump Success Roll.


Commandeering
A model that is on a piece of terrain that controls it can move that terrain as an Action, with a Success Roll with +1 DICE. This Success Roll is Risky if there are any enemies on the terrain piece. The owner of the model taking this Action can move the terrain piece — counting an entire assembly of a platform and anything on top of it as one piece — up to 6”.


This movement is subject to the same Difficult Terrain as models on the ground, and it cannot move through other terrain pieces. Models moved in this way can leave or enter melee combat, counting as a Charge or Retreat for the moving models as appropriate. A terrain piece can be Commandeered, successfully or not, only once per Turn.
Game Length
This scenario lasts four Turns.
                  * Victory Conditions
A player wins this scenario immediately if there are no enemy models on the battlefield or if the opposing Warband flees (typically due to failing a Morale Check). Otherwise, the player with more Victory Points at the end of the game is the winner.
Victory Points
At the end of each Turn, each player scores 2 VPs for each Objective terrain piece they control.
Players earn 1 VP for each scored Glorious Deed, as normal.
                  * Glorious Deeds
Feigned Retreat. A friendly model Retreats from an enemy that is not Down and then hits that enemy with a ranged attack during the same Activation.
Getaway. A friendly model Commondeers an Objective terrain piece and moves it at least partially into its owner’s Deployment Zone.
Parkour. A friendly model succeeds on two Jump and/or Climb Success Rolls during a single Activation. Does not count automatic success.
The Real Killer. A friendly model takes an enemy model that is on Dangerous or Difficult terrain Out of Action. (The battlefield ground always counts as Difficult Terrain for this purpose.)
Ramming Speed. A friendly model Commondeers a terrain piece and moves it into contact with another terrain piece, then takes an enemy on that second terrain piece Out of Action with a melee attack in the same Activation.
Roadrash. A friendly model causes an enemy model to Fall onto the battlefield ground, off of any terrain (e.g. by taking the enemy model Down near a ledge, or by forcing it off a ledge in some way).
The Ritual
The Ritual
  

                  * Forces
Before picking their Forces, the players must decide who is the attacker and who is the defender in this scenario. If one of the players has a Chaos Warband and their opponent does not, the Chaos Warband is the defender. Otherwise, the players roll-off and the winner must decide who will be the attacker and who will be the defender.
                  * The Battlefield
The players roll-off and the winner sets up the terrain for the game, with the following additional rules.
Objectives
After choosing deployment zones, the defender places six 1” objective markers at least 2” away from their deployment zone, and at least 6” away from one another.
Controlling Objectives
A player controls an objective if they have more models within 3” of that objective that are not Down than their opponent.
                  * Deployment
The attacker’s deployment zone is red zones at the top and bottom of the shown map, while the defender’s is the green area within 3” of the center line. The players then alternate deploying their models one at a time, starting with the player who has more models in their Warband (roll-off if both players have the same number of models). Models must be set up wholly within their own Deployment Zone. If a player runs out of models to set up, the other player sets up all their remaining models, one after another until they have none left. Once the players have set up their models, deployment ends and the game begins.
Infiltrators
Infiltrators can deploy normally or by using their special deployment rules. The attacker must place their Infiltrators at least 8” away from all Objectives.
Game Length
This scenario lasts five Turns.
                  * Victory Conditions
A player wins this scenario immediately if there are no enemy models on the battlefield or if the opposing Warband flees (typically due to failing a Morale Check). Otherwise, the player with more Victory Points at the end of the game is the winner.
Victory Points
At the end of each Turn, if the attacker controls an Objective, it is destroyed and removed from the battlefield, and the attacker earns 3 VP. Then the defender earns 1 VP for each objective still on the battlefield.
Players earn 1 VP for each scored Glorious Deed, as normal.
                  * Glorious Deeds
Banished (Defender Only). Take an enemy model Out of Action while it is within 3” of an Objective the attacker controls.
Bloodletting. A friendly model causes an enemy model to suffer a sixth BLOOD MARKER.
Nuisance (Attacker Only). Destroy 2 Objectives with the same friendly model (i.e. it is within 3” of those Objectives and not Down when they are destroyed).
Reaper. Take at least three enemies Out of Action with one of your models during the battle.
Sabateur (Attacker Only). A friendly model ends the battle in the defender’s Deployment Zone.
Stopgap (Defender Only). No Objectives are destroyed during a single Turn. In a campaign game, treat this Glorious Deed as having been performed by 1 ELITE model from the Warband that has the LEADER Keyword if you have one available.
The Inferno
The Inferno
  

                  * Forces
No special restrictions apply to the models the players can include in their Forces in this scenario.
                  * The Battlefield
The battlefield is 48”x48”. The players roll-off and the winner sets up the terrain for the game, with the following additional rules.
Ore
Place Ore as shown. Each Ore is an objective with a 25mm base.
Safe Buildings
Buildings, large enough to enter and be occupied by a number of models of all sizes, should be placed as shown.
                  * Deployment
The player that did not set up the terrain chooses which Deployment Zone will be theirs. The other Deployment Zone is their opponent’s. The players then alternate deploying their models one at a time, starting with the player who has more models in their Warband (roll-off if both players have the same number of models). Models must be set up wholly within their own Deployment Zone. If a player runs out of models to set up, the other player sets up all their remaining models, one after another until they have none left. Once the players have set up their models, deployment ends and the game begins.
Infiltrators
Infiltrators must be placed at least 4" away from any Ore.
                  * Ore
                  * During the game, any model that ends its move in contact with a piece of Ore picks can pick it up as an Action with no roll required. A model can carry only one Ore at a time. That model is now carrying the object and will carry it until it drops it or is destroyed. At the end of its activation, the model chooses to either keep the object, drop it, or pass it to any friendly model within 1” range as long as neither model is in melee combat with an enemy.
                  * If they pass it, the model receiving the object is considered to have ended their Activation this turn, if it has not have done so yet. If a model carrying an object is taken Out of Action the object is immediately thrown D6” in a direction chosen by the opposing player, though never out of the battlefield. Stop if the object moves into contact with a barrier, just outside it. If it lands on top of a model, the model can immediately pick it up, except if there is also an enemy model in melee combat with it. If the model does not pick it up, it is thrown again.
If a model enters close combat with an enemy, it must place any Ore it is holding on the ground within 1” of it.
The Wall of Fire
________________
Beginning 5" from each of the shown corners, place lines representing Walls of Fire at a 45 degree angle.
At the end of each turn besides the last, move each line representing a Wall of Fire 5" away from its starting corner, directly towards the opposite corner, as shown. At the end of the final turn, the lines each move all the way to the center, putting everything on the battlefield behind them.
Any model that moves into the area behind a Wall of Fire line, or that a Wall of Fire line moves past, even partially, is automatically taken Out of Action. Any Ore it was carrying is lost. Additionally, remove any un-carried Ore a Wall of Fire line moves past, even partially.
Any model or Ore that is entirely inside one of the indicated Safe Buildings ignores the effects of the Walls of Fire, but the Walls block line of sight.
A model that is Downed within 1” of a Wall of Fire, including an entrance/exit to a Safe Building once it is overtaken, must take an Action to avoid falling into it, as with falling off of the edge of raised terrain.
Game Length
The battle lasts for five turns, no matter what.
A failed Morale roll can never cause a warband to retreat during this mission, and always results in becoming Shaken during the next round.
If a player’s entire force is removed, their opponent continues to the end of the game, gathering whatever Ore they can and attempting to get their models inside Safe Buildings before the Walls of Fire incinerate them.
                  * Victory Conditions
A player wins this scenario immediately if there are no enemy models on the battlefield or if the opposing Warband flees (typically due to failing a Morale Check). Otherwise, the player with more Victory Points at the end of the game is the winner.
Victory Points
Ore. At the end of the game, each player earns 10 VP for each Ore any of their models on the battlefield is carrying, or that are inside of a Safe Building they control. A player controls a Safe Building if they have at least one model inside of it, and their enemy has none.
Enemies Killed. Each player earns additional VP equal to the total credit values (including battlekit) of enemy models they took Out of Action during the mission divided by 50, plus the Glory cost of those models divided by 10, rounding down after adding them together.
Note that a model taken Out of Action by a Wall of Fire does not count towards VP unless their opponent forced the model into that Wall by one of their own model's actions. It may well be more honorable to dive into the fire than be killed by the enemy!
Survivors. Each player also earns additional VP equal to the credit values (including battlekit) of their own models that are on the battlefield at the end of the game divided by 100, plus the Glory cost of those models divided by 30, rounding down after adding them together. (These will have to be inside of Safe Buildings, as the Walls of Fire overtake the whole battlefield at the end of the final round).
                  * Glorious Deeds
There are no Glorious Deeds in this scenario, only what remains when the fire dies out.
